purpose: Two-layer configuration subsystem. config.py is the startup/static layer: it loads a .env file once (without overriding the real environment), then reads ~90 environment variables into module-level constants covering model/backend selection, llama.cpp/OpenAI/Codex backends, history-compaction thresholds, token-estimation heuristics, RAG/embeddings, prompt-cache hints, Codex OAuth, the single-source-of-truth role enums (ROLE_GM/NPC/COMPACT), per-role reasoning toggles, and sampling presets. runtime_settings.py is the dynamic/persisted layer: UI-controlled inference knobs (per-role reasoning effort/summary, verbosity, tool_choice, streaming, parallel tools, TTS, max tool hops, max output tokens) stored atomically in gm_lab_settings.json, with validation/clamp/normalize logic, legacy migration, a thread-safe cache, and model-capability reconciliation. These two modules are the configuration single source of truth that every other module (clients, orchestrator, agents, web UI) imports from.

publicSurface.name: config._load_dotenv
publicSurface.behavior: Module-init helper. Loads .env from Path(__file__).with_name('.env') then Path.cwd()/'.env' (deduped). Skips blank/comment lines, strips optional leading 'export ', splits on first '='. Validates key against ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$. NEVER overrides keys already in os.environ. Strips matching surrounding single/double quotes; for unquoted values strips a trailing ' #' inline comment. Called once at import time.

publicSurface.name: config._env_bool
publicSurface.behavior: Reads env var; returns default if unset (None). Otherwise True unless the stripped lowercased value is one of ('0','false','no','off',''). Reused by runtime_settings for several flags via config._env_bool.

publicSurface.name: config (module-level constants)
publicSurface.signature: MODEL, LLAMA_HOST, API_BASE, API_KEY, MAX_TOKENS, MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP, BACKEND, USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS, STREAM_GM_CONTENT, GM_HISTORY_TOKENS, GM_KEEP_TURNS, NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS, NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES, EVENTS_CAP, RUMORS_CAP, CHARS_PER_TOKEN, COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS, PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS, RAG_*, PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, PROMPT_CACHE_RETENTION, LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE, CODEX_*, ROLE_GM, ROLE_NPC, ROLE_COMPACT, REASONING_ROLES, GM_THINK, NPC_THINK, SAMPLING_THINK, SAMPLING_PLAIN
publicSurface.behavior: Public constants imported across the codebase. See dataStructures and invariants for exact values/defaults/types.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.settings_path
publicSurface.behavior: Returns _SETTINGS_PATH (the resolved gm_lab_settings.json path).

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.defaults
publicSurface.behavior: Returns a shallow copy (dict(_DEFAULTS)) of the computed default settings dict.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.options
publicSurface.behavior: Returns the allowed-value lists for the UI: reasoning_efforts, reasoning_summaries, reasoning_roles, text_verbosities, tool_choices, plus max_tool_hops_max=MAX_TOOL_HOPS_CAP(100) and max_output_tokens_max=config.MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP. Single source shared with the frontend input.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.get
publicSurface.behavior: Thread-safe (under _LOCK RLock). Lazily loads from disk into module global _CACHE on first call (_load()), returns a shallow copy dict(_CACHE).

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.update
publicSurface.signature: update(values: dict[str, Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any]
publicSurface.behavior: Thread-safe. Reads current settings, applies _clean(values) on top, _normalize()s, atomically _save()s to disk, updates _CACHE, returns a copy. None/empty input is treated as {}.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.reconcile_for_model
publicSurface.signature: reconcile_for_model(model: dict[str, Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any]
publicSurface.behavior: Adjusts per-role reasoning effort/summary ONLY when model metadata makes them invalid. If model isn't a dict, returns get(). Reads model['supports_reasoning_summaries'] and supported_reasoning_efforts(model). If supports is False forces effort/summary to 'none' for any role not already 'none'. If a role's effort isn't in the supported list (and isn't 'none'), resets to default_effort (from default_reasoning_level/default_reasoning_effort or supported[0] or 'none') and fixes summary. If effort=='none' but summary!='none', forces summary 'none'. Persists via update() only if changes were computed.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.supported_reasoning_efforts
publicSurface.behavior: Extracts a deduped list of supported effort strings from model metadata, trying keys supported_reasoning_levels, supported_reasoning_efforts, reasoning_efforts (in that order). List items may be dicts (reads effort/id/value) or scalars. Returns [] for non-dict input.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.role_settings
publicSurface.signature: role_settings(role: str, settings: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> dict[str, str]
publicSurface.behavior: Resolves a role (via _role, defaults invalid role to ROLE_GM). Returns {'effort': <str>, 'summary': <str>} from <role>_reasoning_effort/_summary, lowercased, defaulting to 'none'. If effort=='none' summary is forced 'none'; if summary not in REASONING_SUMMARIES it's forced 'none'.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.reasoning_enabled
publicSurface.signature: reasoning_enabled(think: bool | None, role: str = '') -> bool
publicSurface.behavior: Returns True only if think is truthy AND role_settings(role)['effort'] != 'none'.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.reasoning_for_request
publicSurface.signature: reasoning_for_request(think: bool | None, role: str = '') -> dict[str, str] | None
publicSurface.behavior: Returns None if not think, or if role effort=='none'. Otherwise {'effort': <effort>} plus 'summary' key only when summary != 'none'. This is the per-request reasoning payload sent to the backend.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.role_reasoning_enabled
publicSurface.behavior: Returns role_settings(role)['effort'] != 'none' (ignores the think flag).

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.tool_choice_for_request
publicSurface.behavior: Returns 'none' if no tools. Otherwise the persisted tool_choice if it's in TOOL_CHOICES, else 'auto'.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.parallel_tool_calls_for_request
publicSurface.behavior: Returns True only if has_tools AND persisted parallel_tool_calls (default True).

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.gm_suggest_options_enabled
publicSurface.signature: gm_suggest_options_enabled(settings: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> bool
publicSurface.behavior: Returns bool of gm_suggest_options (default False). Accepts an optional pre-fetched settings dict to avoid a re-read.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.stream_gm_content_enabled
publicSurface.signature: stream_gm_content_enabled(settings: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> bool
publicSurface.behavior: Returns bool of stream_gm_content (default True).

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.tts_enabled
publicSurface.signature: tts_enabled(settings: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> bool
publicSurface.behavior: Returns bool of tts_enabled (default False). NOTE: there is no public tts_autoplay accessor; tts_autoplay is persisted/cleaned but only readable via get()['tts_autoplay'].

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.max_tool_hops
publicSurface.signature: max_tool_hops(settings: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> int
publicSurface.behavior: Returns max(0, int(max_tool_hops or 0)); returns 0 on TypeError/ValueError. 0 means unlimited/no explicit cap at this layer (cap enforced elsewhere is MAX_TOOL_HOPS_CAP=100).

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings.max_output_tokens
publicSurface.behavior: Returns max(0, int(max_output_tokens or 0)); 0 on error. Always re-reads via get() (no settings param).

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings._load (internal)
publicSurface.behavior: Reads/parses _SETTINGS_PATH as JSON. On FileNotFoundError or ANY Exception returns _normalize({}) (defaults). Non-dict JSON is coerced to {} before normalize.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings._save (internal)
publicSurface.behavior: Atomic write: mkdir parents, json.dumps(ensure_ascii=False, indent=2, sort_keys=True), writes to a tempfile via tempfile.mkstemp in the SAME directory, then os.replace() over the target, finally unlinks the temp if it still exists.

publicSurface.name: runtime_settings._normalize / _clean / _migrate_legacy_reasoning / _role / _string / _bool (internal)
publicSurface.behavior: Validation pipeline; see invariants for exact clamp/normalize/migration rules.

dataStructures.name: gm_lab_settings.json (persisted runtime settings)
dataStructures.fields: JSON object, keys (alphabetically, sort_keys=True): compact_reasoning_effort:str, compact_reasoning_summary:str, gm_reasoning_effort:str, gm_reasoning_summary:str, npc_reasoning_effort:str, npc_reasoning_summary:str, text_verbosity:str, tool_choice:str, stream_gm_content:bool, parallel_tool_calls:bool, gm_suggest_options:bool, tts_enabled:bool, tts_autoplay:bool, max_tool_hops:int, max_output_tokens:int
dataStructures.notes: The reasoning keys are generated dynamically as f'{role}_reasoning_effort'/f'{role}_reasoning_summary' for role in config.REASONING_ROLES=('gm','npc','compact'). Written atomically (tempfile + os.replace), UTF-8, indent=2, sort_keys=True, trailing newline. Observed live file confirms all 15 keys. Path defaults to <module dir>/gm_lab_settings.json, overridable via GM_SETTINGS_PATH. _DEFAULTS is the same shape (15 keys).

dataStructures.name: _DEFAULTS (computed default settings dict)
dataStructures.fields: Same 15 keys as the JSON. Reasoning defaults per role: gm=(GM_<env> or _BASE_REASONING_EFFORT='low', _BASE_REASONING_SUMMARY='auto'), npc=same base, compact=('none','none'). text_verbosity='default', tool_choice='auto', stream_gm_content=config.STREAM_GM_CONTENT(True), parallel_tool_calls=True, gm_suggest_options=False, tts_enabled=False, tts_autoplay=False, max_tool_hops=0, max_output_tokens=int(config.MAX_TOKENS or 0)
dataStructures.notes: Built at import time. Reasoning bases come from config.CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT/_SUMMARY via _ROLE_REASONING_BASE, then overridable per-role-and-kind by env GM_<ROLE>_REASONING_<EFFORT|SUMMARY>.

dataStructures.name: options() output (UI allowed-values contract)
dataStructures.fields: reasoning_efforts:list, reasoning_summaries:list, reasoning_roles:list, text_verbosities:list, tool_choices:list, max_tool_hops_max:int(100), max_output_tokens_max:int(config.MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP=200000)
dataStructures.notes: Sent to the frontend so the UI inputs and backend clamps share one source.

dataStructures.name: model metadata dict (input to reconcile_for_model / supported_reasoning_efforts)
dataStructures.fields: supports_reasoning_summaries:bool|None, supported_reasoning_levels|supported_reasoning_efforts|reasoning_efforts:list[str|dict], default_reasoning_level:str, default_reasoning_effort:str, default_reasoning_summary:str
dataStructures.notes: List items may be dicts with effort/id/value keys, or plain strings. This is the GET /v1/models capability shape consumed (not produced) by this subsystem.

dataStructures.name: SAMPLING_THINK / SAMPLING_PLAIN (sampling preset dicts)
dataStructures.fields: SAMPLING_THINK={'temperature':0.6,'top_p':0.95,'top_k':20,'min_p':0,'presence_penalty':1.5}; SAMPLING_PLAIN={'temperature':0.7,'top_p':0.80,'top_k':20,'min_p':0,'presence_penalty':1.5}
dataStructures.notes: Qwen3.6-recommended sampling, selected per-request by the think flag. top_k/min_p are llama.cpp-only fields (see USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS gate).

dataStructures.name: RAG embeddings cache (SQLite)
dataStructures.fields: Path = GM_RAG_CACHE_PATH default <module dir>/gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3
dataStructures.notes: Only the path is configured here; the schema lives in the RAG module, not in config.py. File exists on disk (confirmed via glob).

controlFlow: config.py executes top-to-bottom at import: _load_dotenv() runs first (side-effect: mutates os.environ from .env files, non-overriding), then every constant is computed from os.environ with defaults. There is no class/state; it is a flat constants module. Import order matters: anything reading os.environ before importing config will not see .env-injected vars. runtime_settings.py imports config at top, computes _BASE_REASONING_* and _ROLE_REASONING_BASE and the _DEFAULTS dict at import time (a for-loop over REASONING_ROLES builds the reasoning keys, then a dict .update adds the rest). Runtime access is lazy + cached: get() acquires _LOCK (threading.RLock), populates module-global _CACHE via _load() on first call, returns copies. update() is the only mutator path: get()->_clean(new values)->_normalize()->_save() (atomic)->set _CACHE. _load() is fail-safe (any read/parse error yields defaults). _normalize() layering order is: start from dict(_DEFAULTS); apply _clean(_migrate_legacy_reasoning(data)); then apply _clean(data) (raw data wins over migrated legacy); then post-pass forces summary='none' wherever effort=='none'. reconcile_for_model() is an external-capability-driven adjuster that calls update() only when it computes changes. All public read helpers (role_settings, reasoning_for_request, tool_choice_for_request, etc.) call get() unless passed a pre-fetched settings dict.
externalDeps: os.environ (env vars, ~90 read across both modules)
.env file (optional, in module dir and/or cwd)
filesystem: gm_lab_settings.json (read/write, atomic via tempfile+os.replace)
filesystem: gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 (path only, owned by RAG module)
threading (RLock for cache)
tempfile, json, pathlib (stdlib)
GET /v1/models response shape (consumed indirectly via reconcile_for_model/model metadata)
Local llama.cpp server at LLAMA_HOST default http://localhost:8080
Local embeddings server default http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/embeddings
ChatGPT Codex backend default https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex
Codex OAuth loopback on port 1455
internalDeps: runtime_settings imports config (REASONING_ROLES, ROLE_GM/NPC/COMPACT, CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT/SUMMARY, STREAM_GM_CONTENT, MAX_TOKENS, MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP, _env_bool)
config imports only stdlib (os, re, pathlib) — it has NO internal deps and must be importable first/standalone
Both are leaf-ish config modules imported by clients, orchestrator, agents, RAG, and the web/SSE UI (per project description; not visible in these two files)
invariants: ENUM SINGLE SOURCE: ROLE_GM='gm', ROLE_NPC='npc', ROLE_COMPACT='compact'; REASONING_ROLES=(ROLE_GM, ROLE_NPC, ROLE_COMPACT). config comment: 'Все строковые значения ролей берутся отсюда... Нигде больше эти строки не должны хардкодиться.' runtime_settings re-exports REASONING_ROLES = config.REASONING_ROLES. The Rust port MUST derive all role keys (e.g. settings JSON keys 'gm_reasoning_effort') from this one enum, never hardcode the strings.

TOKEN ESTIMATION HEURISTIC: CHARS_PER_TOKEN = int(GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN or '3') default 3. Comment states it is the SINGLE source for _SYS_EST, _estimate_tokens, and BOTH the GM and NPC compaction gates. A faithful port must use exactly chars/3 for token pressure estimation everywhere.

GM HISTORY COMPACTION GATE: GM_HISTORY_TOKENS default 100000; GM_KEEP_TURNS default 3. Design intent (comment): compact LATE, only when the long context approaches the working ceiling; before that keep append-only history so prompt/KV-cache can reuse the identical prefix. So compaction trigger ~ estimated tokens >= GM_HISTORY_TOKENS, keeping the last GM_KEEP_TURNS turns verbatim. (Exact trigger arithmetic lives in the orchestrator, not here, but the thresholds and chars/3 estimator originate here.)

NPC HISTORY COMPACTION GATE: NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS default 64000; NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES default 6. Same pattern: keep last 6 exchanges verbatim, summarize the rest when NPC history estimate crosses 64000 tokens.

COMPACT SUMMARIZE INPUT WINDOW: COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS = int(GM_COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS or '12000') default 12000. This is how many CHARACTERS of history are fed into the summarize call — for BOTH GM and NPC and ALL backends. Quote: 'Сколько символов истории отдаём в summarize-вызов компакта (ГМ и NPC, все бэкенды).'
PRE-TOOL PRELUDE BRIEF: PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS = int(GM_PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS or '4000') default 4000 — how many chars of pending-resolution context go into the GM pre-tool prelude.
IN-MEMORY CAPS: EVENTS_CAP=GM_EVENTS_CAP default 400 (Events kept in memory); RUMORS_CAP=GM_RUMORS_CAP default 80 (world rumors kept in memory). These are ring/trim caps.

PROMPT-CACHE / KV-CACHE: PROMPT_CACHE_KEY (GM_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, default '') and PROMPT_CACHE_RETENTION (GM_PROMPT_CACHE_RETENTION, default '') are OPTIONAL provider cache hints — default OFF because many OpenAI-compatible proxies reject unknown fields. LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE = int(GM_LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE or '0') default 0 — the llama.cpp cache_reuse param. CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY defaults to PROMPT_CACHE_KEY; comment: empty => CodexClient uses the STABLE thread-id of the current dialog as the cache key, like official Codex. THREAD-ID/CACHE-KEY STABILITY is load-bearing for KV reuse — the port must keep the cache key stable across turns and only emit these fields when non-empty.

APPEND-ONLY HISTORY: the GM_HISTORY_TOKENS comment explicitly motivates keeping history append-only as long as possible so the prompt prefix stays identical and prompt/KV-cache reuse holds. Compaction is the only mutation and happens late.

SECRET / HIDDEN-CANON ISOLATION: RAG index holds ONLY documents open to GM/player (public facts, visible scene, NPC locations, unconfirmed rumors). Quote: 'Hidden canon и NPC secrets туда не попадают.' The embeddings cache (gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3) must never contain hidden canon or NPC secrets — a hard isolation rule for the port.

REASONING ENABLE LOGIC: reasoning_enabled(think, role) = think AND role effort != 'none'. reasoning_for_request returns None when think is falsy OR effort=='none'; includes 'summary' only when summary!='none'. effort=='none' ALWAYS forces summary='none' (enforced in role_settings, _normalize, and reconcile_for_model).

PER-ROLE REASONING DEFAULTS: GM_THINK default True, NPC_THINK default False (config). Compact role base reasoning = ('none','none'). _BASE_REASONING_EFFORT='low', _BASE_REASONING_SUMMARY='auto' (from CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT/SUMMARY). GM/NPC inherit the Codex base; compaction defaults to no reasoning.

VALIDATION ALLOWLISTS: REASONING_EFFORTS=('none','minimal','low','medium','high','xhigh'); REASONING_SUMMARIES=('auto','concise','detailed','none'); TEXT_VERBOSITIES=('default','low','medium','high'); TOOL_CHOICES=('auto','required','none'). NOTE asymmetry in _clean: reasoning_effort is accepted as ANY non-empty lowercased string (comment: 'Codex accepts custom non-empty effort values from model catalogs') and is NOT restricted to REASONING_EFFORTS; reasoning_summary, text_verbosity, tool_choice ARE strictly restricted to their tuples. The port must replicate this: effort is open-set, the others are closed-set.

CLAMP LOGIC: max_tool_hops clamped to [0, MAX_TOOL_HOPS_CAP=100] in _clean (min(100, max(0, int(...)))); on parse error falls back to base value. max_output_tokens clamped to [0, config.MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP] in _clean (default cap 200000 via GM_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP); on error falls back to base. max_tool_hops()/max_output_tokens() accessors additionally floor at 0 and return 0 on error. 0 = unlimited/unset.

NORMALIZE LAYERING ORDER (must port exactly): _normalize starts from dict(_DEFAULTS), applies _clean(_migrate_legacy_reasoning(data)) FIRST, then _clean(data) SECOND (so explicit data wins over migrated legacy), then forces summary='none' for any role whose effort=='none'. update() does get()+_clean(values)+_normalize()+_save().

LEGACY MIGRATION: _migrate_legacy_reasoning maps OLD global keys reasoning_effort/reasoning_summary onto GM and NPC ONLY (not compact). It only sets a per-role key if that key is ABSENT from data. Compact keeps its new default unless the file explicitly has compact_* keys.

_env_bool TRUTHINESS: a value is False ONLY if stripped-lower in ('0','false','no','off',''); everything else (including 'yes','1','true','anything') is True. _bool() in runtime_settings uses the SAME false-set but treats None as False and bool as itself. Replicate exactly.

ATOMIC SAVE: _save uses tempfile.mkstemp in the target's parent dir then os.replace — required for crash-safety; JSON is sort_keys=True, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False (Cyrillic stored raw), trailing '\n'.

CONFIG DERIVED-DEFAULT COUPLING: GM defaults that feed runtime_settings come from config.CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT (default 'low') and CODEX_REASONING_SUMMARY (default 'auto'); STREAM_GM_CONTENT default True feeds stream_gm_content default; MAX_TOKENS feeds max_output_tokens default. Changing config defaults silently changes runtime defaults for fresh installs only (existing JSON wins).

API_BASE and CODEX_BASE_URL are .rstrip('/')'d; MODEL default '' means AUTO-DETECT the loaded model from GET /v1/models (do not depend on a tag). USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS defaults to (BACKEND=='llamacpp') — controls whether llama-only fields chat_template_kwargs/top_k/min_p are sent; external OpenAI providers reject them.

NPC JSON CONTRACT (reasoning/speech/action/claims) and GM tool-arg field names are referenced by the task but are NOT defined in config.py/runtime_settings.py — they live in the agents/orchestrator modules. Flagged in risks; do not invent them from this subsystem.
DETERMINISTIC DICE SEEDING and card_revision are referenced by the task but are NOT present in either of these files — owned by other modules. Flagged in risks.
platformConcerns: Paths use pathlib.Path consistently (Path(__file__).with_name(...), Path.cwd()); cross-platform safe. Rust port should resolve gm_lab_settings.json and gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 relative to a stable app/exe/config dir — note these default to NEXT TO the source file, which in a Tauri bundle should map to an app-data dir, NOT the bundle dir (read-only on macOS).
Atomic write uses os.replace() which is atomic on the SAME filesystem on Windows/macOS/Linux. Rust std::fs::rename has the same semantics; keep temp in the SAME dir as the target (this code does).

tempfile.mkstemp(text=True) — the text flag affects newline translation on Windows; the code then writes '\n' explicitly. Rust should write bytes/LF explicitly and not rely on platform newline translation.

Codex OAuth uses a loopback HTTP server on CODEX_AUTH_PORT default 1455 and CODEX_AUTO_OPEN_BROWSER default True (opens the system browser). Browser-opening and binding a fixed localhost port are OS-touchpoints for the port (webbrowser-equivalent + TCP bind; handle port-in-use).

CODEX_USER_AGENT/ORIGINATOR ('codex_cli_rs') and CODEX_CLIENT_VERSION mimic the official Codex CLR client — likely tied to TLS/HTTP impersonation expectations of the ChatGPT backend. The Python stack elsewhere may use curl_cffi for TLS impersonation; not in these files but the UA/originator constants imply it. Rust port may need a TLS-impersonating HTTP client for the Codex backend.

threading.RLock — map to std::sync::Mutex/RwLock (reentrant not strictly needed since update() calls get() which re-locks the same RLock; a plain Mutex in Rust would DEADLOCK here, so refactor to a non-reentrant lock with an internal un-locked _load helper, or use a reentrant pattern).
Default endpoints are IPv4 localhost (http://localhost:8080, http://127.0.0.1:8080) — fine cross-platform; note localhost vs 127.0.0.1 mismatch between LLAMA_HOST and RAG_EMBEDDINGS_URL defaults.
No subprocess/Popen/ffmpeg/GPU code in these two files (TTS flags exist but the TTS engine is elsewhere).
rustNotes: Model two structs: a static `Config` (loaded once at startup from env+.env) and a `RuntimeSettings` store. Config fields are immutable after load; RuntimeSettings is mutable, persisted, cache-backed.

Replicate _load_dotenv precisely: search <exe/config dir>/.env then cwd/.env, dedup, skip blank/#, strip optional 'export ', split first '=', validate key regex ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$, NEVER override existing env, strip matching surrounding quotes else strip trailing ' #' comment. Consider the `dotenvy` crate but it does NOT do the inline-comment stripping or the non-override-with-this-exact-precedence the same way — a hand-rolled loader matching this logic is safer for a faithful port.
Define role enum once: enum Role { Gm, Npc, Compact } with as_str()='gm'/'npc'/'compact' and derive all JSON keys via format!("{role}_reasoning_effort"). Never hardcode 'gm_reasoning_effort'.

Settings JSON: use serde_json with a BTreeMap or #[serde] struct; emit with sorted keys + 2-space indent + trailing newline + non-escaped UTF-8 to byte-match the Python output (serde_json::to_string_pretty sorts via BTreeMap and does not escape non-ASCII by default — good).

Atomic save: write to NamedTempFile in the target's parent dir, then persist/rename over the target (tempfile crate's NamedTempFile::persist = rename). Floor max_tool_hops at [0,100] and max_output_tokens at [0,cap] during deserialize/clean.

reasoning_effort is an OPEN-SET string (any non-empty value allowed, lowercased), while summary/verbosity/tool_choice are CLOSED-SET — use a validating String for effort and an enum (with fallback) for the others. Do not force effort into an enum.

Concurrency: wrap the cached settings in a Mutex<Option<HashMap>> behind a struct; provide get()/update()/reconcile_for_model(). Because Python uses a reentrant lock and update() calls get(), implement an internal lock-free _load_or_get under one lock acquisition to avoid Rust mutex re-entry deadlock.
_load is intentionally fail-soft (any error -> defaults). Mirror with: on read/parse error, log and return normalized defaults; never panic.
Keep the normalize layering order EXACT: defaults -> clean(migrate_legacy(data)) -> clean(data) -> force summary 'none' where effort 'none'. This ordering is observable behavior.
Expose options()/defaults() as the single contract to the Tauri frontend so UI min/max (max_tool_hops_max=100, max_output_tokens_max=cap) and allowed lists stay in lockstep with the backend clamps.
Treat token estimation as a single function tokens(s) = s.chars().count()/3 (CHARS_PER_TOKEN) and route GM/NPC compaction gates and SYS estimate through it.
For booleans, replicate the false-set ('0','false','no','off','') and treat all else as true; do not use serde's default bool parsing for env values.
suggestedCrates: serde + serde_json (settings (de)serialization, sorted-key pretty output)
tempfile (NamedTempFile + atomic persist/rename, mirrors mkstemp+os.replace)
directories or dirs (resolve cross-platform app-data dir for gm_lab_settings.json / embeddings sqlite, instead of next-to-binary)
once_cell or std::sync::OnceLock (compute Config/_DEFAULTS once at startup)
parking_lot (Mutex/RwLock; or std::sync) for the settings cache
regex (key validation in the .env loader)
open or webbrowser (CODEX_AUTO_OPEN_BROWSER -> open system browser)
reqwest, plus possibly a TLS-impersonating client (e.g. rquest / curl-impersonate bindings) if the Codex backend requires UA/JA3 impersonation implied by CODEX_USER_AGENT='codex_cli_rs'
dotenvy (only if its semantics are wrapped/overridden to match _load_dotenv exactly; otherwise hand-roll)
rusqlite (for the RAG embeddings cache, owned by the RAG module but path configured here)
risks: NPC JSON contract field names (reasoning/speech/action/claims) and GM tool-arg field names are NOT in these two files — they live in agents/orchestrator. Mapping them here would be invention; must be sourced from those modules.
Deterministic dice seeding is NOT in config.py/runtime_settings.py — no seed constant or RNG config here. Owner module unknown from this subsystem.
card_revision is NOT defined here — owned by the card/canon module.

The exact compaction TRIGGER arithmetic (e.g. >= vs >, how 'keep last N' interacts with the token estimate, whether the SYS prompt estimate _SYS_EST is subtracted) is NOT in these files — only the thresholds (GM_HISTORY_TOKENS, GM_KEEP_TURNS, NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS, NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES, CHARS_PER_TOKEN, COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS) originate here; the orchestrator implements the gate.

What exactly gets summarized vs kept verbatim during compaction is described only by intent (keep last N turns/exchanges, summarize the rest) — the concrete prompt and selection live in the compaction module, not here.

TLS impersonation (curl_cffi) is implied by CODEX_USER_AGENT/ORIGINATOR but not present in these files; whether the Codex backend hard-requires JA3 impersonation must be verified against the codex client module before choosing the Rust HTTP client.
tts_autoplay has no dedicated public accessor (only tts_enabled does), yet it is persisted and cleaned — callers must read it via get()['tts_autoplay']; easy to miss in the port.
Default file locations (next to source) are wrong for a packaged Tauri app; the port must decide the app-data dir and the env overrides (GM_SETTINGS_PATH, GM_RAG_CACHE_PATH) must still work.
reconcile_for_model depends on the precise GET /v1/models metadata key names; if the Rust model-listing layer renames keys, reconciliation silently no-ops.
LLAMA_HOST default uses 'localhost' while RAG_EMBEDDINGS_URL default uses '127.0.0.1' on the same port 8080 — a latent inconsistency to preserve or deliberately fix in the port.

purpose: The orchestrator is the per-turn driver: a player's free-text action becomes a stream of UI/CLI events. It runs the GM LLM in a tool-call hop loop (bounded by max_tool_hops), dispatches every GM tool (ask_npc, roll_dice, get_world_fact, set_scene, query/update_world_state, get_npc_profile, advance_time, update_player_character, move_npc/set_npc_presence, set_npc_whereabouts, ask_player, tool_search), invokes NPC sub-agent LLMs on isolated per-NPC clients/threads via ask_npc, supports a generator-critic correction loop (GM re-calls ask_npc with a `correction` that overwrites the same pending draft), maintains an append-only event log with two-phase NPC draft/commit, and produces dual-channel tool results (player-facing `full` text vs model-history `model` text with system-reminders). It also owns history/NPC compaction triggers and token-pressure accounting (it delegates the actual summarization to llm_client/agents).

publicSurface.name: run_turn
publicSurface.behavior: Generator entrypoint for one player turn. Yields event dicts (see ev()) for the web/SSE UI and CLI; drives _drive, then yields a final {kind:'meta_total'} event with per-call metas, context_usage, and cumulative run usage. THE primary external API.

publicSurface.name: Session
publicSurface.signature:
class Session:
    def __init__(self, client, world: world_mod.World | None = None)
publicSurface.behavior: Holds all between-turn party state: GM/NPC clients+threads, gm_messages/gm_summary, npc_messages/npc_summaries, loaded_gm_tools, append-only events log, delivered/_shown visibility boundaries, pending NPC drafts, commitments, world_query_seen cache, run_usage. Constructed by server/dialog_store; serialized by dialog_store.

publicSurface.name: Session.ensure_npc_client
publicSurface.behavior: Returns (creating if needed) the dedicated per-NPC model client/thread used for cache-key isolation and the NPC's private history. Reuses serialized model/session_id/thread_id from npc_client_state.

publicSurface.name: Session.remember_npc_client
publicSurface.behavior: Snapshots a live NPC client's model/session_id/thread_id into npc_client_state for persistence/cache-key stability.

publicSurface.name: Session.reset_npc_memory
publicSurface.behavior: Manual single-NPC memory wipe: clears npc_messages/summaries/client_state/clients/commitments/pending AND pins delivered[npc_id]=_shown[npc_id]=_seq so the reset NPC re-sees only future events. Returns True for any real NPC, False only for unknown/empty id. Never called automatically.

publicSurface.name: Session.apply_debug_edit
publicSurface.signature: def apply_debug_edit(self, npc_id: str, data: dict) -> bool
publicSurface.behavior: Single source of truth for /debug/npc edits: applies card fields, presence (only on actual present-state change), whereabouts, and optional reset_memory (only on truthy flag). Returns False/no-op for unknown npc_id.

publicSurface.name: Session.set_model_for_all_clients
publicSurface.behavior: Sets client_model and propagates model to GM client, all live NPC clients, and all npc_client_state entries.

publicSurface.name: Session.set_run_usage
publicSurface.signature: def set_run_usage(self, usage: dict | None) -> None
publicSurface.behavior: Loads cumulative run usage (used on session restore).

publicSurface.name: Session.add_turn_usage
publicSurface.behavior: Folds one turn's _meta_total into run_usage, bucketing tokens by call scope (gm/npc/other). Returns a copy. Called by run_turn.

publicSurface.name: Session.npc_history_text
publicSurface.signature: def npc_history_text(self, npc_id: str, max_messages: int = 8) -> str
publicSurface.behavior: Human-readable rendering of an NPC's summary + last N messages (used in debug/npc_history events). Rewrites historical 'CURRENT SITUATION' marker to 'PREVIOUS NPC SITUATION'.

publicSurface.name: Session.next_sid
publicSurface.behavior: Returns next stream-element id 's{N}' for grouping streamed deltas with their finalized rows.

publicSurface.name: Session.record_public
publicSurface.signature: def record_public(self, actor: str, kind: str, speech: str = '', action: str = '')
publicSurface.behavior: Appends a public Event (player/dice) immediately to the log with present witnesses; returns the Event.

publicSurface.name: Session.record_player_for
publicSurface.behavior: Records/extends the turn's single player Event with witnesses {player, npc_id}; private routing — only NPCs the GM ask_npc'd hear it; falls back to public at turn end if never called.

publicSurface.name: Session.draft
publicSurface.signature: def draft(self, npc_id, speech, action, claims, user_message=None, assistant_message=None, witnesses=None)
publicSurface.behavior: Stores a provisional NPC reply in session.pending under a stable seq (correction overwrites same seq). Empty speech+action drops the pending entry. NOT logged until commit_turn.

publicSurface.name: Session.snapshot_shown
publicSurface.behavior: Records _shown[npc_id]=_seq: how far this NPC actually saw on this wake, so delivered advances only to what was shown, not to log end.

publicSurface.name: Session.observations
publicSurface.behavior: Returns text of what an NPC saw since delivered[npc_id]: committed events it witnessed (excluding its own and the current player action, which the GM puts in situation) + OTHER NPCs' provisional pending drafts this turn (intra-turn awareness). Capped to last _OBS_CAP=12.

publicSurface.name: Session.commit_text
publicSurface.behavior: Returns the NPC's own memory: last _COMMIT_BLOCKS=8 commitment blocks joined by newlines.

publicSurface.name: Session.commit_turn
publicSurface.behavior: End-of-turn: flushes each pending NPC draft into the event log + commitments (last 8) + npc_messages history + world.record_rumor; advances delivered to _shown; sorts events by seq, caps to EVENTS_CAP=400; clears pending/_shown. Rejected redrafts (overwritten in pending) never reach the log.

publicSurface.name: Session.reset_world_query_cache
publicSurface.behavior: Empties world_query_seen (the per-scope delivered-result dedupe cache). Called after GM history compaction.

publicSurface.name: context_usage
publicSurface.behavior: Approximates active prompt pressure (GM + per-NPC) and the nearest compaction threshold; embedded in meta_total['context'] and used by the UI.

publicSurface.name: ToolExecutionResult
publicSurface.signature: @dataclass(frozen=True) class ToolExecutionResult: full: str; model: str; terminal: bool = False
publicSurface.behavior: Dual-channel tool result: `full` = player-facing/UI text, `model` = text appended to GM history (compact + system-reminder), `terminal` = stop the hop loop after this tool. Returned by every _run_tool/_ask_npc path.

publicSurface.name: ev
publicSurface.behavior: Constructs the streamed event dict {kind, agent, data, sid}. The single event envelope for the entire UI/SSE protocol.

dataStructures.name: Event (event-log envelope, streamed via ev())
dataStructures.fields: {kind: str, agent: str|None, data: any, sid: str|None}
dataStructures.notes: Every yielded item from run_turn. `kind` ∈ {player, delta, gm_thinking, gm_narration, meta, meta_total, gm_tool_call, tool_result, tool_search, player_options, dice, world_fact, world_state_update, world_query, npc_profile, time, player_character_update, npc_whereabouts, scene_update, npc_start, npc_history, npc_thinking, npc_speech, gm_reject, error}. `delta` data = {channel: 'gm_thinking'|'gm_narration'|'npc_speech'|'npc_speech', text}. `sid` groups deltas with their finalized row (live replaces streamed text in place; reload skips deltas and renders the final row once).

dataStructures.name: world_mod.Event (append-only scene event)
dataStructures.fields: seq:int (global monotonic), turn:int, actor:str ('player'|npc_id|'gm'), kind:str ('speech'|'action'|'dice'), speech:str, action:str, witnesses:frozenset
dataStructures.notes: Defined in world.py; constructed by orchestrator. Append-only; sorted by seq; capped to EVENTS_CAP=400 at commit_turn. Witnesses gate observations() visibility.

dataStructures.name: session.pending[npc_id] (provisional NPC draft)
dataStructures.fields: {seq:int, speech:str, action:str, claims:list[str], witnesses:frozenset, user_message:dict|None, assistant_message:dict|None}
dataStructures.notes: Cleared each turn. Correction overwrites same seq. Only non-empty drafts committed at turn end. user_message/assistant_message are the OpenAI-format pair appended to npc_messages on commit (only if both present).

dataStructures.name: NPC output dict (out, JSON contract)
dataStructures.fields: {reasoning:str, speech:str, action:str, claims:list[str]}
dataStructures.notes: Produced by agents.npc_turn_stream via NPC_SCHEMA; normalized by agents._norm_npc. orchestrator reads out['reasoning'|'speech'|'action'|'claims']. THE NPC sub-agent JSON contract; field names load-bearing.

dataStructures.name: GM tool call (call)
dataStructures.fields: {name:str, arguments:dict, id:str}
dataStructures.notes: From llm_client._parse_tool_calls. orchestrator normalizes id to 'gm_{sid}_{i}' if blank and normalizes arguments to source schema shape via _normalize_tool_args. Echoed into history as OpenAI tool_calls via _assistant_with_tool_calls (arguments re-serialized compact).

dataStructures.name: GM history message (assistant w/ tool_calls)
dataStructures.fields: {role:'assistant', content:str, tool_calls:[{id, type:'function', function:{name, arguments(compact JSON str)}}]}
dataStructures.notes: Appended to gm_messages as canonical echo. tool result messages: {role:'tool', tool_call_id, content: model-text}. Append-only; old turns must stay byte-identical for prefix cache reuse.

dataStructures.name: ask_npc GM-facing payload (full text JSON)
dataStructures.fields: {npc_id, npc_name, npc_label, speech_ru, action_ru, gm_instruction}
dataStructures.notes: Returned as result.full from _ask_npc. _compact_ask_npc_payload trims to {npc_id, npc_label, speech_ru, action_ru, already_emitted:true, final_narration_rule}. npc_label = player-known label; npc_name = true name (GM-only).

dataStructures.name: update_world_state item args
dataStructures.fields: {op:'add'|'update'|'delete', type:'fact'|'rumor'|'npc_memory'|'relationship'|'goal'(/'goals'), text, scope/visibility, npc_id, target, entity_id/entity/about, source_npc/source_npc_id, known_name, participants[], location_id/name, region_id/name, scene_id, importance, aliases[], mode:'replace'|'append', id/record_id, expected_hash/record_hash/hash, active, source}
dataStructures.notes: Batch under {items:[...]}. Scope aliasing via _visibility(): public/player_safe->player, player_private/participants->shared, truth/gm_truth->gm, private/npc_private->npc. State-record scope mapping via _state_record_scope (player->public, npc->owner, shared->participants, etc.). Hash conflict => status 'conflict'/'not_added' (not stored).

dataStructures.name: roll_outcome_payload (dice)
dataStructures.fields: {ok, notation, roll_kind, target_kind, target_number, total, grade, margin, natural, detail, sides/count/keep/rolls/kept/modifier/forced (geometry), modifier_note}
dataStructures.notes: Streamed in full (geometry) for UI dice animation; _model_roll_text whitelists only total/grade/margin/natural for the model. detail recorded into public event log as a dice Event.

dataStructures.name: _meta / turn usage
dataStructures.fields: _meta: {label, scope:'gm'|'npc'|'other', in, out, secs, cached, tps, prompt_secs, eval_secs, load_secs}. usage: {turns, calls, in, out, cached, tokens, secs, peak_context, gm_calls, gm_tokens, npc_calls, npc_tokens, other_calls, other_tokens}
dataStructures.notes: scope drives token bucketing in add_turn_usage (decoupled from human label). meta_total adds {calls:[metas], peak_context, sys_estimate:_SYS_EST, context, run}.

dataStructures.name: npc_client_state[npc_id]
dataStructures.fields: {model:str, session_id:str, thread_id:str}
dataStructures.notes: Serializable per-NPC identity persisted by dialog_store; restores cache-key/thread stability across reloads. Live client recreated lazily by ensure_npc_client.

dataStructures.name: world_query_seen[scope_key]
dataStructures.fields: scope_key('player'|'gm'|'npc:{id}'|'fact') -> set[row_key]
dataStructures.notes: Per-scope dedupe of query/fact rows already delivered to the active GM tail. Row key = 'row:{kind}:{id|text}:{sha1[:16]}'. RESET on GM history compaction (delivery cache resets so previously-delivered facts can resurface).

controlFlow: ENTRY run_turn -> _drive. _drive: turn+=1; reset per-turn state (_turn_player_event=None, _turn_time_advances=[], turn_visible_output_seen=False); append agents.gm_user_message(world, player_text, include_player_options_tool) to gm_messages; yield ev('player'); call _maybe_compact(session) (GM history compaction trigger). THEN the HOP LOOP: while max_tool_hops<=0 OR tool_hops<max_tool_hops: tool_hops+=1; sid=next_sid(); gen=agents.gm_turn_stream(client, world, gm_messages, gm_summary, loaded_gm_tools, include_player_options_tool). Drain gen: for each ('thinking',text)->yield delta gm_thinking; for ('content',text)-> if stream_gm_content yield delta gm_narration else buffer into content_deltas. StopIteration.value = (thinking, content, calls, assistant_msg, stats). Emit gm_thinking + a _meta('ГМ — нарратив'|'ГМ — решение', scope='gm'). IF no calls: final narration path — append assistant_msg, yield gm_narration, run _sync_scene_delta (extracts NPC enter/leave moves from accepted text -> world.set_npc_presence, yields scene_update; logged as 'scene sync' meta scope='other'); set fell_through=False; break. IF calls: normalize via _normalize_tool_calls (id_prefix 'gm_{sid}'); _assistant_with_tool_calls; OPTIONAL pre-tool prelude (_should_generate_tool_prelude over _VISIBLE_PRELUDE_TOOLS={ask_npc,ask_player,move_npc,set_npc_presence,set_npc_whereabouts,set_scene,roll_dice} only if no inline prelude_text AND not turn_visible_output_seen) -> _generate_pre_tool_prelude streams under its OWN sid and finalizes its own gm_narration row; append assistant_msg to history; if prelude_text and not prelude_generated, finalize inline narration under THIS sid, set turn_visible_output_seen. Then for each call: name,args; show_tool_event = name!='ask_player'; yield gm_tool_call; result = _run_tool(...) (a generator -> yield from); yield tool_result(full); compute tool_visible_output (ask_npc with non-empty speech/action) and terminal_result; IF (turn_visible_output_seen OR tool_visible_output) and not terminal -> wrap result.model with _VISIBLE_CONTINUATION_REMINDER; append {role:'tool', tool_call_id, content: model-text} to gm_messages; update turn_visible_output_seen; mark player_options_shown; if terminal -> terminal_after_tools=True. After calls, if terminal_after_tools -> break. LOOP repeats: GM sees tool results and either narrates (exit) or calls more tools. AFTER loop: if fell_through -> error 'max hops exceeded'; elif options enabled and not shown -> error. If no player event recorded -> record_public('player','speech'). _finalize_turn_time. commit_turn (flush drafts). run_turn then yields meta_total. _run_tool is a big dispatch by name. _ask_npc: resolve npc; reject if not npc_can_react (npc_not_present error, terminal-ish error result); if correction & npc in pending -> yield gm_reject (generator-critic); record_player_for; ensure_npc_client + _maybe_compact_npc; agents.npc_turn_stream streams incremental speech via extract_json_string(buf,'speech')/json_unescape -> delta npc_speech; on StopIteration get (out, stats); yield npc_thinking+npc_speech+meta(scope='npc'); remember_npc_client; session.draft(...) into pending; return dual-channel result with ask_npc reminder. Correction loop = GM, in a later hop, calls ask_npc with same npc_id + correction; _ask_npc overwrites the same pending seq (draft keeps prev seq).
externalDeps: config (CHARS_PER_TOKEN, GM_HISTORY_TOKENS, GM_KEEP_TURNS, NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS, NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES, COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS, EVENTS_CAP, ROLE_COMPACT, BACKEND, CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY)
agents (gm_turn_stream, gm_prelude_stream, gm_user_message, npc_turn_stream, npc_user_message, gm_tool_catalog, search_gm_tools, initial_gm_tool_names, extract_scene_delta)
prompts (GM_SYSTEM, NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM, GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM)
runtime_settings (gm_suggest_options_enabled, stream_gm_content_enabled, max_tool_hops)

world (World, NPC, Event, state_record_hash, _public_gender; methods: resolve, npc, npcs, npc_can_react, npc_player_label, npc_whereabouts_summary, present_witnesses, record_rumor, fact, roll_outcome_payload, set_scene, set_npc_presence, set_npc_whereabouts, advance_time, update_player_character, npc_profile, npc_scene_slice, scene_export, proper_nouns, add_fact, add_state_records, update_state_records, delete_state_records, state_records_for, hidden_events, rumors, fact_records, state_records, constraints, public, canon, time)
llm_client (make_client, extract_json_string, json_unescape; client.chat_stream/chat_json_stream/chat/summarize/set_model/set_session_identity/session_id/thread_id/model/call_log)
hashlib (sha1 for row/short hashes)
json/re/time (stdlib)
internalDeps: server (consumer: drives run_turn over SSE, manages client_session_id/thread_id)
invariants: PROMPT-CACHE PREFIX ORDERING (agents._gm_request_messages): messages = [system: GM_SYSTEM+summary, system: GM world setup, (optional) system 'STORY SO FAR (compact): '+summary, *gm_messages]. The mutable per-turn roster/public-facts/player-action live in the LATE user tail (_gm_turn_context), NOT the early setup, so an NPC rename/add/edit or public-fact edit only recomputes the tail, never the cached prefix.

APPEND-ONLY HISTORY: gm_messages appended byte-for-byte (assistant echo with tool_calls, then {role:'tool',...} per call). Old turns must stay unchanged so llama/codex prefix caches reuse the long history. Tool-call arguments are re-serialized COMPACT via _json_compact (json.dumps ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',',':')) — must match exactly for cache reuse.

PROMPT_CACHE_KEY STABILITY: codex payload['prompt_cache_key'] = config.CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY or self._thread_id (codex_client.py:467). thread_id/session_id are uuid4 generated once per client and persisted via npc_client_state + client_thread_id/client_session_id. ensure_npc_client restores them via set_session_identity so each NPC keeps a STABLE distinct cache key/thread across reloads. reset_npc_memory drops the live client so a FRESH thread_id+cache key is minted next call.

SECRET / HIDDEN-CANON ISOLATION: each NPC runs on its OWN client/thread (npc_clients/npc_client_state) — NPC secrets never enter the GM thread except via tool results. Player-facing events use world.npc_player_label (public label until known_name recorded); GM-facing ask_npc payload carries true npc_name. _player_facing_payload swaps name->player label on streamed move/whereabouts events. world.canon/hidden_events/truth facts are gm-visibility only; query/profile/world_fact system-reminders forbid revealing gm/npc-scope secrets, ids, hashes, meta in narration.

GM COMPACTION TRIGGER (_maybe_compact): if _messages_tokens(gm_messages) >= config.GM_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 100000) AND number of user-role start markers > GM_KEEP_TURNS (default 3): cut=starts[-GM_KEEP_TURNS]; verbatim-keep last 3 turns; old + prior gm_summary summarized via client.summarize(base, proper_nouns); gm_messages=recent; then reset_world_query_cache(). Token est uses _estimate_tokens = len//CHARS_PER_TOKEN.

NPC COMPACTION TRIGGER (_maybe_compact_npc): if client not None AND _messages_tokens(npc_messages[id]) >= config.NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 64000) AND user-start count > NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES (default 6): keep last 6 exchanges verbatim; old + prior npc_summary summarized via _summarize_npc_history (prompts.NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM, think=True, reasoning_role=ROLE_COMPACT, input clipped to config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS=12000). Invoked inside _ask_npc before generation.

TOKEN ESTIMATION HEURISTIC: _estimate_tokens(text)=max(0,len(text)//config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN), CHARS_PER_TOKEN default 3. _SYS_EST = len(prompts.GM_SYSTEM)//CHARS_PER_TOKEN. _messages_tokens sums _estimate_tokens('{role}: {content}') per message. Compaction & context_usage gauges depend on this exact formula.

TWO-PHASE DRAFT/COMMIT (append-only event log): ask_npc writes provisional reply into session.pending under a STABLE seq (correction overwrites same seq; rejected redrafts never logged). commit_turn flushes only non-empty drafts into events + commitments (capped last _COMMIT_BLOCKS=8) + npc_messages + world.record_rumor; events sorted by seq, capped to EVENTS_CAP=400. delivered[npc_id] advances to _shown (what NPC actually saw), NOT to log end.

VISIBILITY BOUNDARIES: delivered[npc_id]=max seq shown in prior turns; _shown[npc_id]=seq at this wake (snapshot_shown). observations() shows committed events with npc in witnesses (excluding own + current player action) + OTHER NPCs' pending drafts (intra-turn awareness), capped last _OBS_CAP=12. reset_npc_memory PINS delivered=_shown=_seq (so reset NPC sees only future, not whole log).

DETERMINISTIC DICE: roll_outcome_payload uses world._rng (instance RNG) + forced_die_next (one-shot, consumed per roll) / forced_die_all overrides (world.py). Orchestrator does NOT seed — porting must preserve World's RNG + forced-die semantics. roll_dice records public dice Event from `detail`; model sees only total/grade/margin/natural (whitelisted by _model_roll_text).

DUAL-CHANNEL TOOL RESULT: ToolExecutionResult.full (UI/player) vs .model (GM history). .model gets compacted (whitelisted fields) + a per-tool system-reminder appended via _system_reminder (whitespace-collapsed, wrapped in <system-reminder>...</system-reminder>). _TOOL_REMINDERS holds the exact reminder text per tool. _VISIBLE_CONTINUATION_REMINDER appended when prior visible output existed this turn and tool is non-terminal.

GENERATOR-CRITIC CORRECTION: ask_npc with `correction` only honored if npc_id already in session.pending (else correction=None). On redraft failure the rejected draft is popped from pending (not revived). gm_reject event emitted. Correction overwrites same pending seq.
JSON NPC CONTRACT field names: reasoning/speech/action/claims (NPC_SCHEMA, required all four). Streamed incrementally by scanning buf for 'speech' via extract_json_string + json_unescape.

CARD_REVISION: surfaced (not mutated here) via _compact_npc_profile_payload/_compact_player_character_update_payload (card_revision passthrough); bumped by world on card field changes; old snapshots default to 0 (world.py).

MERGE/DEDUPE world-state writes: _find_mergeable_state_record merges by normalized text key + scope/owner/entity/source/location/region/scene (skips relationship/goal); identical active record -> status 'not_added'; participant superset -> status 'merged'; hash mismatch -> status 'conflict' (not stored). Status strings (stored/updated/merged/deleted/not_added/conflict/error) are part of the model-facing contract.

TERMINAL TOOL: only a ToolExecutionResult with terminal=True breaks the hop loop after tools (currently none of the tools set terminal=True in the inspected paths; ask_player just signals options-shown). max_tool_hops from runtime_settings; <=0 means unbounded.
advance_time accumulates into session._turn_time_advances; _finalize_turn_time sums minutes and joins reasons (clipped 300 chars) into world.time.last_advance_minutes/reason; zeroed if no advance.
tool_search mutates session.loaded_gm_tools (adds loaded tool names) — drives progressive tool exposure to the GM model.
platformConcerns: No direct OS/path/subprocess/socket code in orchestrator.py itself — it is pure orchestration over injected clients. Platform concerns are inherited from llm_client/codex_client (HTTP, TLS, OAuth, uuid threads) and server (SSE).
uuid4 thread_id/session_id generation lives in codex_client (cross-platform; Rust: uuid crate v4).
Determinism: dice RNG is in world.py, not here; but a faithful port MUST run World's RNG on all 3 OSes identically (use a seeded PRNG, not OS entropy, if reproducibility is wanted).

Generators are heavily used as the streaming/event protocol — must map to a Rust async Stream / channel; the yield-from of sub-generators (_run_tool, _ask_npc, _sync_scene_delta, _generate_pre_tool_prelude) means nested streams flatten into the parent event stream.
Hashing uses hashlib.sha1 (UTF-8 encoded, hexdigest[:16]) — must match byte-for-byte across platforms (Rust: sha1 crate, .encode('utf-8')).

JSON serialization must use ensure_ascii=False (UTF-8, Cyrillic preserved) and compact separators (',',':') for tool-call echo to preserve prompt-cache prefix bytes — Rust serde_json default is compact+UTF-8 but verify no extra spaces.
rustNotes: Model run_turn as an async fn returning a Stream<Item=Event> (e.g. async-stream / tokio mpsc). The Python nested generators (yield from _run_tool / _ask_npc / _sync_scene_delta / _generate_pre_tool_prelude) flatten into one event stream — in Rust, have helpers take a &mut sender (mpsc::Sender<Event>) and return the ToolExecutionResult, instead of returning streams, to avoid stream-nesting complexity.

ToolExecutionResult -> a struct { full: String, model: String, terminal: bool }. Event -> enum or a tagged struct { kind, agent: Option<String>, data: serde_json::Value, sid: Option<String> }. Keep `kind` as a string-typed enum to match the JS UI protocol exactly.

Tool dispatch (_run_tool) is a big match on name; keep the exact name set and the exact ordering of yields (gm_tool_call BEFORE running, tool_result AFTER) — the UI depends on event order. ask_player suppresses gm_tool_call/tool_result (show_tool_event=false).

Session is mutable shared state — wrap in a struct owned by the turn driver; the per-NPC clients map (HashMap<String, Client>) plus npc_client_state (HashMap<String, NpcClientState{model,session_id,thread_id}>) must serialize/deserialize for dialog_store parity.

Preserve the prompt-cache discipline: build GM request messages as [system, system, optional summary system, ...append-only history]; never rewrite history; serialize tool_calls.arguments with serde_json::to_string (compact). Persist thread_id/prompt_cache_key per client and per NPC.

Token estimation: integer division len/CHARS_PER_TOKEN over the rendered '{role}: {content}' string — keep char-count (not byte-count); Python len() is Unicode scalar count, so use .chars().count() in Rust, NOT .len() (which is bytes) — this materially changes Cyrillic token estimates and thus compaction triggers.

extract_json_string streaming parser (in llm_client) finds the 'speech' field in a growing JSON buffer and json_unescape's it for incremental display — port carefully; it must tolerate partial/incomplete JSON.
The dedupe caches (world_query_seen) use sha1[:16] hex over normalized text — replicate _record_text_key (re.sub(r'\s+',' ', text.lower()).strip()) and _short_hash exactly.

Compaction calls the LLM (client.summarize / _summarize_npc_history) — these are async network calls inside the turn driver; sequence them before generation as Python does. reset_world_query_cache MUST run after GM compaction.
Watch generator-critic: correction is only applied if npc_id in pending; on redraft exception, pop pending. Replicate the seq-stability of draft() (correction keeps prior seq).
suggestedCrates: serde
serde_json (compact UTF-8 JSON, tool-call echo, payload compaction)
tokio (async runtime, mpsc channels for the event stream)
async-stream / futures (Stream<Item=Event> for run_turn)
uuid (v4 thread/session ids)
sha1 (row/short hashes; must match Python hexdigest[:16])
regex (whitespace collapse in _system_reminder, _record_text_key, _query_terms, _fact_text_segments, dice notation parsing)
rand + rand_pcg/rand_chacha (deterministic seedable RNG for World dice, if porting world too)
dashmap or std HashMap (per-NPC client/state maps)
thiserror (tool error variants)
risks: The streaming/generator model is the hardest to port faithfully: Python's `yield from` over sub-generators interleaves child events into the parent stream while also returning a value (StopIteration.value). Rust has no direct equivalent; the (sender, return-value) refactor changes the shape and must be tested event-by-event against the Python UI protocol.

extract_json_string / json_unescape live in llm_client (not read in full here) — the incremental NPC speech streaming depends on their exact partial-JSON tolerance; a separate reader must map them precisely.
Token estimation uses Python len() (Unicode scalars). If a porter naively uses byte length, Cyrillic-heavy histories will hit compaction at the wrong point. Subtle and high-impact.

prompt_cache_key / thread_id semantics span orchestrator + codex_client + server + dialog_store; getting cache-key stability right requires the whole identity-restore chain (set_session_identity, npc_client_state round-trip) to be ported together, not piecemeal.

Many helper functions (the _compact_*/_model_*_text family, _apply_state_record_item) encode the EXACT model-facing tool-result text and field whitelists — these are effectively part of the prompt and must be reproduced verbatim or the GM's behavior shifts. Large surface, easy to drift.
_sync_scene_delta and _generate_pre_tool_prelude make EXTRA LLM calls mid-turn (scope='other'/'gm') and yield their own events under their own sids — easy to miss; affects metas and UI rows.

terminal=True path exists in the loop but no inspected tool sets it; if a future/other reader's tool returns terminal, the break semantics matter. Confirm whether any tool is intended to be terminal (currently appears unused).

World methods (roll_outcome_payload, state record CRUD, fact/query, presence/whereabouts/scene/time) are deep dependencies; this map covers how orchestrator CALLS them but not their internals (deterministic RNG, hash function world_mod.state_record_hash) — those belong to the world subsystem reader and must align on hashing/RNG.

subsystem: Prompt-cache prefix assembly + history/NPC compaction (orchestrator core)
files: E:/gemma/gm-lab/agents.py (message assembly: _gm_system, _gm_world_setup, _gm_turn_context, gm_user_message, _gm_request_messages, gm_turn_stream, gm_prelude_stream, npc_system_message, npc_card_block, npc_user_message, _historical_npc_message, npc_request_messages, npc_turn_stream)
purpose: This subsystem governs how the GM LLM conversation and each NPC sub-agent's private LLM thread are assembled into request messages so that a long, stable cacheable prefix is reused across turns, and how that history is compacted (summarized) only late, once it approaches a working token ceiling. The orchestrator keeps an append-only GM message log (gm_messages) plus a compressed gm_summary, and a per-NPC append-only thread (npc_messages[npc_id]) plus per-NPC npc_summaries; assembly always places stable static content first and mutable content last so prompt/KV caches and the Codex prompt_cache_key/thread-id hit identical prefixes. It also tracks per-NPC client identity (model/session_id/thread_id) so each NPC gets its own stable cache key, and resets caches on compaction and on explicit NPC memory reset.

publicSurface.name: run_turn
publicSurface.behavior: Top-level per-turn generator: yields UI events, drives the GM tool loop via _drive, then yields a final ('meta_total', None, total) event with total['context']=context_usage(session) and total['run']=session.add_turn_usage(total).

publicSurface.name: context_usage
publicSurface.behavior: Computes approximate active prompt pressure: gm_active = _SYS_EST + world_tokens + gm_summary_tokens + gm_history_tokens; per-NPC active = world_tokens + persona + summary + history; returns {'current','world','next_compact','gm','npc','npcs'} where next_compact is the candidate (gm or any has_session NPC) with the least remaining = limit - history.

publicSurface.name: Session.__init__
publicSurface.behavior: Holds gm_messages(list), gm_summary(str), npc_messages(dict[str,list]), npc_summaries(dict[str,str]), npc_clients(dict), npc_client_state(dict[str,dict] of model/session_id/thread_id), client_model/client_session_id/client_thread_id snapshots, loaded_gm_tools(set), world_query_seen(dict), events/delivered/_shown/pending/commitments.

publicSurface.name: Session.ensure_npc_client
publicSurface.behavior: Lazily creates a separate model client per NPC via make_client(), restores its model + session/thread identity from npc_client_state[npc_id] (set_model + set_session_identity), caches it in npc_clients, and calls remember_npc_client. Gives each NPC its own stable prompt_cache_key/thread.

publicSurface.name: Session.remember_npc_client
publicSurface.behavior: Snapshots the live NPC client's model/session_id/thread_id into npc_client_state[npc_id] so identity survives serialization and is reused next turn.

publicSurface.name: Session.reset_npc_memory
publicSurface.behavior: Explicit manual single-NPC reset: pops npc_messages, npc_summaries, npc_client_state, npc_clients, commitments, pending; pins delivered[npc_id]=_shown[npc_id]=self._seq. Drops the live client so the next ask_npc spins a fresh thread + new prompt_cache_key. Returns False only for unknown/empty id. Never auto-invoked on a card edit.

publicSurface.name: Session.apply_debug_edit
publicSurface.signature: apply_debug_edit(self, npc_id: str, data: dict) -> bool
publicSurface.behavior: Applies one /debug/npc edit (card fields, presence on actual change only, whereabouts) and runs reset_npc_memory only when data['reset_memory'] is truthy.

publicSurface.name: Session.set_model_for_all_clients
publicSurface.behavior: Sets client_model and propagates set_model to GM client, all live npc_clients, and all npc_client_state entries.

publicSurface.name: Session.add_turn_usage
publicSurface.behavior: Accumulates run_usage; buckets per-call tokens by call['scope'] into gm/other/npc.

publicSurface.name: Session.npc_history_text
publicSurface.signature: npc_history_text(self, npc_id: str, max_messages: int = 8) -> str
publicSurface.behavior: Debug render of NPC thread: 'Сжатая память:' + summary, then last max_messages, rewriting old 'CURRENT SITUATION...' user blocks to 'PREVIOUS NPC SITUATION (historical...)'. Used only for the npc_history UI event.

publicSurface.name: Session.observations / draft / snapshot_shown / commit_turn / record_player_for / record_public
publicSurface.behavior: Two-phase NPC drafting: draft() stores a provisional reply in pending (same seq on correction); commit_turn() commits pending to events + commitments + npc_messages (appends user_message + assistant_message ONLY when both present) and advances delivered to _shown; events capped to config.EVENTS_CAP.

publicSurface.name: _maybe_compact
publicSurface.behavior: GM history compaction gate. See invariants. Summarizes everything older than the last GM_KEEP_TURNS user-turn boundaries into gm_summary via session.client.summarize(...), replaces gm_messages with the recent tail, and calls session.reset_world_query_cache().

publicSurface.name: _maybe_compact_npc
publicSurface.behavior: Per-NPC thread compaction gate. Summarizes everything older than the last NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES user boundaries into npc_summaries[npc_id] via _summarize_npc_history, replaces npc_messages[npc_id] with the recent tail. Does NOT reset world_query cache and does NOT change NPC client identity.

publicSurface.name: _summarize_npc_history
publicSurface.behavior: Calls client.chat([{system: NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM.format(proper_nouns=...)},{user: text[:COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS]}], think=True, reasoning_role=ROLE_COMPACT); returns content.strip().

publicSurface.name: _estimate_tokens / _messages_tokens / _world_context_tokens
publicSurface.behavior: Token estimation heuristic: max(0, len(text)//config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN). _messages_tokens sums _estimate_tokens(_msg_text(m)) where _msg_text(m)='role: content'.strip().

publicSurface.name: agents._gm_request_messages
publicSurface.behavior: Assembles GM request: [system=_gm_system(GM_SYSTEM static), system=_gm_world_setup (public intro), (if summary) system='STORY SO FAR (compact): '+summary, *gm_messages]. Exact prefix order; see invariants.

publicSurface.name: agents.gm_user_message
publicSurface.behavior: Builds the new user turn via _gm_turn_context: mutable snapshot (TIME STATE, INTERNAL NPC ROSTER, CURRENT PUBLIC FACTS[:12], PLAYER CHARACTER CARD, CURRENT SCENE STATE, ENTITY REFERENCE MARKUP, SCENE CONSTRAINTS, PLAYER OPTION SUGGESTIONS, TURN_RESOLUTION_CHECKLIST, PLAYER ACTION). Appended to gm_messages each turn (append-only).

publicSurface.name: agents.gm_turn_stream / gm_turn
publicSurface.signature: gm_turn_stream(client, world, gm_messages, summary='', loaded_tool_names=None, include_player_options_tool=False) -> generator
publicSurface.behavior: Builds messages via _gm_request_messages and calls client.chat_stream(messages, tools=build_gm_tools_for_model(...), think=True, reasoning_role=ROLE_GM).

publicSurface.name: agents.gm_prelude_stream
publicSurface.behavior: Separate visible pre-tool narration request; system+user only (no history, no tools), think=False, reasoning_role=ROLE_GM; PENDING RESOLUTION CONTEXT JSON clipped to config.PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS.

publicSurface.name: agents.npc_request_messages / npc_card_block / npc_user_message / _historical_npc_message
publicSurface.behavior: NPC assembly: [system=NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC, (if summary) system='YOUR PRIVATE MEMORY SO FAR (compact):'+summary, *map(_historical_npc_message, history), final_turn]. final_turn = copy of user_message with npc_card_block(npc) prepended; the stored history message stays card-free. See invariants.

publicSurface.name: agents.npc_turn_stream / npc_turn
publicSurface.signature: npc_turn_stream(client, npc, situation, observations='', commitments='', feedback=None, constraints=None, scene_slice='', history=None, summary='') -> generator
publicSurface.behavior: Builds messages via _npc_messages and calls client.chat_json_stream(msgs, NPC_SCHEMA, think=True, reasoning_role=ROLE_NPC); returns (_norm_npc(data), stats).

publicSurface.name: llm_client.OpenAICompatClient._payload
publicSurface.signature: _payload(messages, tools=None, think=None, response_format=None, stream=False, reasoning_role=ROLE_GM) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Adds prompt_cache_key/prompt_cache_retention from config ONLY if set; when USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS sets chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking + sampling + n_cache_reuse=LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE (only if >0).

publicSurface.name: llm_client.OpenAICompatClient.summarize
publicSurface.behavior: GM compaction call: system=GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM.format(proper_nouns_line=...), user=text[:COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS], think=True, reasoning_role=ROLE_COMPACT.

publicSurface.name: codex_client.CodexClient.set_session_identity / session_id / thread_id / set_model
publicSurface.behavior: Overwrites _session_id/_thread_id only when the passed value is non-empty. New CodexClient generates fresh uuid4 session_id, thread_id, installation_id.

publicSurface.name: codex_client.CodexClient._payload
publicSurface.behavior: Splits messages via split_messages_for_responses into (instructions, input); sets store=False, stream=True; payload['prompt_cache_key'] = config.CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY or self._thread_id; reasoning via reasoning_for_request; include=['reasoning.encrypted_content'] when reasoning set.

publicSurface.name: codex_client.split_messages_for_responses
publicSurface.behavior: Concatenates ALL system messages (in order) into one instructions string joined by '\n\n'; converts user/assistant/tool into input_items. THIS FLATTENS the system prefix ordering. See invariants/risks.

publicSurface.name: dialog_store._session_to_payload / _session_from_payload
publicSurface.behavior: Serializes/restores the full cache+history state including gm_messages, gm_summary, npc_messages, npc_summaries, npc_client_state(model/session_id/thread_id), client_session_id, client_thread_id, world_query_seen, events, delivered, _shown, pending, commitments.

dataStructures.name: Session (in-memory, partly serialized)
dataStructures.fields: client; client_backend(=config.BACKEND); client_model; client_session_id; client_thread_id; npc_clients: dict[str, client]; npc_client_state: dict[str, {model, session_id, thread_id}]; world; gm_messages: list[dict]; gm_summary: str; npc_messages: dict[str, list[dict]]; npc_summaries: dict[str, str]; loaded_gm_tools: set[str]; world_query_seen: dict[str, set[str]]; run_usage: dict; last_player_action: str; _sid; _turn_time_advances; events: list[Event]; _seq; turn; delivered: dict[str,int]; _shown: dict[str,int]; pending: dict[str,{seq,speech,action,claims,witnesses,user_message,assistant_message}]; commitments: dict[str, list[str]]; _turn_player_event
dataStructures.notes: gm_messages and npc_messages[npc_id] are APPEND-ONLY between compactions. npc_client_state is the serializable per-NPC cache identity. world_query_seen is per-scope delivered-dedup keys, reset on GM compaction.

dataStructures.name: GM message (OpenAI chat shape)
dataStructures.fields: {'role':'system'|'user'|'assistant'|'tool', 'content': str, optional 'tool_calls':[{id,type:'function',function:{name,arguments(JSON string)}}], optional 'tool_call_id': str}
dataStructures.notes: Assistant tool-call echo built by _assistant_with_tool_calls (arguments serialized via _json_compact). Tool result message uses _tool_model_text(result) (compact/whitelisted text), NOT the full payload.

dataStructures.name: ToolExecutionResult (dataclass, frozen)
dataStructures.fields: full: str; model: str; terminal: bool=False
dataStructures.notes: full = UI/debug text; model = what goes into gm_messages tool content (compact + optional appended <system-reminder>). Reminder appended via _system_reminder + _TOOL_REMINDERS[name].

dataStructures.name: NPC normalized output (_norm_npc)
dataStructures.fields: {'reasoning': str, 'speech': str, 'action': str, 'claims': list[str]}
dataStructures.notes: NPC JSON contract field names: reasoning, speech, action, claims. NPC_SCHEMA at agents.py:860. Streaming extracts 'speech' incrementally via extract_json_string(buf,'speech'). Stored assistant message = json.dumps(out) via _assistant_json_message.

dataStructures.name: pending[npc_id]
dataStructures.fields: {seq:int, speech:str, action:str, claims:list, witnesses:frozenset, user_message:dict|None, assistant_message:dict|None}
dataStructures.notes: Provisional NPC reply for the turn; correction overwrites same seq. Committed to npc_messages only at commit_turn and only if both user_message and assistant_message present.

dataStructures.name: Codex Responses payload
dataStructures.fields: {model, instructions(str), input(list of message_item/function_call/function_call_output), tools, tool_choice, parallel_tool_calls, store:False, stream:True, include, client_metadata, prompt_cache_key, reasoning?, text?, max_output_tokens?}
dataStructures.notes: instructions = all system messages joined '\n\n'; input = ordered user/assistant/tool items. prompt_cache_key = CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY or self._thread_id.

dataStructures.name: Persisted session snapshot (dialog_store JSON)
dataStructures.fields: session.{client_model, client_backend, client_session_id, client_thread_id, world, gm_messages, gm_summary, npc_messages{npc_id:[msgs]}, npc_summaries{npc_id:str}, run_usage, npc_client_state{npc_id:{model,session_id,thread_id}}, world_query_seen{scope:[keys]}, last_player_action, sid, events, seq, turn, delivered, shown, pending, commitments}; top: {schema_version, turn_count, session, transcript}
dataStructures.notes: card_revision is serialized on world player-character and NPC records (dialog_store ~875/1004), drives npc_card_block revision field.

controlFlow: run_turn -> _drive. Per turn _drive: (1) session.turn+=1; (2) append agents.gm_user_message(...) to session.gm_messages (append-only new user turn); (3) yield 'player'; (4) _maybe_compact(session) AFTER appending (so the new turn is counted before deciding to compact); (5) tool loop while tool_hops < max_tool_hops: call agents.gm_turn_stream(client, world, session.gm_messages, session.gm_summary, loaded_gm_tools, include_player_options_tool) which rebuilds the full request via _gm_request_messages each hop. If no tool calls -> append assistant_msg, emit gm_narration, run _sync_scene_delta, break. If tool calls -> normalize, build assistant_with_tool_calls, optionally generate a pre-tool prelude (separate gm_prelude_stream request, only if no inline content + not turn_visible_output_seen + a _VISIBLE_PRELUDE_TOOLS call present), append assistant_msg, then for each call run _run_tool, append a {'role':'tool', tool_call_id, content:_tool_model_text(result)} message; ask_npc dispatches _ask_npc. (6) After loop: record player event if none, _finalize_turn_time, commit_turn (commit drafts into events/commitments/npc_messages, cap events to EVENTS_CAP). _ask_npc: ensure_npc_client(npc_id) (separate client/thread), _maybe_compact_npc BEFORE building the request, build npc_user_message + npc_turn_stream, stream speech via extract_json_string, draft() into pending, remember_npc_client. Compaction is lazy/threshold-gated, never every turn.
externalDeps: Default backend: ChatGPT Codex OAuth Responses API (https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses) via codex_client.CodexClient + codex_oauth credential refresh
Alternative backends: local llama.cpp server (OpenAICompatClient, LLAMA_HOST default http://localhost:8080) and external OpenAI-compatible providers (GM_API_BASE/GM_API_KEY); 'mock'
httpx (sync Client, streaming SSE, read timeout=None)
RAG embeddings server (GM_RAG_EMBEDDINGS_URL, default http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/embeddings) - peripheral to this subsystem
SQLite via dialog_store (session persistence) and gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 (RAG cache)

Env vars: GM_HISTORY_TOKENS, GM_KEEP_TURNS, NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS, NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES, GM_EVENTS_CAP, GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN, GM_COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS, GM_PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS, GM_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, GM_PROMPT_CACHE_RETENTION, GM_LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE, GM_CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, GM_THINK, NPC_THINK, plus .env auto-load
internalDeps: agents (message assembly, tool catalog, NPC schema, extract_scene_delta, build_gm_tools_for_model)
runtime_settings (reasoning_for_request/reasoning_enabled, max_output_tokens, max_tool_hops, tool_choice/parallel, gm_suggest_options_enabled, stream_gm_content_enabled)
world (World, NPC, Event, state_records, proper_nouns, scene/time context builders, npc_player_label)
invariants: TOKEN HEURISTIC: config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN default 3 (GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN). _estimate_tokens(text)=max(0, len(text)//3). _SYS_EST = len(prompts.GM_SYSTEM)//CHARS_PER_TOKEN (module-level constant). _msg_text(m)='{role}: {content}'.strip(); _messages_tokens sums per-message estimates.

GM COMPACTION GATE (_maybe_compact): fires only if _messages_tokens(gm_messages) >= config.GM_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 100000) AND number of user-role boundaries > config.GM_KEEP_TURNS (default 3). cut = starts[-GM_KEEP_TURNS] (index of the GM_KEEP_TURNS-th-from-last user message). old=msgs[:cut], recent=msgs[cut:]. KEPT VERBATIM: the last GM_KEEP_TURNS turns (recent). SUMMARIZED: prior gm_summary + old turns, base=(gm_summary+'\n'+old_text).strip(), old_text from _msg_text_for_summary (which strips the 'PLAYER ACTION (latest user input, free roleplay text):' prefix from user msgs). gm_summary = client.summarize(base, proper_nouns=world.proper_nouns()). Then gm_messages=recent and session.reset_world_query_cache().

NPC COMPACTION GATE (_maybe_compact_npc): per-NPC; fires only if client not None AND _messages_tokens(npc_messages[npc_id]) >= config.NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 64000) AND user-boundary count > config.NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES (default 6). cut=starts[-NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES]. KEPT VERBATIM: last NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES exchanges. SUMMARIZED: prior npc_summaries[npc_id] + old (old_text via _msg_text, NOT _msg_text_for_summary). npc_summaries[npc_id]=_summarize_npc_history(...). Does NOT reset world_query cache, does NOT touch NPC client identity.

GM REQUEST PREFIX ORDER (agents._gm_request_messages) is load-bearing for cache reuse: [0] system=_gm_system()=prompts.GM_SYSTEM (fully static, MUST be identical every turn), [1] system=_gm_world_setup()='WORLD SETUP...

PUBLIC INTRO:
'+world.public (changes only on full world recreate), [2] (only if summary) system='STORY SO FAR (compact): '+summary, then [3:] *gm_messages (append-only turns). Mutable per-turn state (NPC roster, public facts, scene, player card, constraints, time) lives in the LATEST user turn (_gm_turn_context), NOT in the early prefix, so per-turn edits invalidate only the tail.

GM TURN CONTEXT ORDER (_gm_turn_context, the new user message): 'CURRENT TURN CONTEXT...' header -> TIME STATE -> INTERNAL NPC ROSTER -> (CURRENT PUBLIC FACTS only first 12) -> PLAYER CHARACTER CARD -> CURRENT SCENE STATE -> ENTITY REFERENCE MARKUP -> (SCENE CONSTRAINTS if any) -> PLAYER OPTION SUGGESTIONS block -> TURN_RESOLUTION_CHECKLIST -> 'PLAYER ACTION (latest user input, free roleplay text):\n'+player_text.strip(). public_facts filtered to record.kind=='public' (truth/rumor/hidden_events excluded).

NPC REQUEST PREFIX ORDER (agents.npc_request_messages): [0] system=NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC (static), [1] (only if summary) system='YOUR PRIVATE MEMORY SO FAR (compact):\n'+summary, then *history each rewritten by _historical_npc_message (old 'CURRENT SITUATION...' -> 'PREVIOUS NPC SITUATION (historical...)' and old scene-slice header rewritten; wrapped 'HISTORICAL NPC EXCHANGE (not the current scene):'), then final_turn. final_turn is a COPY of user_message with npc_card_block(npc) PREPENDED ('CURRENT NPC CARD'); the recorded history user_message stays card-free so a card edit invalidates only this tail.

NPC USER MESSAGE ORDER (npc_user_message): _NPC_PERCEPTION_BRIEF_RULES -> 'CURRENT SITUATION (what's happening now...)' + situation -> (scene_slice) -> (VISIBLE SCENE LIMITS / constraints) -> 'YOUR MEMORY...' commitments -> 'WHAT YOU SAW/HEARD EARLIER:' observations -> (feedback as 'GM NOTE — your previous action did not pass:' + REDO).

PER-NPC CACHE IDENTITY: each NPC gets a SEPARATE client (session.npc_clients[npc_id]) with its own session_id/thread_id. Codex prompt_cache_key = CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY or self._thread_id, so a stable per-NPC thread_id => stable per-NPC cache key.
