Identity is persisted in npc_client_state{model,session_id,thread_id} and restored via set_model + set_session_identity in ensure_npc_client. remember_npc_client re-snapshots after each call and at commit_turn.

CODEX prompt_cache_key precedence: config.CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY (env GM_CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, default = PROMPT_CACHE_KEY which defaults '') falls back to the client's own uuid4 thread_id. GM client uses session.client thread_id; each NPC uses its own. store=False always. session-id/thread-id/x-client-request-id headers also carry the thread/session identity.

OpenAI-compat cache hints are OPT-IN: prompt_cache_key/prompt_cache_retention only sent when config.PROMPT_CACHE_KEY/PROMPT_CACHE_RETENTION non-empty (both default ''). n_cache_reuse only sent when USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS AND config.LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE > 0 (default 0 = off).

COMPACT INPUT CLIP: every summarize call (GM and NPC, all backends) clips input to text[:config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS] (default 12000). PRELUDE call-brief JSON clipped to config.PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS (default 4000).

EVENTS_CAP: config.EVENTS_CAP default 400 (GM_EVENTS_CAP); commit_turn trims session.events to the last EVENTS_CAP after sorting by seq. RUMORS_CAP default 80. _OBS_CAP=12 (observation lines), _COMMIT_BLOCKS=8 (commitment blocks kept per NPC).

APPEND-ONLY HISTORY: gm_messages grows by exactly the new user turn + assistant echo(es) + tool messages each turn; npc_messages grows by [user_message, assistant_message] pairs only at commit_turn and only when BOTH present. Old messages are byte-for-byte unchanged until a compaction replaces them with the recent tail.

world_query delivery cache (world_query_seen) is reset by reset_world_query_cache() ONLY on GM compaction; the 'already_delivered' tool responses explicitly state 'after GM history compaction this delivery cache resets', so cache-key stability is intentionally coupled to compaction.

REASONING/THINK by role: GM_THINK default True, NPC_THINK default False (config). reasoning_role passed: GM=ROLE_GM, NPC=ROLE_NPC, both compaction summarize calls=ROLE_COMPACT (think=True). reasoning_for_request returns None if not think or role effort=='none'. SAMPLING_THINK vs SAMPLING_PLAIN chosen by effective_think (llama path).

NPC JSON contract field names EXACT: reasoning, speech, action, claims (NPC_SCHEMA, _norm_npc). Streaming partial extraction keys on 'speech'. GM assistant tool-call arguments serialized compact via _json_compact (json.dumps separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False).
card_revision: npc_card_block emits revision=int(npc.card_revision or 0); the card block is the LATE override block. Persisted per NPC and per player-character in dialog_store.

ASSISTANT REASONING NOT REPLAYED: _assistant_msg deliberately omits reasoning_content from history ('Qwen не нужно скармливать прошлые мысли в multi-turn') — only role/content/tool_calls stored, preserving prefix stability.
platformConcerns: Codex OAuth flow opens a local browser (CODEX_AUTO_OPEN_BROWSER default True) and binds a loopback callback server on CODEX_AUTH_PORT (default 1455) - cross-platform browser-open + local socket; codex_oauth handles token storage in OS app-data, not the repo.
httpx is used with read timeout=None for streaming SSE; long-lived connections must work on all 3 OSes.

config._load_dotenv reads .env from Path(__file__).with_name('.env') and Path.cwd()/.env - path handling must be cross-platform; Rust should replicate the 'real env wins, .env never overrides' rule and the export-prefix + quote-stripping parsing.
uuid4 generation for session_id/thread_id/installation_id (codex_client) - trivially cross-platform.
RAG cache + dialog store are SQLite files under the module dir by default (gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3); path is config-driven.

TLS: codex uses standard httpx TLS to chatgpt.com; this subsystem itself has no curl_cffi/TLS-impersonation, but the originator/User-Agent/version/installation-id headers mimic the official Codex CLI and must be preserved.
No GPU/ffmpeg in this subsystem; the embeddings/llama.cpp server is external.
rustNotes: Model the prefix as an ordered Vec<Message> where Message is an enum {System(String), User(String), Assistant{content, tool_calls}, Tool{call_id, content}}. Keep a strict builder that ALWAYS emits system blocks in the same order (GM_SYSTEM, world_setup, optional summary) then appends history; do NOT reorder or interleave - cache hits depend on byte-identical prefixes.

Compaction is a pure function over (messages, summary, thresholds) returning (new_summary_input, recent_tail). Implement _maybe_compact / _maybe_compact_npc as: count user-boundary indices, if tokens>=limit AND boundaries>keep then split at starts[len-keep]. Use the SAME token estimator everywhere: tokens = chars / CHARS_PER_TOKEN with saturating_sub for max(0,...). Note GM uses _msg_text_for_summary (strips player-action marker) while NPC uses plain _msg_text - keep this asymmetry.

Represent per-NPC client identity as a struct {model, session_id, thread_id} stored in a HashMap<String, NpcClientState>; the live client wraps it. ensure_npc_client = get-or-create + restore identity; reset_npc_memory = remove all maps for that id AND pin delivered/_shown to current seq. Identity must serialize to/from the snapshot exactly.

prompt_cache_key resolution is a simple precedence: config_override.filter(non_empty).unwrap_or(self.thread_id). For Codex the request also splits messages: collect ALL System contents joined by "\n\n" into `instructions`, everything else into ordered `input` items (user->input_text, assistant content->output_text + function_call items, tool->function_call_output{call_id,output}). Preserve store=false, stream=true.

Use a frozen/value type for ToolExecutionResult{full, model, terminal}; history stores `model` (compact) text, UI shows `full`. The <system-reminder>\n{collapsed-whitespace text}\n</system-reminder> wrapper and the _TOOL_REMINDERS map must be ported verbatim (they shape model behavior).

Keep CHARS_PER_TOKEN, GM_HISTORY_TOKENS, GM_KEEP_TURNS, NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS, NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES, EVENTS_CAP, COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS, PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS, _OBS_CAP, _COMMIT_BLOCKS, RUMORS_CAP as config constants with the exact env-var names and defaults. _SYS_EST must be recomputed from the actual GM_SYSTEM string length at startup.

NPC JSON streaming: port extract_json_string (incremental string-field extractor that returns (partial_or_none, complete)) and json_unescape exactly - they tolerate growing/partial JSON during SSE. The NPC contract dict {reasoning, speech, action, claims} normalization (_norm_npc) must clamp types.

Reasoning-by-role and think flags: encode a Role enum {Gm, Npc, Compact} and a per-role effort/summary lookup; reasoning_for_request returns Option when think false or effort none. Compaction always uses Compact role with think=true.

Persistence: serde the whole Session snapshot with the exact field names in dialog_store (_session_to_payload). world_query_seen serializes as sorted Vec per scope; restore as a Set. Reset world_query_seen on GM compaction only.
suggestedCrates: serde + serde_json (message shapes, snapshots, compact json with no spaces to match _json_compact)
reqwest (rustls) or hyper for streaming SSE; eventsource-stream / a hand-rolled SSE line parser to mirror _iter_events
tokio (async streaming; the Python code is sync but Rust SSE is cleaner async) + futures
uuid (v4 for session_id/thread_id/installation_id)
rusqlite (dialog_store + RAG cache SQLite, cross-platform)
directories / dirs (OS app-data path for Codex tokens, cross-platform)
regex (extract_json_string fallbacks, _system_reminder whitespace collapse, dotenv key validation)
thiserror/anyhow (error handling), once_cell (lazy _SYS_EST and config), webbrowser (open OAuth URL cross-platform)
risks: split_messages_for_responses FLATTENS all system messages into one instructions string for Codex - the careful 3-way system prefix split (GM_SYSTEM / world_setup / summary) only preserves distinct cache boundaries on the OpenAI-compat/llama path. On Codex, cache reuse hinges on instructions being byte-identical; any change to GM_SYSTEM, public intro, or summary invalidates the whole instructions block. A faithful port must reproduce this backend-specific behavior, not 'fix' it.

Token estimation is a crude chars//3 heuristic; the real prompt-cache pressure depends on the actual tokenizer. The compaction thresholds (100k/64k) are tuned to this heuristic - changing CHARS_PER_TOKEN silently shifts when compaction fires.

Compaction quality depends on the LLM summarize call (GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM / NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM prompts in prompts.py, not read here) and is non-deterministic; the port must call the same prompts with the same think/role to preserve behavior. The summarize content is the only memory of dropped turns - a bad summary loses canon irreversibly.

GM compaction resets world_query_seen, which by design re-surfaces previously-delivered query rows as 'new'. This coupling is intentional and documented in the tool result strings; porting must preserve it or the GM will see duplicate/empty deliveries differently.

commit_turn appends to npc_messages ONLY when both user_message and assistant_message are present in pending; a partial/failed NPC turn silently drops the exchange from the NPC thread (but the draft may still have been shown). Edge case must be preserved.

_maybe_compact runs AFTER appending the new user turn but BEFORE the tool loop; assistant/tool messages from the current turn are appended later and are NOT subject to compaction until the next turn. Ordering matters.

The Codex reasoning.encrypted_content include and store=False mean reasoning is not server-persisted; combined with _assistant_msg dropping reasoning_content, no reasoning is ever replayed - intentional for cache stability but means a port that replays reasoning would break prefixes.

prompts.py (GM_SYSTEM, NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC, NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE, COMPACT systems) and world.py context builders were not read in full here; the exact static-vs-mutable boundary inside those strings must be verified before porting to guarantee identical prefixes.

world.proper_nouns(), world.scene_context(), entity_reference_context(), player_character_context(), time_context() are external mutable-state renderers feeding the turn tail; their stability/instability directly determines cache invalidation and is out of scope of the files read.

subsystem: World state model (world.py): World class, NPC cards, SceneState, facts/state-records, rumors, whereabouts, deterministic dice, entity refs, secret isolation
purpose: Authoritative, code-side model of game-world truth for the GM-Lab D&D orchestrator. It holds the deterministic-seeded dice RNG, the NPC roster (with persona/voice/goals/knowledge/secret cards and card_revision), the current SceneState, public/hidden facts, structured state-records (scoped memory), append-capped rumors, and per-NPC whereabouts. The guiding principle (file docstring) is that rules and facts live in CODE not in the model's head: dice are deterministic, secrets/hidden canon are stored separately and deliberately excluded from any player/GM-facing or RAG-facing projection, so a cheap code-side leak check can supplement the LLM critic. It exposes many projection methods (context strings, RAG documents, export dicts, entity registry) consumed by orchestrator/agents/server/rag, and mutators driven by GM tool calls.

publicSurface.name: World.__init__
publicSurface.behavior: Creates dice_seed via OS entropy, builds random.Random(dice_seed), inits forced_die_next/all=None, hidden_events=[], rumors=[], _rumor_seq=0, then _load_seed(seed) or _load_default().

publicSurface.name: World.from_seed
publicSurface.behavior: Constructs a World from an explicit seed dict.

publicSurface.name: World.from_story
publicSurface.behavior: Constructs a World from stories.story_seed(story_id).

publicSurface.name: World.add_state_records
publicSurface.behavior: Coerces and appends new StateRecords with unique ids; returns added list.

publicSurface.name: World.update_state_records
publicSurface.behavior: In-place field-level patch of existing StateRecords keyed by record_id/id; only supplied keys change; empty text ignored.

publicSurface.name: World.delete_state_records
publicSurface.signature: def delete_state_records(self, record_ids, hard: bool = False) -> int
publicSurface.behavior: Soft-deletes (active=False) by default, or removes entirely when hard=True; returns count affected.

publicSurface.name: World.apply_state_record_batch
publicSurface.signature: def apply_state_record_batch(self, *, add=None, update=None, delete=None, hard_delete: bool = False) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Convenience batch returning {'added','updated','deleted'}.

publicSurface.name: World.state_records_for
publicSurface.signature: def state_records_for(self, actor_id: str = 'player', *, kinds=None, active: bool|None=True, owner='', subject='', entity_id='', source_npc='', location_id='', region_id='', scene_id='', scopes=None) -> list[StateRecord]
publicSurface.behavior: Filtered, actor-visibility-gated query over state_records (the core scope-isolation read path).

publicSurface.name: World.state_record_documents
publicSurface.behavior: Builds RagDocument list from actor-visible state records (imports rag.RagDocument lazily).

publicSurface.name: World.state_records_export
publicSurface.signature: def state_records_export(self, actor_id='player', *, kinds=None, active=True, owner='', subject='', entity_id='', source_npc='', location_id='', region_id='', scene_id='', scopes=None) -> list[dict]
publicSurface.behavior: Returns vars(record) dicts plus a 'hash' (state_record_hash) for each visible record.

publicSurface.name: World.npc_known_name
publicSurface.signature: def npc_known_name(self, npc_id: str, actor_id: str = 'player') -> str
publicSurface.behavior: Returns the most recent metadata['known_name'] revealed to actor via state records (durable identity reveal).

publicSurface.name: World.npc_player_label
publicSurface.signature: def npc_player_label(self, npc_id: str, actor_id: str = 'player') -> str
publicSurface.behavior: Best player-facing label: known_name -> public_label -> npc.name (never raw secret/name leak).

publicSurface.name: World.npc_whereabouts_export
publicSurface.signature: def npc_whereabouts_export(self, npc_id: str | None = None) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Returns vars(NPCWhereabouts) for one NPC or all; re-ensures whereabouts first.

publicSurface.name: World.npc_whereabouts_summary
publicSurface.behavior: One-line offscreen whereabouts string; empty if unknown/present-with-no-data.

publicSurface.name: World.retrieval_documents
publicSurface.behavior: Actor-safe RAG corpus: state records (visible), non-truth facts, scene state, scene items, npc_public + npc_whereabouts, player-witnessed rumors. HIDDEN CANON (kind=='truth') AND NPC SECRETS ARE EXCLUDED.

publicSurface.name: World.proper_nouns
publicSurface.behavior: Deduped list of NPC names + extra_proper_nouns + scene title/item/exit names.

publicSurface.name: World.scene_context
publicSurface.behavior: GM-facing multi-line scene summary: title, location, world time, description, present NPCs, offscreen whereabouts, visible objects/exits, tension.

publicSurface.name: World.entity_refs
publicSurface.behavior: Returns {'version':1,'entities':[...]} player-facing entity registry (npc/loc) for markdown refs; secrets/private goals/knowledge excluded.

publicSurface.name: World.entity_reference_context
publicSurface.behavior: Compact ref hint string (first 12 npc and 12 loc) using [[npc:id|label]] / [[loc:id|label]] syntax.

publicSurface.name: World.npc_scene_slice
publicSurface.behavior: NPC-actor-facing view of the scene (others, items, exits, up to 8 public facts, up to 12 actor-visible state memories, constraints, entity refs). Returns 'You are not present...' if absent.

publicSurface.name: World.present_witnesses
publicSurface.behavior: frozenset of sorted(present_npcs | {'player'}) — witness set for events/rumors.

publicSurface.name: World.npc_can_react
publicSurface.behavior: True iff NPC present and presence.visible and presence.can_hear.

publicSurface.name: World.set_npc_presence
publicSurface.signature: def set_npc_presence(self, npc_id, present, location='', visible=True, can_hear=True, activity='', attitude='') -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Adds/removes NPC from scene, updates Presence and whereabouts; returns presence summary dict. Uses resolve() (lenient lookup).

publicSurface.name: World.set_scene
publicSurface.signature: def set_scene(self, title, description, location_id='', present_npcs=None, items=None, exits=None, constraints=None, tension='') -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Replaces scene; moves departed present_npcs to 'previous scene' whereabouts; drops unknown present ids (reported as dropped_present_npcs+repair_hint). Returns scene_export().

publicSurface.name: World.set_npc_whereabouts
publicSurface.signature: def set_npc_whereabouts(self, npc_id, location_id='', location_name='', status='', details='', source='') -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: GM tool to set offscreen whereabouts (no-op overwrite if present, just re-syncs).

publicSurface.name: World.record_rumor
publicSurface.signature: def record_rumor(self, seq, turn, speaker, text, witnesses: frozenset) -> None
publicSurface.behavior: Appends unconfirmed Rumor (bumps _rumor_seq), truncates rumors to last config.RUMORS_CAP.

publicSurface.name: World.scene_export
publicSurface.behavior: Serializes scene (scene_id, location_id, title, description, present_npcs, presence, items, exits, constraints, tension, npc_whereabouts).

publicSurface.name: World.npc
publicSurface.behavior: Strict lookup by id; raises KeyError with available ids.

publicSurface.name: World.resolve
publicSurface.behavior: Lenient lookup by id or (case-insensitive substring) name; raises KeyError if no match.

publicSurface.name: World.time_export
publicSurface.behavior: Computes day_number, time_of_day HH:MM from absolute_minutes via minutes_per_hour/hours_per_day; returns full time dict.

publicSurface.name: World.time_summary
publicSurface.behavior: '[calendar, ]date, HH:MM' string.

publicSurface.name: World.time_context
publicSurface.behavior: Multi-line current time + last advance minutes/reason for GM context.

publicSurface.name: World.advance_time
publicSurface.behavior: Adds non-negative minutes to absolute_minutes, records last_advance_*; raises ValueError on negative/non-int.

publicSurface.name: World.update_player_character
publicSurface.signature: def update_player_character(self, fields: dict, reason='') -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Applies PC field changes; bumps pc.card_revision iff any field changed; returns updated list + revision + private export.

publicSurface.name: World.player_character_export
publicSurface.signature: def player_character_export(self, public: bool = True) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: PC snapshot; gm_notes only included when public=False.

publicSurface.name: World.player_character_context
publicSurface.behavior: GM-facing PC sheet text incl. mechanics JSON (sort_keys), inventory/equipment/features, gm_notes, card revision.

publicSurface.name: World.npc_profile
publicSurface.signature: def npc_profile(self, npc_id, preset='visible', fields=None) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Returns {status,npc_id,label,preset,card_revision,profile,ignored_fields} using NPC_PROFILE_PRESETS; never includes secret/knowledge unless explicitly requested field (secret NOT in any preset/NPC_PROFILE_FIELDS).

publicSurface.name: World.roll
publicSurface.signature: def roll(self, notation: str) -> tuple[int, str]
publicSurface.behavior: Deterministic dice roll; returns (total, detail). Uses self._rng or forced overrides.

publicSurface.name: World.roll_for_outcome
publicSurface.signature: def roll_for_outcome(self, notation, target_number=None, target_kind='', roll_kind='') -> tuple[int, str]
publicSurface.behavior: Graded roll; returns (total, detail) wrapping roll_outcome_payload.

publicSurface.name: World.roll_outcome_payload
publicSurface.signature: def roll_outcome_payload(self, notation, target_number=None, target_kind='', roll_kind='') -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Full graded roll dict incl. dice geometry (sides/count/keep/rolls/kept/modifier/forced), grade, margin, natural; nat20/nat1 crit override for attacks.

publicSurface.name: World.update_npc
publicSurface.signature: def update_npc(self, npc_id: str, fields: dict) -> bool
publicSurface.behavior: Debug/author mutator; sets editable NPC fields; bumps card_revision iff a CONTENT (non-color) field actually changed. Returns False if npc missing.

publicSurface.name: World.add_fact
publicSurface.signature: def add_fact(self, text, kind='public') -> FactRecord | None
publicSurface.behavior: Adds public/truth/rumor FactRecord with auto id '{kind}_dbg_{n}', source='debug', confirmed=(kind!='rumor').

publicSurface.name: World.remove_fact
publicSurface.behavior: Removes fact by id; returns whether anything was removed.

publicSurface.name: World.set_public_intro
publicSurface.behavior: Sets self.public. NOTE: this is the ONLY world mutator that touches the cacheable GM system prefix (_gm_world_setup) and re-caches it once.

publicSurface.name: World.set_story_title
publicSurface.behavior: Sets story_title if non-empty.

publicSurface.name: World.set_hidden_truth
publicSurface.behavior: Sets self.canon AND replaces the 'hidden_truth' truth FactRecord, keeping both copies in sync (canon reaches GM twice via query_world_state).

publicSurface.name: World.set_hidden_events
publicSurface.behavior: Replaces hidden_events list (GM-author-only; never surfaced via fact()).

publicSurface.name: World.add_hidden_event
publicSurface.behavior: Appends a hidden event.

publicSurface.name: World.remove_hidden_event
publicSurface.behavior: Removes hidden event by index with bounds check.

publicSurface.name: World.add_debug_rumor
publicSurface.signature: def add_debug_rumor(self, speaker, text) -> Rumor | None
publicSurface.behavior: Adds rumor (turn=0, witnesses=empty), caps to RUMORS_CAP.

publicSurface.name: World.remove_rumor
publicSurface.behavior: Removes rumor by seq.

publicSurface.name: World.set_rumor_confirmed
publicSurface.signature: def set_rumor_confirmed(self, seq, confirmed: bool) -> bool
publicSurface.behavior: Flips rumor.confirmed.

publicSurface.name: World.patch_scene
publicSurface.behavior: Partial scene edit: round-trips untouched items/exits/present_npcs through set_scene, then re-points self.constraints alias.

publicSurface.name: World.fact
publicSurface.signature: def fact(self, query: str, actor_id: str = 'player') -> WorldFact
publicSurface.behavior: Actor-safe world-fact lookup. Tries RAG (rag.retrieve_world_fact) if config.RAG_ENABLED; else word/substring match over non-truth facts + actor-visible state records + rumors. HIDDEN truth and hidden_events NEVER returned.

publicSurface.name: state_record_hash
publicSurface.behavior: Module-level: deterministic sha256 over a canonical (sort_keys, separators) JSON of the record's normalized fields; participants included only if non-empty.

publicSurface.name: WorldFact.as_tool_payload
publicSurface.behavior: {'status','text'[, 'sources']} dict for tool return.

publicSurface.name: SceneState.visible_items
publicSurface.behavior: Items where visible=True.

publicSurface.name: SceneState.visible_exits
publicSurface.behavior: Exits where visible=True.

dataStructures.name: NPC (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: npc_id, name, persona, voice, goals, knowledge, secret, role='', pronouns='' (M|F|N|PL|OTHER/custom), color='' (hex), public_label='', age='', physical_type='', distinctive_features='', life_status='alive', life_status_note='', condition='', personality='', values='', habits='', pressure_response='', boundaries='', abilities:dict={}, skills:dict={}, saving_throws:dict={}, passive_perception:int|None, ac:Any, hp:dict={}, speed='', senses='', languages='', default_whereabouts:dict|None, card_revision:int=0
dataStructures.notes: secret and knowledge are SENSITIVE: secret never enters any player/GM/RAG projection. card_revision bumps on content-field change (color excluded). Persisted by dialog_store._npc_to_payload/_npc_from_payload with EXACTLY these field names.

dataStructures.name: PlayerCharacter (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: name='Искатель', pronouns='OTHER', class_role, level:int|None=1, background, age, physical_type, distinctive_features, life_status='alive', life_status_note, condition, personality, values, gm_notes, abilities:dict (STR/DEX/CON/INT/WIS/CHA defaults), skills:dict, saving_throws:dict, passive_perception:int|None=14, ac=12, hp:dict={'current':9,'max':9}, speed='30 ft', senses, languages, inventory:list, equipment:list, features:list, card_revision:int=0
dataStructures.notes: gm_notes excluded from public export. Editable fields enumerated in PLAYER_CHARACTER_FIELDS (excludes card_revision).

dataStructures.name: SceneState (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: scene_id, location_id, title, description, present_npcs:set[str]={}, presence:dict[str,Presence]={}, items:list[SceneItem]=[], exits:list[SceneExit]=[], constraints:list[str]=[], tension='', player_seen:list[str]=[]
dataStructures.notes: scene_id==location_id after set_scene/patch_scene. Serialized by _scene_to_payload.

dataStructures.name: Presence (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: npc_id, location, visible=True, can_hear=True, activity='', attitude=''
dataStructures.notes: Per-present-NPC scene presence.

dataStructures.name: SceneItem (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: item_id, name, location, visible=True, portable=False, owner='', details=''

dataStructures.name: SceneExit (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: exit_id, name, destination, visible=True, blocked_by=''

dataStructures.name: NPCWhereabouts (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: npc_id, location_id='', location_name='', status='unknown', details='', source=''
dataStructures.notes: status in WHEREABOUTS_STATUSES = present|known|likely|rumored|unknown|left_scene. Present NPCs always synced to status='present', source='current scene'.

dataStructures.name: FactRecord (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: fact_id, kind ('public'|'truth'|'rumor'), text, keywords:list=[], source='', confirmed=True
dataStructures.notes: kind=='truth' is HIDDEN CANON: excluded from retrieval_documents and fact(). 'hidden_truth' is a reserved fact_id mirroring self.canon.

dataStructures.name: Rumor (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: seq, turn, speaker, text, witnesses:frozenset=frozenset(), confirmed=False
dataStructures.notes: Capped to RUMORS_CAP (80). Only player-witnessed rumors enter retrieval_documents as 'testimony'.

dataStructures.name: StateRecord (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: record_id, kind, text, scope='public', active=True, owner='', subject='', source='', status='known', tags:tuple=(), entity_id='', source_npc='', participants:tuple=(), location_id='', location_name='', region_id='', region_name='', scene_id='', importance='', aliases:tuple=(), metadata:dict={}
dataStructures.notes: kind in STATE_RECORD_KINDS=(fact,rumor,npc_memory,relationship,goal). scope in STATE_RECORD_SCOPES=(public,gm,owner,subject,participants) with aliases private/npc->owner, shared/participant(s)->participants. Visibility gated by _state_record_visible_to. metadata.known_name drives durable identity reveal.

dataStructures.name: Event (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: seq, turn, actor ('player'|npc_id|'gm'), kind ('speech'|'action'|'dice'), speech='', action='', witnesses:frozenset=frozenset()
dataStructures.notes: PUBLIC observable event ONLY: docstring explicitly forbids reasoning/claims/secret/canon. Defined in world.py but OWNED/capped by orchestrator (EVENTS_CAP=400). Persisted via dialog_store._event_*.

dataStructures.name: WorldTime (dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: calendar_name='', absolute_minutes=0, current_date_label='', minutes_per_hour=60, hours_per_day=24, day_names:list=[], month_names:list=[], last_advance_minutes=0, last_advance_reason=''
dataStructures.notes: minutes_per_hour/hours_per_day clamped to >=1.

dataStructures.name: WorldFact (frozen dataclass)
dataStructures.fields: status, text, sources:list[dict]=[]
dataStructures.notes: Return type of World.fact().

dataStructures.name: World snapshot (JSON, dialog_store._world_to_payload)
dataStructures.fields: story_id, story_title, dice_seed:int, forced_die_next, forced_die_all, rng_state:{version,internal[list[int]],gauss}, hidden_events:[str], rumors:[...], rumor_seq:int, npcs:{id:npc_payload}, public, canon, time, player_character, extra_proper_nouns:[str], scene, npc_whereabouts, fact_records:[...], state_records:[...]
dataStructures.notes: REQUIRED on load: story_id, story_title, npcs, public, canon, scene, fact_records, AND dice_seed, AND rng_state (loader raises ValueError if dice_seed/rng_state/npcs/scene/fact_records missing). World object is reconstructed via World.__new__ (bypassing __init__) so RNG is set ONLY from rng_state, not regenerated.

dataStructures.name: rng_state payload
dataStructures.fields: {version:int (default 3), internal:[int...], gauss:float|None}
dataStructures.notes: Mirrors Python random.Random.getstate()=(version, tuple_of_625_uints, gauss_next). MT19937 internal state must be reproduced bit-exactly in Rust for deterministic save/restore.

dataStructures.name: NPC JSON contract (agents.py schema)
dataStructures.fields: {reasoning:string, speech:string, action:string, claims:[string]} — all 4 required
dataStructures.notes: NPC sub-agent output contract. reasoning and claims are NPC-internal/unverified; only speech/action become public Events (mirrors Event's exclusion rule).

controlFlow: Construction: World.__init__ -> _new_dice_seed() (SystemRandom 64-bit) -> random.Random(seed) -> _load_seed (or _load_default via stories.default_story_seed). _load_seed calls _normalize_seed (accepts strict or loose model-produced seed shape), then sequentially: story_id/title, public(intro), canon(hidden_truth), _seed_time, _seed_player_character, extra_proper_nouns, _seed_npcs, _seed_scene, constraints alias, _seed_facts, _seed_state_records, then _ensure_npc_whereabouts (which also _apply_default_story_whereabouts and _sync_present_npc_whereabouts). Restore path (dialog_store._world_from_payload) bypasses __init__ via World.__new__, sets all attrs directly, calls _ensure_npc_whereabouts, then sets dice_seed and reconstructs _rng from rng_state via setstate. Runtime: GM tool calls drive mutators (set_scene/patch_scene/set_npc_presence/set_npc_whereabouts/advance_time/update_npc/update_player_character/apply_state_record_batch/add_fact/record_rumor/roll*). Projection methods (scene_context/retrieval_documents/entity_refs/npc_scene_slice/fact) are read-only and called per-turn by orchestrator/agents to build prompts. Dice: roll_outcome_payload -> _roll_data parses NdM[kh/kl K][+/-mod] via regex, applies forced_die_next (one-shot, consumed) or forced_die_all, else _rng.randint; computes grade via _grade_from_margin and nat20/nat1 attack overrides. Whereabouts have a strict precedence: present NPCs always overwritten to 'present'; card default_whereabouts only seeded when row is unknown with no source.
externalDeps: Python stdlib: random (random.Random=MT19937, random.SystemRandom for seed), re (regex parsing of seeds/dice/ids), hashlib (sha256 for state_record_hash), json (canonical serialization), dataclasses, typing
module 'stories' (story_seed, default_story_seed) — seed source
module 'config' (RUMORS_CAP=80, EVENTS_CAP=400 used by orchestrator, CHARS_PER_TOKEN=3, RAG_ENABLED) — imported lazily inside record_rumor/add_debug_rumor/fact
module 'rag' (RagDocument, retrieve_world_fact) — imported lazily inside state_record_documents/retrieval_documents/fact; optional (try/except: RAG is accuracy layer, not hard dependency)
module 'dialog_store' (external serializer, not imported by world.py) — owns the JSON snapshot contract and rng_state round-trip
module 'agents' (external) — owns the NPC reasoning/speech/action/claims JSON schema
internalDeps: (consumed by) orchestrator, agents, server, dialog_store, mock_server
invariants: SECRET ISOLATION (highest priority): NPC.secret and NPC.knowledge never enter any player/GM/RAG-facing projection. NPC_PROFILE_PRESETS and NPC_PROFILE_FIELDS deliberately exclude 'secret'; npc_profile only returns 'secret' if a caller explicitly names it as a field (must be preserved). retrieval_documents docstring: 'Hidden canon and NPC secrets deliberately do not enter this corpus.'

HIDDEN CANON ISOLATION: FactRecord.kind=='truth' is skipped in retrieval_documents AND in fact(). self.canon is mirrored into a reserved FactRecord(fact_id='hidden_truth', kind='truth'); set_hidden_truth MUST keep self.canon and that record in sync (canon reaches GM twice via query_world_state: as gm_canon and as the truth fact). hidden_events are GM-author-only and MUST NOT surface via fact() (explicit comment: 'No public hidden-events fallback here by design').
EVENT PUBLIC-ONLY RULE: Event holds only speech/action; reasoning/claims/secret/canon are forbidden (class docstring). Mirrors NPC JSON contract where only speech/action are public.

PROMPT-CACHE PREFIX DISCIPLINE (explicit comment block ~lines 2498-2502): all world mutators change data reaching the model via the appended per-turn tail or query_world_state (GM scope) — NOT the cached system prefix — EXCEPT set_public_intro, which sets self.public living in the cacheable prefix (_gm_world_setup) and thus re-caches the prefix ONCE. A faithful port must keep this single exception.

DETERMINISTIC DICE: per-campaign dice_seed via random.SystemRandom().getrandbits(64) at creation only; thereafter rolls follow random.Random(dice_seed) (MT19937) deterministically. The EXACT RNG state (getstate/setstate: version, 625-int internal vector, gauss) is persisted across save/restore. Restore bypasses __init__ and sets _rng via setstate — never reseeds. Rust port MUST reproduce MT19937 + Python's randint(1,sides) (Lemire/getrandbits-based bounded sampling) bit-exactly, or save/restore determinism breaks.

FORCED DIE OVERRIDES: forced_die_next (one-shot, consumed/reset to None after the roll that uses it) takes precedence over forced_die_all (persistent). When forced, every die face = clamp(forced,1,sides). 'forced' flag is in geometry but the model-facing compact payload whitelists fields so geometry never reaches the model.

DICE NOTATION REGEX: r'\s*(\d*)d(\d+)\s*(k[hl]\s*\d+)?\s*([+-]\s*\d+)?\s*' (lowercased). count default 1; kh=keep highest, kl=keep lowest; keep_count must be 1..count. natural recorded only when sides==20 and exactly one kept die.

GRADE LADDER (_grade_from_margin, must match exactly): margin>=15 overwhelming_success; >=10 critical_success; >=5 strong_success; >=0 success; >=-2 near_miss; >=-4 weak_failure; >=-9 failure; >=-14 major_failure; else critical_failure. Attack nat20 -> critical_success, nat1 -> critical_failure overrides margin grade. Grading only applies when roll_kind in {check,save,attack,contest} AND target is not None, else grade='ungraded' (or 'invalid' on bad notation).

ROLL_KIND ALIASES: ability_check->check, saving_throw->save, random->chance, opposed->contest (lowercased, '-'/' ' -> '_'). target_label defaults: attack->AC, check/save->DC, contest->opposed_total, else 'target'.

CAPACITY CAPS: rumors truncated to last config.RUMORS_CAP (default 80) on every record_rumor/add_debug_rumor (self.rumors = self.rumors[-RUMORS_CAP:]). EVENTS_CAP (default 400) applies to Event list but is enforced in orchestrator (self.events[-EVENTS_CAP:]), NOT in world.py.

TOKEN HEURISTIC: config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN=3 (single source for _SYS_EST, _estimate_tokens, GM/NPC compact gates in orchestrator); estimate = max(0, len(text)//3). GM_HISTORY_TOKENS=100000, GM_KEEP_TURNS=3, NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS=64000, NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES=6, COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS=12000 (compaction thresholds live in orchestrator/config, referenced here only via caps).

card_revision: starts 0; old snapshots default to 0. update_npc bumps ONLY when a CONTENT field (all editable fields EXCEPT 'color') actually changes value; color-only edits must NOT bump. update_player_character bumps iff any tracked field changed. Confirmed by test_contracts.py (color edit keeps revision, content edit increments).

STATE-RECORD VISIBILITY (_state_record_visible_to, static, must match exactly): actor defaults to 'player'. scope 'public' -> always visible; GM/debug/system actors (STATE_GM_ACTORS={gm,debug,system}) see everything; scope 'gm' -> only GM actors; 'owner' -> actor==owner; 'subject' -> actor==subject; 'participants' -> actor in {owner,subject,*participants}; unknown scope -> False.

ID SAFETY: _safe_id lowercases and replaces [^a-zA-Z0-9_]+ with '_', strips '_', falls back to provided fallback. Duplicate npc/state-record ids get numeric suffixes (_2, _3...). All ids derived deterministically from name/title when not given.

WHEREABOUTS PRECEDENCE: _sync_present_npc_whereabouts ALWAYS overwrites present NPCs to status='present', location=scene.location_id/title, source='current scene'. _apply_default_story_whereabouts only seeds NPC.default_whereabouts when existing row.status=='unknown' AND no source (so it never clobbers established info). Source tag constants (SOURCE_DEFAULT_LORE='default public lore', SOURCE_PREVIOUS_SCENE='previous scene', SOURCE_CURRENT_SCENE='current scene', SOURCE_MOVE_NPC='move_npc', SOURCE_GM='gm', SOURCE_SEED='seed', SOURCE_NPC_ROSTER='npc_roster') and the SOURCE_RU/WHEREABOUTS_STATUS_LABELS maps are the single source of truth shared with the frontend via /state — must not be duplicated.

state_record_hash canonicalization: json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), default=str) then sha256 hexdigest. 'status' defaults to 'known' if empty. participants key included ONLY if non-empty. Rust must reproduce identical canonical JSON ordering/separators to get matching hashes.
PlayerCharacter mechanics JSON in context uses json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) — deterministic ordering matters for prompt-cache stability.
platformConcerns: random.SystemRandom().getrandbits(64) uses OS CSPRNG (os.urandom under the hood) for the initial campaign dice_seed — cross-platform but must use a real OS entropy source in Rust (getrandom crate), NOT a time-seeded PRNG.

No filesystem, subprocess, socket, TLS, browser, ffmpeg, or GPU usage in world.py itself — it is pure in-memory state. Platform concerns are confined to dependencies: rag (HTTP embeddings server at 127.0.0.1:8080) and dialog_store (SQLite persistence).

All ids are ASCII-normalized; Russian text (Cyrillic) flows through unicode strings — Rust must use UTF-8 String throughout (labels, descriptions, fact text are Russian). _match_words regex [a-zа-яё0-9]+ explicitly tokenizes Cyrillic, so the Rust regex must be unicode-aware for а-яё.
No path handling here; OS path concerns belong to dialog_store/config.
rustNotes: The MT19937 RNG is the single hardest port detail. Use rand with rand_mt (Mt19937GenRng) OR a hand-rolled MT19937 that EXACTLY matches CPython's _randommodule: 624-word state + index, init_by_array seeding, and Python's getrandbits/randint bounded-integer algorithm. dice_seed is 64-bit; Python seeds Random(int) via init_by_array on the int's 32-bit words. Validate against captured Python rng_state vectors. The persisted rng_state has 625 ints (624 state words + 1 index) plus version=3 and gauss; store/restore as-is.

Model dataclasses as structs with serde derive. Use #[serde(default)] generously — old snapshots omit fields (card_revision defaults to 0). NPC/PC/Scene/StateRecord/FactRecord/Rumor/Event/Whereabouts each map to a struct; ac:Any -> serde_json::Value; abilities/skills/hp -> serde_json::Map or BTreeMap<String,Value>.

Use BTreeMap (not HashMap) where deterministic ordering feeds prompts (npcs iteration in retrieval_documents/proper_nouns; mechanics JSON). present_npcs is a set but iterated via sorted() everywhere — use BTreeSet<String> to get sorted order for free.

state_record_hash: serialize the canonical payload with serde_json but you MUST replicate Python's sort_keys=True + separators=(',',':') (no spaces) + ensure_ascii=False. serde_json with sorted keys (BTreeMap) and compact formatter matches; verify byte-for-byte against Python output, then sha2::Sha256.

Dice notation: use regex crate with the exact pattern. Be careful: keep_count bounds, mod sign parsing, and the natural==(sides==20 && kept.len()==1) condition. The forced_die_next one-shot consume semantics must be a mutable field reset to None inside _roll_data.

Visibility/scope and whereabouts-status enums: model as Rust enums with explicit string (de)serialization preserving the alias maps (private/npc->owner, shared/participant(s)->participants; roll_kind aliases). Keep STATE_GM_ACTORS={gm,debug,system} and STATE_DEBUG_ACTORS as const sets.

Lazy 'import config'/'import rag' inside methods is just deferred-dependency wiring; in Rust pass these as parameters or via a config struct / trait object for the RAG retriever (Option<&dyn RagRetriever>) so RAG remains optional (the try/except around RAG must become a graceful Result fallback that ignores errors).

The single prompt-cache-prefix mutator (set_public_intro) should be flagged in the type system or via a returned 'prefix_dirty' signal so the orchestrator port knows to invalidate/recompute the cached GM system prefix exactly once.

Russian-language string constants (ROLE_RU, GENDER_LABELS_RU, WHEREABOUTS_STATUS_LABELS, SOURCE_RU) are load-bearing UI/contract data shared with the frontend — port them verbatim as const maps; do not translate or reformat.
suggestedCrates: serde + serde_json (snapshot (de)serialization, ac:Any -> Value, canonical hashing)
sha2 (state_record_hash sha256)
regex (seed/dice/id parsing; unicode-aware for Cyrillic)
getrandom or rand (OS entropy for the 64-bit dice_seed)
rand_mt or a custom MT19937 module matching CPython exactly (deterministic dice + getstate/setstate round-trip)
BTreeMap/BTreeSet from std (deterministic ordering for prompt stability)
once_cell / phf (static RU label/alias maps)
risks: MT19937 fidelity: random.Random.setstate/getstate and randint(1,sides) must be bit-exact, including Python's _randbelow_with_getrandbits rejection-sampling for non-power-of-two ranges (e.g. d6, d20). A naive modulo or a different MT implementation will produce different rolls after restore — silent divergence, hard to detect. Highest-risk item.

Python's Random.seed(int) for an arbitrary 64-bit int uses init_by_array over the int's 32-bit chunks; getting the seeding wrong only matters for brand-new campaigns (the restore path uses setstate and is more robust), but cross-checking both is needed.

state_record_hash byte-exactness depends on Python json.dumps quirks (ensure_ascii=False keeps Cyrillic raw, sort_keys, no-space separators, default=str for non-serializable). Any difference (e.g. float formatting, key ordering of nested metadata) changes the hash; downstream consumers comparing hashes would see spurious changes.
Event/EVENTS_CAP capping lives in orchestrator, not world.py — easy to miss when porting; the Event dataclass is defined here but its lifecycle/cap is elsewhere.

_normalize_seed is large and lenient, accepting many loosely-shaped model outputs (npcs as list OR dict, items under visible_objects/objects/items, exits under visible_exits/exits, title under several keys). Faithful porting requires reproducing every fallback branch; an incomplete port will reject valid seeds local models produce.

Some state-record fields are only set via _coerce_state_record alias keys (owner_id, subject_id, entity/about, source_npc_id) — the update/add paths accept multiple key spellings; missing an alias silently drops data.

npc_profile can leak 'secret' if a caller passes fields=['secret']; this is intentional in Python (any NPC_PROFILE_FIELDS member) but note 'secret' is NOT in NPC_PROFILE_FIELDS, so it is effectively unreachable via profile — confirm the Rust port keeps secret unreachable through the profile path.

patch_scene relies on set_scene round-tripping live SceneItem/SceneExit objects via dicts; subtle: set_scene regenerates ids from names when id missing, so a faithful patch must pass existing ids back (it does). Re-pointing self.constraints alias after set_scene is required because GM turn context reads self.constraints, not self.scene.constraints directly.

purpose: stories.py is a pure-data catalog of prebuilt D&D scenarios ("stories"). Each story is a static Python dict containing a "seed" describing the starting World: public intro, hidden GM-only truth, the player character card, a list of NPC cards (stats + persona + per-NPC secret), the opening scene (items/exits/constraints/NPC presence), public facts, and pre-known state records. The module exposes 5 thin lookup functions; World.from_story()/_load_default() in world.py deep-copy a seed and hydrate it into a live World. Stories are DATA, not code — there is no story-progression or quest-state engine here; all progression, dice, history, and persistence live elsewhere (world.py / orchestrator). The only logic in this file is id lookup and a deepcopy.

publicSurface.name: DEFAULT_STORY_ID
publicSurface.behavior: Module-level constant; the id used when no story is selected. server.py exposes it as default_story_id; test_contracts.py asserts it equals "turnvale-murder" and that a default World has story_id == DEFAULT_STORY_ID.

publicSurface.name: STORY_DEFINITIONS
publicSurface.behavior: Module-level list of 3 story definition dicts (turnvale-murder, frozen-harbor, glass-garden). Each has top-level keys id/title/description and a nested seed dict. This is the single source of truth; the docstring says new stories are added by appending one dict here.

publicSurface.name: story_ids
publicSurface.behavior: Returns {story["id"] for story in STORY_DEFINITIONS}. server.py uses it to validate a requested story_id before loading (server.py:849, 1070).

publicSurface.name: story_metadata
publicSurface.behavior: Linear scan of STORY_DEFINITIONS; returns {"id","title","description"} for the match. Raises KeyError(f"unknown story: {story_id}") if not found.

publicSurface.name: list_stories
publicSurface.behavior: Returns [story_metadata(id) for each story] — the menu of available scenarios (id/title/description only, no seed). server.py:761 sends this to the client.

publicSurface.name: story_seed
publicSurface.behavior: Linear scan; on match returns copy.deepcopy(story["seed"]) then OVERWRITES seed["id"]=story["id"] and seed["title"]=story["title"] (top-level id/title win over the nested seed copies). Raises KeyError(f"unknown story: {story_id}") otherwise. Consumed by world.World.from_story (world.py:650).

publicSurface.name: default_story_seed
publicSurface.behavior: Returns story_seed(DEFAULT_STORY_ID). Consumed by world.World._load_default (world.py:653).

dataStructures.name: StoryDefinition (top-level dict)
dataStructures.fields: id: str; title: str; description: str; seed: SeedDict. (Exactly these 4 keys per entry in STORY_DEFINITIONS.)
dataStructures.notes: title/description are RU human-facing strings. id is the stable key used everywhere (also becomes World.story_id). description is a one-line teaser shown in the story menu.

dataStructures.name: SeedDict (seed)
dataStructures.fields: id: str; title: str; public_intro: str; hidden_truth: str; player_character: PlayerCharacterCard; proper_nouns: list[str]; public_facts: list[PublicFact]; state_records: list[StateRecordSeed]; npcs: list[NPCCard]; scene: SceneDict. (Note: seed.id/seed.title are present but story_seed() overwrites them from the parent.)
dataStructures.notes: This is the strict/canonical seed shape. world._normalize_seed treats it as already-strict when scene is a dict with items+exits+title AND npcs is a list (world.py:493-495), passing it through untouched. NO 'time' key is present in any of the 3 stories, so World.time defaults (День 1, 60 min/hr, 24 hr/day).

dataStructures.name: PlayerCharacterCard (seed.player_character)
dataStructures.fields: name; pronouns ('F'/'M'); class_role; level: int; background; age; physical_type; distinctive_features; condition; personality; values; gm_notes; abilities: {STR,DEX,CON,INT,WIS,CHA: int}; skills: {SkillName: int}; saving_throws: {Stat: int}; passive_perception: int; ac: int; hp: {current,max}; speed: str; senses; languages; inventory: list[str]; equipment: list[str]; features: list[str]
dataStructures.notes: Loaded by world._seed_player_character -> _apply_player_character_fields. card_revision is NOT present in seed data, so it defaults to 0 (world.py:699 max(0, ... or 0)). gm_notes/class_role/level/inventory/equipment/features are PC-only fields not present on NPC cards.

dataStructures.name: NPCCard (seed.npcs[*])
dataStructures.fields: id; name; role; pronouns; color (hex like '#e6c08a'); public_label; age; physical_type; distinctive_features; life_status ('alive'); condition; personality; values; habits; pressure_response; boundaries; abilities; skills; saving_throws; passive_perception: int; ac: int; hp: {current,max}; speed; senses; languages; persona; voice; goals; knowledge; secret; [optional] default_whereabouts: {location_id,location_name,status,details,source}
dataStructures.notes: Loaded verbatim by world._seed_npcs (world.py:713-747). 'secret' is the HIDDEN per-NPC canon (e.g. borin is a thieves-guild informant). 'knowledge' is what the NPC openly knows. public_label is the player-facing label before identity is learned. default_whereabouts only on offscreen NPCs (mareth, sana, veik, nemir) — those NOT in scene.present_npcs. life_status is always 'alive' in seed data.

dataStructures.name: PublicFact (seed.public_facts[*])
dataStructures.fields: id: str; text: str (RU); keywords: list[str] (RU + EN, lowercase, for RAG keyword matching)
dataStructures.notes: Loaded by world._seed_facts into FactRecord(kind='public', confirmed=True by default). keywords mix Cyrillic and Latin (e.g. ['тёрнвейл','turnvale','town']) so RAG can match either language. NB: seed public_facts here are dicts; _normalize_seed's loose path expects public_facts as list[str] instead — only the strict path is hit for these stories.

dataStructures.name: StateRecordSeed (seed.state_records[*])
dataStructures.fields: id; kind ('fact'); text (RU); scope ('public'); entity_id (e.g. 'borin','mareth'); source ('story_public_setup'); metadata: {known_name: str}
dataStructures.notes: Pre-known facts seeded into World.state_records via _seed_state_records/_coerce_state_record. These tell the engine the player ALREADY knows certain NPC names from public lore (metadata.known_name='Борин'), so the NPC is shown by name not by public_label. entity_id links the record to an NPC id.

dataStructures.name: SceneDict (seed.scene)
dataStructures.fields: id; location_id; title; description; present_npcs: list[str] (npc ids physically on stage); npc_presence: {npc_id: {location,activity,attitude}}; items: list[{id,name,location,visible:bool,portable:bool, owner?:str}]; exits: list[{id,name,destination,visible:bool}]; constraints: list[str]; tension: str
dataStructures.notes: Loaded by world._seed_scene (world.py:760+). present_npcs is the live cast; offscreen NPCs (with default_whereabouts) are NOT listed here. items may carry owner (e.g. 'borin'). Some items encode hidden-clue text in their name (e.g. 'мокрые следы с тёмной пылью'). constraints become World.constraints / scene.constraints.

controlFlow: No runtime control flow inside stories.py beyond linear list scans. Entry points are the 5 functions. Lifecycle: server.py validates story_id via story_ids(); World.from_story(story_id) (world.py:650) calls stories.story_seed(story_id) which deepcopies seed and overwrites id/title; World.__init__ seeds dice_seed/RNG, then _load_seed -> _normalize_seed (pass-through for these strict seeds) -> populates story_id, story_title, public (from public_intro), canon (from hidden_truth), time, player_character, extra_proper_nouns (from proper_nouns + appended NPC names + scene title), npcs, scene, fact_records (public_facts + a synthesized 'hidden_truth' truth FactRecord), state_records, npc_whereabouts. No story-progression/quest state machine exists in this file. There is no async/generator/streaming/IO here — it is a synchronous in-memory data module.
externalDeps: copy.deepcopy (stdlib) — used by story_seed to isolate each session's mutable seed copy from the shared catalog
invariants: story_seed MUST deepcopy the seed before returning (stories.py:1343 seed = deepcopy(story["seed"])). The catalog is a shared mutable list; without a deep copy, in-session World mutations (renaming NPCs, moving items, killing NPCs) would corrupt the global STORY_DEFINITIONS and bleed into the next session. The Rust port MUST clone (owned copy) the seed per session — never hand out a shared reference.

story_seed MUST overwrite seed["id"] and seed["title"] from the TOP-LEVEL story dict AFTER deepcopy (stories.py:1344-1345). Top-level id/title are authoritative over the nested seed copies. Preserve this precedence; World.story_id derives from it and tests assert story_id == DEFAULT_STORY_ID.
DEFAULT_STORY_ID == "turnvale-murder" exactly (stories.py:11). test_contracts.py:75 asserts this literal; test_contracts.py:79 asserts a default World.story_id equals it. Keep the literal stable.

HIDDEN-CANON ISOLATION: seed.hidden_truth is the GM-only campaign secret and each NPCCard.secret is that NPC's private hidden canon. world._load_seed maps hidden_truth -> World.canon and synthesizes a FactRecord(fact_id='hidden_truth', kind='truth', keywords=['hidden truth','truth','secret'], source='seed') (world.py:871-878). The port MUST keep hidden_truth and per-NPC secret OUT of any player-facing/public surface and out of other NPCs' contexts — they are isolated canon, distinct from public_intro/public_facts/knowledge.

NPC JSON CONTRACT field names on the card: persona, voice, goals, knowledge, secret (the in-character generation inputs) plus public_label (pre-identity label). These exact keys are read in world._seed_npcs (world.py:726-735) with RU defaults if absent (voice default 'Естественно, кратко, в образе.'; goals default 'Реагировать правдоподобно...'; knowledge default 'Только то, что очевидно в текущей сцене.'; secret default 'Личная тайна не задана.'). Preserve key names and default strings.

card_revision is NEVER present in seed data; it defaults to 0 for both PC and NPCs (world.py:699; NPC dataclass default card_revision:0). The invariant 'old snapshots default to 0' lives in world.py. The port must initialize card_revision=0 for freshly-seeded cards.

NPC id collision handling: ids are taken from card.id but deduped (world.py:709-712 appends _2,_3...). Within a story the ids are already unique (borin/lysa/mareth, iva/run/sana/veik, ella/milo/nemir/serra). Port should preserve declaration order and dedup deterministically.

present_npcs vs default_whereabouts split: onstage NPCs are listed in scene.present_npcs and scene.npc_presence; offscreen NPCs carry a default_whereabouts dict and are absent from present_npcs (turnvale: present borin+lysa, offscreen mareth; frozen: present iva+run, offscreen sana+veik; glass: present ella+milo, offscreen nemir+serra). This split drives World._ensure_npc_whereabouts. Preserve exactly.

state_records seed the player's pre-known NPC names: each has scope='public', source='story_public_setup', metadata.known_name=<display name>, entity_id=<npc id>. This is what makes the engine show certain NPCs by name vs public_label from turn one. Preserve scope/source/metadata.known_name keys.
public_facts.keywords are bilingual lowercase tokens (RU + EN) used for RAG/keyword retrieval (consumed downstream). The mix is intentional; do not normalize away the Cyrillic or the English aliases.

Seed-shape strictness gate (world.py:493-495): a seed is treated as already-canonical (no normalization) iff scene is a dict containing 'items' AND 'exits' AND 'title' AND npcs is a list. All 3 catalog stories satisfy this, so they bypass the loose normalizer. A faithful port's normalizer must apply the same gate or the strict catalog seeds could be wrongly re-shaped.

Item names carry latent clue text (e.g. 'мокрые следы с тёмной пылью', 'зола с обрывком синей сургучной печати', 'смолёный кусок каната у сапога Руна'). Items also carry visible:bool, portable:bool, optional owner. Preserve these flags and the descriptive names verbatim — they are gameplay-load-bearing.

No 'time' key in any seed => World defaults apply (current_date_label='День 1', minutes_per_hour=60, hours_per_day=24). dice_seed is generated per-World in world.py (NOT in stories.py); the story catalog contributes no dice seed.
All NPC life_status seeds are 'alive'; world default is also 'alive'. life_status_note absent => empty.
platformConcerns: None. stories.py is pure in-memory Python data + deepcopy; no filesystem paths, no subprocess, no sockets, no TLS, no browser, no GPU, no env vars. Fully OS-portable as-is. The only Windows/macOS/Linux concern is source-file encoding: the file is heavily Cyrillic UTF-8 — the Rust port must store these strings as UTF-8 (Rust String is UTF-8 by default, so trivially fine) and any file/console output of them must not assume a non-UTF-8 codepage on Windows.
rustNotes: Model the catalog as static data, not code. Two good options: (a) define Rust structs (StoryDefinition, Seed, NpcCard, PlayerCharacterCard, PublicFact, StateRecordSeed, Scene, SceneItem, SceneExit, NpcPresence, DefaultWhereabouts) and build the 3 stories with a once_cell/LazyLock Vec, or (b) keep the seeds as embedded JSON/RON files via include_str! and deserialize with serde. Given the rewrite wants 'stories are data', embedding RON/JSON (option b) is the most faithful and lets non-programmers add stories — mirrors the docstring's 'append one definition'.

story_seed's deepcopy maps directly to #[derive(Clone)] on Seed and returning seed.clone(). Never return &Seed into the World; clone an owned Seed per session so in-session mutation can't touch the catalog.
Replicate the id/title override: after cloning seed, set seed.id = def.id.clone(); seed.title = def.title.clone();

Use Option<DefaultWhereabouts> for the offscreen-only field. Use serde rename/aliases if you keep JSON, because world.py accepts loose aliases (pronouns|gender, persona|description, location|position, state|activity, mood|attitude, visible_objects|objects|items, visible_exits|exits) — but the strict catalog seeds use only the canonical names, so for the catalog itself you can keep the strict names and put aliases only in the importer for user/LLM-authored seeds.
Errors: story_metadata/story_seed raise KeyError("unknown story: <id>"). In Rust return Result<_, StoryError::UnknownStory(String)> rather than panicking.

abilities/skills/saving_throws/hp are string->int maps; use BTreeMap<String,i64> (or a typed Abilities struct {str_,dex,con,int_,wis,cha}) — typed is safer and matches the fixed STR/DEX/CON/INT/WIS/CHA shape. hp is {current,max}. ac is an int. Keep numbers as i64/u32 consistently.
Keep declaration order of npcs/items/exits/public_facts (Vec, not HashMap) — order is observable (NPC id dedup, present_npcs derivation, fact slicing public_facts[:8] downstream).
Catalog is read-only and Send+Sync — wrap in LazyLock<Vec<StoryDefinition>>; list_stories()/story_ids()/story_metadata() are cheap projections.
suggestedCrates: serde + serde_json (or ron) — if seeds are externalized to data files (recommended for the 'stories are data' goal)
once_cell or std::sync::LazyLock — for the static catalog
thiserror — for StoryError::UnknownStory
include_dir or rust-embed (optional) — to bundle per-story RON/JSON files into the binary while keeping each story in its own file
risks: The seed contract is implicit: stories.py only emits data; the REAL invariants about how each field is consumed (FactRecord synthesis, hidden_truth keywords, card_revision defaulting, present_npcs/whereabouts split, the strict-shape gate, RU default strings) live in world.py (_load_seed/_normalize_seed/_seed_npcs/_seed_facts/_seed_state_records). A port that only translates stories.py without porting world.py's loader will silently drop these behaviors. The mapping I gave for those is sourced from world.py, not stories.py.

Some seed fields appear in the data but are NOT read by world._seed_npcs (verify against the full NPC dataclass): notably npc card 'habits' IS read (world.py:729) but confirm every card key has a sink; unread keys would be lost in a struct-based port. I confirmed habits/pressure_response/boundaries are read; double-check less-obvious ones if you go struct-typed rather than passthrough-map.

public_facts in these stories are dicts with id/text/keywords, but the loose _normalize_seed path (world.py:498-500) expects list[str]; only the strict path preserves the dict form. If the Rust normalizer doesn't gate identically, the rich public_facts could be flattened to bare text and lose ids/keywords (breaking RAG).
Heavy Cyrillic content: any test that compares strings, or any logging on Windows, must be UTF-8 safe. Not a logic risk but an encoding-regression risk during porting.

No quest/progression/branching state exists in stories.py at all — if the Rust design assumes stories carry quest graphs, that assumption is wrong; progression is emergent via World/orchestrator state, not authored here.

story_seed silently overwrites nested seed.id/seed.title — if a future story author relied on a different nested id, it would be ignored. Faithful port must keep this override (it is currently harmless because nested and top-level ids match in all 3 stories).

subsystem: Model roles & tool definitions (agents.py): GM tool schemas, ask_npc/NPC sub-agent contract, prompt builders, tool_guidance
purpose: agents.py is the model-boundary layer of GM-Lab. It defines (1) the static OpenAI-style GM tool schemas and the catalog/search machinery that gates which tools the GM model sees (build_gm_tools, build_gm_tools_for_model, search_gm_tools), (2) the prompt assembly for the GM turn (system + stable world setup + STORY-SO-FAR summary + append-only history + late mutable CURRENT TURN CONTEXT), (3) the NPC sub-agent contract: the strict JSON schema (reasoning/speech/action/claims), the static NPC system prompt, and the late CURRENT NPC CARD block, and (4) world-seed and scene-delta extraction helpers. tool_guidance.py is the single home for shared model-facing capability strings and world_state guidance text spliced into the update_world_state description. gm_tools_guide.md is the developer playbook documenting the cache-safety rules and the add/change/remove-tool workflow. The whole module exists to keep the early prompt prefix and tool payload byte-stable for prompt-cache / KV-cache reuse while live world state is injected only in the late tail.

publicSurface.name: initial_gm_tool_names
publicSurface.behavior: Returns the always-loaded GM tool name set (_INITIAL_GM_TOOL_NAMES), optionally adding 'ask_player'. Used to seed Session.loaded_gm_tools and to union with discovered tools when filtering visible tools.

publicSurface.name: build_gm_tools
publicSurface.behavior: Builds the full STATIC GM tool list (13 OpenAI-style {type:function,function:{...}} dicts). Schemas describe behavior only and never enumerate live world (no roster/dynamic id enums). 'world' is taken only for the static engine enum get_npc_profile.fields (list(world_mod.NPC_PROFILE_FIELDS)).

publicSurface.name: gm_tool_catalog
publicSurface.behavior: Returns build_gm_tools as a dict keyed by tool name; the executable registry.

publicSurface.name: build_gm_tools_for_model
publicSurface.signature: build_gm_tools_for_model(world, loaded_tool_names: set[str]|None = None, include_player_options_tool: bool = False) -> list
publicSurface.behavior: Returns only the tools currently visible to the GM model. If loaded_tool_names is None, returns the whole catalog (legacy). Otherwise returns tools whose name is in (loaded_tool_names | initial_gm_tool_names). Drops 'ask_player' from catalog unless include_player_options_tool. Stale loaded names not in catalog are naturally filtered out.

publicSurface.name: search_gm_tools
publicSurface.signature: search_gm_tools(world, query: str, max_results: int = 5, already_loaded: set[str]|None = None, include_player_options_tool: bool = False) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Implements the tool_search tool. Two modes: 'select:a,b' exact load (case-insensitive, splits missing/known_loaded/selected), or keyword scoring via _score_tool with '+term' required-term prefix. Returns dict{query, matches[], loaded_tools[], already_loaded[], missing[], total_searchable_tools, message}. limit clamped to 1..10. Excludes 'tool_search' itself and already_loaded tools from searchable.

publicSurface.name: gm_user_message
publicSurface.signature: gm_user_message(world, player_text: str, include_player_options_tool: bool = False) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Returns {role:'user', content:_gm_turn_context(...)} — the append-only new user turn carrying the late mutable snapshot + player free text.

publicSurface.name: gm_turn
publicSurface.signature: gm_turn(client, world, gm_messages: list, summary: str = '', loaded_tool_names: set[str]|None = None, include_player_options_tool: bool = False)
publicSurface.behavior: Non-streaming GM step. Builds messages via _gm_request_messages, calls client.chat(..., tools=build_gm_tools_for_model(...), think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM). Returns (thinking, content, calls, assistant_msg).

publicSurface.name: gm_turn_stream
publicSurface.signature: gm_turn_stream(client, world, gm_messages, summary='', loaded_tool_names=None, include_player_options_tool=False)
publicSurface.behavior: Streaming GM step; returns client.chat_stream generator yielding ('thinking'|'content', delta) and returning (thinking, content, calls, assistant_msg, stats). Same tools/think/role as gm_turn.

publicSurface.name: gm_prelude_stream
publicSurface.signature: gm_prelude_stream(client, world, player_text: str, calls: list)
publicSurface.behavior: Streams player-facing pre-tool narration (Russian, think=False, no tools, reasoning_role=ROLE_GM). System prompt forbids resolving the action; user content includes scene_context, entity_reference_context, player_text, and a JSON call_brief truncated to config.PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS (4000).

publicSurface.name: npc_system_message
publicSurface.behavior: Returns the fully static NPC system message {role:'system', content:prompts.NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC}. npc arg ignored (call-site compat); the concrete character is delivered late via npc_card_block.

publicSurface.name: npc_card_block
publicSurface.behavior: Renders the late CURRENT NPC CARD block from prompts.NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE with revision=int(card_revision), name/role/gender(=pronouns or 'OTHER')/public_label/age/physical_type/distinctive_features/life_status/condition/persona/personality/values/habits/pressure_response/boundaries/voice/goals/knowledge/secret, and mechanics=compact JSON of non-empty {abilities,skills,saving_throws,passive_perception,ac,hp,speed,senses,languages}. States it overrides older memory on conflict.

publicSurface.name: npc_user_message
publicSurface.signature: npc_user_message(npc, situation, observations, commitments, feedback: str|None, constraints=None, scene_slice: str = '') -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Builds the NPC user turn: _NPC_PERCEPTION_BRIEF_RULES, then 'CURRENT SITUATION (...): {situation}', optional 'YOUR CURRENT SCENE SLICE', optional 'VISIBLE SCENE LIMITS', 'YOUR MEMORY' (commitments or '(nothing yet)'), 'WHAT YOU SAW/HEARD EARLIER' (observations or '(nothing)'); appends a 'GM NOTE — your previous action did not pass: {feedback}\nREDO:' block when feedback is set. NOTE: card is NOT included here (added later in npc_request_messages copy).

publicSurface.name: npc_request_messages
publicSurface.signature: npc_request_messages(npc, history: list|None, summary: str, user_message: dict) -> list
publicSurface.behavior: Assembles the NPC request: [npc_system_message(), optional {system:'YOUR PRIVATE MEMORY SO FAR (compact):'+summary}, *map(_historical_npc_message, history), final_turn]. final_turn is a COPY of user_message with npc_card_block(npc)+'\n\n' prepended so the recorded history message stays card-free and a card edit invalidates only this tail.

publicSurface.name: npc_turn
publicSurface.signature: npc_turn(client, npc, situation, observations='', commitments='', feedback=None, constraints=None, scene_slice='', history=None, summary='') -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Non-streaming NPC sub-agent call. Builds _npc_messages, calls client.chat_json(msgs, NPC_SCHEMA, think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_NPC), returns _norm_npc(...) dict {reasoning,speech,action,claims}.

publicSurface.name: npc_turn_stream
publicSurface.signature: npc_turn_stream(client, npc, situation, observations='', commitments='', feedback=None, constraints=None, scene_slice='', history=None, summary='')
publicSurface.behavior: Streaming NPC sub-agent. yields ('content', delta); returns (_norm_npc(data), stats) via client.chat_json_stream(msgs, NPC_SCHEMA, think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_NPC).

publicSurface.name: build_world_seed
publicSurface.behavior: Asks the model (chat_json, think=False, WORLD_SEED_SCHEMA) for a new playable world seed, applies _apply_brief_display_names, then runs a repair pass if _seed_needs_npc_repair or _seed_needs_text_repair. Returns the seed dict (World validates it later).

publicSurface.name: extract_scene_delta
publicSurface.behavior: Post-narration state sync (not validation): chat_json(think=False, SCENE_DELTA_SCHEMA) to extract explicit current-scene roster changes (moves[]) from accepted final narration using only existing npc_ids; returns {moves:[...]} or {moves:[]}.

publicSurface.name: NPC_SCHEMA
publicSurface.behavior: Module-level grammar for NPC JSON fallback in chat_json: object with required reasoning/speech/action(strings) and claims(array of strings).

publicSurface.name: SCENE_DELTA_SCHEMA / WORLD_SEED_SCHEMA
publicSurface.behavior: Grammars for extract_scene_delta and build_world_seed respectively.

publicSurface.name: VISIBLE_TOOL_CAPABILITY_TEXT / HIDDEN_TOOL_CAPABILITY_TEXT / GM_TOOL_CAPABILITY_OVERVIEW / MODEL_TOOL_RESULT_GUIDE / TOOL_SEARCH_DESCRIPTION (tool_guidance)
publicSurface.behavior: Shared model-facing capability descriptions; TOOL_SEARCH_DESCRIPTION is used as the tool_search tool description; the WORLD_STATE_* guides are f-spliced into the update_world_state description.

dataStructures.name: GM tool schema (OpenAI function format)
dataStructures.fields: {type:'function', function:{name:str(snake_case,stable), description:str(English), parameters:{type:'object', properties:{...}, required:[...], additionalProperties:False}, [strict:False on update_player_character & update_world_state]}}
dataStructures.notes: 13 tools returned by build_gm_tools in fixed order: ask_npc, move_npc, set_npc_whereabouts, get_npc_profile, set_scene, advance_time, ask_player, update_player_character, update_world_state, query_world_state, roll_dice, get_world_fact, tool_search. Mutable ids (npc_id, location_id, item/exit ids) are always type:'string', never enum. The ONLY static engine enums allowed: whereabouts.status [known,likely,rumored,unknown], roll_dice.roll_kind/target_kind/difficulty_label, get_npc_profile.preset [visible,social,mechanics,status,identity] and fields enum=list(NPC_PROFILE_FIELDS), update_world_state item.type/op/scope/mode, query_world_state.scope.

dataStructures.name: ask_npc args
dataStructures.fields: {npc_id:str(required), situation:str(required, =_SITUATION_DESC neutral 3rd-person NPC-perception brief), correction:str(optional — fill ONLY on redo, omit on first call)}
dataStructures.notes: correction is the redo channel: orchestrator nulls it unless npc_id is already in session.pending (a draft exists this turn). Drives the GM-critic re-ask loop.

dataStructures.name: NPC JSON contract (NPC_SCHEMA)
dataStructures.fields: {reasoning:str, speech:str, action:str, claims:[str]} — all four required
dataStructures.notes: speech/action are player-facing RUSSIAN; reasoning/claims are RUSSIAN internal lab notes. _norm_npc coerces every field via _text/_claims (missing -> '' or []). claims = true relied-on facts only; lies/cover stories excluded. speech = exact spoken words only; action = visible 3rd-person physical behavior.

dataStructures.name: roll_dice args
dataStructures.fields: {roll_kind:enum[check,save,attack,damage,chance,contest](req), notation:str(req), target_number:int, target_kind:enum[DC,AC,opposed_total], check_name:str(req), reason:str(req), difficulty_label:enum[trivial,easy,moderate,hard,very_hard,nearly_impossible,custom], modifier_note:str, stakes:{intent,success,failure,complication}}
dataStructures.notes: check_name/reason/stakes are English; modifier_note is the ONLY player-facing field and is Russian (source of modifier WITHOUT number; UI prints the number). Notation supports 1d20+3, 2d6, 2d20kh1 (advantage), 2d20kl1 (disadvantage).

dataStructures.name: update_world_state item
dataStructures.fields: {op:enum[add,update,delete], id:str, expected_hash:str, type:enum[fact,rumor,npc_memory,relationship,goal], text:str(Russian), npc_id, target, entity_id, known_name, source_npc, participants:[str], location_id, location_name, region_id, region_name, scene_id, importance, aliases:[str], scope:enum[public,gm,npc,shared], witnesses:[str], mode:enum[replace]}
dataStructures.notes: items[] required at top level. Every shared item must include npc_id AND (target or participants). known_name requires entity_id to be an NPC id (GM-authored identity). For op=add, never send id/expected_hash/mode/placeholder hash. mode=replace only for goal.

dataStructures.name: update_player_character args
dataStructures.fields: {fields:{name,pronouns,class_role,level,background,age,physical_type,distinctive_features,life_status,life_status_note,condition,personality,values,gm_notes,abilities,skills,saving_throws,passive_perception,ac,hp,speed,senses,languages,inventory,equipment,features}, reason:str(Russian)}
dataStructures.notes: strict:False. Send ONLY changed fields; never echo whole card. inventory/equipment/features are full-replace arrays only when changed. Result reports changed field names + card revision.

dataStructures.name: set_scene args
dataStructures.fields: {title(req),description(req),location_id,present_npcs:[str npc_ids],items:[{id,name(req),location,visible,portable,owner,details}],exits:[{id,name(req),destination,visible,blocked_by}],constraints:[str],tension,reason(req)}
dataStructures.notes: present_npcs lists only ids from current roster; unknown ids ignored & reported.

dataStructures.name: ask_player args
dataStructures.fields: {question:str(req,Russian), options:[{label:str,message:str}] minItems:4 maxItems:8}
dataStructures.notes: Last tool before final narration when PLAYER OPTION SUGGESTIONS enabled. label=button text, message=full Russian player message sent on click.

dataStructures.name: WORLD_SEED_SCHEMA shape
dataStructures.fields: {public_intro, hidden_truth, proper_nouns:[str], public_facts:[str], npcs:[{id,name,role,gender,persona,voice,goals,knowledge,secret}], scene:{id,location_id,title,description,present_npcs:[str],items:[...],exits:[...],constraints:[str],tension}}
dataStructures.notes: gender marker: M/F/N/PL/OTHER or custom Russian note. Generated by build_world_seed; World validates afterwards. secret never enters GM context.

dataStructures.name: SCENE_DELTA_SCHEMA shape
dataStructures.fields: {moves:[{npc_id,present,location,visible,can_hear,activity,attitude,reason}]} (npc_id,present,reason required per move)
dataStructures.notes: Output of extract_scene_delta; only explicit roster changes, no inference from wishes/plans/rumors.

dataStructures.name: NPC dataclass (world.NPC, consumed by npc_card_block)
dataStructures.fields: npc_id,name,persona,voice,goals,knowledge,secret,role,pronouns,color,public_label,age,physical_type,distinctive_features,life_status='alive',life_status_note,condition,personality,values,habits,pressure_response,boundaries,abilities,skills,saving_throws,passive_perception,ac,hp,speed,senses,languages,default_whereabouts,card_revision=0
dataStructures.notes: secret is rendered into the NPC card but NEVER into GM context. card_revision is bumped by world.py when a content field actually changes.

dataStructures.name: tool_search result dict
dataStructures.fields: {query,matches:[{name,description,loaded,already_loaded}],loaded_tools:[str],already_loaded:[str],missing:[str],total_searchable_tools:int,message}
dataStructures.notes: orchestrator adds loaded_tools to session.loaded_gm_tools; messages are Russian.

controlFlow: GM turn: orchestrator builds _gm_request_messages = [system(GM_SYSTEM), system(_gm_world_setup=PUBLIC INTRO only), optional system('STORY SO FAR (compact): '+summary), *gm_messages(append-only history including prior user turns + assistant tool calls + tool results)]. The new turn is appended by gm_user_message -> _gm_turn_context (late mutable snapshot: TIME STATE, INTERNAL NPC ROSTER, CURRENT PUBLIC FACTS[:12], PLAYER CHARACTER CARD, CURRENT SCENE STATE, ENTITY REFERENCE MARKUP, SCENE CONSTRAINTS, PLAYER OPTION SUGGESTIONS on/off, TURN_RESOLUTION_CHECKLIST, PLAYER ACTION). gm_turn/gm_turn_stream call client.chat with tools=build_gm_tools_for_model(world, session.loaded_gm_tools, include_player_options_tool). The model emits tool calls; orchestrator._run_tool dispatches by name (string match: tool_search/ask_player/roll_dice/get_world_fact/update_world_state/set_scene/ask_npc/... ; unknown -> _tool_error code=unknown_tool). tool_search results add names to session.loaded_gm_tools so the next GM step exposes them. Tool gating: Session.loaded_gm_tools starts = agents.initial_gm_tool_names(); not persisted to SQLite, re-discoverable after restore. ask_npc dispatch -> _ask_npc: resolve npc_id (KeyError->unknown_npc error), npc_can_react check (else npc_not_present error w/ whereabouts), correction nulled unless npc already in session.pending; runs _maybe_compact_npc then the NPC sub-agent (npc_turn_stream) two-phase: draft accumulates in session.pending all turn, commits to log/memory only at commit_turn; other NPCs see provisional drafts via observations(). NPC turn: npc_request_messages -> [static system, optional summary system, historical messages (_historical_npc_message rewrites old CURRENT SITUATION/SCENE SLICE headers to PREVIOUS/HISTORICAL), final user turn = card_block + user_message copy]. _VISIBLE_PRELUDE_TOOLS {ask_npc,ask_player,move_npc,set_npc_presence,set_npc_whereabouts,set_scene,roll_dice} trigger gm_prelude_stream before resolution. Reasoning is enabled per role: GM think=True (ROLE_GM), NPC think=True at call sites but config.NPC_THINK default False overrides via runtime_settings.reasoning_enabled; compaction uses ROLE_COMPACT.
externalDeps: Local OpenAI-compatible LLM via client.chat/chat_stream/chat_json/chat_json_stream/summarize (llm_client.py / codex_client.py) — Ollama / raw llama.cpp / ChatGPT Codex backend
json (stdlib) for all serialization with ensure_ascii=False
re (stdlib) for tool search tokenization and seed name/cyrillic helpers
config (constants: ROLE_GM/NPC/COMPACT, PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS, GM_THINK/NPC_THINK, PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE, history/compaction thresholds)
