prompts (GM_SYSTEM, NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC, NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE, GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM, NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM)
world (World, NPC, NPC_PROFILE_FIELDS, _public_gender, fact_records, scene/player/entity/time context builders, roll_outcome_payload, dice RNG)
invariants: PROMPT-CACHE PREFIX ORDERING (load-bearing): GM request order is exactly [system=GM_SYSTEM] [system=_gm_world_setup (PUBLIC INTRO ONLY)] [optional system='STORY SO FAR (compact): '+summary] [append-only gm_messages] [new user turn=_gm_turn_context]. Mutable data MUST stay in the late _gm_turn_context tail; moving roster/facts/player-card/scene earlier breaks cache. Old turns stay byte-for-byte unchanged.

TOOL SCHEMAS ARE STATIC / NO DYNAMIC ENUMS OF LIVE IDS: build_gm_tools never enumerates the live NPC roster, names, secrets, npc_id/location_id/item/exit id enums, public facts, scene, or whereabouts. Mutable entities are type:'string' validated at runtime; unknown ids return controlled errors (not exceptions). Only static engine enums permitted (whereabouts.status known|likely|rumored|unknown; roll_dice/get_npc_profile/update_world_state/query_world_state enums). parameters.additionalProperties MUST be False on every tool.

NPC JSON CONTRACT field names are fixed: reasoning, speech, action, claims (all required). _norm_npc must always return exactly these four keys with _text-coerced strings and _claims-filtered list. speech/action player-facing Russian; reasoning/claims Russian internal.

_INITIAL_GM_TOOL_NAMES (frozenset) = {ask_npc, roll_dice, get_world_fact, query_world_state, update_world_state, update_player_character, advance_time, tool_search}. _PLAYER_OPTIONS_TOOL_NAME='ask_player' added only when include_player_options_tool. Visible tool set = loaded_tool_names | initial set; loaded_gm_tools NOT persisted to SQLite (runtime Session only, re-discoverable via tool_search after restore).

GM HISTORY COMPACTION: _maybe_compact triggers when _messages_tokens(gm_messages) >= config.GM_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 100000) AND number of user-turn boundaries > config.GM_KEEP_TURNS (default 3). Keeps the last GM_KEEP_TURNS user turns verbatim; everything older + prior gm_summary is summarized in one client.summarize(..., proper_nouns) call (ROLE_COMPACT). Compaction also calls session.reset_world_query_cache() (resets get_world_fact/query_world_state already_delivered suppression).

NPC HISTORY COMPACTION: _maybe_compact_npc triggers when _messages_tokens(npc_messages[id]) >= config.NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 64000) AND user-turn boundaries > config.NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES (default 6). Keeps last NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES exchanges; older + prior npc summary -> _summarize_npc_history via NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM (ROLE_COMPACT).
COMPACT INPUT TRUNCATION: summarize input is truncated to config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS (default 12000) chars. PRELUDE call_brief JSON truncated to config.PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS (default 4000).

TOKEN ESTIMATION HEURISTIC: config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN (default 3, env GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN) is the single source for _estimate_tokens and all GM/NPC compaction gates. _estimate_tokens(text) ~= len(text)//CHARS_PER_TOKEN.

DETERMINISTIC DICE: world._new_dice_seed() = random.SystemRandom().getrandbits(64) (OS entropy) chosen ONCE per campaign; thereafter World._rng = random.Random(dice_seed) is deterministic and the RNG state is persisted across save/restore. Debug overrides forced_die_next (one-shot) / forced_die_all (sticky). roll_dice tool args never carry a seed; world.roll_outcome_payload owns the roll. Notation: 2d20kh1=advantage, 2d20kl1=disadvantage.

SECRET / HIDDEN-CANON ISOLATION: NPC.secret is rendered into the late CURRENT NPC CARD for that NPC ONLY (npc_card_block) and must NEVER appear in GM context. _gm_turn_context public_facts are filtered to record.kind=='public' (truth/rumor records and hidden_events excluded). _gm_world_setup exposes only world.public (PUBLIC INTRO). get_npc_profile returns selected safe fields only (no secrets/private knowledge/goals). RAG index excludes hidden canon & NPC secrets.

ASK_NPC CORRECTION GATE: correction field is honored only when npc_id already exists in session.pending (a draft from this turn). On a fresh action correction must be omitted/ignored. Drives the GM-critic redo loop; redo overwrites the same session.pending seq.

TWO-PHASE NPC DRAFT: ask_npc drafts accumulate in session.pending across the whole turn and are committed to log/long-term memory only at commit_turn; other NPCs see provisional drafts via observations() (intra-turn awareness).

CARD_REVISION: npc_card_block emits revision=int(card_revision or 0); card overrides older memory on conflict (stated in card text and NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC). world.py bumps card_revision only when a content field actually changes (color excluded). update_player_character returns the player card revision.

HISTORY REWRITE FOR NPC: _historical_npc_message rewrites stored NPC user turns, replacing 'CURRENT SITUATION (...)' -> 'PREVIOUS NPC SITUATION (historical...)' (first occurrence) and 'YOUR CURRENT SCENE SLICE...' -> 'PREVIOUS SCENE SLICE...' and prefixing 'HISTORICAL NPC EXCHANGE...', so old CURRENT-SITUATION blocks cannot win against the latest turn.

CARD-FREE HISTORY: npc_card_block is prepended to a COPY of the user_message (final_turn) so the recorded history message stays card-free; a card edit invalidates only the late tail, not stored history.

PROMPT_CACHE / KV REUSE WIRING (llm_client): prompt_cache_key sent only if config.PROMPT_CACHE_KEY non-empty; n_cache_reuse sent only if config.LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE > 0; reasoning enabled per role via runtime_settings.reasoning_enabled(think, reasoning_role). CodexClient uses a stable thread-id when CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY is empty.

tool_search scoring: select: mode is case-insensitive exact; keyword mode tokenizes [\w а-яА-ЯёЁ-]+, '+term' = required term (all must be in tool search text or score 0). _score_tool weights: exact name=100, name word=35, name substring=20, hint match=12, any text=5. limit clamped 1..10 (default 5). search text = name + name-with-spaces + description + parameter descriptions/enums + _TOOL_SEARCH_HINTS[name], lowercased.

ENGLISH-DESCRIPTION / RUSSIAN-VALUES CONTRACT: tool names+descriptions are English; argument VALUES are requested in Russian for the debug UI, EXCEPT roll_dice private mechanical fields (check_name, reason, stakes) which are English; modifier_note is the one roll_dice field that is Russian (and player-facing).
platformConcerns: No direct OS calls in agents.py/tool_guidance.py — fully portable; OS coupling is in collaborators.

world._new_dice_seed uses random.SystemRandom (OS CSPRNG) for the per-campaign seed; Rust must use an OS-entropy source (getrandom) once, then a deterministic seeded PRNG whose state is serialized (see invariants).

config reads many GM_*/env vars (CHARS_PER_TOKEN, GM_HISTORY_TOKENS, NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS, GM_KEEP_TURNS, NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES, COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS, PRELUDE_CALLBRIEF_CHARS, PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE) — env handling must be cross-platform.
RAG embeddings + LLM are local HTTP services (config RAG_EMBEDDINGS_URL http://127.0.0.1:8080; LLM endpoint in client) — Rust port keeps these as async HTTP clients; loopback works on all three OSes.

ChatGPT Codex path (config CODEX_*) opens a browser for OAuth (CODEX_AUTO_OPEN_BROWSER) and stores tokens in local app data — browser-open + app-data path differ per OS (handle in the client crate, not agents).
All JSON uses ensure_ascii=False (Cyrillic preserved) — Rust serde_json preserves UTF-8 by default; ensure no ASCII-escaping.
rustNotes: Represent the 13 GM tools as a const/static catalog of serde_json::Value (or a Tool struct) built ONCE; build_gm_tools_for_model = filter by visible name set. Keep tool order stable (it affects the cached request payload). Do NOT generate schemas from live World — only get_npc_profile.fields needs the static NPC_PROFILE_FIELDS list, which can be a const array.

Model the NPC contract as a struct NpcResponse{reasoning:String, speech:String, action:String, claims:Vec<String>} with serde defaults so missing fields normalize to empty (mirrors _norm_npc). Provide a normalize() that trims and drops empty claims.

Prompt assembly: build an ordered Vec<ChatMessage>. Keep _gm_world_setup (public intro) and _gm_turn_context strictly separated; the turn context is the only place mutable state goes. A faithful port should produce byte-identical earlier messages across turns to preserve provider prompt-cache hits.

tool_search: port _score_tool/_tool_search_text exactly (weights 100/35/20/12/5, '+'=required term, select: exact mode, clamp 1..=10). Use a regex crate for the [\w а-яА-ЯёЁ-]+ tokenizer; lowercase with proper Unicode case folding for Cyrillic.

Compaction: replicate the AND gate (token estimate >= threshold AND user-boundary count > keep-count), keep-last-N verbatim, summarize older+prior summary in one call. token estimate = char_count / CHARS_PER_TOKEN (integer division). Reset the world-query delivery cache after GM compaction.

Dice RNG: use a seedable, serializable PRNG (rand::rngs::StdRng or rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng) seeded once from getrandom; serialize/restore the exact RNG state on save/load. SystemRandom in Python is non-reproducible only for the initial seed.
Reasoning role and think flags: model ROLE enum {Gm,Npc,Compact}; the per-role think default (NPC default off) is resolved in the client/runtime_settings layer, mirror that boundary.
_historical_npc_message string-rewrite must be reproduced verbatim (header replacements + 'HISTORICAL NPC EXCHANGE' prefix) — it is a behavioral invariant, not cosmetic.
Keep the card-free history rule: store the user message WITHOUT the NPC card; prepend the card only on a clone used for the request (mirror final_turn copy).
All Russian text must remain UTF-8; never ASCII-escape JSON (serde_json default is fine).
suggestedCrates: serde + serde_json (tool schemas, NPC/seed/delta JSON, ensure_ascii=False equivalent is default UTF-8)
regex (tool_search tokenizer, name/cyrillic helpers in seed repair)
rand + rand_chacha (seedable, serializable dice PRNG)
getrandom (one-time OS-entropy campaign seed)
reqwest + tokio (async OpenAI-compatible LLM and embeddings HTTP clients)
schemars (optional: derive JSON schemas for grammar/structured-output if kept programmatic)
once_cell / std::sync::OnceLock (static tool catalog construction)
risks: The GM tool DESCRIPTIONS are extremely long and behavior-load-bearing (they encode reality-correction policy, roll-locking, scope/known_name rules). They are part of the model boundary AND of the cache prefix — they must be ported verbatim, not paraphrased. Any drift changes model behavior and invalidates contract tests (test_contracts.py).

update_world_state.description is assembled by f-string splicing of 7 tool_guidance constants (WORLD_STATE_TYPE/SCOPE/SPLIT/CONSOLIDATION/EXAMPLE/SEARCH_ANCHOR guides) — the exact concatenation order and spacing matters for the cached payload.

ask_player and tool_search descriptions encode strict ordering rules ('last tool before final narration', 'call exactly once') that the orchestrator and prompts.GM_SYSTEM also enforce; correctness depends on agents + orchestrator + prompts staying in sync (gm_tools_guide.md is the playbook).

Two-phase NPC draft / session.pending / observations() / commit_turn logic lives in orchestrator (Session), not agents.py — agents only provides the correction arg and message builders; the port must keep that split.

Token estimation is a crude chars/CHARS_PER_TOKEN heuristic; if a Rust tokenizer is substituted, compaction trigger timing changes and could break cache assumptions — keep the same heuristic unless thresholds are re-tuned.
loaded_gm_tools is intentionally NOT persisted; after a session restore the GM must re-run tool_search to regain hidden tools. A port that persists it would silently change behavior.

NPC think flag: call sites pass think=True but config.NPC_THINK defaults False and runtime_settings.reasoning_enabled is the real gate — easy to mis-port. Memory notes a degeneration loop when format+think combine, so the think-off default for NPC JSON matters.

World-seed repair heuristics (_seed_needs_npc_repair, _seed_needs_text_repair via Cyrillic detection, _apply_brief_display_names slug matching with _CYR_TO_LAT transliteration) are fuzzy and locale-specific; faithful Cyrillic handling in Rust requires care.

purpose: This subsystem is the single home for every model-facing system/template string in GM-Lab: the GM orchestrator system prompt, the static NPC actor system prompt, the late-injected NPC character-card block, and the two history-compaction (summarization) system prompts. tool_guidance.py supplies reusable capability/guidance fragments that are f-string-interpolated INTO the GM system prompt at module import time. These strings encode the entire behavioral contract (language rules, prompt-cache discipline, tool routing, roll discipline, JSON output shape) and MUST be ported byte-for-byte: the GM is a tool-using LLM that drives NPC sub-agent LLMs, and prompt/KV-cache hits depend on these prefixes being stable and identical.

publicSurface.name: GM_SYSTEM
publicSurface.signature: GM_SYSTEM: str  # module-level f-string constant, prompts.py:5-459
publicSurface.behavior: The complete GM orchestrator system prompt. Built as an f-string AT IMPORT TIME by interpolating 9 tool_guidance fragments (see invariants). Returned verbatim by agents.gm_system_prompt() (agents.py:1053) and used as the role=system message; len(GM_SYSTEM)//config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN is precomputed as orchestrator._SYS_EST (orchestrator.py:21).

publicSurface.name: NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC
publicSurface.signature: NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC: str  # plain triple-quoted constant, prompts.py:461-561
publicSurface.behavior: Static NPC actor system prompt. Contains NO format placeholders for the runtime character (the character arrives late via NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE). Ends with the literal JSON shape spec. Consumed by agents.npc_system_message() (agents.py:1280) as role=system content. NOTE: because it is a normal (non-f) string but contains literal braces for the JSON example, the example braces are DOUBLED ({{...}}) so .format() would be safe -- but it is used raw, not .format()ed.

publicSurface.name: NPC_SYSTEM_TEMPLATE
publicSurface.behavior: Backward-compat alias pointing at the same string object as NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC, kept so any external import of the old name still resolves.

publicSurface.name: NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE
publicSurface.behavior: Late dynamic 'CURRENT NPC CARD (revision {revision})' block describing the concrete character. Rendered AFTER summary+history and prepended to the final user turn. Filled via str.format() by agents.npc_card_block() (agents.py:1297-1319) with 21 named placeholders. Last line is the literal 'This card overrides older memory if there is a conflict.'

publicSurface.name: NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM
publicSurface.behavior: System prompt for compressing a single NPC's private RP history into a Russian memory note. Single placeholder {proper_nouns}. Filled by orchestrator._summarize_npc_history() (orchestrator.py:2344) via .format(proper_nouns=', '.join(world.proper_nouns())).

publicSurface.name: GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM
publicSurface.behavior: System prompt for compressing a stretch of the GM session into an English (~180 word) 'what happened' recap. Single placeholder {proper_nouns_line}. Filled in llm_client.OpenAICompatClient.summarize() (llm_client.py:319) and codex_client (codex_client.py:435) via .format(proper_nouns_line=_proper_nouns_line(...)).

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.GM_TOOL_CAPABILITY_OVERVIEW
publicSurface.behavior: Composed string listing visible GM tool capabilities then hidden ones loadable via tool_search. Interpolated into GM_SYSTEM TOOL ROUTING.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.MODEL_TOOL_RESULT_GUIDE
publicSurface.behavior: Explains compact structured tool-result format and already_delivered semantics. Interpolated into GM_SYSTEM TOOL RESULT REMINDERS.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.WORLD_STATE_TYPE_GUIDE
publicSurface.behavior: Defines fact/rumor/npc_memory/relationship/goal world-state types. Interpolated into GM_SYSTEM update_world_state routing.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.WORLD_STATE_SCOPE_GUIDE
publicSurface.behavior: Defines public/shared/npc/gm scopes. Interpolated into GM_SYSTEM.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.WORLD_STATE_SPLIT_GUIDE
publicSurface.behavior: Rules for splitting testimony vs fact vs relationship vs goal. Interpolated into GM_SYSTEM.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.WORLD_STATE_CONSOLIDATION_GUIDE
publicSurface.behavior: Rules for consolidating memory by durable meaning. Interpolated into GM_SYSTEM.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.WORLD_STATE_SEARCH_ANCHOR_GUIDE
publicSurface.behavior: Rules for location/region/scene/alias search anchors. Interpolated into GM_SYSTEM.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.VISIBLE_TOOL_CAPABILITY_TEXT
publicSurface.behavior: Comma-joined 'label (`name`)' rendering of the 7 visible tools; feeds GM_TOOL_CAPABILITY_OVERVIEW.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.HIDDEN_TOOL_CAPABILITY_TEXT
publicSurface.behavior: Comma-joined rendering of the 4 hidden tools; feeds GM_TOOL_CAPABILITY_OVERVIEW.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.TOOL_SEARCH_DESCRIPTION
publicSurface.behavior: Description string for the tool_search meta-tool. NOT interpolated into prompts.py; used by the tool-schema builder. Present in module surface.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance.WORLD_STATE_EXAMPLE_GUIDE
publicSurface.behavior: Worked examples of world-state writes. Defined but NOT referenced by GM_SYSTEM (note: WORLD_STATE_CONSOLIDATION_GUIDE is used in GM_SYSTEM, EXAMPLE_GUIDE is not). Available for other callers.

publicSurface.name: tool_guidance._capability_list
publicSurface.behavior: Private helper: joins rows into 'label (`name`)' comma-separated string. Used to build the two CAPABILITY_TEXT constants at import.

dataStructures.name: NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE placeholders (21)
dataStructures.fields: {revision} int, {name}, {role}, {gender}, {public_label}, {age}, {physical_type}, {distinctive_features}, {life_status}, {condition}, {persona}, {personality}, {values}, {habits}, {pressure_response}, {boundaries}, {voice}, {goals}, {knowledge}, {mechanics}, {secret}
dataStructures.notes: Filled by agents.npc_card_block(). Source NPC attrs map: revision<-int(npc.card_revision or 0); name<-npc.name; role<-npc.role or '(не указана)'; gender<-npc.pronouns or 'OTHER'; public_label<-npc.public_label or '(не указан)'; age<-npc.age or '(не указан)'; physical_type<-npc.physical_type or '(не указан)'; distinctive_features<-npc.distinctive_features or '(не указаны)'; life_status<-npc.life_status or 'alive'; condition<-npc.condition or '(не указано)'; persona<-npc.persona; personality<-npc.personality or '(не указано)'; values<-npc.values or '(не указаны)'; habits<-npc.habits or '(не указаны)'; pressure_response<-npc.pressure_response or '(не указано)'; boundaries<-npc.boundaries or '(не указаны)'; voice<-npc.voice; goals<-npc.goals; knowledge<-npc.knowledge; mechanics<-json.dumps(mechanics, ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',',':')); secret<-npc.secret. The Russian fallback literals ('(не указана)' etc.) are part of the contract and must be reproduced exactly. gender accepts M/F/N/PL/OTHER or a custom Russian note (see NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC GENDER MARKER section).

dataStructures.name: mechanics dict (serialized into {mechanics})
dataStructures.fields: abilities (dict), skills (dict), saving_throws (dict), passive_perception (int|None), ac (int|None), hp (dict), speed (str), senses (str), languages (str)
dataStructures.notes: Built in agents.npc_card_block() from npc.* attrs, then filtered to drop keys whose value is in (None, '', {}, []), then json.dumps(..., ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',',':')) — compact, no spaces, raw Cyrillic. Empty mechanics serializes to '{}'. Rust port must use the identical key set, the same emptiness filter, and compact non-ASCII JSON to keep the card byte-stable.

dataStructures.name: NPC JSON output contract (declared in NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC)
dataStructures.fields: reasoning (str, Russian private thoughts), speech (str, Russian spoken words or empty string), action (str, Russian visible third-person physical action or empty string), claims (array of str, true relied-on Russian facts)
dataStructures.notes: Literal required shape at prompts.py:560: {"reasoning":"...","speech":"...","action":"...","claims":["..."]}. Key order and names are load-bearing for the JSON parser downstream. The braces in the template are DOUBLED in source ({{...}}) because the surrounding block was authored to be format-safe, but the string is delivered raw so the player sees single braces.

dataStructures.name: VISIBLE_TOOL_CAPABILITIES (7 rows)
dataStructures.fields: (ask_npc, 'NPC speech/reactions'), (roll_dice, 'dice and D&D-style uncertain outcomes'), (get_world_fact, 'player-safe answer lookup for facts, lore, leads, and testimony'), (query_world_state, 'state-record/id-hash lookup for updates or private npc/gm scopes'), (update_world_state, 'durable world/NPC memory writes'), (update_player_character, 'player character sheet updates'), (advance_time, 'world clock advancement')
dataStructures.notes: tool_guidance.py:5-13. Order matters: it determines the rendered order inside GM_SYSTEM TOOL ROUTING capability overview, which is part of the cached prefix.

dataStructures.name: HIDDEN_TOOL_CAPABILITIES (4 rows)
dataStructures.fields: (set_scene, 'scene changes'), (move_npc, 'NPC presence/movement'), (set_npc_whereabouts, 'offscreen NPC whereabouts'), (get_npc_profile, 'selected NPC card/mechanics fields')
dataStructures.notes: tool_guidance.py:15-20. Loaded on demand via tool_search; order also part of cached prefix string.

dataStructures.name: GM tool arg / field vocabulary referenced by prompts
dataStructures.fields: roll_dice: check_name (English), reason (English), stakes (English), modifier_note (RUSSIAN, player-facing, source name only without number), target_number, target_kind (DC|AC|opposed_total), dice notation 2d20kh1 (advantage) / 2d20kl1 (disadvantage). update_world_state item fields: type, scope, npc_id, target, participants, entity_id, known_name, location_id/location_name, region_id/region_name, scene_id, importance, aliases, op (add). NPC card visible fields: public_label, known_name, role, pronouns, age, physical_type, distinctive_features, life_status, condition. Social fields: persona, personality, values, habits, pressure_response, boundaries, voice. Mechanics: abilities, skills, saving_throws, passive_perception, AC, HP, speed, senses, languages.
dataStructures.notes: These names are quoted inside GM_SYSTEM/NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC prose and must match the actual tool schemas in agents.py / tools layer. Entity-ref markup syntax in narration: [[npc:id|player_label]] and [[loc:id|visible place]].

controlFlow: No runtime control flow inside prompts.py itself — it is a pure constant/template module. The only execution is at IMPORT TIME: (1) prompts.py does `import tool_guidance`; (2) tool_guidance.py computes VISIBLE_TOOL_CAPABILITY_TEXT/HIDDEN_TOOL_CAPABILITY_TEXT via _capability_list() and assembles the composite guidance constants; (3) prompts.py evaluates the GM_SYSTEM f-string, splicing 9 tool_guidance fragments into fixed positions. After import, the five public strings are immutable constants. Templates are filled lazily by callers: NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE.format(...) per NPC turn (agents.npc_card_block), GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM.format(proper_nouns_line=...) / NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM.format(proper_nouns=...) per compaction call. NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC and GM_SYSTEM are emitted unchanged as role=system messages.
externalDeps: tool_guidance module (sibling Python file; the only import in prompts.py)

Indirect at fill time: config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN (default 3, env GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN) used to size GM_SYSTEM; config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS (default 12000, env GM_COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS) bounds compaction input; world.proper_nouns() feeds the proper-noun placeholders; json.dumps for {mechanics}
internalDeps: Consumers (not deps of prompts.py, but the porting contract): agents.py (gm_system_prompt, npc_system_message, npc_card_block), orchestrator.py (_SYS_EST, _summarize_npc_history -> NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM), llm_client.py (summarize -> GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM, _proper_nouns_line), codex_client.py (GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM)
invariants: BYTE-FOR-BYTE VERBATIM: All five strings (GM_SYSTEM, NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC, NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE, NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM, GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM) plus the tool_guidance fragments must be reproduced character-exact in the Rust port. They are LLM prompts AND prompt-cache prefixes; any whitespace/wording drift invalidates KV/prompt cache hits and changes model behavior. The leading-backslash line-continuation form (f"""\ ... and """\) means the strings start immediately with 'You are...' with NO leading newline — reproduce exactly.

STATIC PROMPT CACHE CONTRACT (GM_SYSTEM lines 34-43): the GM system prompt MUST stay static engine policy with NO live data (no NPC rosters, scene facts, memory rows, stat values, entity ids). All mutable data arrives LATE in 'CURRENT TURN CONTEXT' (INTERNAL NPC ROSTER, PLAYER CHARACTER CARD, CURRENT SCENE STATE, ENTITY REFERENCE MARKUP, CURRENT PUBLIC FACTS, latest player action). agents.py:1049-1052 confirms gm_system_prompt() must be 'stable across turns' because 'Prompt/KV caches only hit identical prefixes; mutable scene snapshots belong in append-only turn messages.'

NPC late-injection ordering: NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE comment (prompts.py:566-567) states the card is 'Rendered AFTER summary + history and prepended to the final user turn. Editing it only invalidates the late cache tail.' So cache-prefix order for an NPC call is: [NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC (static, cached)] -> [summary] -> [history] -> [NPC_CARD block prepended to final user turn]. The static system prompt must precede the volatile card so only the tail is invalidated.

card_revision: NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE first line is literally 'CURRENT NPC CARD (revision {revision})'. revision = int(npc.card_revision or 0) (agents.py:1298). When the card changes, revision bumps and the late tail (only) is invalidated. The CURRENT CHARACTER section (prompts.py:485-491) instructs the model that the latest CURRENT NPC CARD overrides any conflicting older memory/summary/history — the override rule is duplicated as the template's last line 'This card overrides older memory if there is a conflict.'

CHARS_PER_TOKEN token estimation: orchestrator._SYS_EST = len(prompts.GM_SYSTEM) // config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN (orchestrator.py:21); config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN default 3 (env GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN). Same divisor used in orchestrator._estimate_tokens (// CHARS_PER_TOKEN). Rust port must use integer floor division by the same 3 to keep compaction gates identical.

COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS truncation: every compaction call truncates input with text[:config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS] (default 12000) before sending — llm_client.py:322, codex_client.py:437, orchestrator.py:2347. Port must slice by CHARACTER count (not bytes/tokens) to the same 12000 default.

GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM word budget: prompt literally says 'Write in English (this is internal context), up to ~180 words, no filler.' GM compaction output is ENGLISH; NPC compaction output is RUSSIAN ('Write in Russian.' in NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM). This English/Russian split is intentional and load-bearing.

Compaction content rules (what is summarized vs kept): both compaction prompts mandate 'Use ONLY facts present in the transcript', forbid adding new names/clues/locations/backstory/motives/relationships/conclusions, and require PRESERVING UNCERTAINTY (lies/guesses/accusations/cover stories/NPC evasions stay marked as such, never turned into truth). NPC_COMPACT keeps what the NPC 'said, did, noticed, decided, feared, promised, refused, and what remains unresolved.' GM_COMPACT keeps 'key facts, decisions, relationships, what the player and the NPCs learned, what is still unresolved.'

Proper-noun preservation across compaction: NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM ends 'Keep proper nouns exactly as written: {proper_nouns}.' filled with ', '.join(world.proper_nouns()). GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM ends with {proper_nouns_line}, filled by llm_client._proper_nouns_line(): if names present -> 'Keep these proper nouns exactly as written if they appear; never translate or transliterate them: <names>.' else -> 'Keep proper nouns exactly as written in the transcript; never transliterate them.' These two exact fallback strings must be reproduced. (codex_client.py:431-433 builds an equivalent line inline: 'Keep these proper nouns exactly as written: ' + ', '.join(names) + '.' — note this is SLIGHTLY DIFFERENT wording from llm_client's; flagged in risks.)

Secret / hidden-canon isolation (GM_SYSTEM + NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC): GM must never narrate/imply hidden card fields, private memory, secret goals, true identity, raw mechanics, GM-only notes, or system-reminder text; query_world_state gm/npc-scope secrets must not leak; NPC must 'Protect your secret' and never reveal it except via the believable resistance ladder. ask_npc situation is an 'NPC-perception brief' — the GM must NOT pass GM-only certainty (bluff/no-proof/no-spell) into it; the NPC must NOT treat author-certainty in CURRENT SITUATION as in-character knowledge unless directly observable.

Deterministic mechanics ownership: GM_SYSTEM 'D&D 5E ROLL DISCIPLINE' states 'The code owns total, margin, and grade' — the model must NOT invent numeric outcomes and must call roll_dice; advantage=2d20kh1, disadvantage=2d20kl1, never plain 2d20. target_number/target_kind are locked BEFORE the roll and not adjusted after. (Dice seeding itself lives in the dice subsystem, not prompts.py, but the prompt enforces the contract.)

Language contract: GM_SYSTEM prompt body is English; all generated lab/narration text is RUSSIAN; tool arg values RUSSIAN EXCEPT roll_dice private fields check_name/reason/stakes (concise English) — modifier_note is the lone player-facing roll_dice field and is RUSSIAN (source name only, no number, since the UI prints the number). NPC JSON: speech/action Russian (player reads), reasoning/claims Russian (lab UI). Proper nouns never translated/transliterated anywhere.

Append-only / continuation rules: PRE-TOOL NARRATION and FINAL NARRATION sections require treating prior same-turn assistant content/tool results as 'already read' and continuing (no restating/rewriting/re-opening). ask_npc output is already player-facing and must not be re-quoted/embellished. This pairs with the append-only history model the cache relies on.

tool_guidance interpolation positions in GM_SYSTEM (must be filled identically): MODEL_TOOL_RESULT_GUIDE (line 46), GM_TOOL_CAPABILITY_OVERVIEW (line 336), WORLD_STATE_TYPE_GUIDE / WORLD_STATE_SCOPE_GUIDE / WORLD_STATE_SPLIT_GUIDE / WORLD_STATE_CONSOLIDATION_GUIDE / WORLD_STATE_SEARCH_ANCHOR_GUIDE (lines 370-374). GM_TOOL_CAPABILITY_OVERVIEW itself nests VISIBLE_TOOL_CAPABILITY_TEXT + HIDDEN_TOOL_CAPABILITY_TEXT. The Rust port can either (a) precompute the fully-spliced GM_SYSTEM as one literal, or (b) reproduce the same fragment-assembly — but the final byte output must be identical.

NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC braces: the JSON shape line (prompts.py:560) and the gender section use literal braces; in source the JSON example braces are DOUBLED because the file author kept it format-safe, but it is delivered RAW (single braces to the model). In Rust there is no .format() escaping, so write SINGLE braces in the literal — do not carry the doubled braces through.
NPC_SYSTEM_TEMPLATE is the SAME object as NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC (alias). Port should expose one constant and, if needed, an alias — not two divergent copies.
platformConcerns: None intrinsic to prompts.py/tool_guidance.py — pure in-memory string constants, no OS calls, no paths, no subprocess, no sockets, no GPU.

Cross-platform text hazard: these strings are authored with LF (\n) line endings and contain Cyrillic (UTF-8). The Rust port MUST embed them as UTF-8 with LF newlines and must NOT let a Windows toolchain rewrite them to CRLF — CRLF would change the bytes and break prompt-cache prefix identity. Keep source files / embedded literals LF-normalized on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

The {mechanics} JSON uses ensure_ascii=False (raw Cyrillic) and separators=(',',':') (no spaces). A Rust serializer must match: emit non-ASCII characters literally (no \uXXXX escaping) and use compact separators, or the card bytes differ per platform/serializer.
rustNotes: Represent each prompt as a `pub const` / `pub static` &str (or a `String` via include_str!). Prefer embedding the FULLY-RESOLVED GM_SYSTEM as a single literal to eliminate any risk of fragment-splice drift, OR reproduce the assembly with a small build step / once_cell::Lazy that concatenates tool_guidance fragments exactly. If you keep assembly, gate it behind a unit test that asserts the assembled string equals a golden snapshot.

For NPC_CARD_TEMPLATE, NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM, GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM use a templating approach that mirrors Python str.format named-placeholder semantics. Either (a) a tiny named-placeholder replacer, or (b) explicit format!/write! with the 21 fields in the documented order. Do NOT use a generic {} positional formatter that could mis-handle the literal braces; with const strings + explicit replacement there is no brace-escaping issue (unlike Python). Reproduce the Russian fallback literals ('(не указана)', '(не указан)', '(не указаны)', '(не указано)', 'alive', 'OTHER') exactly.

Mechanics serialization: build a serde_json::Map / IndexMap in the SAME key order (abilities, skills, saving_throws, passive_perception, ac, hp, speed, senses, languages), drop entries equal to null/empty-string/empty-object/empty-array, then serialize with a compact, non-ASCII-preserving writer. serde_json by default does NOT escape non-ASCII and uses compact separators with to_string() — that matches ensure_ascii=False + separators=(',',':'). Verify empty map serializes to '{}'.

Make CHARS_PER_TOKEN (default 3) and COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS (default 12000) configurable via env (GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN / GM_COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS) read at startup; use them with integer floor division and char-based slicing (text.chars().take(COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS) — slice by CHAR count, not byte index, to match Python's str[:n] on Unicode).

Implement proper_nouns_line as a function matching llm_client._proper_nouns_line exactly (two branch strings). Decide whether codex-path uses the same wording or the slightly different inline wording — see risks; recommend unifying on the llm_client version unless byte-compat with codex output matters.

Add a golden-file test (snapshot) per constant comparing the Rust literal against the Python source byte-for-byte (strip nothing). This is the cheapest guard against the cache-prefix invariant regressing during the port.
suggestedCrates: serde_json (compact, non-ASCII-preserving JSON for {mechanics})
indexmap (deterministic mechanics key ordering if not hard-coded)
once_cell or std::sync::LazyLock (lazy-assemble GM_SYSTEM from fragments if not pre-baked)
include_str! (std macro; embed verbatim prompt text from .txt sidecar files to keep literals clean and LF-stable)
insta (snapshot/golden tests asserting byte-exact prompt reproduction)
risks: Wording divergence between proper-noun lines: llm_client._proper_nouns_line() produces 'Keep these proper nouns exactly as written if they appear; never translate or transliterate them: <names>.' while codex_client.py:431-433 builds 'Keep these proper nouns exactly as written: <names>.' (no 'if they appear; never translate or transliterate them'). The GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM {proper_nouns_line} therefore receives DIFFERENT text depending on backend. A faithful port must preserve this per-backend difference OR deliberately unify it — confirm intended behavior with the maintainer.

WORLD_STATE_EXAMPLE_GUIDE is defined in tool_guidance.py but is NOT interpolated into GM_SYSTEM (only TYPE/SCOPE/SPLIT/CONSOLIDATION/SEARCH_ANCHOR are). Easy to over- or under-include during the port; the GM_SYSTEM update_world_state block uses exactly five guides, not six. Verify against the spliced output, not the constant list.

The exact line numbers cited (e.g. GM_SYSTEM 5-459) are stable in this snapshot but the f-string means GM_SYSTEM's final length depends on tool_guidance content at import time — if tool_guidance changes, GM_SYSTEM (and _SYS_EST) change. Port should treat tool_guidance as part of the same versioned bundle.

NPC_SYSTEM_STATIC contains a literal JSON example with single braces as seen by the model but DOUBLED braces in the .py source. A naive copy that carries the doubled braces into a Rust literal (which does no .format escaping) would emit '{{...}}' to the model — wrong. Must collapse to single braces.

These prompts encode behavior that interacts with the tools layer (agents.py tool schemas) and the dice/world-state subsystems. The prompts.py map alone cannot verify that every quoted tool/field name (e.g. modifier_note, 2d20kh1, known_name, expected_hash, op=add, target_kind) still matches the live schemas — cross-check against the tools subsystem map before relying on them.

Whitespace inside the triple-quoted blocks (leading two-space indents on continuation lines, single trailing newline behavior) is significant for cache identity but invisible in casual reading. Use a byte-diff, not visual inspection, when porting.

subsystem: SQLite persistence (dialog_store.py) — per-chat dialog/session/world snapshot store + embeddings DB relationship
files: E:/gemma/gm-lab/orchestrator.py (Session class fields it serializes, lines 2420-2520, 2570)
purpose: SQLite-backed persistence keyed by (guest_id, chat_id). Each chat row stores the ENTIRE chat state as one JSON blob (`payload`): the orchestrator Session (GM/NPC message histories, summaries, client ids, run_usage, events, pending/commitments) plus the full World (NPCs, scene, facts, state records, rumors, RNG state, dice seed, player character). There is no relational decomposition — the JSON snapshot is the source of truth and defines on-disk compatibility. A second, separate, content-addressed SQLite DB (gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3, owned by rag.EmbeddingCache) caches embedding vectors; dialog_store only touches it on chat delete (best-effort vector purge). Concurrency is via a per-DialogStore in-process RLock plus short-lived WAL connections.

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.__init__
publicSurface.signature: DialogStore(self, db_path: str, client_factory: Callable[[], object])
publicSurface.behavior: Stores abspath(db_path), the client factory (used to rebuild Session clients on load), an in-memory cache dict[(guest_id,chat_id)->DialogRuntime] guarded by an RLock, then calls _init_db() (creates dirs + tables).

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.get
publicSurface.signature: get(self, guest_id: str, chat_id: str | None = None) -> DialogRuntime
publicSurface.behavior: chat_id None -> get_active. Else _get_chat; raises KeyError(f'chat not found: {chat_id}') if missing.

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.get_active
publicSurface.behavior: Returns runtime of the active chat; falls back to latest chat (and pins it active); if none exist, creates a new chat (activate=True). Whole body under _cache_lock.

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.list_chats
publicSurface.behavior: Returns chat metadata rows ordered by updated_at DESC, created_at DESC, chat_id DESC. Each dict: {id,title,preview,turn_count,created_at,updated_at,active}. Self-heals active pointer to rows[0] if dangling.

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.active_chat_id
publicSurface.behavior: Returns active chat id if it still exists, else latest chat id (pinning it active).

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.create_chat
publicSurface.signature: create_chat(self, guest_id: str, *, session: Session | None = None, transcript: list[dict] | None = None, turn_count: int = 0, title: str | None = None, preview: str | None = None, activate: bool = True) -> DialogRuntime
publicSurface.behavior: Allocates a unique chat_id (secrets.token_urlsafe(12)), builds DialogRuntime (session or Session(None)), saves it, optionally sets it active. Title cleaned to 80 chars, preview to 180.

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.merge_all_chats_into_scope
publicSurface.behavior: Moves every chat from all other guest_ids into target_guest_id (re-allocating chat_id on collision), deletes other guests' guest_dialog_state rows, sets target's active to latest, clears the whole in-memory cache. Returns count moved. Raises ValueError if target empty.

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.delete_chat
publicSurface.signature: delete_chat(self, guest_id: str, chat_id: str) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Hard-deletes the chat row (all chat data lives in payload). Fixes active pointer (latest, or deletes guest_dialog_state if none left). Best-effort purges that chat's world-text embeddings from the global cache. Returns {deleted, active_chat_id, embeddings_purged} or {deleted:False, reason}.

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.activate_chat
publicSurface.signature: activate_chat(self, guest_id: str, chat_id: str) -> DialogRuntime | None
publicSurface.behavior: Loads the chat (None if missing), sets it active, returns runtime.

publicSurface.name: DialogStore.save
publicSurface.behavior: THE core write. Recomputes title (_title_for_save) and preview (_derive_preview), coerces turn_count int, json.dumps(_runtime_to_payload(runtime), ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',',':')), UPSERTs into dialog_chats (created_at/updated_at via datetime('now')), reads back created_at/updated_at into the runtime, then writes runtime into the in-memory cache.

publicSurface.name: _runtime_to_payload
publicSurface.behavior: Top-level JSON snapshot: {schema_version, turn_count, session, transcript}.

publicSurface.name: _session_to_payload / _session_from_payload
publicSurface.behavior: Serialize/rebuild a Session (see dataStructures). from_payload constructs Session(None, world) then re-attaches all fields; does NOT recreate live clients (only client ids), uses session.set_run_usage(...) for usage.

publicSurface.name: _world_to_payload / _world_from_payload
publicSurface.behavior: Serialize/rebuild a World. from_payload uses World.__new__(World) (bypasses __init__), validates required keys, rebuilds RNG via random.Random().setstate(...), calls world._ensure_npc_whereabouts().

publicSurface.name: DialogRuntime (dataclass)
publicSurface.signature: DialogRuntime(guest_id, chat_id, session: Session, transcript: list[dict]=[], turn_count: int=0, title: str='', preview: str='', created_at: str='', updated_at: str='', lock: RLock)
publicSurface.behavior: In-memory cached representation of one chat; lock is a per-runtime RLock (repr=False), used by callers (orchestrator/server) to serialize mutations of a single chat.

publicSurface.name: module constants
publicSurface.behavior: Payload schema version (hard-checked on load) and the fallback chat title.

dataStructures.name: dialog_chats (SQLite table)
dataStructures.fields: guest_id TEXT NOT NULL, chat_id TEXT NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, preview TEXT NOT NULL, turn_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, payload TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (guest_id, chat_id)
dataStructures.notes: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dialog_chats (guest_id TEXT NOT NULL, chat_id TEXT NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, preview TEXT NOT NULL, turn_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, payload TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (guest_id, chat_id)). created_at/updated_at are SQLite datetime('now') text (UTC 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'). payload is the JSON snapshot. title<=80 chars, preview<=180 chars (denormalized copies for cheap list_chats).

dataStructures.name: idx_dialog_chats_guest_updated (index)
dataStructures.fields: dialog_chats(guest_id, updated_at)
dataStructures.notes: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dialog_chats_guest_updated ON dialog_chats(guest_id, updated_at). Supports the ORDER BY updated_at queries (note: query also orders by created_at, chat_id which the index does not fully cover).

dataStructures.name: guest_dialog_state (SQLite table)
dataStructures.fields: guest_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, active_chat_id TEXT, created_at TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
dataStructures.notes: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS guest_dialog_state (guest_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, active_chat_id TEXT, created_at TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL). One row per guest; active_chat_id is NOT a FK (can dangle; self-healed on read). UPSERT via ON CONFLICT(guest_id).

dataStructures.name: embeddings (SQLite table, SEPARATE DB owned by rag.EmbeddingCache)
dataStructures.fields: model TEXT NOT NULL, text_hash TEXT NOT NULL, text TEXT NOT NULL, dims INTEGER NOT NULL, vector_b64 TEXT NOT NULL, created_at REAL NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (model, text_hash)
dataStructures.notes: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS embeddings (model TEXT NOT NULL, text_hash TEXT NOT NULL, text TEXT NOT NULL, dims INTEGER NOT NULL, vector_b64 TEXT NOT NULL, created_at REAL NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (model, text_hash)). Lives at config.RAG_CACHE_PATH (default gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3, sibling file to the dialogs DB). text_hash = sha256(text.encode utf-8) hexdigest (text stripped). vector_b64 = base64 of array('f') little-endian float32. created_at = time.time() (float epoch). Content-addressed and shared across ALL chats — NOT chat-keyed. dialog_store only deletes from it (best-effort) on delete_chat.

dataStructures.name: payload (top-level JSON snapshot)
dataStructures.fields: schema_version:int(==1), turn_count:int, session:object, transcript:list[dict]
dataStructures.notes: json.dumps(..., ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',',':')) — compact, UTF-8 literal (non-ASCII NOT escaped). transcript is stored as-is (list of {event:{...}} dicts); _first_player_event_text/_last_transcript_text/_event_text read event.kind/agent/data/text/speech/action.

dataStructures.name: session (JSON object inside payload)
dataStructures.fields: client_model, client_backend, client_session_id, client_thread_id, world, gm_messages:list, gm_summary:str, npc_messages:{npc_id->list}, npc_summaries:{npc_id->str}, run_usage:dict, npc_client_state:{npc_id->{model,session_id,thread_id}}, world_query_seen:{scope->sorted[str]}, last_player_action, sid:int, events:list, seq:int, turn:int, delivered:{npc_id->int}, shown:{npc_id->int}, pending:{npc_id->{...}}, commitments:{npc_id->list[str]}
dataStructures.notes: Mirrors orchestrator.Session attrs. Note serialization renames: session._sid->'sid', session._seq->'seq', session._shown->'shown'. On load world_query_seen lists become sets; delivered/shown values int.

dataStructures.name: pending entry (per NPC, inside session.pending)
dataStructures.fields: seq:int, speech:str, action:str, claims:list[str], witnesses (list on save / frozenset on load), user_message:dict|None, assistant_message:dict|None
dataStructures.notes: Provisional NPC reply for the current turn before commit_turn(). user_message/assistant_message (the raw LLM turn) MUST be persisted: commit_turn() only appends to npc_messages when BOTH present; dropping them loses NPC private history if a save lands between draft() and commit_turn().

dataStructures.name: world (JSON object)
dataStructures.fields: story_id, story_title, dice_seed:int, forced_die_next:int|None, forced_die_all:int|None, rng_state:{version,internal:list[int],gauss}, hidden_events:list[str], rumors:list, rumor_seq:int, npcs:{npc_id->NPC}, public:str, canon:str, time:WorldTime, player_character:PC, extra_proper_nouns:list[str], scene:SceneState, npc_whereabouts:dict, fact_records:list, state_records:list
dataStructures.notes: Required keys validated on load: story_id, story_title, npcs (non-empty), public, canon, scene, fact_records (non-empty), dice_seed (not None), rng_state (must be present) — else ValueError. 'public' and 'canon' are free-text strings; 'hidden_events' and per-NPC 'secret' are hidden-canon (see invariants).

dataStructures.name: rng_state (JSON object)
dataStructures.fields: version:int (default 3), internal:list[int] (627 ints for MT), gauss (float|None)
dataStructures.notes: From random.Random().getstate() -> (version, internal_tuple, gauss). On load: (int(version or 3), tuple(int...), gauss). Restored via Random().setstate(). This is CPython Mersenne-Twister state — see risks.

dataStructures.name: NPC (JSON object)
dataStructures.fields: npc_id, name, persona, voice, goals, knowledge, secret, role, pronouns, color, public_label, age, physical_type, distinctive_features, life_status('alive'), life_status_note, condition, personality, values, habits, pressure_response, boundaries, abilities:dict, skills:dict, saving_throws:dict, passive_perception:int|None, ac, hp:dict, speed, senses, languages, default_whereabouts:dict|None, card_revision:int
dataStructures.notes: 'secret' is the NPC's hidden canon (must NOT leak to other agents/player). card_revision is an int bump counter (see invariants).

dataStructures.name: PlayerCharacter (JSON object)
dataStructures.fields: name('Искатель'), pronouns('OTHER'), class_role, level:int|None, background, age, physical_type, distinctive_features, life_status('alive'), life_status_note, condition, personality, values, gm_notes, abilities, skills, saving_throws, passive_perception:int|None, ac, hp:dict, speed, senses, languages, inventory:list[str], equipment:list[str], features:list[str], card_revision:int
dataStructures.notes: Defaults applied on missing fields. card_revision parsed defensively (TypeError/ValueError -> 0).

dataStructures.name: Event (JSON object, in session.events)
dataStructures.fields: seq:int, turn:int, actor:str, kind:str, speech:str, action:str, witnesses:list (sorted on save -> frozenset on load)
dataStructures.notes: Append-only event log. witnesses serialized sorted for determinism, rebuilt as frozenset.

dataStructures.name: Rumor (JSON object)
dataStructures.fields: seq:int, turn:int, speaker:str, text:str, witnesses (sorted->frozenset), confirmed:bool(default False)
dataStructures.notes: world.rumors; rumor_seq is the monotonic counter.

dataStructures.name: SceneState (JSON object)
dataStructures.fields: scene_id, location_id, title, description, present_npcs(sorted set), presence:{npc_id->Presence{npc_id,location,visible,can_hear,activity,attitude}}, items:[SceneItem], exits:[SceneExit], constraints:list[str], tension, player_seen:list[str]
dataStructures.notes: On load world.constraints is aliased to scene.constraints.

dataStructures.name: FactRecord / StateRecord (JSON objects)
dataStructures.fields: Fact: {fact_id, kind, text, keywords:list[str], source, confirmed:bool(True)}. State: {record_id, kind('fact'), text, scope('public'), active:bool, owner, subject, source, status('known'), tags:list, entity_id, source_npc, participants:list (lowercased/stripped), location_id, location_name, region_id, region_name, scene_id, importance, aliases:list, metadata:dict}
dataStructures.notes: StateRecord.from_payload accepts legacy aliases: id->record_id, owner_id->owner, subject_id->subject, entity/about->entity_id, source_npc_id->source_npc. participants normalized to lower().

dataStructures.name: WorldTime / NPCWhereabouts (JSON objects)
dataStructures.fields: Time: {calendar_name, absolute_minutes:int(>=0), current_date_label('День 1'), minutes_per_hour:int(>=1, def 60), hours_per_day:int(>=1, def 24), day_names:list, month_names:list, last_advance_minutes:int(>=0), last_advance_reason}. Whereabouts: {npc_id, location_id, location_name, status('unknown'), details, source}
dataStructures.notes: Time fields clamped to minimums on load. Whereabouts keyed by npc_id under world.npc_whereabouts.

controlFlow: Entry points are the public methods, all driven by server.py (one global DialogStore at server.py:152). Read path: _get_chat_locked checks the in-memory cache by (guest_id,chat_id); on miss it _load_chat_row (SELECT payload,title,preview,turn_count,created_at,updated_at), then _runtime_from_payload -> json.loads -> schema_version check -> _session_from_payload + _world_from_payload, caches and returns. Write path: save() recomputes title/preview, json.dumps payload, UPSERT (ON CONFLICT(guest_id,chat_id) DO UPDATE), reads back timestamps, updates cache. Active pointer: guest_dialog_state row, self-healed to latest chat whenever the stored pointer is missing/dangling. Every DB op runs inside the _connection() context manager: open a fresh sqlite3 connection (PRAGMA busy_timeout=10000, journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL), yield, commit on clean exit, always close. There is NO explicit BEGIN/transaction wrapping multi-statement methods beyond the single auto-commit-on-context-exit — e.g. merge_all_chats_into_scope and delete_chat issue several execute() calls on one connection and commit once at context exit (so they are atomic per connection as long as no exception, because commit only happens on clean __exit__). All public mutators take the process-wide self._cache_lock (RLock) first, so cross-chat operations are serialized in-process; per-chat mutations by callers use DialogRuntime.lock. No async; everything is synchronous blocking sqlite3.
externalDeps: Python stdlib: sqlite3 (WAL), json, os, random (Mersenne-Twister RNG state), secrets (token_urlsafe for chat ids), threading (RLock), hashlib (via rag for sha256)
orchestrator.Session (the serialized session shape + set_run_usage)

world (world_mod): World, NPC, PlayerCharacter, WorldTime, SceneState, Presence, NPCWhereabouts, SceneItem, SceneExit, FactRecord, StateRecord, Event, Rumor — and World._rng (random.Random), world._ensure_npc_whereabouts(), world.retrieval_documents(), world.npc_whereabouts_export()
rag.purge_embeddings_for_texts + rag.EmbeddingCache (separate gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 DB) — imported lazily inside _purge_embeddings_for_texts
config: CHARS_PER_TOKEN, BACKEND, RAG_ENABLED, RAG_CACHE_PATH, PROMPT_CACHE_KEY/CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE (these constants govern downstream cache/token behavior the payload feeds)
Filesystem: two sibling SQLite files (gm_lab_dialogs.sqlite3 default via GM_DIALOG_DB; gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 via GM_RAG_CACHE_PATH) plus WAL/SHM sidecar files
internalDeps: orchestrator (Session, make_client used as client_factory in server)
Consumed by server.py (DialogStore instantiated with make_client; drives all chat lifecycle endpoints)
invariants: SCHEMA_VERSION = 1. On load, _runtime_from_payload raises ValueError(f'unsupported schema version: {...}') if payload['schema_version'] != 1. No migration code exists — only IF NOT EXISTS table creation. A faithful port must keep payload.schema_version == 1 and reject mismatches.

Append-only event log: session.events / world.rumors are append-only with a monotonic counter (session._seq -> 'seq', world._rumor_seq -> 'rumor_seq'). Event.witnesses and Rumor.witnesses are serialized SORTED (deterministic) and restored as frozenset. Player/dice events commit immediately; NPC replies commit only at end of turn.

Deterministic dice / RNG: world.dice_seed:int and the FULL CPython random.Random state are persisted (rng_state = {version, internal:list[int], gauss}) and restored via random.Random().setstate(). dice_seed and rng_state are REQUIRED on load (ValueError if dice_seed None or rng_state missing). forced_die_next / forced_die_all (int|None) override the next/all die rolls and are persisted. Reproducible dice depend on faithfully restoring this RNG — see risks (MT state is CPython-specific).

prompt_cache_key / thread-id stability: per-NPC isolation is achieved by a separate client/thread per NPC. session.client_thread_id and per-NPC npc_client_state[npc_id] = {model, session_id, thread_id} are PERSISTED so the same thread (hence the same prompt_cache_key) is reused across reloads. Downstream: codex_client uses prompt_cache_key = config.CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY or self._thread_id (codex_client.py:467); llama path sets p['prompt_cache_key']=config.PROMPT_CACHE_KEY and p['n_cache_reuse']=config.LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE (llm_client.py:231,254). The store must round-trip thread_id/session_id EXACTLY or KV/prompt cache is lost. reset_npc_memory() deliberately drops npc_client_state+npc_clients to force a NEW thread/prompt_cache_key — this is the ONLY intended way to rotate the key.

KV-cache prefix ordering (documented contract the persisted data must respect): cacheable prefix = system rules + public intro (keyed by prompt_cache_key); scene/NPC/fact/record/rumor edits go into the APPENDED tail of the turn and must NOT mutate the prefix; the prefix is rebuilt only when the public intro is edited (see DebugPanel.jsx:917 and prompt_cache_architecture.md). The store persists world.public/world.canon (prefix material) separately from the mutable tail material (events, pending, scene deltas) — keep that separation in the port.

world_query_seen: persisted per scope as a SORTED list of result keys already delivered to the active GM tail (sorted for determinism), restored as a set. Prevents re-injecting the same RAG/world-query result into the cacheable tail twice within a run.

Token estimation heuristic: config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN = int(GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN, default '3'). Used by orchestrator (_SYS_EST = len(GM_SYSTEM)//CHARS_PER_TOKEN at orchestrator.py:21; est tokens = max(0, len(text)//CHARS_PER_TOKEN) at 2218). dialog_store persists turn_count and run_usage but does NOT itself compute token estimates — compaction thresholds live in orchestrator, not here. Port note: the store is a faithful container; the //3 heuristic belongs to orchestrator.

Secret / hidden-canon isolation: NPC.secret and world.hidden_events are persisted in the SAME payload as everything else (no separate encrypted store). Isolation is enforced at READ/prompt-build time in orchestrator/world (e.g. retrieval_documents('player') only returns player-visible docs; _chat_embedding_texts uses scope 'player'), NOT at the storage layer. The port must preserve that the snapshot contains secrets but they are gated by scope when surfaced.

card_revision (int) on both NPC and PlayerCharacter is persisted and parsed defensively (TypeError/ValueError -> 0). It is a monotonic bump counter signaling card edits; an ordinary card edit does NOT reset NPC memory (only explicit reset_memory does). Preserve the value verbatim.

NPC JSON contract fields (pending reply): seq, speech, action, claims (list[str]), witnesses, plus user_message + assistant_message (the raw LLM turn). The comment is explicit: both user_message and assistant_message MUST be persisted because commit_turn() appends to npc_messages only when BOTH are present; dropping them loses NPC private history if a save lands between draft() and commit_turn().

GM/NPC history split: session.gm_messages (verbatim recent turns) + session.gm_summary (compacted old turns); per NPC: npc_messages[npc_id] (verbatim private history) + npc_summaries[npc_id] (compacted). The store persists all four verbatim; the compaction logic itself is in orchestrator. delivered[npc_id] (max seq shown in prior turns) and _shown[npc_id] ('shown'; seq boundary at this turn's wake) are visibility cursors into the shared event log, NOT private memory — on reset they are pinned to current _seq, never deleted, to avoid re-surfacing past events as new.

Atomicity: each public method opens ONE connection via _connection() and commits exactly once on clean context exit; an exception mid-method => no commit => no partial write (connection closed in finally without commit). merge_all_chats_into_scope and delete_chat rely on this single-commit-per-connection behavior for atomicity. There is no explicit savepoint/BEGIN IMMEDIATE.

Concurrency model: WAL mode + busy_timeout=10000ms + synchronous=NORMAL on every connection; one short-lived connection per op (no pooling, no shared connection). In-process serialization via self._cache_lock (RLock) wrapping all mutators, plus per-runtime DialogRuntime.lock for caller-side turn mutation. Multiple processes are tolerated by WAL+busy_timeout but the in-memory cache is NOT cross-process coherent.

Active-pointer self-healing: guest_dialog_state.active_chat_id is not a foreign key. Whenever it is missing or dangling, list_chats/active_chat_id/get_active repoint it to the latest chat (ORDER BY updated_at DESC, created_at DESC, chat_id DESC LIMIT 1) and persist that. When the last chat of a guest is deleted, the guest_dialog_state row is deleted.

chat_id allocation: secrets.token_urlsafe(12) retried up to 32 times against existing rows; RuntimeError('could not allocate unique chat id') if exhausted. On merge collisions a fresh id is generated for the target scope.

Timestamps: created_at/updated_at written via SQLite datetime('now') (UTC, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' text), read back into the runtime. Ordering everywhere is (updated_at DESC, created_at DESC, chat_id DESC).

JSON encoding: payload uses ensure_ascii=False and separators=(',',':') — compact, raw UTF-8 (Cyrillic stored literally). title<=80, preview<=180, generic metadata clean limit default 160; truncation appends '...' (rstrip then '...', cut at limit-3).
platformConcerns: File paths: db_path is os.path.abspath'd; parent dirs created with os.makedirs(exist_ok=True). Default DIALOG_DB = os.path.join(HERE, 'gm_lab_dialogs.sqlite3'); default RAG_CACHE_PATH = sibling 'gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3'. Both overridable by env (GM_DIALOG_DB, GM_RAG_CACHE_PATH). Use platform-agnostic path joins in Rust (std::path::PathBuf).

SQLite WAL mode creates -wal and -shm sidecar files next to the DB; on Windows these can hold locks. busy_timeout=10000 mitigates cross-process contention. Ensure the Rust SQLite driver enables WAL + busy_timeout identically.

datetime('now') is UTC and SQLite-specific text formatting — a Rust port must produce the exact same 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' UTC string for on-disk timestamp compatibility, or callers comparing/sorting strings will differ.

No subprocess/socket/TLS/browser/ffmpeg/GPU in THIS module. The embeddings VALUES come from rag.LocalEmbeddingClient via httpx to a local embeddings server, but dialog_store only deletes from the embeddings DB, never fetches.
CPython random.Random Mersenne-Twister state (627-int internal vector) is persisted; this is interpreter-specific and not portable to a different RNG implementation (see risks/rustNotes).
rustNotes: Use rusqlite (bundled feature) with PRAGMA busy_timeout=10000, journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL set per connection, mirroring _connect(). Wrap multi-statement methods (merge/delete) in an explicit transaction (BEGIN; ... COMMIT) to reproduce the single-commit atomicity — rusqlite::Transaction maps cleanly.

Model the snapshot with serde: a top-level Payload{schema_version:u32, turn_count:i64, session:SessionSnapshot, transcript:Vec<serde_json::Value>} and full typed structs for World/Session/NPC/PC/Scene/Event/Rumor/etc. Keep field names EXACTLY (note the rename map: _sid->sid, _seq->seq, _shown->shown). Serialize with no extra whitespace (serde_json::to_string is already compact like separators=(',',':')) and DO NOT escape non-ASCII (serde_json keeps UTF-8 by default — good). Validate schema_version==1 and the required world keys, returning an error on mismatch.

RNG: the persisted Mersenne-Twister state is CPython-specific. To stay bit-compatible with existing saves you must implement (or vendor) a CPython-compatible MT19937 with the same getstate/setstate layout (version, 624 words + index = the 'internal' list of length 625-ish + the 627 representation, gauss for the cached normal). The 'mt19937' crate gives the algorithm but NOT CPython's exact seeding/tempering wrapper — porting dice determinism faithfully is the single hardest part. Alternatively, on first load, migrate by re-seeding from dice_seed and accept a one-time determinism break (call this out to the user).

Concurrency: replace the Python RLock with a std::sync::Mutex/RwLock around the in-memory cache (HashMap<(String,String), DialogRuntime>) and a per-runtime Mutex. Keep one connection per op or use a small r2d2/deadpool pool; WAL allows concurrent readers + one writer.

Keep the embeddings DB strictly separate (own module, own connection) exactly as today. delete_chat must: (1) read the chat's world.retrieval_documents('player') BEFORE deleting the row, collect contextual_text() of each, (2) delete the dialog row + fix active pointer, (3) best-effort delete from embeddings by sha256 hashes (chunks of 400 to stay under SQLite's variable limit). text_hash MUST be sha256(text.strip()) hex to match cache keys; vector encoding is base64 of little-endian f32 array.

Title/preview derivation, _clean_metadata_text (collapse whitespace via split/join, truncate at limit-3 + '...'), and the active-pointer self-heal logic are pure functions — port them verbatim; they affect what users see in the chat list.
Preserve the 'persist user_message+assistant_message in pending' rule and the witnesses sorted-on-save behavior — these are subtle correctness invariants, not cosmetic.
suggestedCrates: rusqlite (with bundled SQLite, set PRAGMAs per connection)
serde + serde_json (snapshot ser/de; compact + UTF-8 preserving)
r2d2 + r2d2_sqlite OR deadpool-sqlite (optional connection pooling)
rand / rand_mt or a custom CPython-compatible MT19937 implementation (for dice RNG state fidelity — see risks)
sha2 (sha256 text hashing to match embedding cache keys)
base64 (encode/decode embedding float vectors)
chrono (UTC timestamps formatted as 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' to match datetime('now'))
rand or getrandom (URL-safe token generation; reproduce secrets.token_urlsafe(12) ~ 16-char base64url of 12 random bytes)
thiserror (typed errors for schema/version/world-validation failures)
risks: CPython random.Random Mersenne-Twister state portability is the biggest risk. Faithfully restoring rng_state in Rust requires a byte-exact CPython MT19937 (including the cached gauss value and CPython's specific state tuple layout). A generic Rust MT crate will NOT produce identical dice sequences from a restored state. Decide: (a) vendor a CPython-compatible MT, or (b) re-seed from dice_seed and accept a determinism break. Code does not document the exact internal-vector length contract beyond 'list of ints'.

No schema migrations exist: bumping SCHEMA_VERSION would orphan all existing chats (hard ValueError on load). The Rust port must either keep version 1 or add a migration path the Python code never had.

'ac' field is stored via _json_value (can be int, str, dict, or None) — it is intentionally loosely typed. Rust must model it as serde_json::Value or an enum, not a fixed type. Same defensive looseness for hp/abilities/skills/metadata (free-form dicts).

transcript is stored as opaque list[dict] (Vec<Value>); its shape is only loosely contracted via _event_text key probing (data/text/speech/action). The port should keep it as untyped JSON to avoid losing fields written by other modules.

Atomicity depends on the implicit 'commit only on clean context-manager exit' pattern; this is easy to get subtly wrong in a port. An exception between executes leaves no commit (good), but a partial in-memory cache update could diverge — note save() updates the cache AFTER the DB write, while merge clears the whole cache; replicate that ordering.

World is rebuilt with World.__new__ (bypassing __init__), so any __init__ side effects/defaults are NOT applied except the fields explicitly set here plus world._ensure_npc_whereabouts(). A Rust port that runs a normal constructor could introduce defaults the Python load never sets — match the explicit field set exactly.

Embedding purge on delete is best-effort and content-addressed; if two chats share identical world text, deleting one chat removes shared vectors (re-embedded on demand). This is intended but means delete is lossy for the global cache — preserve the best-effort/non-raising semantics.

_session_from_payload does not recreate live clients (npc_clients stays empty); only ids are restored. The port must lazily rebuild clients via the client_factory on demand (ensure_npc_client equivalent), not at load.

subsystem: HTTP server / web + SSE UI layer (server.py + tls_cert.py)
files: E:/gemma/gm-lab/orchestrator.py (boundary: run_turn SSE generator, context_usage, Session, ev())
purpose: A single-file, dependency-free Python stdlib HTTP server (http.server.ThreadingHTTPServer + BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass `Handler`) that is the only network entry point for GM-Lab. It serves the inlined SPA (index.html), exposes JSON REST routes for chat/state/settings/debug, streams a D&D game-master turn as Server-Sent Events over POST /turn, proxies/caches text-to-speech to a sidecar microservice (8765) with optional ffmpeg transcode, proxies speech-to-text through Codex OAuth, and optionally runs a SECOND HTTPS listener (self-signed cert from tls_cert.py) so phones on the LAN get a secure context for the microphone. It holds NO domain logic itself: every route is a thin adapter that takes a per-chat lock, calls into orchestrator/world/dialog_store, serializes the result, and persists via dialog_store.save.

endpoints.response: index.html bytes, Content-Type text/html; charset=utf-8, Cache-Control: no-store, must-revalidate. Side effect: calls self._dialog() to ensure an active chat exists.
state(dialog) dict (see payloadShapes 'state'); context_usage recomputed and overwritten right before send. Under dialog.lock.
{events:[...]} = replay_events(dialog): transcript rows' .event, skipping kind=='delta', with NPC names sanitized to player-known labels. Under dialog.lock.
export_data(dialog), JSON indent=2 default=str, Content-Disposition attachment filename=gm-lab-export.json. Under dialog.lock for the snapshot.
debug_data(dialog) (full GM-side truth incl. hidden_truth/canon, npc secrets, facts, rumors, state_records, pending, delivered). Under dialog.lock.
{ok, model, models:[{id,name,supported,...}], settings, settings_options}; ensure_client(dialog) then client.list_models(); on error {ok:false,error,models:[]} code 400. Under dialog.lock.

Creates a chat. brief => agents.build_world_seed (LLM) seeded session; else story_id => World.from_story. Validates story_id against stories.story_ids(); 400 if unknown or if neither brief nor story_id. Returns {ok, active_chat_id, chat:{...}} plus state+transcript if it became active. Errors {ok:false,error} 400.

endpoints.request: chat_id parsed from path (url-decoded). Body ignored.
endpoints.response: {ok:true, chat, state, under dialog.lock, transcript:{events}} or {ok:false,error:'chat not found'} 404
{ok:true, deleted:true, active_chat_id, chats, chat, state, transcript, embeddings_purged} or {ok:false,error} 404. Reloads active dialog after delete.

endpoints.request: RAW audio bytes body (NOT JSON); Content-Type header is forwarded (default audio/webm). Requires config.BACKEND=='codex' else 400.
endpoints.response: {ok:true, text} or {ok:false,error,status}. Delegates to codex_transcribe.transcribe(audio, content_type).
Runs codex_oauth.run_oauth() (opens browser, local OAuth callback). {ok:true, auth} or {ok:false,error,auth} 400.
Sets model on GM + all NPC clients (set_model_for_all_clients), reconcile_for_model, persists. {ok:true, state} or {ok:false,error} 400. Under dialog.lock.

endpoints.request: {settings:{...}} OR a flat dict of settings keys
endpoints.response: runtime_settings.update(...); {ok:true, settings, settings_options, state}. Under dialog.lock (saves dialog).

reset: rebuild Session from current story_id (keeps model/session/thread ids if same backend), clears transcript+turn_count; new+arg: seed a NEW chat from brief and activate; constraint+arg: append world.constraints; event+arg: append world.hidden_events. {ok:true,state} or 400. Under dialog.lock (new uses its own).
Sets world.forced_die_next / forced_die_all (max(1,int) or None). Returns debug_data(dialog). Under dialog.lock.
Session.apply_debug_edit (single source of truth: presence touched only on actual change, memory reset only on explicit truthy flag); debug_data or {ok:false,error} 400 for unknown npc.

Audio bytes (Content-Type ogg/mpeg/wav by GM_TTS_FORMAT or audio/pcm when streaming), X-TTS-Voice header. Disk-cache hit returns immediately; miss synthesizes via sidecar; {ok:false,error} 400 empty text, 503 if sidecar down.
endpoints.streaming: yes when body.stream truthy AND cache miss: head-first PCM proxy from sidecar /speak_stream, content-type audio/pcm, X-Sample-Rate header

endpoints.response: SSE stream (text/event-stream; charset=utf-8, Cache-Control: no-cache, X-Accel-Buffering: no). Each line 'data: '+JSON+'\n\n'. Sequence of ev() objects ending with {kind:'done'}. Under dialog.lock for whole stream; transcript appended + saved.

events.shape: ev(kind,agent,data,sid) = {"kind":str, "agent":str|null, "data":any|null, "sid":str|null}. Wire line: 'data: ' + json.dumps(ev, ensure_ascii=False) + '\n\n'. sid is the stream-element id (s1,s2,...) tying deltas to their finalized row.
events.meaning: Universal envelope produced by orchestrator.ev(). EVERY SSE event uses this shape. The server's push() does NOT add SSE 'event:' lines or ids — all type info is in the JSON 'kind'.
Echo of the player's input at turn start.

Incremental token stream. SKIPPED on replay (REPLAY_SKIP_KINDS={'delta'}). gm_thinking=reasoning, gm_narration=visible GM prose, npc_speech=incremental NPC speech parsed live from streaming JSON via extract_json_string.
Finalized GM reasoning block for this sid.
Finalized visible GM narration. Replaces streamed deltas in-place (same sid). May come from inline content or a generated pre-tool prelude (under prelude's own sid).
Per-LLM-call token/timing stats. scope drives token bucketing in add_turn_usage.
Final aggregate for the whole turn. Emitted by run_turn after _drive completes; includes recomputed context_usage and cumulative run_usage.
GM decided to call a tool. NOT emitted for ask_player (show_tool_event=False).
Full (player/UI-facing) tool output text. NOT emitted for ask_player. Distinct from the compact model-facing text fed back to the GM history.
Result of tool_search tool: which GM tools were loaded/missing.
ask_player tool: clickable suggested player actions (>=4, capped 8).
roll_dice result. Recorded into the public event log via record_public(kind='dice').
get_world_fact lookup result (RAG/fact registry).
update_world_state batch result.
query_world_state result.
get_npc_profile result.
advance_time result.
update_player_character result.

events.shape: {kind:'npc_whereabouts', agent:'ГМ', data: player-facing payload (name swapped to player label)}
events.meaning: set_npc_whereabouts result (offscreen location).
Emitted by move_npc/set_npc_presence, set_scene, AND _sync_scene_delta (agent='scene_sync') which auto-applies enter/leave deltas parsed from accepted narration.
An NPC sub-agent is about to speak; label is the player-known name.
Debug/diagnostic dump of the NPC's private history fed to its sub-agent.
NPC sub-agent reasoning (from NPC JSON 'reasoning' field).
Finalized NPC turn. The 4-field NPC JSON contract: reasoning(->npc_thinking), speech, action, claims.
GM (critic) sent the prior NPC draft back for a redraft with a note.
Per-event error (model call failure, unknown NPC/tool, tool-hop limit, ask_player-not-called, npc_not_present, etc). Does NOT terminate the stream.
Terminal sentinel always pushed last in /turn (even after a top-level exception that was emitted as a kind:'error' event).

payloadShapes: state(dialog) -> {model, backend, stream_gm_content:bool, settings:{...runtime_settings.get()}, settings_options:{...}, run_usage:{turns,calls,in,out,cached,tokens,secs,peak_context,gm_calls,gm_tokens,npc_calls,npc_tokens,other_calls,other_tokens}, context_usage:{...}, story_id, story_title, public, time:World.time_export(), player_character:World.player_character_export(public=true), scene:World.scene_export(), entities:World.entity_refs(), status_labels:WHEREABOUTS_STATUS_LABELS, npcs:[public_npc], codex_auth?(only when backend==codex)}
public_npc -> {id, name(=label), label, known_name, public_label, role(_public_role), pronouns(_public_gender), color, physical_type, distinctive_features, condition, life_status}

context_usage(session) -> {current:int, world:int, next_compact:{scope,label,used,limit,remaining}, gm:{active,history,summary,limit,remaining}, npc:{id,name,active,history,summary,limit,remaining}, npcs:[{id,name,color,has_session,active,history,summary,limit,remaining}]}

export_data -> {meta:{model,backend,turns,run_usage,story_id,story_title}, world:{story_id,story_title,public,time,player_character(public=false),constraints,scene,rumors,state_records,npc_commitments,npc_summaries,npc_messages,npc_client_state,events}, transcript, gm_messages}

debug_data -> {ok, meta, runtime:{settings, cache:{prompt_cache_key, thread_id, store:false}}, story:{id,title,objective,public_intro,hidden_truth(=world.canon),constraints,hidden_events}, scene, time, player_character(public=false), roll_override:{next,all}, status_labels, facts:[{id,kind,text,keywords,source,confirmed}], state_records, rumors:[{seq,turn,speaker,text,witnesses,confirmed}], npcs:[full card incl secret/knowledge/goals/mechanics/card_revision/summary/commitments/history], memory:{gm_summary, gm_messages:int, loaded_gm_tools, events:[last 80 _debug_event], pending:{npc_id->{seq,speech,action,claims,witnesses}}, delivered}}
DialogRuntime (in-memory, dataclass) -> {guest_id, chat_id, session:Session, transcript:list[{turn:int, event:ev-dict}], turn_count:int, title, preview, created_at, updated_at, lock:threading.RLock}
transcript row -> {turn:int, event:{kind,agent,data,sid}} (event is the exact ev() object that was streamed; delta rows are stored but skipped on replay)
_chat_response -> {id,title,preview,turn_count,created_at,updated_at,active}

NPC sub-agent JSON contract (out dict) -> {reasoning:str, speech:str, action:str, claims:list[str]} (server/orchestrator never sees raw JSON; agents parse it; streamed live by extracting the 'speech' string field)
TTS request body -> {text, voice?, role?, npc_id?, stream?}; cache key = sha1(f'{voice}\n{text}'); files <key>.{ogg|mp3|wav}
auth_status -> {authenticated:bool, account_id:str|null, expires_at:str|null, message:str}

settings (runtime_settings) -> {gm_reasoning_effort, gm_reasoning_summary, npc_reasoning_effort, npc_reasoning_summary, compact_reasoning_effort, compact_reasoning_summary, text_verbosity, tool_choice, stream_gm_content, parallel_tool_calls, gm_suggest_options, tts_enabled, tts_autoplay, max_tool_hops, max_output_tokens}
settings_options -> {reasoning_efforts,reasoning_summaries,reasoning_roles,text_verbosities,tool_choices,max_tool_hops_max,max_output_tokens_max}
invariants: TOKEN ESTIMATION: CHARS_PER_TOKEN = int(GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN, default 3). _estimate_tokens(text) = max(0, len(text)//CHARS_PER_TOKEN). Integer floor division, char count of role+': '+content joined. _SYS_EST = len(prompts.GM_SYSTEM)//CHARS_PER_TOKEN computed once at import.

GM HISTORY COMPACTION (_maybe_compact, called at START of every turn after appending the player message): trigger ONLY if _messages_tokens(gm_messages) >= GM_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 100000, env GM_HISTORY_TOKENS). Then find indices of role=='user' messages ('starts'); if len(starts) <= GM_KEEP_TURNS (default 3) do nothing. Otherwise cut = starts[-GM_KEEP_TURNS]; old=msgs[:cut], recent=msgs[cut:]; gm_summary = client.summarize((gm_summary+'\n'+old_text).strip(), proper_nouns=world.proper_nouns()); gm_messages=recent; session.reset_world_query_cache(). VERBATIM KEPT: last GM_KEEP_TURNS turns (boundaries = user messages).
