SUMMARIZED: everything older + prior summary, in ONE summarize() call. _msg_text_for_summary strips the 'PLAYER ACTION (latest user input...)' marker prefix for user messages.

NPC HISTORY COMPACTION (_maybe_compact_npc, called inside _ask_npc before generating): trigger ONLY if _messages_tokens(npc_messages[npc_id]) >= NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 64000, env NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS); starts = role=='user' indices; if len(starts) <= NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES (default 6) skip; cut=starts[-NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES]; summarize old via prompts.NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM (think=True, reasoning_role=ROLE_COMPACT) into npc_summaries[npc_id]; npc_messages[npc_id]=recent. Summarize input clipped to COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS (default 12000). No-op if client is None.

COMPACT IS DELIBERATELY LATE to preserve a stable append-only prefix for prompt/KV-cache reuse (config.py comment lines 82-84): 'держим append-only историю и давать prompt/KV-cache переиспользовать одинаковый префикс'. The Rust port MUST keep history append-only between compactions and only mutate the prefix at a compaction boundary.

PROMPT-CACHE / THREAD-ID STABILITY: debug_data reports cache = {prompt_cache_key: config.CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY or session.client_thread_id, thread_id: session.client_thread_id, store: False}. CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY defaults to PROMPT_CACHE_KEY (env GM_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, default ''). When empty, the per-dialog stable client_thread_id IS the cache key. thread_id/session_id are persisted per chat (session.client_thread_id/client_session_id) and per NPC (npc_client_state[npc_id]={model,session_id,thread_id}) and restored on load so the SAME cache prefix is reused across server restarts. ensure_client calls client.set_session_identity(session_id, thread_id) to pin them.

BACKEND-SWITCH INVALIDATION: _session_matches_backend(session) = (not stored client_backend) or stored==config.BACKEND. When ensure_client detects a backend mismatch it CLEARS client_model, client_session_id, client_thread_id and npc_client_state (new thread => new cache key). reset_npc_memory() also drops the live client/thread so the next call gets a fresh thread + new prompt_cache_key.
llama cache_reuse: config.LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE = int(GM_LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE, default 0). Passed through to the llama.cpp client (not consumed in server.py itself).

DETERMINISTIC DICE SEEDING: world carries dice_seed:int and a full Python random.Random state. dialog_store persists rng_state = {version, internal:[int...], gauss} via _rng.getstate()/setstate() so dice rolls are REPRODUCIBLE across save/load. forced_die_next (one-shot) and forced_die_all (sticky) override rolls; set via POST /debug/roll with max(1,int(value)) or None. A faithful Rust port must reproduce CPython's Mersenne-Twister state EXACTLY or change the persistence contract (this is the single hardest portability item).

SECRET / HIDDEN-CANON ISOLATION: world.canon (hidden_truth) and NPC.secret/knowledge/goals are exposed ONLY by GET /debug and GET /export (GM-side views). GET /state uses public_npc() which exposes only role(_public_role)/pronouns(_public_gender)/public_label/known_name/condition/life_status — never secret/knowledge/persona/mechanics. RAG index (config comment) excludes hidden canon and NPC secrets. Player-facing tool events run through _player_facing_payload (swaps true name->player label); replay_events runs _sanitize_player_name over kinds {npc_start,npc_speech,gm_reject} (agent field) and {scene_update,npc_whereabouts} (data.name) to resolve to the currently-known label. The model-facing tool text is compact whitelisted text, distinct from the richer UI 'tool_result'.

APPEND-ONLY EVENT LOG + TWO-PHASE NPC COMMIT: session.events is append-only and capped at EVENTS_CAP=400 (env GM_EVENTS_CAP) on each commit_turn (keep last 400). NPC replies are DRAFTS in session.pending all turn (correction overwrites the SAME seq); only commit_turn() at end of turn appends them to events, NPC commitments (capped _COMMIT_BLOCKS=8), npc_messages, and world rumors. delivered[npc_id] advances to _shown[npc_id] (what the NPC actually saw on its wakeup) NOT to end-of-log, so an NPC never 'sees' replies it could not have witnessed. monotonic _seq, per-turn _sid counter.
OBSERVATION CAP: _OBS_CAP=12 lines surfaced to an NPC; _COMMIT_BLOCKS=8 own-memory blocks; npc_history_text default max_messages=8 (debug uses 6).

CARD_REVISION: NPC.card_revision and PlayerCharacter.card_revision are ints persisted and surfaced in debug/npc_profile. Card edits bump revision; presence/whereabouts edits do NOT reset NPC memory. Memory reset happens ONLY on explicit reset_memory truthy flag (apply_debug_edit), never on a normal card save.

SCENE-SYNC SIDE EFFECT: after a no-tool GM narration, _sync_scene_delta parses NPC enter/leave from the accepted text (only if some npc.name literally appears) and applies presence changes, emitting scene_update events with agent='scene_sync'. This is a hidden write path triggered by narration content.

TURN BUDGET: max_tool_hops from runtime_settings (default 0 = unlimited, cap MAX_TOOL_HOPS_CAP=100). If hops exhausted without a terminal/no-call result => error 'Превышен лимит вызовов инструментов'. If gm_suggest_options enabled but ask_player never produced PLAYER OPTIONS => error event.

REPLAY CONTRACT: REPLAY_SKIP_KINDS={'delta'} — delta rows are stored in the transcript but never replayed (the finalized gm_narration/npc_speech under the same sid is the canonical row). /turn appends EVERY ev (incl deltas and the final {kind:'error'}) to dialog.transcript with {turn:turn_no, event:ev}; {kind:'done'} is NOT appended to transcript (pushed only).

PERSISTENCE ATOMICITY OF A TURN: the entire /turn SSE loop runs under dialog.lock; dialog.turn_count is incremented BEFORE streaming; transcript is appended per-event; dialog_store.save(dialog) is called in finally (so a mid-turn crash still persists partial transcript). _pending_to_payload deliberately persists user_message/assistant_message so an NPC's private history is not lost if a save lands between draft() and commit_turn().

CHAT SCOPE: CHAT_SCOPE_ID = (env GM_CHAT_SCOPE_ID or 'shared'); on startup main() runs dialog_store.merge_all_chats_into_scope(CHAT_SCOPE_ID) folding all guest scopes into one shared scope. Handler._chat_scope_id() returns this constant for every request (single-tenant shared model). chat_id = secrets.token_urlsafe(12).

SQLITE PAYLOAD: one row per chat holds the ENTIRE chat (session+world+transcript+secrets) as a JSON 'payload' column; SCHEMA_VERSION=1 strictly checked on load (raises on mismatch). Deleting the row deletes all chat data; embeddings are content-addressed in a separate global cache and best-effort purged on delete.

STREAMING NPC SPEECH PARSE: live npc_speech deltas come from extract_json_string(buf,'speech')+json_unescape on the partial streaming JSON — the port needs an incremental JSON-string extractor that tolerates unterminated JSON.
platformConcerns: THREADING: ThreadingHTTPServer (daemon_threads=True on the HTTPS variant) — one thread per request. Concurrency safety relies on per-DialogRuntime threading.RLock (dialog.lock) and DialogStore._cache_lock. The Rust port (Tauri) must replicate: a per-chat async mutex/RwLock guarding the full read-modify-serialize-save cycle, and a store-level lock around the in-memory chat cache.

DUAL LISTENER: plain HTTP always on HOST:PORT (env GM_HOST default 127.0.0.1, GM_PORT default 8000). Optional HTTPS on GM_HTTPS_PORT (default 8443) auto-enabled when GM_HOST is 0.0.0.0/'' (LAN exposure) OR GM_HTTPS=1 OR both GM_TLS_CERT+GM_TLS_KEY set; GM_HTTPS=0 forces off. HTTPS runs on a daemon thread; HTTP on the main thread.

_SniffingHTTPSServer: peeks 1 byte (socket.MSG_PEEK, 8s timeout) on each accepted connection; if first byte==0x16 (TLS ClientHello) wraps with ssl_ctx.wrap_socket(server_side=True), else leaves raw so the handler 308-redirects http://host:8443 -> https://host:8443. MSG_PEEK + per-conn timeout + SSLSocket type check is the trick to port (Rust: peek via tokio TcpStream peek or a rustls acceptor that falls back).

TLS SELF-SIGNED (tls_cert.py): uses Python 'cryptography' (RSA-2048, SHA256, 10-year validity, SAN=localhost + all local IPs via socket.getaddrinfo(gethostname()) and a UDP connect to 8.8.8.8:80 to discover the primary outbound IPv4). Cert cached at <project>/.tls/gmlab-cert.pem + gmlab-key.pem; regenerated only if missing. lan_ipv4() filters to non-loopback IPv4 for the printed phone URLs. Rust: rcgen for self-signed, get_if_addrs/local-ip-address for SANs.

TTS PROXY: HTTP to a sidecar at GM_TTS_URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8765) endpoints /speak (full WAV) and /speak_stream (raw PCM with X-Sample-Rate header, default 24000). 120s timeouts. Streaming pulls 16384-byte chunks and stops if the client socket dies (so the GPU stops). curl_cffi is NOT used here (plain urllib).

FFMPEG TRANSCODE: subprocess.run(['ffmpeg','-hide_banner','-loglevel','error','-f','wav','-i','pipe:0', *enc_args,'pipe:1'], input=wav, 60s timeout). Formats: ogg(libopus 32k mono), mp3(libmp3lame 56k mono), wav(passthrough). GM_TTS_FORMAT default 'ogg'. Falls back to raw WAV if ffmpeg missing/fails. Rust: spawn ffmpeg via std::process or use a Rust opus/lame binding; must tolerate ffmpeg absent.
TTS DISK CACHE: GM_TTS_CACHE_DIR default <project>/tts_cache; atomic write via tmp+os.replace; key sha1(voice\ntext); lookup tries configured ext then wav. Best-effort (OSError swallowed).
STT: POST /transcribe forwards RAW request body bytes + Content-Type to codex_transcribe.transcribe (Codex OAuth); ONLY available when BACKEND=='codex'.

BROWSER OPEN: webbrowser.open(url) only if GM_OPEN_BROWSER=='1'. Codex OAuth (run_oauth) also opens a browser and runs a local OAuth callback server on CODEX_AUTH_PORT (default 1455). Tauri should replace with shell open / tauri-plugin-shell + a localhost callback listener.

PATHS: HERE=os.path.dirname(abspath(__file__)); index.html, .tls/, tts_cache/, gm_lab_dialogs.sqlite3 (env GM_DIALOG_DB), gm_lab_settings.json, gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 all resolved relative to the script dir. The port must use a real app-data dir per-OS (Tauri path API).

CORS: NONE. No Access-Control-* headers are ever set. The SPA is same-origin (served from / by this server). A Tauri webview is same-origin too, but if any cross-origin fetch is added the port must add CORS or it will silently work only same-origin.

STATIC SERVING: only index.html (and any /index* path) is served, with Cache-Control: no-store, must-revalidate (single inlined bundle, no cache-busting). No general static file server, no directory traversal surface.

BROKEN-PIPE TOLERANCE: _write_body and the SSE push swallow BrokenPipeError/ConnectionAbortedError/ConnectionResetError; /turn catches BrokenPipeError to stop the generator when the client disconnects. log_message is overridden to silence access logs.
rustNotes: (see suggestedCrates in invariants/platformConcerns) Core crates: axum + hyper + tokio (server/SSE), tower-http (NOT for CORS unless added), rusqlite or sqlx (SQLite WAL), serde + serde_json (payloads/events, UTF-8 by default), rcgen + tokio-rustls (self-signed TLS + sniffing acceptor), reqwest (TTS/STT/Codex HTTP, streaming + timeouts), tokio::process or audiopus/opus (ffmpeg replacement), async-stream or tokio mpsc (run_turn generator), dashmap + tokio::sync::Mutex (per-chat locking), local-ip-address/get_if_addrs (LAN SANs), an MT19937 implementation (rand_mt or hand-rolled) ONLY if preserving existing dice saves, sha1/sha2 (TTS cache key), tauri + tauri-plugin-shell (browser open / OAuth callback).
Keep the SSE wire format byte-identical: 'data: ' + compact-or-default JSON + '\n\n', UTF-8, no event: lines, all four ev keys present (data/sid null when absent). index.html parses this exact shape.
Hold the per-chat lock for the WHOLE /turn stream (serializes turns per chat) and persist on both success and error paths, mirroring Python's try/except/finally + trailing save.
Strictly enforce schema_version==1 on payload load (hard error), mirror PRAGMA busy_timeout=10000 / WAL / synchronous=NORMAL, and store the entire chat as one JSON payload column.
risks: Full route surface was confirmed from server.py, but the request/response BODY shapes of the /debug/* and tool payloads depend on world.py methods (add_fact, patch_scene, apply_state_record_batch, roll_outcome_payload, npc_profile, scene_export, entity_refs, time_export, player_character_export, set_npc_presence, npc_player_label, etc.) which I did NOT read in full — their exact field names should be mapped from world.py before porting (this map gives the call sites and arg names only).

The NPC JSON contract fields (reasoning/speech/action/claims) and GM tool-arg schemas are consumed in orchestrator/agents; I confirmed the 4 NPC fields and every GM tool's args from _run_tool, but the authoritative tool JSON-Schemas (parameter names/types/required) live in agents.py/prompts.py which were not read — verify there before finalizing the tool contract.

extract_json_string / json_unescape (llm_client) drive live npc_speech streaming; their exact partial-JSON tolerance behavior was not read — the Rust incremental parser must match or live streaming will differ.

client.summarize() and client.chat()/list_models()/set_model()/set_session_identity()/chat-stream stats keys (prompt_eval_count, eval_count, cached_tokens, *_duration in ns) are an llm_client contract surfaced into meta events; only the consumed keys are documented here — the full client interface (codex/llamacpp/openai/mock) is a separate subsystem.
context_usage 'next_compact' uses min(remaining) across GM + per-NPC candidates; the UI depends on this exact shape — confirmed from code.

Whether {kind:'done'} should be appended to the transcript: Python pushes it but does NOT append it (it's pushed after dialog_store.save in the lock's tail). A port that appends it would corrupt replay. Confirmed it is push-only.
tls_cert.local_ips() does a live UDP connect to 8.8.8.8:80 to find the outbound IP — offline machines just skip it (exception swallowed); the Rust equivalent must be equally non-fatal.

MIGRATED_CHAT_COUNT / merge_all_chats_into_scope runs on EVERY startup and rewrites rows for non-shared scopes; in a single-tenant Tauri app this may be a no-op but the migration must be ported or explicitly dropped (decide whether the multi-guest legacy path still matters).

subsystem: llm_client (OpenAI-compatible / llama.cpp chat client, mock backend, JSON streaming extraction)
purpose: llm_client.py is the inference transport layer. It builds OpenAI /v1/chat/completions requests (messages, tools, sampling, llama.cpp-only chat_template_kwargs/cache fields), drives both non-streaming (httpx POST) and streaming (SSE `data:` lines) calls, parses llama.cpp/Qwen3.6 response shapes (message.reasoning_content / message.content / message.tool_calls with arguments as a JSON string), extracts tool calls, auto-detects the loaded model via GET /v1/models, and normalizes per-call stats into a `_meta`-style dict consumed by the orchestrator. It also provides a deterministic MockClient that walks a fixed NPC-redo scenario without any model, and free-function helpers (extract_json_string, json_unescape) used by the orchestrator to incrementally surface NPC speech from a growing JSON buffer during streaming. make_client() is the single factory selecting mock / codex / OpenAICompat backend from config.BACKEND.

publicSurface.name: make_client
publicSurface.behavior: Factory. Returns MockClient if config.BACKEND=='mock', CodexClient (lazy import from codex_client) if 'codex', else OpenAICompatClient. Called by server.py, orchestrator.Session, smoke/test harnesses.

publicSurface.name: extract_json_string
publicSurface.signature: extract_json_string(buf: str, field: str) -> tuple[str | None, bool]
publicSurface.behavior: Incremental parser over a GROWING JSON buffer. Finds the string value of `field` and returns (raw_value_or_None, complete:bool). Returns partial value with complete=False until the closing quote arrives. Used by orchestrator.py:3318 to stream NPC 'speech' as it generates. Handles backslash escaping but returns the raw (still-escaped) string.

publicSurface.name: json_unescape
publicSurface.behavior: Manual JSON-string unescape: \" -> ", \n, \t, \/ -> /, \\ -> \. Used by orchestrator on the raw value from extract_json_string to display partial NPC speech. NOTE: does not handle \uXXXX.

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.__init__
publicSurface.behavior: Builds chat/models URLs from (config.API_BASE or config.LLAMA_HOST).rstrip('/'); if base ends with /v1 appends /chat/completions and /models, else appends /v1/... . Sets Authorization: Bearer header if config.API_KEY. Creates httpx.Client with Timeout(connect=10, read=None, write=60, pool=None). Resolves self._model = config.MODEL or self._detect_model(base). Inits self.call_log=[].

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.model
publicSurface.behavior: Returns current resolved model id.

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.set_model
publicSurface.behavior: Sets self._model if the trimmed arg is non-empty. Called by server.py and orchestrator on model switch.

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.list_models
publicSurface.behavior: GET /v1/models (timeout 10s). Reads data['data'] or data['models']; per entry maps id = id|slug|model, name = name|id, supported=True. On any exception or empty result returns a single-element fallback [{'id': self._model, 'name': self._model, 'supported': True}].

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.chat
publicSurface.signature: chat(self, messages, tools=None, think=False, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM) -> tuple[str, str, list, dict]
publicSurface.behavior: Non-streaming. Returns (thinking, content, calls, assistant_msg). thinking=_think(message.reasoning_content); content=_clean(message.content or ''); calls=_parse_tool_calls(message.tool_calls); assistant_msg=_assistant_msg(content, raw_tool_calls). Logs stats via _remember('chat'). Used by agents.gm_turn and orchestrator compaction.

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.chat_json
publicSurface.signature: chat_json(self, messages, schema, think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Free-text JSON path: POST with think; _loads(message.content). If empty dict, re-POST with think=False and response_format={'type':'json_object'} and _loads('{}' fallback). `schema` param is accepted but NOT sent to the server (only used by callers/grammar fallback conceptually). Logs 'chat_json' / 'chat_json_fallback'.

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.summarize
publicSurface.behavior: Compaction. Builds system from prompts.GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM.format(proper_nouns_line=_proper_nouns_line(proper_nouns)); user = text[:config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS]; calls chat(think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_COMPACT); returns content.strip(). Used by orchestrator GM/NPC history compaction.

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.chat_stream
publicSurface.signature: chat_stream(self, messages, tools=None, think=False, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM) -> Generator[tuple[str,str], None, tuple[str,str,list,dict,dict]]
publicSurface.behavior: Generator: yields ('thinking', delta) for delta.reasoning_content and ('content', delta) for delta.content; accumulates tool_calls by delta index (id, function.name, function.arguments concatenated). On StopIteration returns (thinking, content, calls, assistant_msg, stats). Used by agents.gm_turn_stream / gm_prelude_stream.

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.chat_json_stream
publicSurface.signature: chat_json_stream(self, messages, schema, think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM) -> Generator[tuple[str,str], None, tuple[dict,dict]]
publicSurface.behavior: Generator: yields ('content', delta) for each content delta. Returns (parsed_dict, stats) where parsed = _loads(joined content); if empty, falls back to self.chat_json(messages, schema, ...). Used by agents.npc_turn_stream; orchestrator scans the streamed buffer with extract_json_string.

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient.call_log
publicSurface.behavior: Append-only per-call stats rows (label + _stats fields + tokens). orchestrator.py:2389/2395 snapshots len(call_log) before a turn and slices [before:] to attribute stats to that turn.

publicSurface.name: MockClient
publicSurface.behavior: Drop-in stand-in (model='mock'). Implements the SAME public surface. chat() drives a 3-step scenario keyed by count of role=='tool' messages (0 -> ask_npc; 1 -> ask_npc with correction; else final narration). chat_json() branches on system/user text markers to return canned world-seed / scene-delta / NPC JSON. Used by tests and GM_BACKEND=mock.

publicSurface.name: OpenAICompatClient._payload (internal but contract-critical)
publicSurface.signature: _payload(self, messages, tools=None, think=None, response_format=None, stream=False, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Builds the request body. See invariants for exact field gating (prompt_cache_key, prompt_cache_retention, max_tokens, tools/tool_choice/parallel_tool_calls, response_format, chat_template_kwargs, sampling spread, n_cache_reuse, stream_options).

dataStructures.name: request payload (OpenAI /v1/chat/completions)
dataStructures.fields: model:str; messages:list; stream:bool; [prompt_cache_key:str]; [prompt_cache_retention:str]; [max_tokens:int]; [tools:list]; [tool_choice:'auto'|'required'|'none']; [parallel_tool_calls:bool]; [response_format:{type:'json_object'}]; [chat_template_kwargs:{enable_thinking:bool}]; [temperature,top_p,top_k,min_p,presence_penalty]; [n_cache_reuse:int]; [stream_options:{include_usage:true}]
dataStructures.notes: chat_template_kwargs + top_k/min_p + n_cache_reuse only emitted when config.USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS is true; otherwise only temperature/top_p/presence_penalty are copied from the sampling preset. tools only present when truthy.

dataStructures.name: non-stream response (llama.cpp/Qwen)
dataStructures.fields: choices[0].message.{reasoning_content:str, content:str, tool_calls:list}; usage.{prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens | input_tokens_details.cached_tokens}; timings.{prompt_ms, predicted_ms}; injected client field _client_elapsed_ms:float
dataStructures.notes: content is empty string on a pure tool-call turn. reasoning_content is the separate thinking channel.

dataStructures.name: stream chunk (SSE delta)
dataStructures.fields: choices[0].delta.{reasoning_content, content, tool_calls:[{index, id, function:{name, arguments}}]}; top-level usage; top-level timings
dataStructures.notes: Lines arrive as 'data: {json}'; '[DONE]' terminates. tool_call arguments arrive as concatenated string fragments keyed by index.

dataStructures.name: raw tool_call (OpenAI shape, as stored in history)
dataStructures.fields: {id:str, type:'function', function:{name:str, arguments:str (JSON-encoded string)}}
dataStructures.notes: This is the shape put into assistant_msg['tool_calls'] and re-sent in subsequent turns. arguments stays a STRING here.

dataStructures.name: parsed call (cross-module, returned to orchestrator)
dataStructures.fields: {name:str|None, arguments:dict, id:str}
dataStructures.notes: _parse_tool_calls decodes arguments string via _loads into a dict; empty dict if unparseable. id defaults to ''.

dataStructures.name: assistant_msg (appended to history)
dataStructures.fields: {role:'assistant', content:str, [tool_calls:list]}
dataStructures.notes: reasoning is deliberately NOT stored (comment: Qwen must not be fed prior thoughts in multi-turn).

dataStructures.name: _stats / _meta stats row
dataStructures.fields: load_duration:int(=0); cached_tokens:int; prompt_eval_count:int; eval_count:int; prompt_eval_duration:int(ns); eval_duration:int(ns); total_duration:int(ns)
dataStructures.notes: Durations are nanoseconds (ms*1e6). orchestrator.py:2135-2141 divides eval/prompt/total/load_duration by 1e9 and reads prompt_eval_count/eval_count/cached_tokens. Field names are an Ollama-compatible contract and MUST be preserved.

dataStructures.name: call_log row
dataStructures.fields: {label:str, ...(_stats fields), tokens:int}
dataStructures.notes: tokens = prompt_eval_count + eval_count. labels seen: 'chat','chat_json','chat_json_fallback','chat_stream','chat_json_stream'. Mock uses _mock_stats().

dataStructures.name: NPC JSON contract (agents.NPC_SCHEMA)
dataStructures.fields: reasoning:str; speech:str; action:str; claims:list[str]; all four required
dataStructures.notes: Produced by NPC via chat_json/chat_json_stream; orchestrator streams 'speech' incrementally with extract_json_string. This contract is the load-bearing NPC output shape.

dataStructures.name: model entry (list_models)
dataStructures.fields: {id:str, name:str, supported:bool}
dataStructures.notes: Normalized to this shape regardless of provider field naming (id|slug|model, name|id).

controlFlow: make_client() picks the backend once. For OpenAICompatClient, every chat/chat_json/summarize goes through _payload -> _post (httpx.Client.post, raise_for_status, .json(), inject _client_elapsed_ms via time.perf_counter). Streaming variants go through _payload(stream=True) -> _stream, a generator that opens self._http.stream('POST', ...), iterates r.iter_lines(), skips lines not starting with 'data:', strips the 'data:' prefix, breaks on '[DONE]', json.loads each chunk (silently skipping parse errors via continue), and on exit records self._last_stream_elapsed_ms. chat_stream is itself a generator that consumes _stream, yields ('thinking'|'content', delta) tuples to the caller, accumulates tool-call fragments by index, and returns the final tuple via StopIteration.value. _detect_model runs at construction only if config.MODEL is empty: GET /v1/models, read data['data'][0]['id'], else 'default'. There is NO retry loop and NO explicit error handling around _post/_stream beyond raise_for_status — exceptions propagate to callers (orchestrator/agents catch them, e.g. orchestrator.py:3327 turns an NPC stream exception into a tool_error). list_models and _detect_model are the only methods that swallow exceptions. think param: None -> no sampling/thinking fields at all; not-None -> runtime_settings.reasoning_enabled(think, role) decides effective_think, which selects SAMPLING_THINK vs SAMPLING_PLAIN.
externalDeps: httpx (sync Client + streaming; Timeout connect=10 read=None write=60 pool=None)
json, re, time (stdlib)

config module (BACKEND, API_BASE, LLAMA_HOST, API_KEY, MODEL, PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, PROMPT_CACHE_RETENTION, LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE, USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS, SAMPLING_THINK/PLAIN, COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS, ROLE_GM/NPC/COMPACT)
runtime_settings module (max_output_tokens, tool_choice_for_request, parallel_tool_calls_for_request, reasoning_enabled)
prompts module (GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM)
codex_client.CodexClient (lazy import only when BACKEND=='codex')
External services: llama.cpp server or any OpenAI-compatible /v1 endpoint at LLAMA_HOST/API_BASE (default http://localhost:8080)
invariants: PROMPT-CACHE PREFIX ORDERING: This module does NOT build the system/history prefix — it sends `messages` as given by agents._gm_request_messages (order: system gm_system, system gm_world_setup, optional system 'STORY SO FAR (compact): '+summary, then gm_messages). The faithful port must keep this append-only ordering so the llama.cpp KV/prompt cache prefix stays stable. _assistant_msg deliberately OMITS reasoning_content from history (comment lines 121-122) — feeding prior thoughts back would change the prefix and break cache reuse; this omission MUST be preserved.

prompt_cache_key: p['prompt_cache_key'] is sent ONLY if config.PROMPT_CACHE_KEY is non-empty (env GM_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, default ''). Off by default (comment: many OpenAI proxies reject unknown fields).
prompt_cache_retention: sent only if config.PROMPT_CACHE_RETENTION non-empty (env GM_PROMPT_CACHE_RETENTION, default '').

llama cache_reuse: p['n_cache_reuse'] = config.LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE sent ONLY when (think is not None) AND config.USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS AND config.LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE > 0. Default LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE=0 (env GM_LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE) => not sent. USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS defaults to (BACKEND=='llamacpp').

THINK / SAMPLING GATE: if think is None, NO sampling params and NO chat_template_kwargs are added. If think is not None: effective_think = runtime_settings.reasoning_enabled(think, reasoning_role) = bool(think) AND role_settings(role)['effort'] != 'none'. sampling = config.SAMPLING_THINK if effective_think else config.SAMPLING_PLAIN. SAMPLING_THINK = {temperature:0.6, top_p:0.95, top_k:20, min_p:0, presence_penalty:1.5}; SAMPLING_PLAIN = {temperature:0.7, top_p:0.80, top_k:20, min_p:0, presence_penalty:1.5}.

TEMPLATE-KWARGS BRANCH: if USE_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS: set chat_template_kwargs={'enable_thinking': bool(effective_think)}, spread ALL sampling fields (incl top_k/min_p), and optionally n_cache_reuse. ELSE (standard providers): copy ONLY temperature, top_p, presence_penalty from sampling (top_k/min_p/chat_template_kwargs dropped because external providers reject them).

MAX_TOKENS: p['max_tokens'] = runtime_settings.max_output_tokens() only if > 0. That value is clamped to config.MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP (default 200000) and seeded from config.MAX_TOKENS (env GM_MAX_TOKENS, default 0 => omitted).

TOOLS GATE: tools only added if truthy. tool_choice = runtime_settings.tool_choice_for_request(True) in {'auto','required','none'} (default 'auto'). parallel_tool_calls = runtime_settings.parallel_tool_calls_for_request(True) AND tool_choice != 'none'.
STREAM USAGE: when stream=True, p['stream_options']={'include_usage': True} so the final SSE chunk carries usage/timings.

MODEL AUTO-DETECTION: if config.MODEL is empty, _detect_model GETs /v1/models and uses data['data'][0]['id'], else 'default'. This means the lab follows whatever model the server has loaded (env GM_MODEL='' default).

TOOL-CALL ARGUMENTS ARE A JSON STRING: in the OpenAI/llama.cpp wire format function.arguments is a STRING. _parse_tool_calls decodes it via _loads -> dict (empty dict on failure). In streaming, fragments are concatenated by index then decoded. The raw string form is preserved in assistant_msg['tool_calls'] for re-sending.

_loads robustness: tries json.loads; on failure does re.search(r'\{.*\}', text, DOTALL) and parses the first brace span; returns {} if all fail. NPC free-JSON path relies on this for fence/garbage tolerance.

TEXT CLEANING: _clean strips chat-template tokens via _TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r'</?\|?[a-zA-Z_]+\|?>') and leading channel labels via _LEAD_CHANNEL_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*(thought|analysis|final|commentary)\b[\s:]*', re.I). _think strips </?think> tags. These guard against template-token leakage into player-visible text and MUST be ported.

STATS UNIT CONTRACT (Ollama-shaped _meta): _stats converts timings.prompt_ms/predicted_ms to nanoseconds (int(ms*1e6)); total_duration = (pm+em)*1e6; load_duration hardcoded 0; cached_tokens from usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens or input_tokens_details.cached_tokens. orchestrator divides these by 1e9. Field names prompt_eval_count/eval_count/prompt_eval_duration/eval_duration/total_duration/load_duration/cached_tokens are a fixed contract.

_remember elapsed fallback: if no server timings but elapsed_ms known, synthesizes timings={'prompt_ms':0,'predicted_ms':elapsed_ms} so predicted (eval) duration is approximated from client wall-clock.

COMPACT INPUT TRUNCATION: summarize sends text[:config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS] (default 12000 chars). The compaction prompt (prompts.GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM) forbids adding new names/clues, must preserve uncertainty (suspected vs established), and outputs English up to ~180 words. proper_nouns_line: if names present, 'Keep these proper nouns exactly as written... ' + comma-joined; else generic keep-proper-nouns instruction. This summarize is the SAME entrypoint used for both GM and NPC compaction (reasoning_role=config.ROLE_COMPACT).

HISTORY/COMPACTION THRESHOLDS (set in config, gated by orchestrator using CHARS_PER_TOKEN, but the values the port must honor): GM_HISTORY_TOKENS=100000, GM_KEEP_TURNS=3, NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS=64000, NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES=6, EVENTS_CAP=400, RUMORS_CAP=80, CHARS_PER_TOKEN=3 (token estimate heuristic). Comment (config lines 82-84): compaction is done LATE to keep append-only history and let prompt/KV cache reuse the identical prefix.

ROLE ENUM SINGLE SOURCE: config.ROLE_GM='gm', ROLE_NPC='npc', ROLE_COMPACT='compact'; default reasoning_role for all client methods is config.ROLE_GM. compact role defaults to effort/summary 'none' (reasoning off for compaction).

GM_THINK default True, NPC_THINK default False (config): GM reasons to plan tool-calls; NPC kept thinking-off for short JSON. The think arg passed by agents is True for gm_turn and False for gm_prelude_stream; NPC turns pass think driven by NPC settings.

NPC JSON FIELD NAMES (contract): reasoning, speech, action, claims (all required). orchestrator streams 'speech' live via extract_json_string(buf,'speech')+json_unescape. A faithful port must emit/parse exactly these keys.

DICE SEEDING / hidden-canon / secret isolation / thread-id stability / card_revision: NOT present in this module. Dice and RAG secret isolation live elsewhere (orchestrator/world/rag). card_revision is rendered in agents.npc_card_block, not here. The port should not invent these in the client layer.

chat_json `schema` param is accepted but never sent to the server — only the response_format={'type':'json_object'} fallback constrains output. The grammar/schema enforcement is conceptual, not wired into the request here.
platformConcerns: No subprocess/Popen, no browser-open, no ffmpeg/GPU in this file (those live in codex_client/server). Pure HTTP via httpx.
Network endpoint defaults to http://localhost:8080 (LLAMA_HOST). Cross-platform fine; ensure the Rust port reads the same env (LLAMA_HOST, GM_API_BASE, GM_API_KEY).
TLS only matters for external HTTPS providers (API_BASE). No curl_cffi / TLS impersonation here (that is a codex_client concern). Standard rustls/native-tls is sufficient.

httpx Timeout(connect=10, read=None, write=60, pool=None): read timeout is INFINITE (None) — important for long streaming generations. The Rust HTTP client must NOT impose a read/response timeout on the streaming path or it will cut off long generations; keep only a connect timeout.
SSE parsing is manual line-based ('data:' prefix, '[DONE]' sentinel) — no platform issue, but newline handling must tolerate both \n and \r\n from iter_lines.
config._load_dotenv reads .env from module dir and cwd (pathlib) — cross-platform; runtime_settings persists JSON via tempfile.mkstemp + os.replace (atomic rename) which is fine on Win/macOS/Linux.
rustNotes: Use reqwest with two client configs or per-request timeout overrides: a connect timeout (~10s) but NO total/read timeout for streaming. For non-streaming, a write/overall timeout (~60s) is acceptable; replicate httpx Timeout(connect=10, read=None, write=60).

Model the return of chat() as a struct ChatResult { thinking: String, content: String, calls: Vec<ParsedCall>, assistant_msg: AssistantMsg } rather than a 4-tuple, and chat_stream as an async Stream of an enum Delta::Thinking(String)|Delta::Content(String) plus a final ChatResult+Stats (e.g. return via the stream's terminal item or a separate awaited future). The generator-with-return-value idiom (StopIteration.value) maps to either a futures::Stream that ends with a final value, or returning (Stream, oneshot::Receiver<Final>).

Define a Backend trait with chat / chat_json / summarize / chat_stream / chat_json_stream / list_models / set_model / model so OpenAICompat, Mock, and Codex are interchangeable (mirrors the duck-typed Python interface). make_client returns Box<dyn Backend>.

Tool-call arguments are a JSON STRING on the wire: keep arguments as serde_json::Value parsed from a string for the public ParsedCall, but store the original string form in the history AssistantMsg.tool_calls so re-sent requests keep the byte-identical prefix (cache stability).

Reproduce _loads exactly: try serde_json::from_str; on failure, regex-extract the first balanced-ish {...} (DOTALL) and retry; return empty object on total failure. Use the regex crate with (?s) for DOTALL. Same for _TOKEN_RE/_LEAD_CHANNEL_RE/_think cleaning regexes.

Reproduce extract_json_string as a hand-rolled scanner over &str with the same escape handling (esc flag, stop at unescaped quote) and return (Option<String>, bool). json_unescape must replicate the limited replacement set (note: does NOT handle \uXXXX — keep that limitation for byte-faithfulness, or document the deviation if you add it).
Stats: use i64 nanoseconds; keep the Ollama-style field names in the serialized struct (serde rename) because orchestrator/UI read them. load_duration hardcoded 0.

_payload field gating is order-and-presence sensitive for cache stability — build the JSON object inserting keys in the same order Python does (serde_json::Map preserves insertion order with the preserve_order feature). Honor every conditional exactly (see invariants).

_detect_model and list_models swallow errors and fall back to a single-model list; everything else propagates errors (use Result and let callers map to tool errors, as orchestrator does). Do not add retries — the original has none.

Config/env parsing: replicate env names and defaults from config.py / runtime_settings.py precisely (GM_BACKEND, LLAMA_HOST default http://localhost:8080, GM_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, GM_LLAMA_CACHE_REUSE, GM_LLAMA_TEMPLATE_KWARGS default = backend==llamacpp, SAMPLING_THINK/PLAIN values, GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN=3, GM_COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS=12000, GM_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP=200000, GM_HISTORY_TOKENS=100000, GM_KEEP_TURNS=3, NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS=64000, NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES=6).
suggestedCrates: reqwest (with json + stream features) for HTTP/SSE
tokio (async runtime)
futures / async-stream (to model the yield-then-return generator pattern as a Stream + final value)
serde + serde_json (with preserve_order feature for stable key ordering)
regex (DOTALL token/channel cleaning and _loads brace extraction)
eventsource-stream or reqwest-eventsource (optional, for robust 'data:'/'[DONE]' SSE parsing; manual line parsing also fine)
thiserror / anyhow (error propagation matching propagate-not-retry behavior)
risks: chat_json's `schema` argument is dead at the wire level (never sent) — a porter might wrongly assume server-side JSON-schema/grammar enforcement. The only enforcement is response_format json_object on the fallback. Verify whether the real llama.cpp deployment relied on a grammar elsewhere; the docs mention a 'fallback grammar' but it is not in this file.
json_unescape does not handle \uXXXX or other escapes; partial NPC speech streamed via it could mis-render unicode escapes. Faithful port should match, but this is a latent bug worth flagging.

The generator/return-value duality (yield deltas, return final tuple via StopIteration.value) has no direct Rust equivalent; getting the final Stats/result delivery right is the trickiest part of the port.
read timeout = None (infinite) is essential for streaming; an over-eager Rust default timeout would silently truncate long generations.
_detect_model uses data['data'][0]['id'] with no guard beyond a broad except — assumes that exact response shape; non-llama.cpp providers may differ (handled loosely by returning 'default').

Dice seeding, hidden-canon/secret isolation, thread-id and prompt_cache_key stability across turns, append-only history enforcement, and card_revision are NOT implemented in this module — they are referenced by the task but live in orchestrator/world/world_mod/rag. Mapping those invariants requires those files; do not assume they are in the client.

config.py / runtime_settings.py were read for constants but the orchestrator's actual compaction trigger logic (how CHARS_PER_TOKEN and the *_TOKENS thresholds gate summarize) lives in orchestrator.py and was only sampled; exact trigger conditions should be confirmed when mapping that subsystem.

subsystem: Codex ChatGPT OAuth backend (PKCE auth flow + Responses API client)
purpose: Two modules that let GM-Lab talk to ChatGPT's internal "Codex" backend using a personal ChatGPT subscription instead of a paid OpenAI API key. codex_oauth.py implements the browser PKCE OAuth flow against auth.openai.com (loopback callback on port 1455/1457), stores the resulting credential as a local JSON file, and transparently refreshes it before expiry. codex_client.py adapts GM-Lab's small chat-completions-like interface (chat/chat_stream/chat_json/chat_json_stream/summarize) to the ChatGPT Codex Responses API at https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses: it splits messages into instructions+input items, converts GM-Lab tool schemas to OpenAI strict tools, sets reasoning effort/summary per role, sends a stable prompt_cache_key + thread-id, parses the streaming SSE event protocol (text deltas, reasoning deltas, function_call accumulation, usage), and normalizes everything back to GM-Lab's (thinking, content, tool_calls, assistant_msg, stats) shape.

publicSurface.name: CodexCredential
publicSurface.signature: @dataclass class CodexCredential(access_token: str, refresh_token: str, id_token: str|None=None, expires_at: int|None=None, account_id: str|None=None, credential_type: str='openai_codex_oauth')
publicSurface.behavior: In-memory credential record. expires_at is epoch MILLISECONDS. Has from_dict(data)->CodexCredential (classmethod) and to_dict()->dict (serializes with key 'type' for credential_type).

publicSurface.name: CodexCredential.from_dict
publicSurface.behavior: Builds credential from JSON dict; reads 'type' OR 'credential_type'; coerces empty strings to None; expires_at via _int_or_none.

publicSurface.name: CodexCredential.to_dict
publicSurface.behavior: Serializes to {type, access_token, refresh_token, id_token, expires_at, account_id}.

publicSurface.name: credential_path
publicSurface.behavior: Returns token file path. Precedence: env GM_CODEX_CREDENTIAL_PATH (expanduser) > %APPDATA%/gm-lab/codex-oauth.json (if APPDATA set, i.e. Windows) > ~/.config/gm-lab/codex-oauth.json (POSIX fallback).

publicSurface.name: load_credential
publicSurface.behavior: Returns None if file missing. Raises ValueError if not a JSON object or no access_token. Reads UTF-8.

publicSurface.name: save_credential
publicSurface.behavior: mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) on parent, writes JSON indent=2 ensure_ascii=False. NON-atomic write (unlike runtime_settings which uses tempfile).

publicSurface.name: delete_credential
publicSurface.behavior: Unlinks the credential file; swallows FileNotFoundError.

publicSurface.name: auth_status
publicSurface.behavior: UI-safe status (never exposes tokens). Returns {authenticated: bool, account_id, expires_at, message}. message is Russian text. On load exception returns authenticated False with the exception in message.

publicSurface.name: ensure_fresh_credential
publicSurface.behavior: Loads credential, raises RuntimeError (Russian) if unauthenticated; if near expiry (_is_near_expiry) and has refresh_token, calls refresh_credential + save_credential, else raises. Called on EVERY request by CodexClient._auth_headers. http is an optional shared httpx client.

publicSurface.name: refresh_credential
publicSurface.behavior: POSTs grant_type=refresh_token (+refresh_token, client_id) to token endpoint via _post_token. Keeps old refresh_token/id_token if response omits them; recomputes expires_at and account_id. Does NOT save (caller saves).

publicSurface.name: run_oauth
publicSurface.behavior: Full interactive PKCE flow: generates verifier+challenge+state, binds loopback callback server, builds authorize URL, optionally opens browser, blocks on single handle_request (server.timeout=300s), validates state, exchanges code (grant_type=authorization_code) for tokens, saves and returns credential.

publicSurface.name: revoke_credential
publicSurface.behavior: Loads credential; POSTs to ISSUER/oauth/revoke with {token, token_type_hint, [client_id if refresh]}; ALWAYS deletes the local file in finally. No-op if no credential.

publicSurface.name: CodexClient
publicSurface.behavior: Constructs httpx.Client (connect=10s, read=None i.e. unbounded for streaming, write=60s). Sets _responses_url, _models_url from config.CODEX_BASE_URL. _model = config.CODEX_MODEL or config.MODEL. Generates fresh uuid4 for _session_id, _thread_id, _installation_id. _turn_state='' and call_log=[].

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.model
publicSurface.behavior: Current model slug.

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.set_model
publicSurface.behavior: Sets _model if non-empty after strip.

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.session_id
publicSurface.behavior: Returns _session_id.

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.thread_id
publicSurface.behavior: Returns _thread_id (also the default prompt_cache_key and x-client-request-id).

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.set_session_identity
publicSurface.signature: def set_session_identity(self, session_id: str='', thread_id: str='') -> None
publicSurface.behavior: Overrides _session_id and/or _thread_id when non-empty. Used to make thread-id/cache-key stable across a saved game session.

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.list_models
publicSurface.behavior: GET models_url with param client_version, auth headers. Maps raw models to {id,slug,name,description,supported,visibility,priority,context_window,default_reasoning_level,default_reasoning_summary,supports_reasoning_summaries,supported_reasoning_levels,default_verbosity,support_verbosity}. Filters visibility!='list' unless slug==current model. Sorts by (not supported, -priority, name).

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.chat
publicSurface.signature: def chat(self, messages, tools=None, think=False, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM) -> tuple[str,str,list[dict],dict]
publicSurface.behavior: Non-streaming collect. Returns (thinking, content, tool_calls, assistant_msg). thinking/content stripped via _think/_clean.

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.chat_stream
publicSurface.signature: def chat_stream(self, messages, tools=None, think=False, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM) -> Generator
publicSurface.behavior: Generator yielding ('thinking'|'content', text) deltas; final return value (via StopIteration.value) is (thinking, content, tool_calls, assistant_msg, stats).

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.chat_json
publicSurface.signature: def chat_json(self, messages, schema, think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Structured output. think defaults True. Sets text.format json_schema with strict=False. Returns _loads(content) (robust JSON extraction).

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.chat_json_stream
publicSurface.signature: def chat_json_stream(self, messages, schema, think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM) -> Generator
publicSurface.behavior: Streams ONLY 'content' channel deltas; final return value is (_loads(content), stats). Suppresses thinking deltas to caller.

publicSurface.name: CodexClient.summarize
publicSurface.signature: def summarize(self, text: str, proper_nouns=None) -> str
publicSurface.behavior: History compaction. Builds messages from prompts.GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM (with proper_nouns_line) + user text truncated to config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS (12000). Calls self.chat(..., think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_COMPACT). Returns stripped content.

publicSurface.name: split_messages_for_responses
publicSurface.behavior: Module-level. Splits chat messages into (instructions string, input_items list) for the Responses API. system->joined instructions ('\n\n'.join); user->message input_text item; assistant text->message output_text item then its tool_calls->function_call items; tool->function_call_output {call_id, output}.

publicSurface.name: strict_schema_for_responses
publicSurface.behavior: Recursively converts a permissive JSON schema into OpenAI strict-tool form: additionalProperties=False, required=ALL property keys, optional (originally not-required) props made nullable via _nullable_schema. Recurses anyOf/oneOf/allOf/items/properties.

publicSurface.name: convert_tool_for_responses
publicSurface.behavior: Flattens {type:function,function:{...}} to Responses tool {type:function,name,description,parameters,strict}. strict defaults True; applies strict_schema_for_responses when strict, else deep-copies params.

publicSurface.name: extract_output_text
publicSurface.behavior: Joins all output[].content[] parts where type=='output_text'; falls back to response['output_text'].

publicSurface.name: extract_tool_calls
publicSurface.behavior: From final response.output items type=='function_call' -> [{id,name,arguments(parsed dict)}]; call_id falls back to id then f'responses_call_{index}'.

dataStructures.name: codex-oauth.json (token storage file)
dataStructures.fields: {type: str (default 'openai_codex_oauth'), access_token: str, refresh_token: str, id_token: str|null, expires_at: int|null (epoch MILLISECONDS), account_id: str|null}
dataStructures.notes: Plaintext JSON, indent=2, UTF-8. Location per-OS: env override GM_CODEX_CREDENTIAL_PATH > %APPDATA%/gm-lab/codex-oauth.json (Windows) > ~/.config/gm-lab/codex-oauth.json. Contains live OAuth secrets in cleartext (no encryption / no OS keychain).

dataStructures.name: auth_status() return
dataStructures.fields: {authenticated: bool, account_id: str|null, expires_at: int|null, message: str}
dataStructures.notes: UI contract. message strings are Russian. Never includes tokens. Crosses module boundary to web/UI layer.

dataStructures.name: OAuth token endpoint response (parsed JSON)
dataStructures.fields: {access_token, refresh_token, id_token, expires_in (SECONDS)}
dataStructures.notes: From auth.openai.com/oauth/token. expires_in is seconds; converted to epoch-ms via _expires_at = now_ms + expires_in*1000.

dataStructures.name: callback dict (set on HTTPServer instance)
dataStructures.fields: server.callback={code: str, state: str}; server.callback_error: str
dataStructures.notes: Loopback handler stashes parsed query onto the server object; run_oauth reads via getattr.

dataStructures.name: JWT claims (decoded, not stored)
dataStructures.fields: chatgpt_account_id at top-level OR under 'https://api.openai.com/auth'.chatgpt_account_id OR key 'https://api.openai.com/auth.chatgpt_account_id'
dataStructures.notes: _decode_jwt_claims base64url-decodes the JWT payload segment (no signature verification). account_id derived from id_token first, then access_token.

dataStructures.name: Responses API request payload
dataStructures.fields: {model, instructions, input:[items], tools:[converted], tool_choice, parallel_tool_calls: bool, store: False, stream: True, include:[], client_metadata:{application:'gm-lab', provider:'codex-oauth'}, prompt_cache_key, [reasoning:{effort,[summary]}], [text:{[verbosity],[format:{type:json_schema,name:'gm_lab_json',strict:False,schema}]}], [max_output_tokens]}
dataStructures.notes: store always False; stream always True. include becomes ['reasoning.encrypted_content'] when reasoning present.

dataStructures.name: Responses input item shapes
dataStructures.fields: message: {type:'message', role, content:[{type:'input_text'|'output_text', text}]}; function_call: {type:'function_call', call_id, name, arguments(JSON string)}; function_call_output: {type:'function_call_output', call_id, output}
dataStructures.notes: user->input_text, assistant->output_text; tool result role 'tool' maps to function_call_output keyed by tool_call_id.

dataStructures.name: Converted tool
dataStructures.fields: {type:'function', name, description, parameters(strict JSON schema), strict: bool}
dataStructures.notes: Note: flat name/description, NOT nested under 'function' (Responses API form, differs from Chat Completions).

dataStructures.name: GM-Lab tool_call (internal normalized)
dataStructures.fields: {id: str, name: str, arguments: dict}
dataStructures.notes: Output of _parse_tool_calls/extract_tool_calls. arguments always a dict (JSON-parsed).

dataStructures.name: raw_tool_call (Chat-Completions style, for assistant_msg)
dataStructures.fields: {id, type:'function', function:{name, arguments(JSON string)}}
dataStructures.notes: Produced by _raw_tool_calls; embedded in assistant_msg['tool_calls'] for append-only history.

dataStructures.name: assistant_msg
dataStructures.fields: {role:'assistant', content: str, [tool_calls: list[raw_tool_call]]}
dataStructures.notes: Returned to orchestrator to append to history.

dataStructures.name: usage stats (normalized to Ollama shape)
dataStructures.fields: {prompt_eval_count, eval_count, cached_tokens, prompt_eval_duration:0, eval_duration(ns), total_duration(ns), load_duration:0}
dataStructures.notes: _usage_stats maps Responses usage {input_tokens|prompt_tokens, output_tokens|completion_tokens, input_tokens_details|prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens} to GM-Lab's Ollama-like keys. elapsed_ms->ns.

dataStructures.name: call_log row
dataStructures.fields: {label: str, ...usage stats..., tokens: prompt_eval_count+eval_count}
dataStructures.notes: Appended per call by _remember; label is one of 'chat','chat_stream','chat_json','chat_json_stream'.

dataStructures.name: internal stream tool-call accumulator entry
dataStructures.fields: {output_index:int, item_id:str|None, id:str|None, name:str, arguments_raw:str}
dataStructures.notes: Built incrementally across SSE events; finish() sorts by output_index and parses arguments_raw via _loads.

controlFlow: OAuth flow (run_oauth): generate PKCE verifier (32 random bytes, urlsafe-b64 no padding) + S256 challenge + 32-byte state -> _bind_callback_server tries config.CODEX_AUTH_PORT (default 1455) then FALLBACK 1457 on 127.0.0.1 -> redirect_uri = http://localhost:{port}/auth/callback -> build authorize URL -> if config.CODEX_AUTO_OPEN_BROWSER open browser -> server.timeout=300s, server.handle_request() BLOCKS for exactly ONE request -> _CallbackHandler.do_GET parses /auth/callback query, stashes {code,state} or callback_error onto server, returns Russian HTML page -> server_close -> verify no error, callback present, state matches -> _post_token(authorization_code grant + code_verifier) -> build+save CodexCredential. Refresh (ensure_fresh_credential, on EVERY request): load -> if _is_near_expiry (expires_at <= now_ms + 5min) and refresh_token present -> refresh_credential (refresh_token grant) -> save. Responses request (CodexClient): chat*/summarize -> _payload (split messages, convert tools, compute tool_choice/parallel/reasoning/text/max_output_tokens) -> _collect_stream or _stream_generator -> _iter_events opens httpx.stream POST with SSE Accept; reads turn_state header; manually parses SSE: accumulates 'data:' lines, on blank line joins them, '[DONE]' breaks, else yields parsed JSON event -> _StreamAccumulator.handle dispatches by event.type: output_text.delta->content; reasoning_summary_text.delta/reasoning_text.delta->thinking; output_item.added/done + function_call_arguments.delta/done -> _ToolCallAccumulator; response.completed -> capture usage, fallback extract text/tool_calls, set done; response.failed/incomplete/error -> raise. finish() prefers streamed tool calls, falls back to completed_tool_calls. Generators return final tuple via StopIteration value.
externalDeps: auth.openai.com (OAuth authorize/token/revoke endpoints)
chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex (Responses + models endpoints, requires personal ChatGPT subscription)
httpx (HTTP/streaming client; created in both modules)
python stdlib http.server.HTTPServer/BaseHTTPRequestHandler (loopback OAuth callback)
webbrowser (opens system browser)
secrets, hashlib (sha256), base64 (PKCE)
config (CODEX_* constants, ROLE_* )
runtime_settings (reasoning/tool_choice/parallel/verbosity/max_output_tokens)
prompts (GM_COMPACT_SYSTEM)
env vars: GM_CODEX_CREDENTIAL_PATH, APPDATA, GM_CODEX_* (see config), GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN, GM_COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS
internalDeps: config (CODEX_CLIENT_ID, CODEX_BASE_URL, CODEX_MODEL, CODEX_CLIENT_VERSION, CODEX_ORIGINATOR, CODEX_USER_AGENT, CODEX_AUTH_PORT, CODEX_AUTO_OPEN_BROWSER, CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT, CODEX_REASONING_SUMMARY, ROLE_GM/NPC/COMPACT, COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS, MODEL)
invariants: PKCE: verifier = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(secrets.token_bytes(32)).rstrip('='); code_challenge = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(sha256(verifier.encode('ascii')).digest()).rstrip('='); code_challenge_method='S256'. state is a separate 32-byte urlsafe token and MUST match on callback (else 'OAuth state mismatch').

Authorize URL params (exact set, order via urlencode): response_type=code, client_id=config.CODEX_CLIENT_ID (default 'app_EMoamEEZ73f0CkXaXp7hrann'), redirect_uri, scope='openid profile email offline_access api.connectors.read api.connectors.invoke', code_challenge, code_challenge_method=S256, id_token_add_organizations='true', codex_cli_simplified_flow='true', state, originator=config.CODEX_ORIGINATOR ('codex_cli_rs'). Endpoint ISSUER='https://auth.openai.com', authorize at /oauth/authorize, token at /oauth/token, revoke at /oauth/revoke.

Callback path is EXACTLY '/auth/callback'; redirect host is 'localhost' but server binds '127.0.0.1'. Ports: preferred = config.CODEX_AUTH_PORT or DEFAULT_AUTH_PORT=1455, fallback=1457. OAUTH_TIMEOUT_SECS=300.0 (single handle_request). TOKEN_TIMEOUT_SECS=30.0 for token POSTs.

Token grants: token POST uses form-encoded (data=form) NOT JSON. authorization_code grant body: {grant_type, code, redirect_uri, client_id, code_verifier}. refresh_token grant body: {grant_type:'refresh_token', refresh_token, client_id}. _post_token requires response.is_success and dict JSON.

Expiry math: expires_at stored in MILLISECONDS = int(time.time()*1000) + expires_in*1000. REFRESH_MARGIN_MS = 5*60*1000 (5 minutes). _is_near_expiry: False if expires_at is None, else expires_at <= now_ms + 5min. If expires_at is None the token is treated as never-expiring (never auto-refreshed).

Refresh preserves secrets: new refresh_token defaults to old if absent; new id_token defaults to old if absent; account_id falls back to old. ensure_fresh_credential raises (Russian) if not authenticated; raises 'Codex OAuth token expired; reconnect Codex.' if near-expiry but refresh_token blank.

account_id (= ChatGPT-Account-Id header) is extracted by base64url-decoding the JWT payload (NO signature verification) from id_token FIRST then access_token; keys checked in order: 'chatgpt_account_id', then nested 'https://api.openai.com/auth'.chatgpt_account_id, then flat key 'https://api.openai.com/auth.chatgpt_account_id'.

Request headers (CodexClient._auth_headers, set on EVERY request): Authorization='Bearer '+access_token.strip(); originator=config.CODEX_ORIGINATOR; User-Agent=config.CODEX_USER_AGENT (default 'codex_cli_rs/0.133.0 (GM-Lab)'); version=config.CODEX_CLIENT_VERSION ('0.133.0'); session-id=_session_id; thread-id=_thread_id; x-client-request-id=_thread_id (SAME value as thread-id); x-codex-installation-id=_installation_id; +Accept:text/event-stream when streaming; +ChatGPT-Account-Id if account_id; +x-codex-turn-state if _turn_state non-empty.

Turn-state round-trip: server returns 'x-codex-turn-state' response header on the stream; stored in self._turn_state and echoed back on the next request as request header 'x-codex-turn-state'. Must be preserved across turns for conversation continuity.

prompt_cache_key = config.CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY or self._thread_id. config.CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY defaults to config.PROMPT_CACHE_KEY (env GM_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY, default ''). So when unset, the cache key IS the per-CodexClient thread_id (a fresh uuid4 per process unless set_session_identity overrides). STABILITY of thread_id == stability of prompt-cache reuse; persist/restore thread_id with the game session via set_session_identity.

session_id, thread_id, installation_id are each str(uuid.uuid4()) generated once in __init__. set_session_identity only overrides session_id/thread_id when non-empty; installation_id is never overridable.
payload constants always: store=False, stream=True. include=[] unless reasoning present then ['reasoning.encrypted_content']. client_metadata={application:'gm-lab', provider:'codex-oauth'}.

Reasoning (runtime_settings.reasoning_for_request): returns None if think falsy OR role effort=='none'; else {effort, [summary if summary!='none']}. Valid efforts: ('none','minimal','low','medium','high','xhigh'). Valid summaries: ('auto','concise','detailed','none'). Per-role base: GM and NPC inherit CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT (default 'low') + CODEX_REASONING_SUMMARY (default 'auto'); COMPACT defaults to no reasoning. role_settings forces summary='none' whenever effort=='none'.

Role usage: chat/chat_stream/chat_json default reasoning_role=config.ROLE_GM='gm'; summarize uses config.ROLE_COMPACT='compact'. chat/chat_stream default think=False; chat_json/chat_json_stream default think=True. NPC role string is 'npc'.

tool_choice (runtime_settings.tool_choice_for_request): 'none' if no tools; else settings tool_choice in ('auto','required','none') default 'auto'. parallel_tool_calls = parallel_tool_calls_for_request(has_tools) AND tool_choice!='none' (i.e. only when tools present, default GM_PARALLEL_TOOL_CALLS=True).

Strict tool conversion (strict_schema_for_responses): for any object schema sets additionalProperties=False and required = ALL property keys (every property listed); properties NOT in the original required are wrapped nullable via _nullable_schema (type list gains 'null', or anyOf with {type:null}, enums gain None). Recurses into anyOf/oneOf/allOf/items/properties. convert_tool_for_responses: strict defaults True; only strict tools get this transform.

JSON structured output: text.format = {type:'json_schema', name:'gm_lab_json', strict:False, schema} (NOTE strict:False here, unlike tools). text.verbosity only set when settings text_verbosity != 'default' (valid: default/low/medium/high). max_output_tokens only added when >0 (capped at config.MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP elsewhere; settings key from config.MAX_TOKENS).

SSE parse contract (manual, NOT a library): only lines starting 'data:' collected (slice [5:].strip()); blank line flushes accumulated data lines joined by '\n'; payload '[DONE]' breaks. Event dispatch by 'type': response.output_text.delta(delta)->content; response.reasoning_summary_text.delta / response.reasoning_text.delta(delta)->thinking; response.output_item.added/done(item)->tool accumulator; response.function_call_arguments.delta(delta)/.done->tool accumulator; response.completed(response.usage,fallback output)->done; response.failed/response.incomplete/error->raise _event_error_message.

Tool-call streaming reassembly: keyed by output_index AND item_id/id; arguments accumulated as string 'arguments_raw' then parsed by _loads at finish(); calls sorted by output_index; id fallback chain call_id -> id -> item_id -> f'responses_call_{output_index}'. finish() prefers streamed calls, else completed_tool_calls from response.completed.
Robust JSON parse _loads: json.loads first; on failure regex r'\{.*\}' DOTALL to grab first/largest object; returns {} if not a dict. _think strips </?think> tags; _clean strips whitespace.

Token estimation lives in config not these files: CHARS_PER_TOKEN=int(GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN, default 3). summarize truncates input to config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS=12000 chars. Actual token usage for Codex comes from response.usage (input_tokens/output_tokens/cached_tokens), normalized to Ollama keys; the CHARS_PER_TOKEN heuristic is only used by other GM-Lab modules for pre-send estimation.

Secret isolation: auth_status() is the ONLY status surface exposed to UI and never returns tokens; tokens live only in the JSON file and in-memory CodexCredential. _redacted_provider_error truncates provider error bodies to 2000 chars (does not redact tokens but caps size).
Models filtering/sort: visibility default 'list'; non-'list' models dropped unless slug==current model. Sort key (not supported, -priority, name).
platformConcerns: Token storage path is OS-branched: %APPDATA%/gm-lab/codex-oauth.json on Windows vs ~/.config/gm-lab/codex-oauth.json on macOS/Linux. The Rust port should use a proper per-OS dir (e.g. directories/dirs crate: config_dir) and ideally honor GM_CODEX_CREDENTIAL_PATH override. Current Python does NOT use macOS ~/Library/Application Support — it falls into the ~/.config branch on mac (APPDATA unset). A faithful port should keep this exact fallback OR document the intentional change.

Loopback OAuth callback HTTP server binds 127.0.0.1 on port 1455 (fallback 1457). Must handle port-in-use (OSError) by trying fallback, then erroring. Browser is opened via webbrowser.open (maps to ShellExecute / open / xdg-open). redirect_uri uses host 'localhost' — must resolve to the bound 127.0.0.1.
webbrowser.open is best-effort and can fail silently; gated by CODEX_AUTO_OPEN_BROWSER. Rust port should print the URL as fallback so headless/remote users can copy it.

No TLS impersonation / curl_cffi / Cloudflare bypass is used here — plain httpx over standard TLS. (Despite the focus hint, this code does NOT use curl_cffi.) The Codex backend is reached with normal headers spoofing the codex_cli_rs client (originator/User-Agent/version), not via JA3/TLS fingerprint impersonation. A Rust port can use reqwest with rustls directly; if the backend later starts JA3-fingerprinting, that would require an impersonation layer (not present today).

Streaming uses httpx read-timeout=None (unbounded) — Rust must use a streaming body reader with no overall read timeout (only connect/idle). The SSE parsing is hand-rolled, not via an SSE library; the byte/line semantics (data: prefix, blank-line dispatch, [DONE]) must be reproduced exactly.
File write in save_credential is non-atomic (plain open/write); on Windows a crash mid-write could corrupt. Rust port should write atomically (temp file + rename), as runtime_settings already does.
Credential file stores OAuth secrets in plaintext with no permission hardening (no chmod 600 on POSIX, no DPAPI on Windows). Port should consider OS keychain or at least restrictive file perms.
rustNotes: Model CodexCredential as a serde struct; serialize key is 'type' (use #[serde(rename="type")] on credential_type) and accept both 'type' and 'credential_type' on read (serde alias). expires_at is i64 milliseconds, Option. None expires_at => never refresh.

PKCE: verifier = base64::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD of 32 rand bytes (use rand or getrandom); challenge = base64 url-safe no-pad of Sha256(verifier ascii). state = separate 32-byte url-safe no-pad token. Compare state with constant-time eq ideally.

Loopback server: use tiny_http or hyper to serve exactly one GET on 127.0.0.1:1455 (fallback 1457), parse /auth/callback?code&state&error, return the same Russian HTML pages, then shut down. Apply a 300s accept timeout. Bind with SO error handling to try the fallback port.

HTTP: reqwest (rustls, async or blocking). Token endpoint posts are form-urlencoded (.form(&map)); Responses endpoint posts JSON with stream. Set per-request headers exactly (Authorization, originator, User-Agent, version, session-id, thread-id, x-client-request-id==thread-id, x-codex-installation-id, Accept for SSE, ChatGPT-Account-Id, x-codex-turn-state).

JWT decode for account_id: do NOT verify signature — just base64url-decode the middle segment (pad to %4) and json-parse; check the three key locations in order. A small helper, jsonwebtoken is overkill (no verification wanted).

SSE: implement the hand-rolled line parser (don't assume an off-the-shelf SSE crate matches the 'collect data: lines, dispatch on blank line, [DONE] sentinel' behavior). Use reqwest bytes_stream + a line buffer. Capture x-codex-turn-state response header before consuming the body and store it on the client for the next turn.

Strict-schema transform: implement strict_schema_for_responses over serde_json::Value recursively — set additionalProperties=false, required=all property names, wrap non-required props as nullable (extend type with 'null' / anyOf with {type:null} / enum + null). Keep the json_schema text.format strict=false vs tools strict=true distinction.

Stable identity: store thread_id (and optionally session_id) in the persisted game session and feed via an equivalent of set_session_identity so prompt_cache_key stays stable across process restarts. installation_id can be a per-install persisted uuid rather than per-process to better match real Codex clients (current code regenerates it per process — confirm desired behavior with maintainer).
Tool-call accumulator: replicate the (output_index, item_id/id) keying and arguments_raw string concatenation, then JSON-parse at finish, sorting by output_index, with the exact id fallback chain.

Refresh-on-demand: replicate ensure_fresh_credential being invoked inside header-building for every request; guard with the 5-minute REFRESH_MARGIN_MS. Persist the refreshed credential immediately. Consider a mutex so concurrent requests don't double-refresh.
Robust JSON extraction (_loads) and tag stripping (_think) are needed for non-strict model outputs — port the regex {.*} DOTALL fallback and </?think> removal.
suggestedCrates: reqwest (with rustls-tls + stream features)
serde + serde_json
tokio (async runtime for streaming)
tiny_http or hyper (loopback OAuth callback server)
url (build/parse authorize URL and callback query)
base64
sha2
rand / getrandom (PKCE + uuid entropy)
uuid (session/thread/installation ids)
directories or dirs (per-OS config path)
webbrowser (open system browser)
futures-util (StreamExt for bytes_stream)
anyhow/thiserror (error handling)
risks: The credential file is plaintext OAuth secrets with no encryption or file-permission hardening; a faithful port inherits this risk. Recommend (but flag with maintainer) tightening perms / keychain — a behavior change.

macOS path: Python falls back to ~/.config (not ~/Library/Application Support) because APPDATA is unset on mac. Unclear whether this is intentional; a 'correct' Rust dirs::config_dir would change the mac path and orphan existing credentials. Confirm before changing.
save_credential is non-atomic — potential corruption on crash; the OAuth module (unlike runtime_settings) does not use a temp-file rename.

installation_id is regenerated every process (uuid4 in __init__), so it is NOT a stable installation identity despite the name; if the Codex backend ever correlates installation_id across sessions this could look anomalous. Verify intended semantics.

thread_id/prompt_cache_key default to a fresh per-process uuid; unless set_session_identity is called from the session-restore path, prompt caching is effectively per-process only. The exact persistence/restore call site is in another module (orchestrator) not shown here — must trace it to port cache stability faithfully.

No TLS impersonation today; if ChatGPT's Codex backend adds Cloudflare JA3 fingerprint checks, plain reqwest/httpx may be blocked and an impersonation layer would be needed (currently absent — do not pre-build it without evidence).

The exact Responses SSE event-type set is inferred from handle(); other event types (e.g. response.created, response.in_progress, content_part.added) are silently ignored. If the backend changes event names for text/reasoning deltas, streaming would silently produce empty output. Brittle to API drift.

client_version, originator, User-Agent are spoofed to a specific Codex CLI version (0.133.0 / codex_cli_rs). These may need updating over time; the backend could reject stale versions. ToS/risk consideration: this impersonates the official Codex client against a personal ChatGPT account.

_post_token/refresh do not surface the provider error body (only status code), making refresh failures hard to debug; consider including body like _redacted_provider_error does for the responses path.
Token rotation: refresh response may or may not return a new refresh_token; code keeps the old one if absent — must replicate exactly to avoid invalidating sessions.

subsystem: RAG (hybrid retrieval: OpenAI-compatible embeddings client + SQLite embedding cache + dense/BM25/RRF reranker)
files: E:/gemma/gm-lab/world.py (corpus builders + consumer: retrieval_documents/state_record_documents lines 1155-1499, fact() lines 2618-2634)
purpose: RAG is the accuracy layer for the GM's "world fact" lookup tool. It embeds an actor-safe corpus of world-memory documents (public facts, current scene, scene items, NPC public info + whereabouts, player-witnessed testimony/rumors) via a local OpenAI-compatible /v1/embeddings server, caches the float32 vectors in a global content-addressed SQLite table (base64-encoded), and ranks documents against a GM query using a hybrid of cosine-dense + BM25-keyword scores fused by Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with small linear tiebreaks and a status boost. It deliberately NEVER indexes hidden canon, GM "truth" facts, or NPC private secrets. It is wrapped in try/except at the call site so the game still runs if the embeddings server is down.

publicSurface.name: RagDocument
publicSurface.signature: @dataclass(frozen=True) class RagDocument(doc_id: str, kind: str, text: str, status: str = "known", source: str = "", visibility: str = "player", tags: tuple[str, ...] = (), metadata: dict[str, Any] = {})
publicSurface.behavior: Immutable corpus document. Built by world.py (state_record_documents + retrieval_documents). visibility is set but NOT used for filtering inside rag.py — filtering happens upstream in world.py.

publicSurface.name: RagDocument.contextual_text
publicSurface.behavior:
Returns the text actually embedded AND tokenized for BM25: a metadata header ('RPG world memory block.
Kind: {kind}.
Status: {status}.' + optional Source/Tags lines) then '
Text: ' + self.text.strip(). Both dense and keyword channels use this exact string, so the contextual prefix must be reproduced byte-for-byte in Rust.

publicSurface.name: RagHit
publicSurface.signature: @dataclass(frozen=True) class RagHit(document: RagDocument, score: float, dense_score: float, keyword_score: float)
publicSurface.behavior: One ranked result. score = fused RRF+tiebreak+boost final; dense_score = raw cosine; keyword_score = raw BM25.

publicSurface.name: EmbeddingCache
publicSurface.behavior: SQLite-backed content-addressed vector cache (table `embeddings`). Constructor stringifies path via Path() and calls _init_db (mkdir parents + CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS).

publicSurface.name: EmbeddingCache.get_many
publicSurface.signature: def get_many(self, model: str, texts: list[str]) -> dict[str, list[float]]
publicSurface.behavior: Looks up cached vectors keyed by (model, sha256(text)). Returns {text_hash: normalized_vector}. NOTE: it hashes the texts as passed in (does NOT strip here); LocalEmbeddingClient strips before calling.

publicSurface.name: EmbeddingCache.put_many
publicSurface.signature: def put_many(self, model: str, rows: list[tuple[str, list[float]]]) -> None
publicSurface.behavior: INSERT OR REPLACE rows (model, sha256(text), text, len(vec), base64(float32 bytes), time.time()). No-op on empty.

publicSurface.name: EmbeddingCache.delete_by_text_hashes
publicSurface.behavior: Deletes cached vectors for given content hashes across ALL models, chunked at 400 per IN(...) to stay under SQLite bound-var limit. Returns total rows removed.

publicSurface.name: LocalEmbeddingClient
publicSurface.behavior: Production embedder. Reads url/model/encoding_format/batch_size/timeout from config and owns an EmbeddingCache. Default embedder of RagEngine.

publicSurface.name: LocalEmbeddingClient.embed
publicSurface.behavior: Strips each text, checks cache by sha256(stripped), POSTs only cache-misses to the embeddings server in batches, decodes+normalizes vectors, writes them back to cache, returns vectors aligned to the ORIGINAL input order (one per input text).

publicSurface.name: HashEmbeddingClient
publicSurface.behavior: Deterministic offline/test embedder. blake2b(token, digest_size=8) hashed into a dims-length signed bag-of-words vector, normalized. Used by tests via set_default_engine.

publicSurface.name: HashEmbeddingClient.embed
publicSurface.behavior: Returns normalized hash-bucket vectors; no network, no cache.

publicSurface.name: RagEngine
publicSurface.behavior: Orchestrates a single search. embedder defaults to LocalEmbeddingClient(). Duck-typed: any object with .embed(list[str])->list[list[float]] works.

publicSurface.name: RagEngine.search
publicSurface.signature: def search(self, query: str, documents: list[RagDocument], top_k: int | None = None) -> list[RagHit]
publicSurface.behavior: Filters out blank-text docs; returns [] if query blank or no docs. Embeds [query_instruction]+[doc.contextual_text() for docs] in ONE embed() call (query is element 0). Computes dense cosine (dot of normalized vecs), BM25 keyword scores, RRF fusion + tiebreaks + status boost, sorts, applies min-dense/keyword filter, returns top_k.

publicSurface.name: default_engine
publicSurface.behavior: Lazily creates and returns a module-global singleton RagEngine (_DEFAULT_ENGINE).

publicSurface.name: set_default_engine
publicSurface.behavior: Replaces the global engine (tests inject HashEmbeddingClient engine; pass None to reset). Mutable global state.

publicSurface.name: retrieve_world_fact
publicSurface.signature: def retrieve_world_fact(query: str, documents: list[RagDocument]) -> dict | None
publicSurface.behavior: Top-level entrypoint used by world.fact(). Returns None if RAG disabled or no hits. Else takes top RAG_FACT_SELECT_K hits, builds {status, text, sources}. status='known' iff ALL selected docs have status in _GOOD_STATUS else 'unknown'. text is numbered lines '[i] {known|unconfirmed}: {doc.text}'. sources is a list of per-hit dicts.

publicSurface.name: purge_embeddings_for_texts
publicSurface.behavior: Best-effort delete of cached vectors for given raw texts (strips, sha256, delete across models). No-op if RAG disabled or no texts. NEVER raises (swallows all exceptions, returns 0). Called by dialog_store on chat delete.

publicSurface.name: docs_debug_json
publicSurface.behavior: json.dumps([doc.__dict__ ...], ensure_ascii=False, indent=2). Debug-only dump of corpus including metadata; not used in production paths.

dataStructures.name: SQLite table `embeddings`
dataStructures.fields: model TEXT NOT NULL, text_hash TEXT NOT NULL, text TEXT NOT NULL, dims INTEGER NOT NULL, vector_b64 TEXT NOT NULL, created_at REAL NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (model, text_hash)
dataStructures.notes: Content-addressed, GLOBAL (shared across all chats/threads), keyed by (model, sha256(stripped_text)). vector_b64 = base64(little-endian float32 array bytes) of the NORMALIZED vector AS RETURNED — note: get_many normalizes again on read via _decode_embedding, so stored vectors are already unit-normalized and re-normalized on load (idempotent). dims = len(vec). created_at = time.time() (Unix epoch float). DB opened with PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, timeout=10. Default path config.RAG_CACHE_PATH = <module dir>/gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3.

dataStructures.name: RagDocument (cross-module dict-like contract)
dataStructures.fields: doc_id, kind, text, status, source, visibility, tags(tuple), metadata(dict)
dataStructures.notes: Constructed in world.py. doc_id namespaced by prefix: 'state:<record_id>', 'fact:<fact_id>', 'scene:<scene_id>', 'scene_item:<scene_id>:<item_id>', 'npc_public:<npc_id>', 'npc_whereabouts:<npc_id>', 'testimony:<seq>'. status values seen: 'known','current','present','unconfirmed', plus record.status passthrough. kind values: state_<kind>, public_fact, claim, scene_state, scene_item, npc_public, npc_whereabouts, testimony.
