dataStructures.name: retrieve_world_fact return dict
dataStructures.fields: { status: 'known'|'unknown', text: str, sources: list[dict] }
dataStructures.notes: text = space-joined numbered lines '[i] known|unconfirmed: <doc.text>'. Consumed by world.fact() into WorldFact(status, text, sources).

dataStructures.name: source dict (one per selected hit)
dataStructures.fields: { n: int(1-based), doc_id: str, kind: str, status: str, source: str, score: round(final,5), dense: round(dense_score,5), keyword: round(keyword_score,5), metadata: dict }
dataStructures.notes: Scores rounded to 5 decimals. metadata is the RagDocument.metadata (e.g. fact_id/record_id/npc_id/scene_id/location_id/seq/turn/speaker/witnesses/confirmed...).

dataStructures.name: embeddings POST request body
dataStructures.fields: { model: str, input: list[str] (one batch), encoding_format: str (default 'base64') }
dataStructures.notes: POST to RAG_EMBEDDINGS_URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/embeddings). httpx.post with timeout=RAG_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, raise_for_status().

dataStructures.name: embeddings POST response body
dataStructures.fields: { data: [ { index: int, embedding: str(base64) | list[float] }, ... ] }
dataStructures.notes: Parsed via response.json().get('data') or []. Each item decoded by index (falls back to enumeration idx if 'index' missing). embedding may be base64 string OR raw float list — _decode_embedding handles both.

controlFlow: Single synchronous request/response per search; no async, no streaming, no generators. Entrypoint world.fact(query, actor_id) -> retrieve_world_fact(query, documents) -> default_engine().search(...). search() flow: (1) drop blank-text docs; early-return [] if query blank or docs empty; (2) build embed input list = [_query_instruction(query)] + [doc.contextual_text() for doc in docs] and call embedder.embed() ONCE — query is index 0, docs follow; (3) dense_scores[i] = sum(q*d) over zipped normalized vectors (cosine since both unit-normalized); (4) keyword_scores = _bm25_scores(query, docs) over doc.contextual_text() tokens; (5) dense_rank/keyword_rank = _rank_map (1-based ranks among positive scores only); (6) for each doc compute final RRF+tiebreak+boost; (7) sort by (score, dense_score, keyword_score) desc; (8) filter to hits where keyword_score>0 OR dense_score>=RAG_MIN_DENSE_SCORE; (9) return filtered[:top_k]. LocalEmbeddingClient.embed flow: strip texts -> cache.get_many -> collect misses -> for each batch of RAG_BATCH_SIZE: POST, parse data, decode by index, write cache.put_many -> reassemble output in original order. Cache writes happen per-batch. The whole RAG path in world.fact() is wrapped in try/except that swallows everything and falls through to a non-RAG keyword fallback — RAG failure is non-fatal.
externalDeps: Local OpenAI-compatible embeddings HTTP server at GM_RAG_EMBEDDINGS_URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/embeddings) — in this project a llama.cpp/qwen3-embedding-4b-q4 server
httpx (sync httpx.post)
sqlite3 (stdlib) with WAL
stdlib: array, base64, hashlib (sha256, blake2b), math, json, time, re (imported lazily via __import__('re')), dataclasses, pathlib

Env vars: GM_RAG_ENABLED, GM_RAG_EMBEDDINGS_URL, GM_RAG_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL (default qwen3-embedding-4b-q4), GM_RAG_ENCODING_FORMAT (default base64), GM_RAG_CACHE_PATH, GM_RAG_BATCH_SIZE (16), GM_RAG_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (20), GM_RAG_TOP_K (6), GM_RAG_MIN_DENSE_SCORE (0.60), GM_RAG_RRF_K (60), GM_RAG_KEYWORD_TIEBREAK (0.002), GM_RAG_DENSE_TIEBREAK (0.001), GM_RAG_STATUS_BOOST (1.04), GM_RAG_FACT_SELECT_K (4), GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN (3, defined in config but NOT used in rag.py)
Embeddings SQLite DB file on disk (default gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 beside the module)
internalDeps: world.py imports rag.RagDocument, rag.retrieve_world_fact (consumer of the corpus + fact lookup)
tests: test_rag.py uses set_default_engine/RagEngine/HashEmbeddingClient; test_chat_delete.py uses purge_embeddings_for_texts
invariants: SECRET/HIDDEN-CANON ISOLATION (highest value): The RAG corpus is built ONLY in world.retrieval_documents()/state_record_documents() and is actor-safe by construction. fact_records with kind=='truth' are SKIPPED ('if record.kind == "truth": continue'). Rumors are included ONLY if 'player' in rumor.witnesses. NPC whereabouts included only if where.status != 'unknown'. visibility field on RagDocument exists but rag.py does NOT filter on it — the isolation guarantee lives entirely in the corpus builder, NOT in rag.py. A faithful port must keep truth/secrets out of the corpus upstream; rag.py trusts its input.

EMBED INPUT ORDERING: search() embeds exactly [query_instruction] ++ [doc.contextual_text() for doc in docs] in ONE call; vectors[0] is the query, vectors[1:] are docs in the same order as the filtered `docs` list. Any reordering breaks dense scoring.

_query_instruction() text is fixed and must be byte-identical (it is an instruct-style prefix the embedding model expects): 'Instruct: Given a game master's query, retrieve relevant public world facts, current scene facts, known NPC whereabouts, evidence, and unconfirmed witness statements for a tabletop RPG. Do not retrieve hidden canon or private secrets.\nQuery: ' + query.strip(). The embedded query asymmetric to docs.

contextual_text() format is the embedded+tokenized unit and must match exactly: 'RPG world memory block.
Kind: {kind}.
Status: {status}.' then optional 'Source: {source}.' and 'Tags: a, b, c.' lines joined by '
', then '
Text: ' + text.strip().

RRF + TIEBREAK + BOOST math (quote exactly): RAG_RRF_K=60. For each doc idx: final=0.0; if in dense_rank: final += 1.0/(RAG_RRF_K + dense_rank[idx]); if in keyword_rank: final += 1.0/(RAG_RRF_K + keyword_rank[idx]); if max_keyword>0: final += RAG_KEYWORD_TIEBREAK(0.002) * (keyword_scores[idx]/max_keyword); final += RAG_DENSE_TIEBREAK(0.001) * max(0.0, dense_scores[idx]); if doc.status in _GOOD_STATUS: final *= RAG_STATUS_BOOST(1.04). Ranks are 1-based.

_rank_map: only scores STRICTLY > 0 are ranked; sorted desc by score; returns {orig_idx: rank} with rank = position+1 (1-based). Docs with score<=0 are absent from the map (so contribute 0 to that channel's RRF term). This applies to BOTH dense and keyword channels — a doc with non-positive dense cosine gets no dense RRF term.
FINAL SORT key: hits.sort(key=lambda h:(h.score, h.dense_score, h.keyword_score), reverse=True) — stable Python sort, descending lexicographic on the 3-tuple.
POST-RANK FILTER: keep hit iff hit.keyword_score>0 OR hit.dense_score>=RAG_MIN_DENSE_SCORE(0.60). Then truncate to top_k (default RAG_TOP_K=6).

fact selection: retrieve_world_fact takes hits[:RAG_FACT_SELECT_K] (default 4). Overall status='known' iff ALL selected docs.status in _GOOD_STATUS, else 'unknown'. Per-line label = 'known' if doc.status in _GOOD_STATUS else 'unconfirmed'.
_GOOD_STATUS = ('known','current','present') — exact tuple; used for status boost, overall status, and per-line label.

CACHE KEY = sha256(text) where text is the STRIPPED text. LocalEmbeddingClient.embed strips before _sha; purge_embeddings_for_texts strips before _sha (comment: 'Match EmbeddingCache.embed() normalization'). EmbeddingCache.get_many/put_many themselves do NOT strip — callers must pre-strip. PRIMARY KEY (model, text_hash): cache is per-model; delete_by_text_hashes ignores model (deletes across all models).

VECTOR ENCODING: array('f') => little-endian float32 => raw bytes => base64 ascii. _decode_embedding: base64.b64decode -> array('f').frombytes -> normalize. Vectors are L2-normalized on BOTH encode-time (vectors come pre-normalized from embed) and decode-time (_decode_embedding normalizes). _normalize returns input unchanged if norm==0.

BM25 params fixed: k1=1.5, b=0.75. idf = log(1 + (n_docs - df + 0.5)/(df + 0.5)). denom = tf + k1*(1 - b + b*dl/max(avgdl,1)). score += idf * (tf*(k1+1))/denom. avgdl = total terms / max(1,len(docs)). dl = len(terms) or 1. Tokens come from doc.contextual_text() (includes the Kind/Status/Source/Tags header), NOT just doc.text — header words participate in BM25.

TOKENIZER (_tokens): regex r'[a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ0-9_«»\-]+' on text.lower(); each match stripped of '«»-.' then lowered; kept iff len>=3 AND not in _STOPWORDS. _STOPWORDS is a fixed bilingual EN/RU set (24 listed). Must reproduce regex, min-length 3, strip chars, and stopword list exactly for BM25 parity.
RAG_ENABLED gate: retrieve_world_fact returns None and purge_embeddings_for_texts returns 0 when config.RAG_ENABLED is false.
purge_embeddings_for_texts NEVER raises (try/except Exception: return 0). world.fact()'s RAG block is also fully try/excepted — RAG is 'an accuracy layer, not a hard dependency'.
delete_by_text_hashes chunk size = 400 ('stay under SQLite's bound-var limit').

NOTE: CHARS_PER_TOKEN / token-estimation heuristics, prompt-cache prefix ordering, KV-cache reuse, llama cache_reuse, history/NPC compaction thresholds, deterministic dice seeding, thread-id/prompt_cache_key stability, append-only history, card_revision, and NPC JSON contract (reasoning/speech/action/claims) are NOT present in rag.py — they live in other modules. rag.py's only stability contract is the global content-addressed embedding cache key (model, sha256(stripped text)).
platformConcerns: SQLite DB path: default config.RAG_CACHE_PATH = Path(__file__).with_name('gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3') — beside the module. In Rust+Tauri this should map to an app-data dir, not next to the binary. Path() is used to normalize; parent dirs created with mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True).
WAL mode (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL) creates -wal/-shap sidecar files; works cross-platform but watch file-locking semantics; timeout=10s for busy DB.
HTTP to 127.0.0.1:8080 — plain HTTP, no TLS, no auth. No curl_cffi/TLS impersonation here (unlike the LLM client elsewhere). Just httpx.post.
No subprocess/Popen, no browser opening, no ffmpeg, no GPU code in this module.

float32 endianness: array('f') is native-endian; on all target platforms (x86/ARM Windows/macOS/Linux) this is little-endian, so cached base64 vectors are portable in practice, but a Rust port should explicitly use little-endian f32 to be safe and to read existing caches.

Unicode/Cyrillic: tokenizer regex and stopwords include Cyrillic; ensure UTF-8 handling and case-folding parity (Python str.lower() vs Rust to_lowercase() differ on some edge chars — Cyrillic ё/Ё handled fine).
rustNotes: Define RagDocument as a struct; contextual_text() must produce the exact same string (use \n joins, trim text). BM25 and dense both consume this string.

Embed client: reqwest (blocking or async) POST JSON {model, input, encoding_format}; deserialize {data:[{index, embedding}]}. embedding field is untagged enum: String(base64) | Vec<f32>. Decode base64 -> bytes -> little-endian f32 via byteorder/bytemuck; then L2-normalize. Preserve input ordering by sorting/placing on item.index (fallback to enumeration index).

Embedding cache: rusqlite with the identical schema and PRAGMAs (journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=10s). Key = (model, hex sha256 of stripped text). Store vector as base64 of little-endian f32 bytes. Reuse the EXISTING gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 to avoid re-embedding — schema must match byte-for-byte including base64 little-endian f32.

RRF/tiebreak/boost: port the formula verbatim with f64 arithmetic; replicate 1-based ranks and the 'score>0 only' rank-map rule. Use a stable sort with the (score,dense,keyword) desc key — Rust's sort_by is stable; sort by Ordering reversed.
BM25: replicate k1=1.5,b=0.75, the idf/denom formulas, and that scoring is over contextual_text() tokens. Watch integer/float: n_docs, df are ints but formula uses 0.5 offsets — use f64.

Tokenizer: port the regex (regex crate, pattern [a-zA-Zа-яА-ЯёЁ0-9_«»\-]+), lowercase, strip leading/trailing «»-. , min len 3, exclude the exact _STOPWORDS set (store as a HashSet<&str>). Lowercasing must be Unicode-aware.
_normalize: return as-is when norm==0 to avoid div-by-zero.
Keep the RAG_ENABLED early returns and the swallow-all-errors behavior of purge (return 0) and the caller's try/except (RAG failure must never crash the game).
delete_by_text_hashes: chunk IN(...) at 400 params (SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER default 999, but they chose 400 — keep it).
Config: surface all RAG_* as a config struct read from env with the exact defaults listed; CHARS_PER_TOKEN belongs to other subsystems, not RAG.

Global default engine + set_default_engine pattern: replace module-global mutable singleton with explicit dependency injection (pass an Embedder trait object) — cleaner in Rust and matches the duck-typed .embed() contract.

Round scores to 5 decimals in the sources output to match Python's round() (banker's rounding — Python round() is round-half-to-even; use a matching strategy if exact byte-equality of debug output matters, otherwise tolerance is fine).
suggestedCrates: reqwest (HTTP client to embeddings server)
rusqlite (SQLite cache, with bundled feature)
serde + serde_json (request/response + JSON contract)
sha2 (sha256 cache key)
blake2 (HashEmbeddingClient parity for tests)
base64 (vector encoding)
byteorder or bytemuck (little-endian f32 <-> bytes)
regex (tokenizer)
once_cell (default engine / stopword set)
anyhow/thiserror (error handling; but preserve swallow-on-failure semantics at boundaries)
risks: round() in Python uses round-half-to-even (banker's rounding); naive Rust rounding may differ in the last digit of sources scores — only matters if exact debug parity is required.
Python's str.lower() and Rust to_lowercase() can diverge on exotic Unicode, though the tokenizer regex limits chars to Latin/Cyrillic/digits/_/«»- so risk is low.
Vectors stored already-normalized AND re-normalized on read; double normalization is idempotent, but a port must not skip read-time normalization or scores shift for list-input embeddings.

embedding response 'index' field: code assumes vectors_by_index[idx] exists for every batch position idx; if the server returns fewer/misindexed items, Python raises KeyError (caught upstream). Rust must decide the same fail-safe behavior.

EmbeddingCache.get_many does NOT strip but LocalEmbeddingClient passes stripped texts; if a future caller passes unstripped texts the key mismatches — a latent inconsistency to preserve or fix consciously.

The actor-safe isolation guarantee is NOT enforced in rag.py — it depends entirely on world.py's corpus builder. A port that adds any new corpus source must re-audit truth/secret exclusion (fact kind=='truth' skip, rumor 'player' in witnesses, whereabouts status!='unknown').

visibility field is dead-ish in rag.py (set but unused for filtering) — do not assume rag.py filters by it; the comment in retrieval_documents notes private NPC beliefs would need a separate actor-filtered path that does not yet exist.

CHARS_PER_TOKEN and all prompt-cache/KV/compaction/dice-seed invariants requested in the brief are absent from this module; they must be mapped from history/LLM-client/world modules, not inferred here.

purpose: Two halves of GM-Lab audio. (1) STT: codex_transcribe.py posts user-recorded audio to ChatGPT's undocumented /backend-api/transcribe endpoint using the Codex subscription OAuth token, sent through curl_cffi with Chrome TLS impersonation to defeat Cloudflare bot mitigation; returns plain text. (2) TTS: a separate GPU microservice (hf_models/faster-qwen3-tts/tts_server.py, faster-qwen3 Qwen3-TTS) exposes /speak (full WAV) and /speak_stream (raw PCM16 head-first). GM-Lab's main HTTP server (server.py) is a thin proxy in front of it: it picks the voice by NPC grammatical gender, checks an on-disk compressed-clip cache keyed by (voice, exact text), proxies to the sidecar on a cache miss, transcodes the WAV to ogg/mp3 via ffmpeg, caches the result, and degrades gracefully (503) when the sidecar is down. The Rust+Tauri port must own STT directly, manage the TTS sidecar as a single launched child process, proxy it, cache clips, and run fine without it.

publicSurface.name: codex_transcribe.transcribe
publicSurface.signature: transcribe(audio: bytes, content_type: str = "audio/webm", filename: str | None = None) -> str
publicSurface.behavior: Core STT entrypoint. Validates non-empty audio, fetches a fresh Codex OAuth credential, builds a curl_cffi multipart POST (Chrome impersonation) to the transcribe URL, parses JSON {text|transcript} and returns text.strip(). Empty string is a VALID result (silence) and is returned as-is. Raises TranscribeError on auth/dep/network/CF/HTTP/no-text failure.

publicSurface.name: codex_transcribe.transcribe_url
publicSurface.behavior: Computes endpoint: GM_CODEX_TRANSCRIBE_URL override if set, else config.CODEX_BASE_URL with a trailing '/codex' stripped + '/transcribe' (route lives under /backend-api, NOT /backend-api/codex).

publicSurface.name: codex_transcribe._filename_for
publicSurface.behavior: Maps a MIME type substring to a filename: webm->audio.webm, ogg->audio.ogg, mp4/m4a/aac->audio.mp4, mpeg/mp3->audio.mp3, wav/wave/pcm->audio.wav, default audio.webm.

publicSurface.name: codex_transcribe.TranscribeError
publicSurface.signature: class TranscribeError(RuntimeError): __init__(self, message: str, status: int | None = None)
publicSurface.behavior: Error type carrying an optional HTTP status attribute; server.py reads getattr(ex,'status',None) and returns it in the JSON error.

publicSurface.name: tts_server.synth
publicSurface.behavior: Sidecar non-streaming synthesis. Resolves voice cfg (default 'gm'), applies stress_text, holds _lock, calls model.generate_voice_clone(language='Russian', ref_audio, ref_text, xvec_only, append_silence=True, **SAMPLING), concatenates chunks to float32, writes a WAV (PCM_16) and returns the bytes.

publicSurface.name: tts_server.Handler.do_POST
publicSurface.behavior: Sidecar HTTP contract. Reads JSON body {text, voice}, voice defaults 'gm', 400 on empty text; /speak -> 200 audio/wav synth(text,voice); /speak_stream -> _stream(); 500 JSON {error} on exception.

publicSurface.name: tts_server.Handler._stream
publicSurface.behavior: Streams raw PCM16 mono head-first as the model generates. Sends 200 audio/pcm with header X-Sample-Rate on the first chunk; writes _pcm16(chunk) and flushes per chunk; breaks on client disconnect; 204 if nothing generated. chunk_size=8.

publicSurface.name: tts_server.Handler.do_GET
publicSurface.behavior: GET /health -> 200 {"ok": true, "voices": ["gm","male","female"]}; everything else 404. This is the readiness probe the Rust port should poll after spawning the sidecar.

publicSurface.name: tts_server.stress_text
publicSurface.behavior: If RUAccent loaded: process_all() inserts '+' before each stressed vowel; STRESS_MODE 'double' doubles the vowel, 'mark' appends U+0301 combining acute; on error returns original. No-op if _accent is None.

publicSurface.name: tts_server._pcm16
publicSurface.behavior: Flattens chunk, clips to [-1,1], scales by 32767, casts to little-endian int16 ('<i2'), returns bytes. Mono.

publicSurface.name: tts_server._load
publicSurface.behavior: Sidecar startup: loads RUAccent (if stress on), loads FasterQwen3TTS on cuda/bfloat16, _warmup(prefill_len=100), then warms each voice with a tiny generate (max_new_tokens=8). Blocking; must finish before /health passes meaningfully.

publicSurface.name: server._npc_voice
publicSurface.behavior: Voice selection by grammatical gender. Reads npc.pronouns (upper). F* or contains 'ЖЕН' -> 'female'; M* or 'МУЖ' -> 'male'; everything else (neuter/plural/unknown/error) -> 'male' (the default character voice).

publicSurface.name: server._tts_synth
publicSurface.behavior: Proxy: POST JSON {text,voice} to TTS_URL+'/speak', 120s timeout, returns WAV bytes. Raises on connection failure (caught upstream -> 503).

publicSurface.name: server._proxy_tts_stream
publicSurface.behavior: Streaming proxy. POST to TTS_URL+'/speak_stream' (raises before headers if sidecar down), reads X-Sample-Rate, sends 200 audio/pcm + X-Sample-Rate + X-TTS-Voice + Cache-Control:no-store, relays 16384-byte chunks to the browser, accumulates the full PCM; ONLY if stream completed AND client stayed connected AND pcm non-empty, transcodes (_pcm_to_wav -> _compress_audio) and stores in cache.

publicSurface.name: server._tts_cache_path
publicSurface.signature: _tts_cache_path(voice: str, text: str, ext: str) -> str
publicSurface.behavior: Cache key = sha1(f"{voice}\n{text}").hexdigest(); path = TTS_CACHE_DIR/{key}.{ext}. Voice and EXACT text are the only inputs (no normalization, no model/sampling/stress in the key).

publicSurface.name: server._tts_cache_lookup
publicSurface.behavior: Returns (bytes, content_type) for a cached clip or (None,None). Tries the configured-format ext first, then falls back to 'wav'/audio/wav. Treats size==0 / missing as miss.

publicSurface.name: server._compress_audio
publicSurface.behavior: Transcodes WAV->configured format by piping through ffmpeg (pipe:0 -> pipe:1, 60s timeout). Returns (bytes, content_type, ext). On wav format, or any ffmpeg failure/absence, returns the raw WAV as (bytes,'audio/wav','wav').

publicSurface.name: server._tts_cache_store
publicSurface.signature: _tts_cache_store(voice: str, text: str, audio: bytes, ext: str) -> None
publicSurface.behavior: Atomic best-effort write: mkdir cache dir, write to {path}.tmp, os.replace to final path. Swallows OSError.

publicSurface.name: server._pcm_to_wav
publicSurface.behavior: Wraps raw PCM16 mono in a WAV container (1 channel, sampwidth 2, given sample rate) using the wave module. Used to re-containerize streamed PCM before ffmpeg compression for caching.

publicSurface.name: server POST /tts
publicSurface.signature: HTTP POST /tts  body {text, voice?, role?, npc_id?, stream?}
publicSurface.behavior: Public TTS endpoint. 400 on empty text. Voice resolution: explicit voice in {gm,male,female} wins; else role=='gm' or missing npc_id -> 'gm'; else _npc_voice(dialog, npc_id). Cache lookup first (hit -> no GPU). Miss+stream -> _proxy_tts_stream. Miss+non-stream -> _tts_synth then _compress_audio then cache. Responds with audio bytes + X-TTS-Voice header; 503 {ok:false,error} if sidecar unreachable.

publicSurface.name: server POST /transcribe
publicSurface.signature: HTTP POST /transcribe  raw audio body, Content-Type header
publicSurface.behavior: Public STT endpoint. Requires config.BACKEND=='codex' (else 400). Reads raw body (no multipart), Content-Type defaults 'audio/webm', calls codex_transcribe.transcribe(audio, content_type) -> {ok:true,text} or {ok:false,error,status}.

publicSurface.name: codex_oauth.ensure_fresh_credential
publicSurface.behavior: Returns a non-expired Codex OAuth credential (refreshing if needed). transcribe() relies on credential.access_token (Bearer) and credential.account_id (ChatGPT-Account-Id header).

dataStructures.name: Transcribe request (multipart/form-data)
dataStructures.fields: part 'file' (filename per content_type, content_type, raw audio bytes); field 'model' (TRANSCRIBE_MODEL, default 'gpt-4o-mini-transcribe'); field 'language' (TRANSCRIBE_LANGUAGE='ru', omitted if empty)
dataStructures.notes: Wire shape required by ChatGPT /backend-api/transcribe. Field order in code: file, model, language. Empty language string disables the hint.

dataStructures.name: Transcribe request headers
dataStructures.fields: Authorization: 'Bearer '+access_token.strip(); originator: config.CODEX_ORIGINATOR ('codex_cli_rs'); version: config.CODEX_CLIENT_VERSION ('0.133.0'); session-id, thread-id, x-codex-installation-id: each a fresh uuid4 per call; ChatGPT-Account-Id: account_id if present
dataStructures.notes: DELIBERATELY no User-Agent — curl_cffi's impersonate profile sets a consistent browser UA+client-hints; overriding it re-triggers Cloudflare. session-id/thread-id/installation-id are random per request, NOT stable.

dataStructures.name: Transcribe response JSON
dataStructures.fields: {"text": str} primary; fallback {"transcript": str}; if non-JSON, the raw response text
dataStructures.notes: text.strip() returned; '' is a valid (silence) result. cf-mitigated response header presence -> CF challenge error regardless of status.

dataStructures.name: GM-Lab /transcribe response
dataStructures.fields: {ok: bool, text?: str, error?: str, status?: int|null}
dataStructures.notes: status mirrors TranscribeError.status (the upstream HTTP code) when present.

dataStructures.name: TTS sidecar /speak,/speak_stream request JSON
dataStructures.fields: {text: str, voice: str}  voice in {gm, male, female}, default 'gm'
dataStructures.notes: Sent by both server._tts_synth and server._proxy_tts_stream. Unknown voice silently falls back to 'gm' in the sidecar.

dataStructures.name: TTS sidecar /health response JSON
dataStructures.fields: {ok: true, voices: ["gm","male","female"]}
dataStructures.notes: Readiness probe.

dataStructures.name: VOICES registry (tts_server)
dataStructures.fields: gm:{ref_audio: HERE/ref_audio_2.wav, ref_text:'', xvec_only:True}; male:{ref_audio: HERE/ref_audio.wav, ref_text:'', xvec_only:True}; female:{ref_audio: HERE/female_ref_deepwarm.wav, ref_text:FEMALE_REF_TEXT, xvec_only:False}
dataStructures.notes: gm and male are x-vector clones (no ref_text). female is ICL and its ref_text MUST exactly match the audio. gm=narrator, male=male+default chars, female=female chars.

dataStructures.name: SAMPLING (tts_server)
dataStructures.fields: temperature=0.75, top_k=50, top_p=1.0, repetition_penalty=1.1
dataStructures.notes: Fixed sampling for all sidecar voices. Differs from demo/cli defaults (temp 0.9, rep_pen 1.05). NOT part of the cache key — changing it silently invalidates nothing.

dataStructures.name: GM-Lab /tts request JSON
dataStructures.fields: {text: str, voice?: str, role?: str, npc_id?: str, stream?: bool}
dataStructures.notes: voice optional; role/npc_id drive _npc_voice fallback; stream=true selects head-first PCM proxy.

dataStructures.name: GM-Lab /tts response (non-stream)
dataStructures.fields: binary body; headers Content-Type (audio/ogg|audio/mpeg|audio/wav), Content-Length, X-TTS-Voice
dataStructures.notes: ctype from _TTS_FMT or audio/wav fallback.

dataStructures.name: GM-Lab /tts response (stream)
dataStructures.fields: binary audio/pcm; headers X-Sample-Rate, X-TTS-Voice, Cache-Control:no-store
dataStructures.notes: Raw little-endian PCM16 mono; browser must wrap/play it; sample rate from sidecar's X-Sample-Rate (default 24000).

dataStructures.name: On-disk TTS cache
dataStructures.fields: directory GM_TTS_CACHE_DIR (default server-dir/tts_cache); files {sha1(voice+'\n'+text)}.{ext}; ext in {ogg,mp3,wav}
dataStructures.notes: Flat dir of compressed clips. Atomic write via .tmp + os.replace. No index, no eviction, no TTL, no size cap.

dataStructures.name: _TTS_FMT table (server)
dataStructures.fields: ogg:('ogg','audio/ogg',['-c:a','libopus','-b:a','32k','-ac','1','-f','ogg']); mp3:('mp3','audio/mpeg',['-c:a','libmp3lame','-b:a','56k','-ac','1','-f','mp3']); wav:('wav','audio/wav',None)
dataStructures.notes: Exact ffmpeg encode args. Opus 32k mono / MP3 56k mono. wav=passthrough (no ffmpeg).

dataStructures.name: OpenAI-compat SpeechRequest (examples/openai_server.py — NOT used by GM-Lab)
dataStructures.fields: {model:str='tts-1', input:str, voice:str='alloy', response_format:str='wav'(wav|pcm|mp3), speed:float=1.0(unused)}
dataStructures.notes: Alternate server shape; reference only. voices.json maps name->{ref_audio,ref_text,language}. WAV/PCM stream; MP3 buffers fully via pydub+ffmpeg.

dataStructures.name: demo streaming SSE payload (demo/server.py — NOT used by GM-Lab)
dataStructures.fields: {type:'chunk'|'done'|'error'|'queued', audio_b64, sample_rate, ttfa_ms, voice_clone_ms, rtf, total_audio_s, elapsed_ms, ...}
dataStructures.notes: Reference only; the GM-Lab sidecar (tts_server.py) uses raw PCM, NOT this base64 SSE shape.

dataStructures.name: CodexCredential
dataStructures.fields: access_token:str, refresh_token:str, id_token:str|None, expires_at:int|None, account_id:str|None, credential_type:str='openai_codex_oauth'
dataStructures.notes: STT auth source. Only access_token and account_id are consumed by transcribe().

controlFlow:
STT path: browser records audio -> POST /transcribe (raw body) to GM-Lab server -> (guard BACKEND==codex) -> codex_transcribe.transcribe -> ensure_fresh_credential -> import curl_cffi -> build CurlMime multipart -> curl_cffi Session(impersonate='chrome').post(url, headers, multipart, timeout=120) -> check cf-mitigated header -> check 2xx -> parse JSON text -> return. Synchronous, one request per dictation.

TTS path: browser POST /tts {text,voice|role|npc_id,stream} -> resolve voice (explicit -> gm/role -> _npc_voice by pronouns) -> _tts_cache_lookup(voice,text). HIT: stream file bytes back, no GPU. MISS + stream=true: _proxy_tts_stream opens /speak_stream on the sidecar, relays PCM chunks head-first to the browser while accumulating; on clean completion, _pcm_to_wav -> _compress_audio (ffmpeg) -> _tts_cache_store. MISS + non-stream: _tts_synth -> sidecar /speak returns full WAV -> _compress_audio -> _tts_cache_store -> send bytes. Sidecar unreachable at any point -> 503 JSON.

Sidecar internal: do_POST reads {text,voice} -> synth() or _stream(). Both serialize on a single threading.Lock (model not concurrency-safe). synth: stress_text -> generate_voice_clone -> concat -> WAV. _stream: generate_voice_clone_streaming(chunk_size=8) -> per chunk send header-on-first then _pcm16 bytes + flush. Sidecar is a blocking ThreadingHTTPServer started by `python tts_server.py` after _load() (which loads model + warms voices). GM-Lab NEVER spawns it — it is launched manually/externally and discovered via GM_TTS_URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8765). The whole TTS subsystem is optional: tts_enabled/tts_autoplay runtime settings (default False) gate the UI, and any sidecar failure degrades to 503 without breaking the app.
externalDeps: curl_cffi (requests + CurlMime) — Chrome TLS/HTTP2 impersonation for STT; hard dependency only when /transcribe is used (imported lazily inside transcribe())
ChatGPT backend /backend-api/transcribe (network, Cloudflare-fronted)
Codex subscription OAuth token (codex_oauth.ensure_fresh_credential) — Platform API keys rejected
ffmpeg binary on PATH (TTS transcode WAV->ogg/mp3; absence falls back to raw WAV)
faster-qwen3-tts (FasterQwen3TTS) + torch + CUDA GPU + soundfile + numpy (sidecar only)
RUAccent (+ onnxruntime) for Russian stress; turbo3.1 omograph model, workdir HERE/ruaccent_models (sidecar only, optional)
HF model weights: local qwen17b_base/model.safetensors if present else downloads Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-Base; HF_HOME=.hf-home
reference WAVs on disk: ref_audio_2.wav, ref_audio.wav, female_ref_deepwarm.wav (sidecar)
Python stdlib: http.server, urllib.request, subprocess, wave, hashlib, json

Env vars: GM_TTS_URL, GM_TTS_CACHE_DIR, GM_TTS_FORMAT, GM_CODEX_TRANSCRIBE_MODEL, GM_CODEX_TRANSCRIBE_LANGUAGE, GM_CODEX_TRANSCRIBE_TIMEOUT, GM_CODEX_TRANSCRIBE_IMPERSONATE, GM_CODEX_TRANSCRIBE_URL, TTS_HOST, TTS_PORT, TTS_MODEL_ID, TTS_STRESS, GM_TTS_ENABLED, GM_TTS_AUTOPLAY
internalDeps: server.py imports codex_transcribe (lazy, inside handler), config, runtime_settings, world (pronoun/gender helpers), dialog_store (DialogRuntime for npc lookup)
codex_transcribe imports config (CODEX_BASE_URL, CODEX_ORIGINATOR, CODEX_CLIENT_VERSION) and codex_oauth (ensure_fresh_credential, CodexCredential)
tts_server.py imports faster_qwen3_tts.FasterQwen3TTS and optionally ruaccent; standalone — no GM-Lab imports
runtime_settings exposes tts_enabled()/tts_autoplay flags consumed by server state()
invariants: CACHE KEY: sha1(f"{voice}\n{text}").hexdigest(); inputs are ONLY voice and the EXACT text (no trimming beyond the caller's .strip(), no model id, no sampling params, no stress mode in the key). Newline '\n' separator is load-bearing. Port must replicate byte-for-byte or cache hits diverge.
CACHE LOOKUP ORDER: try configured-format ext first ((GM_TTS_FORMAT in {ogg,mp3,wav} else 'ogg')), THEN fall back to ('wav','audio/wav'). size>0 required; 0-byte/missing = miss.
CACHE WRITE: atomic — write to {path}.tmp then os.replace; mkdir -p first; all errors swallowed (best-effort, never fatal).
STREAM CACHE GATE: a streamed clip is cached ONLY if (stream completed normally) AND (client stayed connected) AND (pcm non-empty). Aborted/partial streams are never cached.

VOICE-BY-GENDER: pronouns uppercased; startswith('F') or contains 'ЖЕН' -> 'female'; startswith('M') or contains 'МУЖ' -> 'male'; ALL else (neuter, plural, unknown, exception) -> 'male'. 'male' is the explicit default character voice, NOT 'gm'.

VOICE RESOLUTION ORDER in /tts: explicit voice in {gm,male,female} wins; else role=='gm' OR npc_id missing -> 'gm'; else _npc_voice(npc_id). Only these three voice ids are valid; sidecar silently maps anything else to 'gm'.

SIDECAR VOICE TABLE: gm=ref_audio_2.wav xvec_only=True ref_text=''; male=ref_audio.wav xvec_only=True ref_text=''; female=female_ref_deepwarm.wav xvec_only=False ref_text=FEMALE_REF_TEXT. female is ICL so its ref_text MUST exactly equal the recording's transcript; gm/male are x-vector clones with empty ref_text.
FEMALE_REF_TEXT is a fixed Russian paragraph (3 sentences) that must remain verbatim and byte-exact with female_ref_deepwarm.wav, else female ICL degrades.

SIDECAR SAMPLING is fixed: temperature=0.75, top_k=50, top_p=1.0, repetition_penalty=1.1, language always 'Russian', append_silence=True (non-stream). These differ from demo/CLI defaults (0.9/1.05) — use the sidecar values.
STREAMING chunk_size=8 in the sidecar; demo default also 8, openai_server default 12. Sidecar uses 8.
PCM FORMAT: mono, little-endian int16 ('<i2'), clipped to [-1,1] then *32767. Sample rate carried in X-Sample-Rate (default 24000 if absent). Proxy relays in 16384-byte reads.

FFMPEG ENCODE ARGS exact: ogg= -c:a libopus -b:a 32k -ac 1 -f ogg ; mp3= -c:a libmp3lame -b:a 56k -ac 1 -f mp3 ; wav=passthrough(no ffmpeg). Pipe pipe:0->pipe:1, -hide_banner -loglevel error, 60s timeout. Any ffmpeg failure/absence => return raw WAV as ('audio/wav','wav') — must not crash.

STT ENDPOINT DERIVATION: strip a trailing '/codex' from CODEX_BASE_URL then append '/transcribe' (route is under /backend-api, NOT /backend-api/codex — the /codex path 404s). GM_CODEX_TRANSCRIBE_URL overrides entirely.

STT TLS IMPERSONATION is mandatory: curl_cffi Session(impersonate='chrome'). Plain httpx/requests get a Cloudflare 403 challenge. Do NOT set a custom User-Agent — it must come from the impersonation profile or CF re-triggers. Presence of response header 'cf-mitigated' => treat as challenge/block even on 2xx.

STT HEADERS: session-id, thread-id, x-codex-installation-id are FRESH uuid4 PER REQUEST (intentionally non-stable, unlike the GM chat thread/prompt-cache keys elsewhere). originator='codex_cli_rs', version='0.133.0'. Authorization='Bearer '+access_token.strip(). ChatGPT-Account-Id only when account_id present.

STT MULTIPART field order: file (with filename derived from content_type), model, language. language part omitted entirely when TRANSCRIBE_LANGUAGE is empty (enables auto-detect). model default 'gpt-4o-mini-transcribe', language default 'ru'.

STT RESULT: empty-string transcription is VALID (silence) and returned as-is; '' must not be treated as an error. Response text key 'text' with fallback 'transcript'; non-JSON falls back to raw response text.
STT GUARD: /transcribe only works when config.BACKEND=='codex' (else 400). Timeout default 120s.
SIDECAR CONCURRENCY: a single threading.Lock serializes ALL generate calls (model not concurrency-safe). Port must serialize sidecar inference (one in-flight generation).

OPTIONALITY: GM_TTS_ENABLED and GM_TTS_AUTOPLAY default False; the entire TTS subsystem is optional and any sidecar/ffmpeg failure must degrade (503 / raw-WAV) without breaking the app. Cache is read even when the sidecar is down (offline replay of prior clips).

SIDECAR IS NOT AUTO-SPAWNED in the Python code: GM-Lab discovers it via GM_TTS_URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8765); it must be started separately (python tts_server.py). The Rust port is ADDING single-launch process management — there is no existing spawn logic to copy, only the HTTP contract (/speak, /speak_stream, /health) to honor.

STRESS: TTS_STRESS in {double,mark,off}; 'double' duplicates the RUAccent-marked vowel (Qwen lengthens repeats ~= stress), 'mark' inserts U+0301. Applied to text BEFORE synthesis but is NOT part of the cache key — changing TTS_STRESS does not invalidate cached clips.

MODEL SELECTION: TTS_MODEL_ID override > local HERE/qwen17b_base if model.safetensors exists > Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-Base. Warmup: _warmup(prefill_len=100) then per-voice generate(max_new_tokens=8).
platformConcerns: curl_cffi bundles a patched libcurl with BoringSSL for TLS fingerprint impersonation — the single hardest cross-platform piece. A Rust port needs an equivalent impersonating HTTP client (rquest / curl-impersonate bindings); plain reqwest/hyper will get Cloudflare-403'd. Must work on Windows, macOS, Linux.

ffmpeg must be on PATH and invoked as a child process with pipes (stdin WAV -> stdout encoded). On Windows the executable is ffmpeg.exe; bundling or locating ffmpeg is the port's responsibility; absence must fall back to raw WAV, not crash.

TTS sidecar requires CUDA GPU + torch + faster-qwen3-tts; effectively NVIDIA-only and heavyweight. The Rust app must run perfectly with NO sidecar present (cache-only / 503). Process spawning of a Python sidecar from Rust must handle Windows (no fork, CREATE_NO_WINDOW), graceful shutdown, and orphan cleanup across all OSes.
Sidecar binds 127.0.0.1:8765 (TTS_HOST/TTS_PORT); GM server binds 127.0.0.1:8000 (GM_HOST/GM_PORT). Loopback sockets; port collision handling needed.

Cache dir default is server-dir/tts_cache (relative to the executable's location) — the Rust port should use a proper per-OS app data dir instead of cwd-relative. sha1 hex filenames are filesystem-safe on all OSes.

HF model/asset download paths: HF_HOME defaulted to ../.hf-home; demo server falls back to /tmp (POSIX-only) for assets in read-only HF Spaces — irrelevant to the Windows desktop port but shows the original assumed POSIX tmp.
RUAccent uses onnxruntime (native lib) and downloads omograph models to ruaccent_models/ — extra native dependency for Russian stress, optional.
Newline in cache key is '\n' (LF) — keep LF regardless of OS line-ending conventions.
Random uuid4 generation per STT request — trivially cross-platform but must be cryptographically-fresh per call.
rustNotes: STT: use an impersonating HTTP client. rquest (formerly reqwest-impersonate) with a Chrome preset is the closest match to curl_cffi(impersonate='chrome'); confirm it sets a matching UA + client-hints and does NOT let you override the UA. Build multipart with file/model/language parts in that order; detect cf-mitigated header; treat 2xx-with-cf-mitigated as failure; return empty string as Ok("").

STT endpoint derivation and uuid-per-request headers are simple; reuse codex_oauth equivalent for the Bearer token + account id. Keep session-id/thread-id/installation-id RANDOM per request (do not reuse the chat thread id).

TTS proxy: implement as an axum/hyper handler. Cache lookup is a pure fn over (voice,text,format) -> sha1 hex path; use sha1 crate, hex encode lowercase, key string exactly format!("{voice}\n{text}"). Try configured ext then wav.

Streaming proxy: open the sidecar /speak_stream, read X-Sample-Rate, set response audio/pcm + X-Sample-Rate + X-TTS-Voice + Cache-Control:no-store, relay bytes (Stream/Bytes) while teeing into a buffer; on clean EOF + client-connected + non-empty, wrap PCM in a WAV header and pipe through ffmpeg via tokio::process to produce the cached clip. Replicate the 'only cache a complete clip' gate.
ffmpeg: spawn via tokio::process::Command, feed WAV on stdin, read encoded stdout; 60s timeout; on any error return raw WAV with audio/wav. Hardcode the exact arg vectors per format.

Sidecar lifecycle (NEW in the port): single launch with a once-guard (OnceCell/Mutex<Option<Child>>), poll GET /health until {ok:true} or timeout, expose readiness to the UI, kill child on app exit (tokio Child::kill / job objects on Windows to avoid orphans). All TTS calls must no-op/return 503 if the sidecar is disabled or not ready.
Keep voice selection logic identical: uppercase pronouns, F*/ЖЕН->female, M*/МУЖ->male, default male; /tts resolution order explicit>gm/role>npc lookup; only {gm,male,female} valid.
WAV writing: build the PCM16 mono header manually (see examples/openai_server _wav_header) or use the hound crate; sample width 2, 1 channel, given sr.
Gate everything behind tts_enabled/tts_autoplay settings (default false). Cache must be readable for offline replay even with the sidecar down.
Put cache dir under a per-OS data dir (directories crate) rather than cwd/tts_cache.
suggestedCrates: rquest (or curl-impersonate bindings) — Chrome TLS/HTTP2 impersonation for STT
reqwest or hyper/axum — sidecar proxying and the GM HTTP server
tokio (+ tokio::process) — async runtime, ffmpeg/sidecar child processes
sha1 + hex — cache key hashing
hound — WAV read/write (PCM16 mono)
bytes + futures — streaming relay
serde / serde_json — request/response JSON
uuid (v4) — per-request STT header ids
directories — per-OS cache/data dirs
once_cell / parking_lot — sidecar singleton + serialization lock
thiserror / anyhow — error types incl. an STT error carrying an HTTP status
risks: curl_cffi Chrome impersonation is the make-or-break dependency: if the Rust impersonating client's fingerprint doesn't match a real Chrome closely enough, Cloudflare returns 403/challenge and STT silently breaks. Hard to test without live ChatGPT credentials.

The /backend-api/transcribe endpoint is undocumented and unstable; field names, headers (originator/version), and CF behavior can change. version='0.133.0' / originator='codex_cli_rs' may need bumping.

demo/server.py and examples/openai_server.py are reference servers NOT used by GM-Lab — their SSE base64 / OpenAI /v1/audio/speech shapes could mislead a porter into implementing the wrong contract. The live contract is tts_server.py's /speak (WAV) and /speak_stream (raw PCM16 + X-Sample-Rate).

No existing sidecar spawn code exists in Python (it's launched manually) — the Rust single-launch/health-poll/orphan-kill lifecycle is genuinely new design, not a port; cross-platform child management (Windows job objects vs unix process groups) is error-prone.

Cache has no eviction/TTL/size cap and key excludes model/sampling/stress — switching model or sampling silently serves stale-voice clips; a porter may 'helpfully' add those to the key and break compatibility with existing caches. Preserve the exact key unless intentionally migrating.
Sample rate is assumed 24000 when X-Sample-Rate is absent; a model emitting a different sr would corrupt cached WAV duration.

RUAccent stress + onnxruntime is GPU/native-heavy and Russian-specific; replicating exact stress behavior in Rust is nontrivial and likely out of scope for the sidecar (which stays Python) — only the proxy/cache/STT side is being ported.
Empty-text 400 vs empty-transcription '' (valid) is a subtle asymmetry easy to get wrong.

subsystem: mock_server — offline/test HTTP+SSE backend that fakes GM/NPC/tool-call responses
purpose: A standalone, dependency-light HTTP server (stdlib http.server.ThreadingHTTPServer) that impersonates the real GM-Lab backend so the React frontend can be smoke-tested with no real LLM/model running. It serves the built index.html plus canned JSON for /state, /models, /settings, /transcript, /debug, /chats, and a scripted Server-Sent-Events /turn that emits, in a fixed sequence, every event kind the real app can produce (streaming deltas, gm_thinking/gm_narration, gm_tool_call, dice, world_fact, npc subagent lifecycle, npc_speech with claims, scene_update, gm_reject, meta/meta_total, player_options, done). It is deterministic: no randomness, fixed dice results, fixed token/timing numbers, hardcoded Russian scenario ("Ледяной порт"). It is explicitly NOT part of the app — test only — but the Rust port must reproduce its exact JSON contract and event ordering so the existing frontend tests pass against the Rust test harness.

publicSurface.name: meta
publicSurface.signature: def meta(label, secs=2.0, tps=44, tin=1200, tout=320, cached=0) -> dict
publicSurface.behavior: Builds one per-LLM-call meta dict. Returns {label, secs, tps, in=tin, out=tout, cached, prompt_secs=0.6, eval_secs=round(secs-0.6,2), load_secs=0}. Note 'in'/'out' keys come from tin/tout args.

publicSurface.name: meta_total
publicSurface.behavior: Builds the aggregate run-meta dict for a turn: {secs:5.1, tokens:2680, in:2100, out:580, cached:800, calls:[{label,in,out,tps,secs} x2], sys_estimate:1500, context: STATE['context_usage']}. The two calls are GM (in1200/out320/tps44/secs2.0) and 'NPC Борин' (in900/out260/tps51/secs1.7).

publicSurface.name: _chats_payload
publicSurface.behavior: Returns MOCK_CHATS each augmented with created_at='2026-06-19 10:00', updated_at='2026-06-20 12:30', active=(id==ACTIVE_CHAT_ID['value']).

publicSurface.name: _chat_one
publicSurface.behavior: Finds chat by id in MOCK_CHATS and returns it with created_at/updated_at fixed timestamps and active=True; if not found returns a synthetic 'Новый чат' stub with empty fields and active=True.

publicSurface.name: _seed_block
publicSurface.behavior: Returns the ordered event list for one canned full turn numbered i. Uses sid g=f'g{i}' for GM events and n=f'n{i}' for NPC events. Exercises player, gm_thinking, gm_tool_call(get_world_fact, roll_dice), world_fact, dice, gm_narration, gm_tool_call(ask_npc), npc_start/npc_thinking/npc_speech, scene_update, meta, meta_total.

publicSurface.name: _showcase_block
publicSurface.behavior: Returns one extra turn (sid g='gs', n='ns') exercising the remaining tool cards: set_npc_whereabouts, ask_npc first call then ask_npc with a 'correction' arg followed by gm_reject, move_npc, set_scene, scene_update, meta, meta_total, and a player_options block with 7 options.

publicSurface.name: seed_transcript
publicSurface.behavior: Builds the full canned transcript: for i in range(1,3) extend with _seed_block(i) (so blocks 1 and 2), then extend with _showcase_block(). This is what /transcript and chat create/activate/delete return.

publicSurface.name: Handler
publicSurface.behavior: The single request handler implementing do_GET, do_POST and the SSE streamer. Routes by urlparse(self.path).path.

publicSurface.name: Handler.log_message
publicSurface.behavior: Overridden to suppress all default access-log output.

publicSurface.name: Handler._json
publicSurface.behavior: Serializes obj with json.dumps(obj, ensure_ascii=False), UTF-8 encodes, sends response with Content-Type 'application/json; charset=utf-8' and Content-Length, writes body. ensure_ascii=False is required (Cyrillic content).

publicSurface.name: Handler._body
publicSurface.behavior: Reads Content-Length bytes, json.loads them; returns {} on missing length, parse error, or non-dict payload.

publicSurface.name: Handler._handle_chats
publicSurface.behavior: Handles POST /chats (create), /chats/<id>/activate, /chats/<id>/delete. See controlFlow and dataStructures for exact response shapes. chat_id parsed as parts[1] when path splits to 3 parts.

publicSurface.name: Handler.do_GET
publicSurface.behavior: Routes GET: '/' or startswith('/index') -> serve index.html (text/html); /state -> STATE; /models -> MODELS; /settings -> {ok,settings,settings_options}; /transcript -> {events: seed_transcript()}; /export -> {mock:True}; /debug -> DEBUG; /chats -> {ok,active_chat_id,chats}; else 404 {error:'not found'}.

publicSurface.name: Handler.do_POST
publicSurface.behavior: Routes POST: /chats or /chats/* -> _handle_chats; /settings -> merge body['settings'] into SETTINGS, return {ok,settings,settings_options,state}; /cmd,/model,/codex/login,/codex/logout -> {ok,state}; /turn -> SSE stream; /debug/roll,/debug/fact,/debug/fact_delete,/debug/npc,/debug/player,/debug/story,/debug/scene,/debug/state_record,/debug/rumor -> mutate DEBUG and return DEBUG; else 404.

publicSurface.name: Handler._push
publicSurface.behavior: Writes one SSE frame: 'data: ' + json.dumps(ev, ensure_ascii=False) + '\n\n', UTF-8 encoded, then flushes. This is the wire format every event uses.

publicSurface.name: Handler._stream
publicSurface.behavior: Word-by-word streaming: splits text on ' ', for each word pushes {kind:'delta', sid, data:{channel, text: word+' '}} and time.sleep(0.02). channel is one of gm_thinking/gm_narration/npc_speech.

publicSurface.name: Handler._stream_turn
publicSurface.behavior: The scripted live /turn SSE sequence (see controlFlow for exact order). Wraps everything in try/except for BrokenPipeError/ConnectionAbortedError/ConnectionResetError (swallowed). Ends with {kind:'done'}.

publicSurface.name: main
publicSurface.behavior: Creates ThreadingHTTPServer(('127.0.0.1', PORT), Handler), prints 'mock GM-Lab on http://127.0.0.1:{PORT}', serve_forever().

dataStructures.name: STATE (GET /state)
dataStructures.fields: model:'qwen2.5:14b', backend:'ollama', stream_gm_content:True, public:SCENE, scene:{title, present_npcs:[id], npc_whereabouts:{npc_id:{status,location_name}}}, npcs:NPCS, status_labels:dict(WHEREABOUTS_STATUS_LABELS), settings:SETTINGS, settings_options:SETTINGS_OPTIONS, context_usage:{current:12300, world:1800, next_compact:{label,used,limit,remaining}, gm:{active,history,summary,limit,remaining}, npc:{name,active,history,summary,limit,remaining}, npcs:[{id,name,color,has_session,active,history,summary,limit,remaining}]}
dataStructures.notes: context_usage is the central token-budget shape; limits gm=100000, npc=64000. STATE is also embedded under 'state'/'context' keys in many other responses and in meta_total().

dataStructures.name: NPCS (module constant)
dataStructures.fields: list of {id, name, role, pronouns, color}. Three NPCs: n_borin(Борин/капитан стражи/он/#e6c08a), n_liza(Лиза/торговка/она/#c4a7e7), n_maret(Капитан Марет/моряк/он/#9ccfd8).
dataStructures.notes: Reused everywhere; color and pronouns are part of the contract.

dataStructures.name: MODELS (GET /models)
dataStructures.fields: ok:True, model:'qwen2.5:14b', models:[{id,name,supported}], settings:SETTINGS, settings_options:SETTINGS_OPTIONS. Models: qwen2.5:14b(supported), llama3.1:8b(supported), gpt-oss:20b(NOT supported).
dataStructures.notes: 'supported' flag drives frontend model picker disabling.

dataStructures.name: SETTINGS / SETTINGS_OPTIONS
dataStructures.fields: SETTINGS = runtime_settings.defaults() (a copy of _DEFAULTS dict). SETTINGS_OPTIONS = runtime_settings.options() = {reasoning_efforts, reasoning_summaries, reasoning_roles, text_verbosities, tool_choices, max_tool_hops_max:MAX_TOOL_HOPS_CAP, max_output_tokens_max:config.MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS_CAP}.
dataStructures.notes: Mock mirrors runtime_settings so the smoke test never drifts from the real app. POST /settings mutates the live SETTINGS dict in place (SETTINGS.update). For the Rust port, the canonical settings shape must come from the real settings module, not be hardcoded here.

dataStructures.name: PLAYER_CHARACTER
dataStructures.fields: name, pronouns, class_role, level, background, age, physical_type, distinctive_features, life_status('alive'), life_status_note, condition, personality, values, gm_notes, abilities:{STR,DEX,CON,INT,WIS,CHA}, skills:{Insight,Perception,Persuasion,Deception}, saving_throws:{WIS,CHA}, passive_perception:14, ac:13, hp:{current,max}, speed, senses, languages, inventory:[..], equipment:[..], features:[..], card_revision:0
dataStructures.notes: card_revision starts at 0 — the optimistic-concurrency token for the character card. POST /debug/player mutates this in place via fields update.

dataStructures.name: DEBUG (GET /debug)
dataStructures.fields: ok:True, meta:{model,backend,turns,run_usage:{input,output,cached_tokens}, context_usage:{current,limit,remaining}}, runtime:{settings:SETTINGS, cache:{prompt_cache_key:'gm-lab:mock-thread-abc123', thread_id:'mock-thread-abc123', store:False}}, time:{current_date_label}, player_character:PLAYER_CHARACTER, story:{title,objective,public_intro,hidden_truth,constraints:[],hidden_events:[]}, roll_override:{next:None, all:None}, status_labels, facts:[{id,kind,text,keywords:[]}], state_records:[{record_id,kind,scope,text,entity_id,active}], rumors:[{seq,speaker,text,confirmed}], scene:{title,location_id,present_npcs,tension,description,constraints,items:[{item_id,name,location,visible,portable,owner,details}],exits:[{exit_id,name,destination,visible,blocked_by}]}, npcs:[{...NPCS fields, present, public_label, whereabouts:{location_name,status,details}, persona, voice, goals, knowledge, secret, mechanics:{abilities,skills,saving_throws,passive_perception,ac,hp,speed,senses,languages}, summary:'', commitments:[], messages:0, history:''}], memory:{gm_summary:'', loaded_gm_tools:['ask_npc','roll_dice'], events:[]}
dataStructures.notes: The whole debug/state-inspector contract. Critically reveals real-app field names: cache.prompt_cache_key & thread_id (stable per chat, store:False), story.hidden_truth & hidden_events (secret/hidden canon), facts.kind in {public,truth,rumor}, state_records.kind in {fact,rumor,npc_memory,relationship,goal} & scope in {public,gm,owner,subject,participants}, npc.secret/knowledge (hidden), card_revision.

dataStructures.name: facts entry
dataStructures.fields: {id, kind:'public'|'truth'|'rumor', text, keywords:[str]}
dataStructures.notes: truth = hidden GM-only canon; public = player-visible; rumor = unconfirmed. POST /debug/fact appends with id=f'dbg_{len+1}', keywords:[]. POST /debug/fact_delete removes by id.

dataStructures.name: state_records entry
dataStructures.fields: {record_id, kind:'fact'|'rumor'|'npc_memory'|'relationship'|'goal', scope:'public'|'gm'|'owner'|'subject'|'participants', text, entity_id, active:bool}
dataStructures.notes: scope='owner' (e.g. sr_liza_note) marks NPC-private memory; isolation matters. POST /debug/state_record add->record_id=f'sr_dbg_{len+1}', active:True; delete by record_id.

dataStructures.name: rumors entry
dataStructures.fields: {seq:int, speaker, text, confirmed:bool}
dataStructures.notes: POST /debug/rumor action in {add,delete,confirm}; add->seq=len+1, confirmed:False.

dataStructures.name: scene.items / scene.exits
dataStructures.fields: item: {item_id,name,location,visible,portable,owner,details}. exit: {exit_id,name,destination,visible,blocked_by}. Note: set_scene tool args use shorter keys (item {id,name}; exit {id,name,destination,visible,blocked_by}).
dataStructures.notes: Two slightly different item/exit schemas exist: the canonical DEBUG.scene form vs the set_scene tool-arg form.

dataStructures.name: MOCK_CHATS / ACTIVE_CHAT_ID
dataStructures.fields: MOCK_CHATS: list of {id,title,preview,turn_count}. ACTIVE_CHAT_ID: {'value': str} (mutable single-cell). Seed chats: chat_ice(active), chat_garden, chat_turn.
dataStructures.notes: In-memory only, mutated by /chats POST routes. New chat id pattern 'chat_new_%d'%(len+1); on delete-all a 'chat_fresh' is created.

dataStructures.name: meta event data
dataStructures.fields: {label, secs, tps, in, out, cached, prompt_secs:0.6, eval_secs:round(secs-0.6,2), load_secs:0}
dataStructures.notes: Per-call timing/token telemetry pushed as {kind:'meta', data:meta(...)}.

dataStructures.name: meta_total event data
dataStructures.fields: {secs:5.1, tokens:2680, in:2100, out:580, cached:800, calls:[{label,in,out,tps,secs}], sys_estimate:1500, context:STATE['context_usage']}
dataStructures.notes: Aggregate pushed as {kind:'meta_total', data:meta_total()}.

dataStructures.name: dice event data
dataStructures.fields: {ok:True, notation, sides, count, keep, rolls:[int], kept:[int], modifier, total, modifier_note(optional), grade, natural, target_number, target_kind:'DC', roll_kind:'check', detail}
dataStructures.notes: Deterministic fixed rolls — seed block: 1d20+3 -> rolls[14], total17, grade success, natural14, target13, detail '1d20+3 -> [14] +3 = 17 vs DC 13: grade=success, margin=+4, natural=14'. Live stream: 1d20+4 -> rolls[11], total15, target12, margin=+3.

dataStructures.name: npc_speech event data
dataStructures.fields: {speech:str, action:str, claims:[str]}
dataStructures.notes: THE canonical NPC structured-output contract field names. Note: the mock does NOT include a 'reasoning' field in npc_speech (reasoning is carried separately as npc_thinking events). 'claims' is the list of factual assertions the NPC makes (for the claims/canon verification subsystem).

dataStructures.name: gm_tool_call event data
dataStructures.fields: {name:str, arguments:dict}. Tool names & arg schemas observed: get_world_fact{query}; roll_dice{notation,reason,modifier_note?}; ask_npc{npc_id,situation, correction?}; set_npc_whereabouts{npc_id,status,location_name,source,details}; move_npc{npc_id,present,visible,can_hear,location,activity,attitude,reason}; set_scene{title,description,location_id,present_npcs,items,exits,constraints,tension,reason}
dataStructures.notes: Exact GM tool-arg field names — load-bearing for the tool dispatcher port. 'correction' on ask_npc triggers a redo that yields gm_reject.

dataStructures.name: SSE event envelope
dataStructures.fields: Common keys across events: kind (required), data, sid, agent, channel. kinds: player, gm_thinking, gm_narration, gm_tool_call, world_fact, dice, npc_start, npc_thinking, npc_speech, npc_whereabouts, scene_update, gm_reject, delta, meta, meta_total, player_options, done.
dataStructures.notes: delta payload: {kind:'delta', sid, data:{channel, text}}. npc_start: {kind:'npc_start', agent, sid}. gm_reject: {kind:'gm_reject', agent, data:str}.

controlFlow: Entrypoint main() -> ThreadingHTTPServer on 127.0.0.1:PORT, serve_forever(); one Handler instance per request, threaded. GET routing (do_GET) by exact path: '/' or '/index*' streams index.html bytes; /state,/models,/settings,/transcript,/export,/debug,/chats each return canned JSON; unknown->404. POST routing (do_POST): /chats* -> _handle_chats; /settings mutates SETTINGS in place; /cmd,/model,/codex/* are no-ops returning {ok,state}; /turn -> sets SSE headers then _stream_turn(); /debug/* mutate the global DEBUG dict then echo it back. _handle_chats branches: POST /chats (create) inserts new chat at head, sets active, returns {ok,active_chat_id,chat,state,transcript}; .../activate sets ACTIVE_CHAT_ID and returns {ok,chat,state,transcript}; .../delete filters MOCK_CHATS, on missing returns 404 {ok:False,error}, reassigns active to MOCK_CHATS[0] or '', if empty creates chat_fresh, returns {ok,deleted,active_chat_id,chats,chat,state,transcript,embeddings_purged:7}. The /turn SSE stream (_stream_turn) emits in EXACT fixed order: player -> sleep0.1 -> _stream(gm_thinking,'live',...) -> gm_tool_call(roll_dice 1d20+4) -> sleep0.05 -> dice(total15) -> world_fact -> sleep0.05 -> _stream(gm_narration,'live',...) -> gm_tool_call(ask_npc n_maret) -> sleep0.05 -> npc_start(Капитан Марет,sid npc_live) -> sleep0.05 -> npc_thinking -> _stream(npc_speech,'npc_live',...) -> npc_speech(full structured {speech,action,claims}) -> scene_update -> meta(secs2.4,cached900) -> meta_total -> done. _stream() emits word-by-word delta frames each followed by time.sleep(0.02). All writes are SSE frames via _push (data: <json>\\n\\n + flush). seed_transcript() (used by /transcript and chat routes) is the non-streamed canned history: _seed_block(1), _seed_block(2), _showcase_block().
externalDeps: Python stdlib only at runtime: json, os, time, http.server (BaseHTTPRequestHandler, ThreadingHTTPServer), urllib.parse.urlparse
Local module runtime_settings (defaults(), options()) — for SETTINGS / SETTINGS_OPTIONS
Local module world (world.py) imported as world_mod — only WHEREABOUTS_STATUS_LABELS dict is used
index.html file (the built frontend) read from HERE dir and served at '/'
Env vars: PORT, GM_PORT (fallback chain PORT||GM_PORT||'8000')
internalDeps: runtime_settings (defaults, options) — the single source of truth for settings shape; mock deliberately mirrors it
invariants: DETERMINISM is the whole point: NO randomness anywhere. All dice are hardcoded. Seed-block roll: 1d20+3 with rolls=[14], kept=[14], modifier=3, total=17, natural=14, target_number=13, target_kind='DC', roll_kind='check', grade='success', detail='1d20+3 -> [14] +3 = 17 vs DC 13: grade=success, margin=+4, natural=14'. Live-stream roll: 1d20+4 with rolls=[11], kept=[11], modifier=4, total=15, natural=11, target_number=12, grade='success', detail='1d20+4 -> [11] +4 = 15 vs DC 12: grade=success, margin=+3, natural=11'. The Rust mock must reproduce these EXACT numbers and the EXACT detail strings (including the ' -> ', ' +N = ', ' vs DC N: grade=...', 'margin=+N', 'natural=N' formatting).

prompt_cache_key and thread_id are STABLE and EQUAL strings in the mock: both 'mock-thread-abc123' for thread_id, and prompt_cache_key='gm-lab:mock-thread-abc123' (note the 'gm-lab:' namespace prefix). store=False. These live under DEBUG.runtime.cache. The real app's cache key = 'gm-lab:' + thread_id; the port must keep that prefix-join contract.

Token-budget / context limits are fixed constants: GM limit=100000, NPC limit=64000. STATE.context_usage.current=12300, world=1800. gm:{active=12300,history=12800,summary=900}. npc(Борин):{active=5300,history=4100,summary=600}. next_compact label='ГМ', used=12800, remaining=87200. These are display numbers, not computed — but they encode the active/history/summary three-bucket model used by the real compaction subsystem (active context vs raw history vs compressed summary).

Token-estimation telemetry constants in meta(): prompt_secs hardcoded 0.6, eval_secs = round(secs-0.6, 2), load_secs=0. meta_total(): secs=5.1, tokens=2680, in=2100, out=580, cached=800, sys_estimate=1500. run_usage in DEBUG.meta: input=2100, output=580, cached_tokens=800. 'cached' (default 0; passed 800/900 in turns) models prompt-cache hit tokens — must surface as the same field name.

NPC structured-output JSON contract field names are EXACTLY: speech, action, claims (list of strings). There is NO 'reasoning' key inside npc_speech.data — NPC reasoning is a separate npc_thinking event (data is a plain string). The real NPC schema is reasoning/speech/action/claims; the mock splits reasoning into the npc_thinking stream and speech/action/claims into npc_speech.

GM tool-call arg field names are load-bearing and must match the real dispatcher: roll_dice{notation,reason,modifier_note}; ask_npc{npc_id,situation,correction}; get_world_fact{query}; set_npc_whereabouts{npc_id,status,location_name,source,details}; move_npc{npc_id,present,visible,can_hear,location,activity,attitude,reason}; set_scene{title,description,location_id,present_npcs,items,exits,constraints,tension,reason}.

ask_npc 'correction' field drives the critic/redo loop: a second ask_npc with a 'correction' string is followed by a {kind:'gm_reject', agent:<name>, data:<reason>} event (NPC reply rejected & to be rewritten). The redo/critic doubling is part of the contract the frontend renders.

Secret/hidden-canon isolation surfaces: DEBUG.story.hidden_truth and DEBUG.story.hidden_events are GM-only; facts.kind=='truth' is hidden canon; npc.secret and npc.knowledge are private; state_records.scope in {gm,owner,subject,participants} gate visibility. POST /debug/story ONLY allows mutating title, public_intro, hidden_truth, and hidden_events (whitelist) — other story fields are immutable via that route.

Append-only / monotonic id generation: facts append id=f'dbg_{len(facts)+1}'; state_records record_id=f'sr_dbg_{len(state_records)+1}'; rumors seq=len(rumors)+1; new chat id='chat_new_%d'%(len(MOCK_CHATS)+1). These are length-based counters (NOT max+1) so they can collide after deletes — a quirk to replicate or consciously fix in the port.
card_revision starts at 0 on PLAYER_CHARACTER — the optimistic-concurrency revision token. /debug/player updates fields but does NOT bump card_revision in the mock (real app presumably does).

SSE wire format is fixed: each event = literal 'data: ' + json.dumps(ev, ensure_ascii=False) + '\n\n', then flush. /turn terminates with {kind:'done'}. ensure_ascii=False is REQUIRED throughout (Cyrillic) — JSON must be UTF-8 with raw non-ASCII, not \uXXXX escapes.

/turn event ORDER is fixed and exhaustive (see controlFlow). seed_transcript() order is also fixed: _seed_block(1)+_seed_block(2)+_showcase_block(). sid conventions: GM sid 'g{i}'/'gs'/'live'; NPC sid 'n{i}'/'ns'/'npc_live'. delta events carry data.channel in {gm_thinking,gm_narration,npc_speech}.

Sleeps encode pacing but are NOT correctness: _stream sleeps 0.02s per word; _stream_turn sleeps 0.1 once then 0.05 between several events. A faithful test port may keep or drop these, but the EVENT SEQUENCE must be preserved.

WHEREABOUTS status vocabulary (from world.WHEREABOUTS_STATUS_LABELS): present, known, likely, rumored, unknown, left_scene — these are the only valid 'status' values for set_npc_whereabouts / npc.whereabouts.
platformConcerns: Binds 127.0.0.1 (loopback only) — must use the same loopback bind on Win/macOS/Linux in the Rust test server.
Port resolution: env PORT then GM_PORT then default 8000 — preserve this precedence.

Serves index.html read from the script's own directory (HERE = dirname(abspath(__file__))) in binary mode — Rust must locate the built frontend asset relative to the test binary/crate, cross-platform path handling.

Uses ThreadingHTTPServer (one thread per connection) so the long-lived SSE /turn stream doesn't block other requests — the Rust equivalent needs an async or multi-threaded HTTP server so SSE and polling coexist.

Broken-pipe handling: _stream_turn catches BrokenPipeError/ConnectionAbortedError/ConnectionResetError (ConnectionAbortedError is the Windows-flavored disconnect) — Rust must tolerate client disconnect mid-SSE without panicking.
No TLS, no subprocess, no browser-open, no GPU, no ffmpeg, no curl_cffi — this mock is intentionally dependency-free; none of the heavier platform concerns of the real backend apply here.
wfile.flush() after each SSE frame is needed for incremental delivery — Rust must disable response buffering / explicitly flush per event.
rustNotes: Implement as a small test-only HTTP server. axum or hyper both work; axum gives clean SSE via axum::response::sse::{Sse, Event} with a futures Stream. Keep it behind a #[cfg(test)] / a 'mock' feature or a separate bin target so it never ships in release.

Model the SSE events as a serde-tagged enum or, simpler and safer for byte-exact output, as serde_json::Value built explicitly — the frontend depends on EXACT field names and key presence (e.g. npc_speech has speech/action/claims but no reasoning). An internally-tagged enum with #[serde(tag="kind")] mirrors the {kind, ...} envelope well, but watch the reserved 'in'/'out' keys in meta — use #[serde(rename="in")]/#[serde(rename="out")].

Reproduce dice as literal constants, not via an RNG — define the two canned roll Values once. If you instead route through the real dice engine for parity, seed it deterministically and assert the engine produces exactly rolls=[14] for the seed turn and [11] for the live turn; otherwise hardcode. The detail string format must be byte-identical.

JSON must be emitted with non-ASCII preserved (serde_json does this by default — it does NOT escape non-ASCII, unlike Python's default; this happens to match ensure_ascii=False, good). Ensure Content-Type carries charset=utf-8.

Pull SETTINGS/SETTINGS_OPTIONS and WHEREABOUTS_STATUS_LABELS from the ported runtime_settings and world modules, NOT hardcoded, to keep the mirror-the-real-app invariant. Only the scenario fixtures (SCENE, NPCS, PLAYER_CHARACTER, DEBUG, transcript blocks) should be inline constants.
Use a shared mutable state behind Arc<Mutex<..>> (or RwLock) for MOCK_CHATS/ACTIVE_CHAT_ID/DEBUG/SETTINGS since handlers mutate globals; Python relied on GIL + module globals.
Preserve the length-based id counters (chat_new_N, dbg_N, sr_dbg_N, seq=len+1) exactly if existing tests assert those ids; flag the post-delete collision quirk to the porter.

Reproduce per-event flush + optional small delays via tokio sleeps inside the stream; delays are cosmetic for tests, the order is the contract. Wrap the stream so a client disconnect ends the task quietly (map send errors to stream termination).
Keep prompt_cache_key = format!("gm-lab:{}", thread_id) and store=false to honor the cache-key-namespace invariant.
Provide a single helper that builds STATE['context_usage'] so meta_total().context and /state stay identical (Python shares the same dict object by reference; in Rust clone the same value).
suggestedCrates: axum (or hyper) — HTTP server with native SSE support
tokio — async runtime, sleeps, task spawning for the stream
serde + serde_json — JSON shapes; serde_json preserves non-ASCII by default (matches ensure_ascii=False)
tower-http (ServeFile/ServeDir) — serving index.html cross-platform
futures / async-stream — building the SSE event Stream ergonomically
once_cell or std::sync::LazyLock — static fixtures (NPCS, SCENE, PLAYER_CHARACTER, DEBUG)
risks: The mock's numbers (token counts, timings, dice) are illustrative fixtures, NOT computed from the real engine. The Rust port must decide per-test whether to assert against these exact constants (for frontend-contract tests) or against the real engine output. Mixing them up will produce false failures.

Two divergent item/exit schemas coexist: DEBUG.scene.items use {item_id,name,location,visible,portable,owner,details} while set_scene tool args use {id,name} for items and {id,name,destination,visible,blocked_by} for exits. The real backend's canonical schema is not fully knowable from this file alone — confirm against server.py/world.py before unifying.
Length-based id/seq generation (len+1) can produce duplicate ids after deletes; whether existing tests depend on this exact (buggy) behavior is unknown — verify before 'fixing' in Rust.

npc_speech omits 'reasoning' here but the real NPC JSON contract is described elsewhere as reasoning/speech/action/claims; the mock encodes reasoning as separate npc_thinking events. Confirm the real subagent module's exact JSON before porting the parser, since this file only shows the rendered/streamed shape, not the raw model output schema.

SETTINGS is a live mutable dict shared between /state, /models, /settings, and DEBUG.runtime by reference; POST /settings mutates it globally. Tests run against this server may see cross-test state bleed — the Rust port should consider per-test isolation or reset.

This file does not reveal the actual cache_reuse / KV-cache / llama prompt-cache logic, real compaction thresholds, CHARS_PER_TOKEN, or RAG behavior — those numbers here are display fixtures only. The authoritative invariants for prompt-cache prefix ordering and compaction live in other modules (e.g. server.py, world.py, the GM/NPC agent modules), not in mock_server.py.

The /turn live stream and seed_transcript tell slightly different stories (live uses n_maret, seed uses n_borin/n_liza); ensure the port keeps both fixtures distinct since the frontend may snapshot-test each.

subsystem: Web client API/SSE/timeline/TTS/dev-settings compatibility contract (the exact surface the Rust server must satisfy so the existing React frontend works unchanged)
purpose: Defines the exact HTTP + SSE wire contract between the React SPA and the Python backend, plus the client-only stores (timeline folding, per-message TTS playback, developer-mode visibility, status labels). The Rust+Tauri port must reproduce this byte-for-byte at the boundary: identical endpoint paths/methods, identical JSON field names, identical SSE event `kind` names and `data` shapes, identical settings object keys, identical TTS response headers (Content-Type audio/pcm vs decoded clip, X-Sample-Rate), and identical localStorage key/shape — otherwise the unmodified frontend breaks.

endpoints.response: index.html (text/html). Server side-effect: calls _dialog() to ensure an active dialog exists. Header Cache-Control: no-store, must-revalidate

state(dialog) dict: {model, backend, stream_gm_content, settings, settings_options, run_usage, context_usage, story_id, story_title, public, time, player_character (public=true), scene, entities, status_labels (world.WHEREABOUTS_STATUS_LABELS -> RU label map; consumed by statusContext StatusLabelsContext), npcs[] (public_npc shape), optional codex_auth if backend==codex}. context_usage is recomputed and overwritten just before sending.

debug_data(dialog): {ok, meta, runtime{settings, cache{prompt_cache_key, thread_id, store:false}}, story{id,title,objective,public_intro,hidden_truth,constraints,hidden_events}, scene, time, player_character(full), roll_override{next,all}, status_labels, facts[], state_records[], rumors[], npcs[] (full card incl. card_revision, mechanics, secret, persona, summary, commitments, history), memory{gm_summary, gm_messages, loaded_gm_tools[], events[-80:], pending, delivered}}. Consumed by DebugPanel.jsx.
{ok, model, models:[{id,name,supported,...}], settings, settings_options}. On error {ok:false, error, models:[]} with HTTP 400.
{events: [...replay_events]} — replayable event log with kind=='delta' filtered out (REPLAY_SKIP_KINDS) and NPC display names sanitized to current player labels. Fed to timeline.dispatchMany().
export_data(dialog) JSON as a downloadable blob; headers Content-Disposition attachment filename=gm-lab-export.json. Client api.export() saves it as gm-lab-export.json via object URL.

endpoints.request: JSON {brief?, story_id?, title?, activate? (default true)}. Requires brief OR story_id; unknown story_id -> 400.
endpoints.response: {ok, active_chat_id, chat:{...}, [state, transcript] if newly active}

endpoints.request: id is URL-encoded in path (encodeURIComponent on client)
endpoints.response: {ok, chat, state, transcript:{events}} or 404 {ok:false, error:'chat not found'}
{ok, deleted:true, active_chat_id, chats[], chat, state, transcript:{events}, embeddings_purged} or 404

endpoints.request: JSON {settings:{...}} (client api.updateSettings wraps as {settings}); server also accepts bare body if no 'settings' key
JSON {cmd, arg}. cmd in {reset, new, constraint, event}. 'new'+arg seeds a NEW chat; 'reset' rebuilds session from catalog story; 'constraint'/'event' append to world.

endpoints.response: text/event-stream; headers Cache-Control: no-cache, X-Accel-Buffering: no. Each SSE frame is 'data: ' + JSON + '\n\n'. Stream ends with a {kind:'done'} frame which the client SKIPS (does not dispatch). See events list. Errors during the run emit {kind:'error',agent:'ГМ',data:str}.

endpoints.request: RAW audio bytes (NOT JSON); Content-Type = blob.type || 'audio/webm'. Only when backend==codex (else 400).
endpoints.response: {ok:true, text} or {ok:false, error, status?}. Client transcribeAudio() throws if !resp.ok || !data.ok.

endpoints.request: JSON {text, stream?:bool, role?|voice?|npc_id?}. Client sends stripMarkup(text) plus seg.body which is {role:'gm'} or {voice:'male'|'female'}. Server resolves voice: voice in {gm,male,female} used directly; else role=='gm' or no npc_id -> 'gm'; else _npc_voice(npc_id) by pronouns.
endpoints.response: On cache HIT or non-stream: complete audio clip, Content-Type audio/ogg|audio/mpeg|audio/wav, Content-Length set, header X-TTS-Voice. On cache MISS + stream:true: head-first raw PCM16, Content-Type audio/pcm, headers X-Sample-Rate (default 24000), X-TTS-Voice, Cache-Control no-store. Client branches on Content-Type containing 'audio/pcm'. Errors -> {ok:false,error} with 400/503.
endpoints.streaming: yes for PCM miss path (chunked body, NOT SSE)

endpoints.request: JSON {next?, all?} (die override values; '' or null clears)
JSON {id, ...card/presence/whereabouts fields, reset_memory?}. Delegates to Session.apply_debug_edit.
JSON {patch:{...}} (client api.updateScene wraps; server falls back to bare body)

events.meaning: Every SSE turn event from run_turn/_drive is ev(kind,agent,data,sid). orchestrator.ev() ALWAYS emits all four keys (agent/sid may be null). timelineStore reads ev.kind, ev.agent (a), ev.data (d), ev.sid.
Terminal frame. streamTurn() explicitly does `if (ev.kind==='done') continue;` — it is NOT passed to onEvent/timeline. Pure stream terminator.
Player turn echo. timeline: push {type:'player', text:d}.

Streaming token delta. channel in {gm_thinking, gm_narration, npc_speech}. timeline appends to the live row keyed by sid: gm_thinking->'gm_think' row (text+=), gm_narration->'narration' row (text+=), npc_speech->existing 'npc' row (revealed:true, speech+=). delta events are FILTERED from /transcript replay (REPLAY_SKIP_KINDS).
Finalized GM reasoning. timeline: ensure 'gm_think' row by sid, set text=d (replaces deltas). Only if d.trim().
Finalized GM visible narration. timeline: ensure 'narration' row by sid, set text=d. Same sid as its deltas so it replaces them in place (one row live, one row on replay).
Per-model-call token/timing stats. data shape: {label, scope:'gm'|'npc'|'other', in, out, secs, cached, tps, prompt_secs, eval_secs, load_secs}. timeline: push {type:'meta', data:d}.

events.shape: {kind:'meta_total', agent:null, data:{calls:[meta...], in, out, cached, tokens, peak_context, secs, sys_estimate, context:context_usage(), run:run_usage}}
events.meaning: End-of-turn aggregate (yielded once after _drive). timeline: push {type:'meta_total', data:d}.

GM is invoking a tool. timeline: if name=='ask_player' -> ignored; else push {type:'tool', name:d.name, args:d.arguments, result:undefined}. Result filled later by the matching outcome event (attachResult).
Raw tool result text. timeline: explicitly NOT rendered (internal). The structured per-tool events below carry the renderable payload.

events.shape: {kind:'dice', agent:'ГМ', data:<roll payload dict OR detail string>}
events.meaning: roll_dice outcome. data is the full payload (ok,notation,roll_kind,target_kind,target_number,total,grade,margin,natural,sides,count,keep,rolls,kept,modifier,forced,detail,modifier_note?). timeline: if object -> attachResult('roll_dice', d, {resultLive:live}) else push standalone {type:'dice_roll', roll:d, resultLive:live}; resultLive=true only for a live roll so restored history snaps (no re-tumble).
get_world_fact result. timeline: if object -> toolResult('get_world_fact', d); else (legacy string) push {type:'fact', text:d}. MEMORY_TOOLS-gated in UI.
update_world_state result. timeline: toolResult('update_world_state', d).
query_world_state result. timeline: toolResult('query_world_state', d).
get_npc_profile result. timeline: toolResult('get_npc_profile', d).
advance_time result. timeline: toolResult('advance_time', d). PLAYER_TOOLS (player-safe).
update_player_character result. timeline: toolResult('update_player_character', d). PLAYER_TOOLS.
tool_search result. timeline: toolResult('tool_search', {text:d}). MEMORY_TOOLS.

set_scene/move_npc/set_npc_presence/scene_sync. timeline: if d.title||d.scene_id -> push {type:'scene_update', scene_id,title,description,present_npcs}; else push {type:'scene_update', name,present,present_npcs}.
set_npc_whereabouts (player-facing payload). timeline: push {type:'npc_whereabouts', name,present,current_scene,whereabouts}.

An NPC begins responding. timeline: ensure 'npc' row by sid: {type:'npc', sid, name:a, npc_id:d.npc_id, speech:'', typing:true, revealed:false, action:null, claims:null, hidden:null}. agent is the player-facing LABEL not true name.

events.shape: {kind:'npc_thinking', agent:<npc true name>, data:<reasoning text>, sid}
