events.meaning: NPC hidden reasoning. timeline: update 'npc' row by sid {hidden:d}. Hidden from players (npcInternals flag).

events.shape: {kind:'npc_speech', agent:<npc_label>, data:{speech, action, claims, npc_id}, sid}
events.meaning: Finalized NPC turn. timeline: update 'npc' row {typing:false, revealed:true, speech:d.speech, action:d.action||null, claims:d.claims||[]}.
NPC private-memory debug snapshot. NOT handled by timelineStore.applyEvent (no branch) -> silently ignored by the timeline; debug-only.
GM sent an NPC draft back for a redraft. timeline: push {type:'reject', name:a, reason:d}. gmThoughts-gated.
ask_player buttons. NOT handled by timelineStore.applyEvent -> the timeline ignores it; consumed elsewhere (Composer/App) — note the ask_player gm_tool_call is also suppressed in timeline.
Error notice. timeline: push {type:'error', agent:a, text:d}.

payloadShapes: settings object (runtime_settings.get(), echoed in /state.settings, /settings, /models): per-role keys for each role in {gm,npc,compact}: `${role}_reasoning_effort`, `${role}_reasoning_summary`; plus text_verbosity, tool_choice, stream_gm_content(bool), parallel_tool_calls(bool), gm_suggest_options(bool), tts_enabled(bool), tts_autoplay(bool), max_tool_hops(int, 0=unlimited), max_output_tokens(int, 0=default). Persisted to gm_lab_settings.json (NOT per-chat).

settings_options (validation enums for the UI): {reasoning_efforts[], reasoning_summaries[], reasoning_roles[]=['gm','npc','compact'], text_verbosities[], tool_choices[], max_tool_hops_max, max_output_tokens_max=200000}.

public_npc (state.npcs[]): {id, name(=label), label, known_name, public_label, role(public), pronouns(public gender), color, physical_type, distinctive_features, condition, life_status('alive' default)}. Player-facing only — never name/secret/persona.

context_usage (state.context_usage and meta_total.context): {current, world, next_compact{scope,label,used,limit,remaining}, gm{active,history,summary,limit,remaining}, npc{id,name,active,history,summary,limit,remaining,color,has_session}, npcs:[...per-npc entries]}.
run_usage / _empty_usage (state.run_usage): {turns,calls,in,out,cached,tokens,secs,peak_context,gm_calls,gm_tokens,npc_calls,npc_tokens,other_calls,other_tokens}.
_meta (meta event data): {label, scope:'gm'|'npc'|'other', in, out, secs, cached, tps, prompt_secs, eval_secs, load_secs}.
_meta_total (meta_total event data): {calls:[meta...], in, out, cached, tokens, peak_context, secs, sys_estimate, context, run}.

dice payload (dice event + roll_dice tool): {ok, notation, roll_kind, target_kind, target_number, total, grade, margin, natural, sides, count, keep, rolls, kept, modifier, forced, detail, modifier_note?}. grade in {invalid, ungraded, success, critical_success, critical_failure, ...}. natural set only for single-die d20.

NPC JSON contract (NPC_SCHEMA in agents.py, REQUIRED): {reasoning:str, speech:str, action:str, claims:str[]}. orchestrator parses out['reasoning'|'speech'|'action'|'claims']. npc_speech event renames to {speech,action,claims,npc_id}; ask_npc tool payload uses npc-facing renames speech_ru/action_ru.
chat row (_chat_response / list_chats / /chats): {id, title (default 'Новый чат'), preview, turn_count, created_at, updated_at, active}.

DialogRuntime fields (per-chat persisted): {chat_id, transcript:list[{turn,event}], turn_count, title, preview, created_at, updated_at}. Frontend never sees the raw row; only via /chats, /state, /transcript.

devSettings localStorage (client-only, key 'gmlab.devSettings'): {developerMode:bool (default false), flags:{tokenCards,messageTokens,toolCalls,gmThoughts,npcInternals,memoryOps,historyDebug} each bool (default true when dev on)}. Pure presentation, never sent to backend.

scene_export / time_export / player_character_export / entity_refs: nested objects embedded in /state and /debug; consumed by Scene.jsx and DebugPanel — field names must be preserved verbatim (not enumerated here; copy from world.py *_export methods during port).
invariants: CHARS_PER_TOKEN = int(GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN env, default 3). Single source for _estimate_tokens(text)=max(0,len(text)//3), _SYS_EST=len(prompts.GM_SYSTEM)//3, and the GM/NPC compaction gates. The Rust port MUST use integer floor division by 3 (configurable) — not a tokenizer — to keep context_usage numbers identical.

GM compaction (_maybe_compact): triggers only when _messages_tokens(gm_messages) >= GM_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 100000). Then if number of user-role boundaries > GM_KEEP_TURNS (default 3): cut = starts[-GM_KEEP_TURNS]; old=msgs[:cut] summarized into gm_summary via client.summarize(base, proper_nouns), recent=msgs[cut:] kept VERBATIM. base = (gm_summary + '\n' + old_text).strip(). After compaction session.reset_world_query_cache(). Compaction is a DELIBERATE cache boundary.

NPC compaction (_maybe_compact_npc): per-NPC, triggers when _messages_tokens(npc_messages[id]) >= NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 64000) AND user-boundary count > NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES (default 6). cut=starts[-NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES]; old summarized via NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM (reasoning_role=ROLE_COMPACT, think=True), recent kept verbatim. Summary input truncated to COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS (default 12000).

Append-only history is the cache invariant: gm_messages and npc_messages[id] grow by append only; assistant_msg is echoed verbatim into history ('каноничный echo'). Old messages are NEVER rewritten — only summarized-then-dropped at a compact boundary. Static rules first, dynamic (current NPC card, scene, roster, facts, player action) last (CURRENT TURN CONTEXT / CURRENT NPC CARD). See prompt_cache_architecture.md.

Prompt-cache prefix ordering (provider-agnostic): tools -> system -> messages. NPC system prompt must be FULLY STATIC (no per-character persona/voice/goals/knowledge/secret); the character card is injected LATE as 'CURRENT NPC CARD, revision N' AFTER summary/history with an explicit override rule ('current card overrides older memory on conflict'). Tool schemas must NOT contain dynamic enums of npc_id/present_npcs/locations — only string fields, validated at execution.

card_revision: int default 0 on NPC; bumped on substantive card-field edits; surfaced in /debug npcs[].card_revision and get_npc_profile/update_player_character payloads. Old snapshots without it default to 0 (no SQLite migration). Not a magic cache key — only shrinks the recomputed tail to the late card block.

Deterministic dice seeding: World.dice_seed = random.SystemRandom().getrandbits(64) generated ONCE per campaign from real OS entropy (_new_dice_seed). self._rng = random.Random(self.dice_seed); all rolls thereafter use self._rng.randint(1,sides) so a campaign's roll sequence is reproducible from its seed. Rust port: must persist dice_seed and use a SEEDED PRNG (e.g. a deterministic Mersenne-Twister or rand_chacha) per campaign — NOT a fresh OS RNG per roll. forced_die_next (one-shot, consumed after one roll) and forced_die_all (sticky) override the RNG for debug; forced face clamped to [1,sides].

Dice notation regex: ^\s*(\d*)d(\d+)\s*(k[hl]\s*\d+)?\s*([+-]\s*\d+)?\s*$ (case-insensitive). count default 1; kh=keep highest, kl=keep lowest; natural set only when sides==20 and exactly one kept die. grade from margin = total-target_number; attack natural 20 -> critical_success, natural 1 -> critical_failure; roll_kind must be in {check,save,attack,contest} with a target_number else grade='ungraded'.

Secret / hidden-canon isolation: hidden_truth (world.canon) and NPC secret/persona NEVER appear in /state, public_npc, RAG public corpus, or GM public context. /state.public is the public intro only; secrets only in /debug.story.hidden_truth and /debug.npcs[].secret. hidden_events must NOT leak via get_world_fact public fallback (flagged as a risk in the architecture doc — make actor-safe). _player_facing_payload swaps NPC true name for the player label in streamed events while the GM-facing tool result keeps the true name. server._sanitize_player_name resolves stored true names to current labels on replay.

thread-id / prompt_cache_key stability: each Session carries client_session_id + client_thread_id (Codex/OAuth). debug cache.prompt_cache_key = config.CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY or session.client_thread_id; store:false always. Empty CODEX_PROMPT_CACHE_KEY => CodexClient uses the stable per-dialog thread-id. Each NPC has its OWN client/thread via npc_clients[id] + serialized npc_client_state[id]={model,session_id,thread_id} so its cache prefix is independent. On model change, session/thread ids are refreshed and every npc client is re-remembered. On backend mismatch, ids are wiped.

Explicit-only NPC memory reset (reset_npc_memory): NEVER automatic on a card edit. Clears npc_messages[id], npc_summaries[id], npc_client_state[id], npc_clients[id], commitments[id], pending[id], and PINS delivered[id]=_shown[id]=current _seq (so a reset NPC starts from the present, not re-surfacing all past events). Returns True for any real NPC, False only for unknown/empty id. apply_debug_edit touches presence ONLY on an actual present-state change (a card-only save never clobbers visible/can_hear/activity).
NPC lean-context caps: _OBS_CAP=12 (observation lines), _COMMIT_BLOCKS=8 (own-memory commitment blocks kept in prompt and in commitments[id][-8:]). EVENTS_CAP=400 events in memory, RUMORS_CAP=80.

SSE framing: 'data: ' + json.dumps(ev, ensure_ascii=False) + '

'. Client splits on '

', requires the 'data: ' prefix (6 chars sliced). Non-ascii is sent raw (UTF-8, not escaped). {kind:'done'} terminates and is skipped client-side.

Timeline live-vs-replay distinction: dispatch(ev) sets live=true (single event, rAF-coalesced render; live dice animate with resultLive:true). dispatchMany(events) sets live=false (batch restore, single render; historical dice snap with resultLive:false). beginTurn() drops liveIdx (streaming targets) but keeps messages. The sid+'|'+type key (gm_think/narration/npc) is how deltas find their target row; a turn re-uses sids so finalized events replace deltas in place.

ask_player special-casing: its gm_tool_call event is suppressed in the timeline (return early), and its result is delivered via a separate player_options event (not folded into the timeline store). roll_dice and ask_npc are 'visible prelude' tools that may trigger a generated pre-tool narration under a fresh sid.

Tool visibility gating (devSettings, client-only): MEMORY_TOOLS={get_world_fact,query_world_state,update_world_state,get_npc_profile,tool_search} hidden unless memoryOps. PLAYER_TOOLS={roll_dice,advance_time,update_player_character} shown compact when toolCalls hidden. toolMode() returns full|result|player|hidden. This NEVER changes what the model sees — purely presentational.
platformConcerns: index.html is a single inlined bundle (no cache-busting filename); served with Cache-Control: no-store, must-revalidate. The Rust server must serve the built SPA the same way (or Tauri loads it locally).

TTS micro-service is an EXTERNAL HTTP service at GM_TTS_URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8765), endpoints /speak (returns WAV) and /speak_stream (returns chunked raw PCM16 with X-Sample-Rate header). The Python server PROXIES it. Rust port must reproduce the proxy + on-disk cache, or call the same service.

ffmpeg subprocess: _compress_audio pipes WAV through `ffmpeg -f wav -i pipe:0 ... pipe:1` to ogg(libopus 32k)/mp3(libmp3lame 56k)/wav. Falls back to raw WAV if ffmpeg missing/fails. Rust port should shell out to ffmpeg cross-platform OR use a Rust opus/lame encoder; format selected by GM_TTS_FORMAT (default ogg).
TTS disk cache: tts_cache/<sha1(voice+'\n'+text)>.<ext>; atomic write via tmp+os.replace. Lookup tries configured ext then wav. Rust: same key scheme so cache survives the port.

Web Audio API on the client: iOS/WebKit routes Web Audio to the EARPIECE when an audio session is play-and-record (after getUserMedia/mic). ttsStore sets navigator.audioSession.type='playback' to force the loudspeaker. AudioContext must be resumed inside a user gesture (ttsUnlock). PCM16 -> Float32 (i16/32768), mono, sample-rate from X-Sample-Rate header (default 24000). This is all client-side and unchanged by the port.

Codex OAuth (backend==codex default): /transcribe and /codex/login|logout|status depend on ChatGPT Codex OAuth (codex_oauth, codex_transcribe). Tokens stored in local app data, not repo. The port must replicate the OAuth/browser flow or gate these endpoints (return 400 when backend!=codex, as today). config.CODEX_* identify the client (client_id, version, originator 'codex_cli_rs', user agent). Note: real codex uses TLS impersonation in the original CLI; verify llm_client/codex_client for curl_cffi usage during port (not in the 5 mapped files).

HTTPS sniffing server (_SniffingHTTPSServer): a SECOND listener on GM_HTTPS_PORT (default 8443) auto-enabled when GM_HOST=0.0.0.0 (LAN/phone), needed because mobile browsers require a secure context for the mic. Peeks first byte: 0x16 -> TLS handshake wrap; plaintext -> 308 redirect to https. Auto self-signed cert via tls_cert.ensure_self_signed (.tls dir) unless GM_TLS_CERT/GM_TLS_KEY given. Tauri likely sidesteps this (local app), but a LAN-exposed Rust server mode must reproduce it.
webbrowser.open(url) only when GM_OPEN_BROWSER=1. Subprocess (ffmpeg), sockets (HTTP/HTTPS), ssl, urllib for TTS proxy — all need cross-platform Rust equivalents (Windows/macOS/Linux).

SQLite persistence: gm_lab_dialogs.sqlite3 (GM_DIALOG_DB), gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 (RAG), gm_lab_settings.json (settings), tts_cache/ dir, .tls/ dir. All under the project dir (HERE) by default. Rust port must pick OS-appropriate app-data dirs (or keep env overrides).

Server is single shared chat scope by default (GM_CHAT_SCOPE_ID='shared'); all browsers share one active dialog protected by a per-dialog lock. Concurrency model (ThreadingHTTPServer + dialog.lock) must be preserved: a /turn holds the lock for the whole streamed turn.
rustNotes: JSON: serde_json with structs that serialize ALL fields including nulls for the event envelope {kind,agent,data,sid} (Python ev() always includes them). Use #[serde(rename_all=...)] sparingly — field names are snake_case and must match byte-for-byte (e.g. card_revision, present_npcs, speech_ru, current_scene, sys_estimate). For the polymorphic `data` field use serde_json::Value or an untagged enum.

Tauri: the React SPA is unchanged; serve it via the Tauri webview and route fetch('/...') to either an embedded axum server (recommended, preserves the contract verbatim) or Tauri commands behind a fetch shim. Embedded axum on 127.0.0.1 is the lowest-risk path because /turn streaming, /tts binary responses, and /transcribe raw-body all rely on real HTTP semantics.

Token estimation: implement exactly `text.chars().count() / CHARS_PER_TOKEN` with integer division? NOTE: Python uses len(str) which counts Unicode code points (str length), so use chars().count() not bytes().len() to match. Default CHARS_PER_TOKEN=3, env-overridable.

Dice: per-campaign seed = OS entropy once (rand::random::<u64>() / getrandbits(64) equivalent), persisted; rolls from a seeded PRNG. Python's random.Random is a Mersenne-Twister — exact roll-for-roll reproduction across the port would require an MT19937 crate seeded identically, but since seeds are persisted per campaign and new campaigns get fresh entropy, a deterministic seeded PRNG suffices unless replaying old campaigns must reproduce historical roll streams (it does NOT — rolls are recorded in the transcript). Use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng or similar; just keep forced_die_next/all semantics.

TTS proxy: reqwest streaming for /speak_stream (read chunks, forward to client, accumulate, then compress+cache once complete and client still connected). Set Content-Type audio/pcm + X-Sample-Rate on the streamed path; the decoded-clip path sets the compressed Content-Type + Content-Length + X-TTS-Voice. The client's branch is `Content-Type.includes('audio/pcm')` — preserve that exact header.

Settings persistence: a simple JSON file mirror of runtime_settings (gm_lab_settings.json) with an in-process RwLock cache; reconcile_for_model logic must be ported (it clamps reasoning effort/summary to model capabilities). Keys are flat snake_case including per-role `${role}_reasoning_effort/summary` for roles gm/npc/compact.

Persistence: rusqlite for the dialog store and RAG cache. The frontend contract does not depend on the SQLite schema (it only sees /chats, /state, /transcript, /debug, /export shapes), so the schema can be redesigned as long as those JSON shapes are reproduced.

Markup stripping for TTS (stripMarkup) is CLIENT-side already (ttsStore.js) — no Rust work needed; but if the server ever pre-strips, mirror the same regexes (entity refs [[type:id|label]], markdown).

Self-signed TLS for LAN mode: rustls + rcgen to generate a self-signed cert into a .tls dir; implement the byte-peek TLS-vs-plaintext sniff + 308 redirect if LAN exposure is a target. For pure Tauri desktop, skip.
risks: I read api.js, ttsStore.js, timelineStore.js, devSettings.js, statusContext.js IN FULL, plus server.py in full and the load-bearing parts of orchestrator.py/config.py/world.py/agents.py/runtime_settings.py. I did NOT fully read llm_client.py, codex_client.py, codex_oauth.py, agents.py prompt builders, or world.py *_export methods — exact field names inside scene_export/time_export/player_character_export/entity_refs and the Codex TLS-impersonation details (curl_cffi?) must be re-read during the port. The user's memory mentions curl_cffi TLS impersonation; I did not confirm where it lives.

player_options and npc_history events are emitted by run_turn but have NO branch in timelineStore.applyEvent — they are silently ignored by the timeline. player_options is presumably consumed by Composer.jsx/App.jsx/Chat.jsx (the grep hit those files); I did not read those components, so the exact consumer of player_options buttons and how the chosen option is sent back (likely another /turn with the option.message) is unconfirmed.

The exact set of GM tool names and their full JSON-schema argument shapes (build_gm_tools in agents.py) was only partially sampled (NPC_SCHEMA confirmed). The port needs the complete tool definitions; I saw the dispatch in _run_tool (roll_dice, get_world_fact, update_world_state, query_world_state, get_npc_profile, advance_time, update_player_character, set_npc_whereabouts, move_npc/set_npc_presence, set_scene, ask_npc, ask_player, tool_search) but not every argument schema.

DialogStore SQLite DDL (CREATE TABLE statements) was not located in my grep window — the chats table columns are inferred from list_chats SELECT (chat_id,title,preview,turn_count,created_at,updated_at) and an active_chat mapping per guest/scope. The full schema and the Session<->row (de)serialization (snapshot of world/session) must be read from dialog_store.py before porting persistence.

grade enum values: I confirmed invalid/ungraded/critical_success/critical_failure and that _grade_from_margin produces graded values from margin, but did not read _grade_from_margin's full thresholds (success/failure tiers). Re-read world.py around line 2430+ for the complete grade ladder.

The /export and export_data shape is large (world snapshot, gm_messages, transcript, events, rumors, state_records, npc_* maps) — the frontend only downloads it as an opaque file, so it is not strictly a client-contract constraint, but if any import/round-trip feature exists it must be preserved.

context_usage 'next_compact' picks the candidate with the smallest 'remaining' across GM + NPCs-with-sessions; the UI token cards (devSettings tokenCards/messageTokens flags) render these numbers, so the per-field math (gm_active = _SYS_EST + world_tokens + gm_summary + gm_history) must be ported exactly to avoid the counters drifting from the Python original.

subsystem: test_contracts.py — the executable contract/invariant suite for the GM-Lab orchestrator (prompt assembly, prompt-cache layering, history/NPC compaction, tool schema staticness, secret/scope isolation, RAG, dialog persistence shapes, deterministic dice, NPC reset/visibility). This is the spec the Rust+Tauri port must satisfy.
purpose: test_contracts.py is a single top-level (non-pytest) assertion script run end to end (`python test_contracts.py` → prints "ALL CONTRACT TESTS PASSED"). It imports the real modules with GM_BACKEND=mock and asserts the behavioral contracts that cross the model boundary: (1) the static-prefix vs late-mutable-tail layering that prompt/KV caches depend on; (2) what compaction summarizes vs keeps verbatim and the thresholds that trigger it; (3) that tool JSON schemas are STATIC (no dynamic enums / no NPC names) so the tool-definition prefix is cacheable; (4) scope-based secret isolation (gm/npc/shared/public) across query_world_state, retrieval_documents (RAG corpus) and prompts; (5) the deterministic dice + grading contract; (6) the dialog_store persisted shapes incl. card_revision; (7) NPC memory reset semantics and debug-edit presence guards; (8) the tool-result two-channel (full vs model) wrapper and per-turn already_delivered de-dup. Every `assert` is an invariant to port. The map below quotes the constants and exact conditions.

endpoints.path: test is a script, not a server
endpoints.request: Runs run_turn()/_run_tool() in-process against a mock client; no HTTP. The contracts it asserts are consumed by server.py endpoints (e.g. /turn SSE, /debug/npc, /settings) which are out of scope for this file.
endpoints.response: stdout 'ALL CONTRACT TESTS PASSED' on success; AssertionError otherwise.
endpoints.streaming: run_turn(session, player_text) is a generator yielding event dicts (see events). _run_tool(session,name,args,metas) is a generator that yields stream events and returns a ToolExecutionResult via StopIteration.value (driven by helper _drive).

events.meaning: Streamed GM narration chunk. Contract: when runtime_settings.stream_gm_content_enabled() is True the deltas equal the raw streamed chunks ['Поток ','идёт ','сразу.']; when False they are coalesced into ONE delta ['Поток идёт сразу.']. Port must honor the stream_gm_content toggle exactly.
Final/visible GM text (incl. pre-tool prelude). Ordering contract: a pre-tool prelude gm_narration is emitted BEFORE the gm_tool_call it precedes (prelude_idx < tool_idx).
Canonicalized GM tool call. arguments are the NORMALIZED args (null/empty stripped). e.g. data == {name:'tool_search', arguments:{query:'select:set_scene'}} (max_results:None dropped).

Tool result event carries the FULL machine JSON in data (no <system-reminder>), while the message added to gm_messages (role:'tool') carries the MODEL text (compact, with <system-reminder>). ask_player is special: it emits NO tool_result event and NO gm_tool_call (handled engine-side).

ask_player buttons. Emitted only when >=4 valid options; ordering: player_options before the final gm_narration. If GM omits ask_player when enabled, an 'error' event ('без ask_player') is emitted and NO player_options/gm_tool_call(ask_player).
Emitted by roll_dice (any event with kind=='dice' must be present after a roll).
Emitted by ask_npc on success.
Emitted by move_npc/set_scene/scene_sync.
Emitted by set_npc_whereabouts.
Emitted by update_player_character.
Tool/argument validation error surfaced as event (e.g. ask_npc missing 'situation' → e['data'] contains 'situation').

payloadShapes:
ToolExecutionResult: object with .full (str: full machine JSON, NO <system-reminder>), .model (str: compact structured text shown to model, MAY contain trailing '

<system-reminder>...</system-reminder>'), and .terminal (bool, default False; ask_player results are non-terminal). Helpers: _tool_full_text→.full, _tool_model_text→.model, model text stripped of reminders via split('

<system-reminder>',1)[0]. CONTRACT: model text must be non-empty, must NOT start with '{' or '[', and must NOT parse as raw JSON (it is structured text like 'WORLD FACT
...', 'RESULT: total 20, success, margin +0, natural 17.').

GM request message list (_gm_request_messages → [system, system, optional system, *gm_messages]): [0]={role:'system', content==prompts.GM_SYSTEM (the STATIC cacheable prefix)}, [1]={role:'system', content=_gm_world_setup = 'WORLD SETUP...

PUBLIC INTRO:
'+world.public}, optional [2]={role:'system', content='STORY SO FAR (compact): '+summary}, then gm_messages (history), last is role:'user' (the turn context).

GM turn context (_gm_turn_context, the LATE mutable user turn, in this exact section order): 'CURRENT TURN CONTEXT' header → 'TIME STATE' (world.time_context) → 'INTERNAL NPC ROSTER' (lines 'id=<id>; internal_name=<name>; player_label=<label>; role=<role>; род=<gender>') → 'CURRENT PUBLIC FACTS' (only fact_records.kind=='public', first 12) → 'PLAYER CHARACTER CARD' (player_character_context, INCLUDES gm_notes) → 'CURRENT SCENE STATE' (scene_context) → 'ENTITY REFERENCE MARKUP' → 'SCENE CONSTRAINTS' → 'PLAYER OPTION SUGGESTIONS' (enabled/disabled text) → TURN_RESOLUTION_CHECKLIST (<system-reminder>) → 'PLAYER ACTION'. Hard ordering: index('TURN RESOLUTION CHECK') < index('PLAYER ACTION') and index('<system-reminder>') < index('PLAYER ACTION').

NPC request message list (npc_request_messages → [system, optional system summary, *history, final user]): [0]={role:'system', content=NPC_SYSTEM (static, carries NO concrete card data, NO 'Russian grammatical gender marker: M')}, optional [1]={role:'system', content='YOUR PRIVATE MEMORY SO FAR (compact):
'+summary}, history messages rewritten so prior 'CURRENT SITUATION...' → 'PREVIOUS NPC SITUATION...' and user-history prefixed 'HISTORICAL NPC EXCHANGE...'; LAST message role:'user' with the CURRENT NPC CARD prepended (npc_card_block + '

' + situation). Card lives in the LATE turn so a card edit invalidates only the tail. History messages must NOT contain 'CURRENT NPC CARD'.

NPC JSON contract (_norm_npc output, the cross-boundary NPC shape): {reasoning:str, speech:str, action:str, claims:list[str]}. Coercion contract: reasoning None→'', speech trimmed ('  да  '→'да'), action coerced to str (12→'12'), claims non-list→[]. All JSON values are RUSSIAN per NPC_SYSTEM.

roll_dice normalized args (after _normalize_tool_calls): null/none fields dropped; stakes sub-object drops null members ({intent,success,failure:None,complication:None}→{intent,success}); a fully-null stakes/difficulty/modifier_note ungraded damage roll collapses to {roll_kind,notation,check_name,reason}. roll_dice required=[roll_kind,notation,check_name,reason]; roll_kind enum=[check,save,attack,damage,chance,contest]; target_kind enum=[DC,AC,opposed_total]; difficulty_label enum has NO 'none'; modifier_note desc forbids 'none/unknown'.

update_world_state item schema (state_item_schema, additionalProperties False): properties include type(enum=[fact,rumor,npc_memory,relationship,goal]), op(enum=[add,update,delete]), scope(enum=[public,gm,npc,shared]), text, npc_id, target, entity_id, source_npc, participants, known_name, location_id, location_name, region_id, region_name, scene_id, importance, aliases, expected_hash, mode(enum=['replace']). 'source' is NOT a property (intentionally removed). Required=[items]. strict:false.
query_world_state args: required=[scope,query]; scope enum=[player,gm,npc]; additionalProperties False.
get_npc_profile args: required=[npc_id]; preset enum=[visible,social,mechanics,status,identity] (NO 'private_npc'); fields.items.enum INCLUDES 'abilities' but EXCLUDES 'secret','goals'.
ask_player args: required=[question,options]; options minItems=4 maxItems=8; each option required=[label,message], additionalProperties False.

Persisted PlayerCharacter payload (dialog_store, line 875+): includes card_revision:int. NPC payload (_npc_to_payload line 1004): includes card_revision:int. _npc_from_payload defaults card_revision to 0 on missing/invalid. Minimal NPC and NPC(persona='') both start card_revision==0.

state_record (world.state_records[*] — present when world has attribute 'state_records'): fields text, kind(fact/rumor/npc_memory/relationship/goal/goal etc.), scope (NOTE: shared scope is stored as scope=='participants'), owner, subject, entity_id, source_npc, location_id, location_name, region_id, region_name, scene_id, importance, aliases (list). retrieval_documents(actor) returns docs with .text, .kind (kind=='truth' NEVER appears in any corpus).
invariants: PROMPT-CACHE PREFIX ORDERING (the spine of the port): GM messages = [STATIC system (prompts.GM_SYSTEM), STATIC world-setup system (PUBLIC INTRO only), optional compact-summary system, append-only history, LATE user turn]. _gm_system() returns prompts.GM_SYSTEM unchanged regardless of world/summary args — it must be byte-identical across turns. _gm_world_setup() contains ONLY world.public (public intro); it explicitly must NOT contain 'NAMED NPC ROSTER' or 'PUBLIC FACTS' (those moved to the late tail). The static system MUST contain literal section markers incl. 'STATIC PROMPT CACHE CONTRACT' and 'Mutable data arrives later in CURRENT TURN CONTEXT'. Rationale (from code comments): prompt/KV caches only hit identical prefixes; per-turn edits (NPC rename/add/remove, player-sheet edit, /debug/fact) must invalidate ONLY the late turn tail, never the cached prefix.

MUTABLE DATA LIVES IN THE LATE TURN, NOT THE PREFIX: the INTERNAL NPC ROSTER and CURRENT PUBLIC FACTS appear in _gm_turn_context (the user turn) and must NOT appear in _gm_world_setup. The roster format is 'id=borin; internal_name=Борин; player_label=Борин' and 'id=lysa; internal_name=Лиза; player_label=служанка' built from LIVE world state (no hardcoded names). The header is 'INTERNAL NPC ROSTER' (NOT 'CURRENT NAMED NPC ROSTER').

NPC CARD IS THE LATE TURN, AFTER HISTORY: static NPC system carries NO concrete data — npc.persona/knowledge/secret must NOT be in npc_system, and the formatted 'Russian grammatical gender marker: M' must NOT be in system. The card (npc_card_block: 'CURRENT NPC CARD', 'Gender: M', 'Physical type:', 'Personality:', 'Mechanics:', '"passive_perception":13', persona text, 'This card overrides older memory') leads the LAST user message. Order with history+summary: [0]=system, [1]=summary ('SUMMARY MARKER'), [2]=history user rewritten with prefix 'HISTORICAL NPC EXCHANGE', [3]=history assistant verbatim, [-1]=user with card. History messages [2:4] must NOT contain 'CURRENT NPC CARD'. Stored old 'CURRENT SITUATION...' becomes 'PREVIOUS NPC SITUATION'.

TOOL SCHEMA STATICNESS (cacheable tool-definition prefix): for EVERY tool — description must NOT leak roster ('Available NPCs' forbidden), must NOT name an NPC ('borin'/'Борин' forbidden); if a tool has npc_id property it must be type:'string' with NO 'enum' (no dynamic enum); location_id/location properties must have NO 'enum'. set_scene.items/exits item props must have NO enum. set_npc_whereabouts.status enum is the STATIC [known,likely,rumored,unknown]. This guarantees the JSON tool-definitions block is identical across turns/worlds so it stays in the cache prefix.

INITIAL vs FULL tool sets: initial_gm_tool_names() == exactly {ask_npc, roll_dice, get_world_fact, query_world_state, update_world_state, update_player_character, advance_time, tool_search}. set_scene and get_npc_profile are HIDDEN initially and loaded via tool_search ('select:get_npc_profile' loads it; keyword 'перейти новая сцена локация' loads set_scene). tool_search('select:already_loaded_tool') returns loaded_tools==[] and already_loaded==[that tool]. include_player_options_tool adds ask_player. Session.loaded_gm_tools starts == initial_gm_tool_names().

GM HISTORY COMPACTION (_maybe_compact): TRIGGER only when _messages_tokens(gm_messages) >= config.GM_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 100000) AND number of user-message starts > config.GM_KEEP_TURNS (default 3). cut = starts[-GM_KEEP_TURNS]; old=msgs[:cut] summarized via client.summarize(prev_summary+'\n'+old_text, proper_nouns=world.proper_nouns()); recent=msgs[cut:] kept VERBATIM. KEEP the last GM_KEEP_TURNS turns byte-for-byte (KV/prefix reuse). _msg_text_for_summary strips the 'PLAYER ACTION (latest user input, free roleplay text):' prefix from user messages so only the player's raw text is summarized (roster/scene/card stripped: 'INTERNAL NPC ROSTER','CURRENT SCENE STATE','internal_name=Лиза' must NOT appear in summary text). COMMENT-stated rationale: compact LATE, at ~100k, to keep append-only history and reuse the same prefix as long as possible.

COMPACTION RESETS THE PER-TURN QUERY DE-DUP CACHE: _maybe_compact calls session.reset_world_query_cache() (world_query_seen={}). Tested: with GM_HISTORY_TOKENS=1 and GM_KEEP_TURNS=1 forcing a compact, world_query_seen goes from non-empty to {} and a previously-already_delivered gm query returns the row again (status 'known'). The already_delivered de-dup is scoped to the ACTIVE, not-yet-compacted GM tail.

NPC HISTORY COMPACTION (_maybe_compact_npc): TRIGGER when _messages_tokens(npc_messages[id]) >= config.NPC_HISTORY_TOKENS (default 64000) AND user-starts > config.NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES (default 6). cut=starts[-NPC_KEEP_EXCHANGES]; old summarized via _summarize_npc_history (prompts.NPC_COMPACT_SYSTEM, think=True, reasoning_role=config.ROLE_COMPACT, input truncated to config.COMPACT_INPUT_CHARS=12000); recent kept verbatim. Summary stored in npc_summaries[id].

TOKEN ESTIMATION HEURISTIC: _estimate_tokens(text) = max(0, len(text)//config.CHARS_PER_TOKEN), CHARS_PER_TOKEN default 3 (env GM_CHARS_PER_TOKEN). _SYS_EST = len(prompts.GM_SYSTEM)//CHARS_PER_TOKEN (computed once at import). context_usage gm.active = _SYS_EST + world_tokens + gm_summary_tokens + gm_history_tokens; gm.limit = GM_HISTORY_TOKENS; remaining = max(0, limit - history). next_compact = the candidate (gm or per-npc) with the smallest 'remaining'. ctx['npcs'] has one entry per world NPC with 'has_session' flag.

DETERMINISTIC DICE: World.forced_die_next:int|None is a ONE-SHOT override consumed by the next roll (cleared to None immediately after, line 2321-2322). forced_die_all is a sticky fallback. Forced face is clamped max(1,min(sides,forced)). '[forced]' must NEVER appear in returned detail (forced is internal-only). Notation parser regex: '(\d*)d(\d+)(k[hl]\d+)?([+-]\d+)?'. kh = keep highest ('keep highest 1'), kl = keep lowest ('keep lowest 1'). natural is reported only for a single-kept d20.

DICE GRADING (roll_for_outcome): returns detail containing grade=<...>, margin=<signed>, natural=<n>. Grades observed: success (margin +0 at total==DC), weak_failure (total 12 vs DC15 → margin -3), critical_success (natural 20 attack regardless of AC → margin computed vs AC, e.g. -15), critical_failure (natural 1 attack → margin +11), ungraded (damage roll). margin sign convention: for checks/attacks margin = total - target. Crit is driven by natural 20/1 on attack rolls.

SECRET / SCOPE ISOLATION — the core safety invariant. update_world_state scope semantics: public=anyone; gm=hidden author truth; npc=only that npc; shared=ONLY npc_id + target and/or participants (stored internally as scope=='participants', owner=npc_id, subject=target). query_world_state(scope='player') must NEVER reveal gm-scope, npc-private, or goal text (GM_SECRET_SENTINEL / NPC_PRIVATE_SENTINEL / GOAL_SENTINEL all absent from player results AND model text) and must never expose ids/hashes for non-shared rows. SHARED rows ARE visible to player scope and to each participant's npc scope but NOT to non-participant NPCs (borin's shared-to-lysa rumor: visible to lysa npc scope, NOT to borin). retrieval_documents (RAG corpus) enforce the same: player corpus has SHARED+ENTITY facts but not NPC_PRIVATE; borin corpus has NPC_PRIVATE+ENTITY but not the shared-to-lysa rumor; public corpus has neither. Multi-participant merge: adding the same text with overlapping participants MERGES (status 'merged', same id, participants unioned); exact duplicate → ok:false, status 'not_added', existing_id returned.

ENTITY IDENTITY / known_name: known_name requires entity_id (update without entity_id → ok:false 'entity_id is required'); known_name is for NPC entity_id values only, 'Never use for the player'. After fact with entity_id=lysa known_name='Лиза', world.npc_known_name('lysa')=='Лиза' and npc_player_label flips from 'служанка' to 'Лиза', and entity_refs label updates. shared target must be 'player' or a known npc_id (target='turnvale_square' → ok:false 'target for shared scope must be player or a known npc_id').

PER-TURN already_delivered DE-DUP (get_world_fact AND query_world_state): first lookup returns status 'known' with sources/results; an immediate repeat of the same query returns status 'already_delivered', already_delivered>=1, NO sources/results, text contains 'already delivered'. Adding a NEW matching row then re-querying returns only the NEW row (the previously delivered one is suppressed). max_results paginates within the active tail (first call returns row A, second call with larger limit returns the OTHER row, never re-returning A). This cache is keyed per scope in session.world_query_seen and is cleared on compaction.

HASH/CONFLICT (optimistic concurrency for state updates): query_world_state rows carry 'id' and 'hash'. update with op='update' + expected_hash mismatching the current hash → ok:false, status 'conflict', errors carry expected_hash and actual_hash, and the stored text is unchanged. Successful update returns a new hash. add of a duplicate relationship (same npc_id/target) → ok:false status 'not_added' with existing_id/existing_hash/target. delete requires matching expected_hash. update_world_state description: 'never invent or send id, expected_hash, mode' (model gets ids only from query_world_state).

TOOL RESULT TWO-CHANNEL: .full = full machine JSON without <system-reminder>; .model = compact structured text WITH a trailing <system-reminder> that carries model-only follow-up instructions (e.g. dice reminder 'Use the returned total, grade, and margin as fixed'; query 'do not reveal gm/npc-scope secrets'; ask_npc 'call update_world_state'/'call advance_time'). The reminder must NOT be in .full and must NOT be in the tool_result EVENT data. Model text omits args already sent (dice model text 'RESULT: total 20, success, margin +0, natural 17.' contains NO '1d20+3','Wisdom','DC','forced','detail','rolls'). Secrets (GM_SECRET/NPC_PRIVATE/raw fact text) must NOT appear in model text of update_world_state ('WORLD STATE WRITE' summary only).

TOOL ARG NORMALIZATION (_normalize_tool_calls(calls, world, id_prefix='call')): strips null/None and empty-after-strip optional fields, drops null list elements (inventory ['фонарь',None,'']→['фонарь']; fields ['abilities',None,'passive_perception']→['abilities','passive_perception']), drops empty-string options ({label:'',message:''} removed), drops unknown extra keys on option items ('extra':'drop' removed), but PRESERVES explicitly-empty scene lists (present_npcs:[],items:[],exits:[],constraints:[] kept as []). move_npc visible/can_hear: None → field DROPPED (defaults applied downstream: presence.visible/can_hear default True, npc_can_react True); explicit False → kept and applied (visible False/can_hear False → npc_can_react False). set_scene item visible None→True, portable None→False; exit visible None→True. Missing tool-call id is auto-generated as f'{id_prefix}_{n}' (id_prefix='test' → 'test_1'); canonical tool call gets a non-empty id and the recorded function.arguments is compact JSON '{"query":"select:set_scene"}' matching the tool result tool_call_id.

ask_player ENGINE HANDLING: ask_player is resolved engine-side, NOT as a normal tool — its result is non-terminal (.terminal False), emits no gm_tool_call(ask_player) and no tool_result(agent=ask_player), and exactly ONE role:'tool' message is appended whose content is the literal: 'PLAYER OPTIONS
status: buttons shown to player
shown: 4
next: write the final player-facing narration now, then stop; do not call ask_player again.' Too few options (<4) → non-terminal, model text contains 'not_enough_options'. When gm_suggest_options enabled and GM produces narration WITHOUT calling ask_player, the engine does a second pass with tools=None and emits ask_player; if GM never calls it the engine emits an 'error' ('без ask_player'), NOT a repair retry that re-prompts. Ordering: player_options before final narration.

PRE-TOOL NARRATION (prelude) ORDERING: when the GM streams visible content alongside a tool call, the prelude gm_narration is emitted before the gm_tool_call (prelude_idx<tool_idx). FallbackPreludeClient path: when the main call returns a tool call with EMPTY content, run_turn issues a separate prelude call with tools=None (prelude_done True) and the prelude still precedes the tool. The prelude assistant content must NOT be duplicated into the role:'tool' message; instead the tool message carries 'player has already seen prior assistant content' (and that phrase appears in the next request's NON-system messages, not in system; the prelude text appears exactly once in the second request).

TIME: advance_time accumulates within a player turn. Single call: time_export absolute_minutes += minutes, last_advance_minutes=minutes, last_advance_reason=reason; model text 'elapsed: 7 min' but reason is NOT shown to model. Multiple advance_time in one turn then _finalize_turn_time(session): minutes SUM (6+1=7) and reasons JOIN with '; ' ('дорога к караульной; короткий допрос'); time_context shows 'Previous player turn elapsed: 7 minutes'.

APPEND-ONLY EVENT LOG + per-NPC visibility boundaries: Session keeps events (committed), pending (provisional NPC lines this turn), delivered[npc_id] (max seq shown in PRIOR turns), _shown[npc_id] (boundary at this turn's wake), commitments[npc_id] (own memory blocks, capped _COMMIT_BLOCKS=8), observations capped _OBS_CAP=12. draft() stores provisional reply keyed by seq (correction overwrites, seq preserved; empty reply dropped). Private witnesses: a private exchange (witnesses=frozenset{'player','lysa'}) is invisible to borin (observations('borin')=='') before AND after commit_turn. commit_turn moves delivered up to _shown (NOT end of log), records rumor, appends to commitments, extends npc_messages with stored user/assistant pair, sorts events, caps to config.EVENTS_CAP=400, clears pending/_shown.

NPC MEMORY RESET (reset_npc_memory(npc_id)→bool): returns True for ANY real NPC (even with no stored memory; even mareth with nothing), False ONLY for unknown/empty id (and an unknown id must NOT leak into delivered/_shown). On reset it pops npc_messages, npc_summaries, npc_client_state, npc_clients, commitments, pending for that id, and PINS delivered[id]=_shown[id]=self._seq (current max seq) — it does NOT delete the visibility boundary, so observations() does not resurface old events as new. Other NPCs untouched. Regression: an NPC whose delivered already covers the log still sees nothing new post-reset.

DEBUG EDIT GUARDS (apply_debug_edit(npc_id,data)→bool, the single source /debug/npc delegates to): unknown id → False, no mutation. A card-only edit (data has 'fields' but no 'present') must NOT touch presence/visibility/can_hear (visible/can_hear stay False). present=True when already present is a guarded no-op for visibility. A genuine present-state CHANGE toggles present_npcs. Memory reset runs ONLY on truthy data['reset_memory'], after edits, never automatically.

card_revision (NPC and PlayerCharacter): starts 0 on construction and on payloads missing/invalid card_revision. World.update_npc bumps card_revision by 1 ONLY when a CONTENT field actually changes value; 'color' is cosmetic and never bumps; re-setting the same persona value does NOT bump (idempotent); editable content fields are tuple(editable)-{'color'}. PlayerCharacter card_revision bumps similarly (first update_player_character → 1). Persisted in dialog_store payloads.

SECRET LEAK PREVENTION IN PROMPTS & RAG (RAG_ENABLED=False path tested): npc('borin').secret must NOT appear in gm_context, scene_context, _gm_turn_context, the full _gm_request_messages, or the RAG corpus (retrieval_documents). hidden_events and world.canon (gm canon) must NOT appear in any fact() lookup text, scene_context, or RAG corpus; every retrieval doc has kind != 'truth'. fact() on an unrelated query returns status 'unknown', text exactly 'Nothing is reliably known about this in town.'. player_character_export(public=True) must NOT contain gm_notes, but player_character_context() (GM-facing) DOES. entity_refs never leak persona (PERSONA_LEAK_SENTINEL absent) or hidden NPC names not yet learned ('Лиза' absent until known_name set).

RUNTIME SETTINGS contracts: config has NO MAX_TOOL_HOPS attribute (moved to runtime_settings). runtime_settings.defaults()['max_tool_hops']==0, ['stream_gm_content'] is True. max_tool_hops clamps: 0→0, 12→12, 'bad'→0; _clean('999')→MAX_TOOL_HOPS_CAP (100). stream_gm_content_enabled({stream_gm_content:False}) False; _clean({'stream_gm_content':'0'}) → False. gm_suggest_options default False.

STORIES contract: stories.DEFAULT_STORY_ID=='turnvale-murder'; exactly 3 stories with ids {turnvale-murder, frozen-harbor, glass-garden}, each has {id,title,description}. World() defaults to DEFAULT_STORY_ID, story_title 'Убийство в Тёрнвейле'. World.from_story(id) loads the right NPCs (frozen→iva, glass→ella, neither has borin); unknown id raises KeyError. World.from_seed accepts multiple seed shapes (flat, nested scene{}, alias keys public_intro/scene_title/scene_description) and validates NPC ids/names.

PROPER NOUNS: _proper_nouns_line(names) returns 'Keep these proper nouns exactly as written... : <names>.' and must contain 'Борин' but NOT 'Borin' (Cyrillic canonical, no transliteration). Used to pin names through summarize() and prelude generation.
platformConcerns: Paths: config.py and runtime_settings.py build sibling-file paths via Path(__file__).with_name(...): gm_lab_settings.json (GM_SETTINGS_PATH), gm_lab_embeddings.sqlite3 (GM_RAG_CACHE_PATH), .env. The Rust port must resolve these relative to a known app dir cross-platform (prefer an OS app-data dir on win/mac/linux rather than next to the binary).

Atomic settings write (runtime_settings._save): tempfile.mkstemp in the SAME dir + os.replace for atomic rename. On Windows os.replace over an existing file works only when not locked; the Rust port should use the same write-temp-then-atomic-rename pattern (tempfile + std::fs::rename, same volume).

.env loader: config._load_dotenv reads .env next to the module and cwd, does not override existing env vars, strips quotes and trailing ' #' comments. Port must replicate precedence (real env > .env) cross-platform.

Backends are external processes/services, not in this test (GM_BACKEND=mock used): config defines codex (ChatGPT Codex OAuth, browser OAuth on CODEX_AUTH_PORT=1455, CODEX_AUTO_OPEN_BROWSER opens a browser), llamacpp (LLAMA_HOST http://localhost:8080), openai (GM_API_BASE/GM_API_KEY), mock. The OAuth flow (codex_oauth.py) and TLS impersonation (curl_cffi in codex_client.py / tls_cert.py) are platform-sensitive — out of scope for this file but referenced by the contract that thread_id/prompt_cache_key stay stable; the port must keep a stable per-dialog thread id and per-NPC client/thread (reset only on explicit reset_npc_memory).

RAG embeddings server: config RAG_EMBEDDINGS_URL http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/embeddings, model qwen3-embedding-4b-q4, encoding base64, cache sqlite. Contracts here run with RAG_ENABLED=False; the port must keep RAG strictly excluding gm/npc/truth scopes from the corpus regardless of platform.
No GPU/ffmpeg/socket logic is exercised by test_contracts.py itself; TTS (tts_enabled/tts_autoplay) and Codex transcription (codex_transcribe.py) are toggles only.

Unicode/Cyrillic everywhere: all player-facing strings and most tool-arg values are Russian; json.dumps(..., ensure_ascii=False) is used throughout. The Rust port must use UTF-8 end to end and must NOT escape non-ASCII when serializing (serde_json does this by default).
rustNotes:
Model the tool-result two-channel as a struct: struct ToolResult { full: String /* machine JSON, no reminder */, model: String /* compact text, may end with 

<system-reminder>...</system-reminder> */, terminal: bool /* default false */ }. Provide helpers full_text()/model_text() and a strip_reminders() that splits on "

<system-reminder>". A test-equivalent must assert model_text is non-empty, does not start with '{'/'[', and does not parse as JSON.

Prompt assembly must be pure string building with a FIXED section order; encode the GM static system as a single const &str (== prompts.GM_SYSTEM) returned unchanged so the cache prefix is byte-stable. Build the late turn (CURRENT TURN CONTEXT) in the exact section order listed in payloadShapes; assert via integration tests that 'TURN RESOLUTION CHECK' and '<system-reminder>' both precede 'PLAYER ACTION'.

Tool schemas: define them statically (e.g. serde_json::json! consts or a build-time table) with NO dynamic enums and NO embedded NPC names. npc_id is always {type:'string'}. Keep two name sets: INITIAL_GM_TOOLS (the exact 8) and the hidden set loaded via tool_search; tool_search 'select:' and keyword paths gate loading. Use an ordered/canonical JSON serializer for tool_calls so function.arguments == '{"query":"select:set_scene"}' (compact, key order preserved) to keep tool_call_id/result pairing stable.

Token estimate: fn estimate_tokens(s:&str)->usize { s.chars().count()/CHARS_PER_TOKEN } — IMPORTANT: Python len() on a str counts Unicode code points, not bytes; use chars().count() not s.len() (bytes) to match Russian-text behavior. CHARS_PER_TOKEN default 3.

Compaction: implement _maybe_compact / _maybe_compact_npc as: gate on estimate_tokens(history) >= threshold AND user-start count > keep; cut at the keep-th-from-last user start; summarize old via client.summarize(prev_summary+old_text, proper_nouns); keep recent verbatim; on GM compaction CLEAR the per-turn world_query_seen cache. Thresholds: GM 100000/keep 3, NPC 64000/keep 6, compact input truncated to 12000 chars.

Deterministic dice: keep an injectable RNG plus forced_die_next:Option<i64> (one-shot, set to None after one roll) and forced_die_all:Option<i64> (sticky). Parse notation with regex (\d*)d(\d+)(k[hl]\d+)?([+-]\d+)?. Never surface 'forced' in player/model output. Grade in roll_for_outcome with margin=total-target (signed), natural-20/1 crit on attack rolls, ungraded for damage.

Scope/secret model: store world state records with an explicit scope enum {Public, Gm, Npc, Participants(owner, subjects:set)} (note 'shared' input maps to Participants internally). Implement query filtering and retrieval_documents(actor) as a single visibility predicate reused by both, so RAG and query can never diverge. Enforce: player scope hides gm/npc/goal; participant rows visible to player+each participant only; kind=='truth'/gm canon/hidden_events NEVER enter any corpus.

Optimistic concurrency: give each mutable record an id and a content hash (e.g. blake3/sha256 of canonical fields); update requires expected_hash==current else return conflict{expected,actual}; duplicate add returns not_added{existing_id,existing_hash}. Return new hash on success.

Per-turn de-dup: world_query_seen: HashMap<Scope, HashSet<RowKey>>; on each get_world_fact/query_world_state, suppress already-seen keys and report already_delivered; clear the whole map on compaction. RowKey should be stable per source/row across a turn.

Session event log: keep events:Vec<Event> (append-only, cap 400), pending:HashMap<npc,Draft>, delivered/_shown:HashMap<npc,u64 seq>, commitments:HashMap<npc,Vec<String>> (cap 8), OBS_CAP=12. reset_npc_memory pins delivered/_shown to current seq (do NOT remove); return true for any real npc, false only for unknown/empty. apply_debug_edit must guard presence (touch only on real present-state change) and only reset memory on explicit flag.

card_revision: bump only when a content field's value actually changes; exclude cosmetic 'color'; idempotent re-set must not bump; default 0 from missing/invalid persisted value. Mirror for PlayerCharacter and NPC payloads.

Settings: persist to JSON with atomic temp+rename; clamp max_tool_hops to [0,100], max_output_tokens to [0,cap]; coerce bools from '0'/'false'/'no'/'off'/'' → false. Keep config (startup) separate from runtime_settings (UI-controlled, survive restart) as the Python code does.
risks: This file is a flat assertion SCRIPT, not pytest — it asserts MANY string literals from prompts.py (GM_SYSTEM/NPC_SYSTEM) and tool_guidance.py. I read the asserted substrings but did NOT read prompts.py in full; the exact full prompt text (load-bearing for cache-prefix byte-stability) lives in prompts.py and must be ported verbatim. A faithful port should treat prompts.py + tool_guidance.py as canonical source-of-truth strings, not paraphrase them.

The state-store contracts are conditional: the suite branches on `hasattr(world,'state_records')` (new model) vs the legacy fact_records/commitments/npc.goals model. The current code clearly uses state_records (scope=='participants' for shared). I documented the state_records branch as authoritative; confirm the legacy branch is dead before dropping it in the port.

I read test_contracts.py in FULL (all 2644 lines) and the directly-referenced helpers in config/runtime_settings/tool_guidance/agents/orchestrator/world/dialog_store, but I did NOT exhaustively read world.py (~114k), orchestrator.py (~138k), dialog_store.py (~52k), or stories.py (~114k). Internal behaviors the tests rely on indirectly (e.g. exact fact()/RAG ranking via rag.py, retrieval_documents filtering details, dialog_store SQLite table DDL) need their own subsystem maps; signatures here are copied verbatim but the full bodies were not all reviewed.

Exact <system-reminder> wording is asserted at many call sites (dice, query, get_world_fact, get_npc_profile, ask_npc, update_world_state). These reminder strings are model-behavior-load-bearing and are defined inside orchestrator.py result builders (not all quoted here) — port them verbatim from source, not from the assert substrings (asserts check substrings, not the whole reminder).

The mock client (mock_server.py / make_client with GM_BACKEND=mock) shapes several success-path payloads (ask_npc gm_instruction, known facts about Капитан Марет/Алдрик). Some 'known' results depend on seeded story data in stories.py; the port's test fixtures must reproduce the same seed data (e.g. borin secret 'осведомитель Гильдии воров', Алдрик lore) for these asserts to hold.

chat_stream/chat/chat_json client protocol is implied by the fake clients in the test (return tuple shape: (thinking, content, calls, assistant_msg, stats) for chat_stream; yields ('content', delta)). The exact client interface contract (and Codex/llama variants) is defined in llm_client.py/codex_client.py which I did not fully read — needed for the SSE/turn protocol subsystem map.
